The current loop also closed CallExtern replay receiver-prefix handling with
method-table receiver transfer synthesis, empty-interface concrete-itab
acceptance, dynamic aggregate/integer target preparation, dynamic packed signed
sequence reads, filtered OSR loop-entry coverage for dynamic regressions, and
the OSR CallExtern stack-base refresh gap. The dynamic-assignment
branch-condition audit was filtered as already owned by
`FuncBuilder::canonical_jump_condition` and is now covered by a
codegen/verifier proof. The subsequent red-team pass closed `spawn_call`
raw-slot transfer validation, value-receiver method-expression metadata when
the method table points at an `$iface` wrapper, empty-`param_types` receiver
validation with scalar suffixes, and non-null aggregate interface data
ownership across VM replay, runtime FFI, and dynamic aggregate unboxing. The
latest red-team closure also rejects empty-`param_types` receiver calls with
GC-visible suffix slots at both CallExtern replay and `spawn_call`, writes
canonical FFI return object refs back to VM-visible return slots, and turns
malformed remote SpawnFiber method-receiver payloads into structured errors
instead of closure-build panics. The latest gate-coverage pass also closes the
Studio wasm serialized-bytecode verifier bypass by routing `StudioVoVm`
construction through the same checked decoder used by compile-cache paths,
adds a host-executable app-contract source proof for that decoder, and promotes
the prepared-call stack OSR regression to an `osr-only` contract case with a
loop-entry requirement. The current pass also closes the promoted-method
wrapper receiver metadata split, the wasm `os` sentinel-error interface boxing
fork, and the stale dynamic-map interface assignment proof, with `app-contract`
now passing the affected wasm regressions. This pass also closes same-island
`WakeFiber` activation de-duplication, remote `SpawnFiber` endpoint-registry
rollback after post-unpack build failure, wasm extension-bridge input ABI
metadata validation, and app-contract selection for the host-executable
vo-web runtime-wasm source-contract proof. The follow-up pass closes remote
`WakeFiber` and endpoint-response activation de-duplication, low-level wasm
extension-bridge dispatch ABI validation, and the deferred interface method
wrapper cache identity split that let `io/fs.File.Close` metadata be reused
for `net.Conn.Close`. The latest red-team closure also makes remote island
command preflight own command source/shape before publication, requires select
receive wakes to carry their value payload before scheduler wakeup, restores
runtime rollback on OSR borrow-lease transition rejection, and moves wasm
extension bridge dispatch ABI ownership from a process-global extern-id side
table into the resolved extern call context.

Current Rust source remains the implementation truth; this file is a
release-readiness tracker and execution record, not proof by itself.

This plan names the contracts, workstreams, tests, and performance gates that
must become true in source and CI before the VM is called production-ready.

## Document Role

This file is the top-level readiness program. It does not replace the focused
VM, verifier, GC, JIT, or runtime-boundary documents. Instead, it ties them to
one production gate and makes tradeoffs explicit.

Use this document to decide:

- which VM/JIT/GC/verifier work is release-blocking;
- which invariant a fix must prove;
- which tests and benchmarks must be updated with each landing slice;
- when JIT may be considered production-enabled instead of best-effort;
- when a document claim is backed by executable source and CI evidence.

Do not use this file as proof that the VM is production-ready. The proof is the
source, tests, fuzz/model coverage, and performance gates passing together.

## Production-Ready Definition

The VM is production-ready only when all of these are true at the same time:

- verified bytecode cannot trigger Rust panic, out-of-bounds access, or
  undefined behavior through bytecode shape alone;
- recoverable language failures become language panics or `VmError`, not host
  process crashes;
- every scheduler mutation that parks, wakes, resumes, replays, or crosses
  fibers goes through the runtime-boundary applier or command bridge;
- every root-bearing boundary, wait record, pending payload, frame, replay log,
  endpoint, and JIT materialization record is visible to the precise GC root
  scanner or advances the dirty-root protocol before GC can observe stale
  roots;
- JIT, OSR, extern bridge, and interpreter paths have the same observable
  semantics for production-supported bytecode;
- unsafe blocks have a local proof chain from verifier fact, runtime check,
  type invariant, or GC/root invariant to the raw operation;
- hot interpreter and JIT paths keep their measured performance envelope;
- the production gate is runnable through `vo-dev` and stable in CI.

## Non-Negotiable Engineering Rules

1. Correctness, architecture, and performance are co-equal gates. A patch that
   improves one while regressing another is not complete.
2. The hot interpreter opcode loop must not absorb broad runtime-boundary
   virtualization. Boundary unification belongs at cold scheduler/runtime
   edges, not in every instruction arm.
3. Load-time verification should prove bytecode and metadata shape whenever
   possible. Runtime checks should be defensive and cheap, with cold error
   paths returning structured errors.
4. JIT is an accelerator over VM semantics, not a second semantics engine. JIT
   facts must derive from `vo-jit/src/semantics` and shared verifier facts.
5. Runtime-boundary docs are contracts, not proof. Every invariant must have at
   least one focused regression or model test.
6. Fuzz crashes and model failures must be reduced into deterministic
   regressions before the corresponding fix lands.
7. Performance-sensitive changes must record the hot path they touch and the
   benchmark or measurement that protects it.

## Current Release Blockers

Status, 2026-06-18 follow-up audit: the ledger below is the current blocker
list. Rows marked `Open` remain active release blockers. Rows marked
`Source-closed` have proof tests and source fixes, but rows whose focused
proofs have not executed from the current source state still require gate
closure. Historical 2026-06-16 validation remains evidence for the previous
blocker set, not current signoff.

Final gate closure, 2026-06-23: the previously restored source state
`vm-production-current-source:218a1bfaa378141b5c0be94d11b18ec34d7d07e392950f677efc0db233d656f3`
is now historical. The 2026-06-23 follow-up rows below are closed by focused

`vm-production-current-source:db37d60cb448118ff83cbbcdc74fc3649cdef48be32025e0e6b7a4021418d880`.
Rows below whose closure-gate cells preserve earlier default-target

blockers.

Current source-state update, 2026-06-24: the uncommitted hardening/refactor
work has changed the source-state hash after the tracked 2026-06-23 evidence.
Production signoff remains withdrawn until the final selectors pass again and
write matching evidence for this source state.

Hardening policy during withdrawal:

- do not claim VM production readiness while any active blocker row remains
  open;
- do not add VM/JIT/GC/runtime-boundary feature work unless it closes, proves,
  or explicitly scopes a readiness blocker or preserves the source contracts
  below;
- land tests before or with fixes, and name the blocker ID in the test or
  tracker entry;
- update `eng/tasks.toml` and `cmd/vo-dev` when a blocker exposes a missing
  production-gate surface;
- keep JIT production status conditional until interpreter/JIT/OSR/extern
  parity gates cover the affected bytecode;
- prefer contract tests that enumerate every producer or raw access site over
  one-off shape regressions.

| ID | Status | Contract | Blocking Shape | First Proof Artifact | Closure Gate |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| VM-WAKEFIBER-TRANSPORT-SOURCE-062 | Closed, 2026-06-23 | A raw `WakeFiber` island command must not be an unauthenticated remote wake path; cross-island queue waiters must resume through endpoint responses, while same-island wake commands must authenticate the transport envelope. | Runtime-boundary audit found that `dispatch_island_command_from` ignored the envelope `source_island_id` for `IslandCommand::WakeFiber`, so a forged remote command could wake a local queue waiter. The same audit found sibling side paths that still constructed or published bare `WakeFiber` for remote queue waiters instead of converging on endpoint responses, including the low-level `VmState::wake_waiter` remote branch. | Failing-first proofs `vm_direct_wake_fiber_ingress_062_rejects_forged_source_before_local_wake` and `remote_waiter_rejects_direct_wakefiber_bypass_062` accepted forged or direct remote `WakeFiber` wake paths before the fix. The fix rejects `WakeFiber` when the envelope source is not the current island, mirrors that rejection in island-thread command handling, removes remote bare-waiter `WakeFiber` publication from runtime-boundary wake staging, deletes the direct `send_wake_to_island` helper, and makes remote ordinary waiters runtime-boundary-only so endpoint responses own cross-island wake semantics. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-wake-proof cargo test -p vo-vm vm_direct_wake_fiber_ingress_062 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-wake-proof cargo test -p vo-vm remote_waiter_rejects_direct_wakefiber_bypass_062 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-wake-proof cargo test -p vo-vm remote_wake -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, and `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-wake-proof cargo test -p vo-vm vm_endpoint_request_publication_013_request_publishers_are_boundary_owned -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-WAKEFIBER-QUEUED-INGRESS-SOURCE-062 | Closed, 2026-06-23 | Transport or queued island ingress must reject every bare `WakeFiber`; envelope `source_island_id == current_island_id` is not authentication because the command carries no semantic source or endpoint-response obligation. | Follow-up runtime-boundary red-team found a forged inbound frame could set `target_island_id == source_island_id == victim_current_island`, enter `process_island_commands`, and pass the earlier source-equality check before waking a blocked local queue waiter. | Failing-first proof `vm_queued_wake_fiber_ingress_062_rejects_same_source_transport_frame_before_local_wake` queues a same-source `WakeFiber` frame and requires rejection with the fiber still blocked and its queue wait registration preserved. The fix routes transport/queued command processing through `dispatch_queued_island_command_from`, rejects `WakeFiber` there unconditionally, and makes the std island-thread handler reject `WakeFiber` transport ingress as the same contract. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-wake-proof cargo test -p vo-vm vm_queued_wake_fiber_ingress_062 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-wake-proof cargo test -p vo-vm island_thread_wake_ingress_rejection -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, and the direct/remote WakeFiber proof set; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-HARDENING-GATE-SELECTION-062 | Closed, 2026-06-23 | `vm-hardening` blocker proofs must be selected by `contract`, `vm-production`, and `pr`, not only validated as well-shaped tasks that can drift outside release gates. | Gate-coverage audit found the task lint required hardening tasks to be unfiltered crate tests, but did not require the `vm-hardening` group itself to be reachable from the semantic release selectors. A future manifest edit could keep the tasks valid while dropping the blocker proof surface from `vm-production`. | Failing-first semantic proof tests `lint_vm_hardening_requires_contract_gate_062`, `lint_vm_hardening_requires_vm_production_gate_062`, `lint_vm_hardening_requires_pr_gate_062`, `lint_vm_hardening_requires_all_hardening_tasks_062`, and `lint_vm_hardening_accepts_gate_owned_hardening_selector_062` cover the selector contract. The fix adds `lint_vm_production_selects_vm_hardening_contract`, requiring all three parent gates and every unfiltered hardening crate task through one selector fact source. | Focused validation passed `cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- lint tasks --strict` and `cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- task plan vm-production --format json`, which selected `cargo-test-common-core-hardening`, `cargo-test-jit-hardening`, `cargo-test-vo-source-contract`, `cargo-test-vm-hardening`, and `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`. Direct `cargo test -p vo-dev ...` proof execution hung at test-binary startup in this workspace and is not counted as passed. Selected by `vm-production` through `eng-lint-tasks` and `vo-dev-contract`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-ANALYSIS-PRODUCTION-SCOPE-062 | Closed, 2026-06-23 | Analysis/typechecker source changes selected by VM readiness changed-mode routes must also be covered by the durable `vm-production` selector. | Changed-mode routing already required `lang/crates/vo-analysis/**` to run `cargo-test-analysis` and `vo-test-compile`, but `vm-production` did not include `typechecker-contract`, so final VM evidence could omit the same analysis contract while changed-mode evidence claimed it as readiness coverage. | Failing-first proof `lint_vm_readiness_changed_prefixes_requires_vm_production_analysis_scope_062` rejects a `vm-production` selector that omits `cargo-test-analysis` for the analysis prefix. The fix makes `typechecker-contract` part of `vm-production` in both `[groups]` and group metadata and teaches task lint to compare readiness changed-prefix requirements against the resolved `vm-production` selector. | Focused validation passed `cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- lint tasks --strict` and `cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- task plan vm-production --format json`, which selected `cargo-test-analysis` and `vo-test-compile`. Direct `cargo test -p vo-dev ...` proof execution hung at test-binary startup in this workspace and is not counted as passed. Selected by `vm-production` through `typechecker-contract`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-DOCS-PENDING-FINAL-GATE-SIGNOFF-062 | Closed, 2026-06-23 | Source-closed rows whose closure gate is still pending final selectors are signoff blockers, even when no current row status starts with `Open`. | Gate/docs red-team found docs-lint guarded restored-signoff wording only when current rows started with `Open`; a ledger containing only `Source-closed ... production signoff withdrawn pending final gates` rows could mechanically drift back to restored wording before fresh final evidence. | Failing-first docs-lint self-test classifies pending-final-gates rows as signoff blockers and rejects restored-signoff wording while such rows are present. The fix adds `isSignoffBlockingVmProductionBlockerRow`, uses it for current signoff guarding, and keeps closed final-gate rows non-blocking. | Focused validation passed `node --check scripts/ci/docs_lint.mjs` and `node scripts/ci/docs_lint.mjs`; selected by `vm-production` through `docs-contract`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-HTTP-JIT-CONTRACT-SELECTION-062 | Closed, 2026-06-22 | A readiness row that claims HTTP JIT language proof coverage must be selected by the durable `jit-contract` / `vm-production` manifest path, not only by an ad hoc `vo-test run-plan-job` artifact. | `bugs.2026-01-31-jit-http-server-client` carried `tags = ["jit", "regression"]` but also `skip = ["jit", "osr", "nostd", "wasm"]`, so `vo-test-jit-contract` could match the case by tag and then skip the actual JIT job while the readiness row claimed HTTP JIT contract coverage. Follow-up audit found the case was shape-checked only if present; deleting or renaming it could leave only the OSR sibling, which is not a full-JIT HTTP/native extern proof. | Failing-first strict manifest lint rejected the case with `must not skip target jit`; proof tests `vm_readiness_jit_contract_cases_must_not_skip_jit_061` and `vm_readiness_jit_contract_cases_select_jit_target_061`; fix adds `contract`, removes the `jit` skip, keeps unsupported targets skipped, and updates the reason to current source truth. Required-case proof `vm_readiness_required_cases_must_be_present_062` now requires both the base full-JIT HTTP case and the OSR sibling. | Focused validation passed `cargo test -p vo-dev vm_readiness`, `vo-dev test lint --suite lang --strict`, and the escalated loopback run `./d.py test jit tests/lang/cases/bugs/2026_01_31_jit_http_server_client.vo` with `bugs.2026-01-31-jit-http-server-client::jit` passing; selected by `vm-production` through `jit-contract`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-HTTP-OSR-CONTRACT-SELECTION-062 | Closed, 2026-06-22 | HTTP readiness must prove both full-function JIT execution and the OSR hot-loop path when the readiness row claims JIT/OSR coverage. | The fixed HTTP JIT case deliberately skipped `osr`, leaving no durable OSR-only manifest case with `jit_loop_entries_min`; `vm-production` could therefore select the JIT proof without proving that the HTTP server/client hot loop entered OSR. | Failing-first strict manifest lint rejected the missing required case `bugs.2026-01-31-jit-http-server-client-osr`; fix adds an `osr-only` case on the same source, tags it `jit`, `osr`, and `contract`, requires `jit_loop_entries_min = 1`, and includes it in `VM_READINESS_OSR_LOOP_CONTRACT_CASES_061` plus the required-case lint. | Focused validation passed `cargo test -p vo-dev vm_readiness`, `vo-dev test lint --suite lang --strict`, and the escalated loopback run `./d.py test osr tests/lang/cases/bugs/2026_01_31_jit_http_server_client.vo` with `bugs.2026-01-31-jit-http-server-client-osr::osr` passing; selected by `vm-production` through `jit-contract`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-HTTP-REQUIRED-PATH-CONTRACT-062 | Closed, 2026-06-22 | Required VM readiness language proof IDs must be bound to the intended source path, so a same-ID manifest entry cannot be replaced by an unrelated JIT/OSR loop case. | The strict required-case lint checked only that `bugs.2026-01-31-jit-http-server-client` and `bugs.2026-01-31-jit-http-server-client-osr` existed. A manifest entry could keep the required ID and OSR shape but point at another loop source while readiness evidence still read as HTTP server/client proof coverage. | Mechanical proof `vm_readiness_required_cases_must_use_expected_paths_062` fails when the required HTTP OSR ID points at a non-HTTP case. The fix replaces the required-ID list with required `id -> path` facts for both HTTP cases and keeps the check in strict manifest lint. | Focused validation passed `cargo test -p vo-dev vm_readiness`; selected by `vm-production` through `vo-dev-contract` and by `jit-contract` manifest lint, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-ENDPOINT-RESPONSE-AUTH-DRIFT-062 | Closed, 2026-06-22 | A local endpoint response obligation must be de-duplicated and preflighted from transition-local activation and authority facts, not from endpoint-response authorization that can change during the same commit. | A same-island `EndpointResponse` command that was unauthorized during preflight did not participate in endpoint-response activation de-duplication. A preceding same-transition closed endpoint wake could create local tombstone response authority and consume the pending response before the explicit command ran; a same-transition `EndpointTombstone::with_response_source` could likewise make a stale response active before island command dispatch. Follow-up audit found the reverse drift too: an already authorized same-island response could be invalidated by an earlier same-transition tombstone source replacement and silently no-op after preflight accepted it. | Failing-first proofs `vm_endpoint_response_activation_062_rejects_same_island_authorization_drift_before_commit`, `vm_endpoint_response_activation_062_rejects_tombstone_authorization_drift_before_commit`, and `vm_endpoint_response_activation_062_rejects_tombstone_authority_revocation_before_commit` reproduced late authorization creation and revocation before preflight rejection. The fix makes same-island non-`Closed` endpoint responses with a valid local source participate in the activation set regardless of current authorization, and compares endpoint-response authorization before and after pre-island wake/tombstone effects so both unauthorized-to-authorized and authorized-to-unauthorized drift reject before commit. | Focused validation passed `cargo test -p vo-vm vm_endpoint_response_activation_ -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture` and `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_endpoint_response_activation_ -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-TRANSPORT-TOMBSTONE-REBIND-062 | Closed, 2026-06-22 | Transport unpack of queue handles must preserve endpoint tombstones as authoritative endpoint state; an inbound handle may not recreate a live endpoint over a tombstone while pending responses can still rely on that tombstone authority. | `try_resolve_unpacked_queue_handle` treated a tombstoned endpoint id as absent because `get_live` returned `None`, then created a new remote proxy for an inbound handle with the same endpoint id and home island. That replaced the tombstone before pending endpoint responses had quiesced, so later response authorization could drift or reject against a recreated live endpoint. | Failing-first proof `vm_transport_handle_validation_062_rejects_tombstoned_endpoint_rebind` reproduced a tombstoned endpoint id being rebound as a live remote proxy. The fix adds a structured `TombstonedEndpoint` validation error and rejects tombstoned ids before remote-proxy registration, keeping tombstone lifecycle ownership inside the endpoint registry rather than making transport unpack a bypass. | Focused validation passed `cargo test -p vo-vm vm_transport_handle_validation_ -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-SCHED-RAW-WAKE-CALLSITE-GATE-062 | Closed, 2026-06-22 | Raw scheduler wake appliers must remain owned by the runtime boundary or explicit VM bridge adapters; the hardening gate must cover crate-internal `pub(crate)` surfaces and future production call-site bypasses. | The existing source-contract gate searched only `pub fn try_wake_fiber` / `pub fn wake_queue_waiter`-style strings. Follow-up audits found it also missed `wake_queue_sender_closed`, UFCS or multi-line raw calls, raw identifiers, direct `ready_queue` mutation, assignment/extend/clear/retain/UFCS/destructured-binding runqueue variants, macro-forwarded raw wake/runqueue identifiers, `Scheduler` aliases and function-item forwarding, inherent `Scheduler` wrapper methods that forward raw wake authority transitively, trait impls masquerading as inherent `Vm` / `VmState` boundary owners, scheduler-file free helper indirection, whole-file allowlists for `vm/types.rs` and `runtime_boundary.rs`, public raw runqueue authority, textual `#[cfg(test)]` truncation, production text after the first real test module, and non-`tests` test-module names. Current production call sites were already boundary-owned. | Failing-first proof extended the old surface string list to include `pub(crate) fn try_wake_fiber` and reproduced the blind spot. The final proof `vm_scheduler_wake_boundary_013_raw_wake_appliers_are_vm_boundary_owned` now scans normalized production `.rs` call sites for raw wake member references across receiver aliases/function items/raw identifiers, `wake_queue_sender_closed`, UFCS/multi-line calls, macro-forwarded raw scheduler identifiers, direct run-queue field ownership, and derived inherent `Scheduler` method aliases that forward raw wake calls; `scheduler.rs` grants raw mutation ownership only inside inherent `impl Scheduler`, `runtime_boundary.rs` only inside inherent `impl Vm` `apply_runtime_command` / `apply_runtime_wake`, and `vm/types.rs` only inside the three inherent `impl VmState` bridge functions. Probe proofs cover sender-closed/UFCS/runqueue assignment/extend/clear/retain/destructuring and renamed, shorthand, qualified, and match destructuring mutation, scheduler aliases/function items/raw identifiers, inherent and transitive raw-wake alias methods, macro-forwarded `try_wake_fiber` / `ready_queue`, non-applier runtime helpers, trait-impl owner spoofing, scheduler-file free helpers, same-name free/helper spoofing, comment-spoofed bridge function names, textual `#[cfg(test)]` split bypasses, production code after a test module, and arbitrary `#[cfg(test)] mod <name>` spans by blanking only real test-module ranges. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-main cargo test -p vo-vm vm_scheduler_wake_boundary_013 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture` (8 tests), including the alias/function-item/raw-identifier, trait-impl owner spoof, scheduler-file free-helper, renamed and shorthand destructuring, and transitive inherent raw-wake alias and raw-identifier alias proofs; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-JIT-CALLBACK-SCHEDULER-MUTATOR-GATE-062 | Closed, 2026-06-22 | JIT callbacks must publish scheduler effects as pending `RuntimeTransition`s; scheduler mutation and wake ownership belongs to the VM boundary applier. | Existing callback source tests only rejected direct `apply_runtime_transition`, so a callback could still call scheduler mutators such as `try_wake_fiber`, `wake_queue_sender_closed`, `schedule_next`, or `ready_queue.push_back` directly while still avoiding the old sentinel. Follow-up audits found the first hardening pass omitted raw scheduler variants and VM-owned JIT ABI entries, found the source set was hand-curated instead of derived from `callbacks/mod.rs` / `JitContext`, then found private-module re-export, alias-based unresolved `JitContext` function pointers, multiline `JitContext` `*_fn` assignments, `None`-valued cfg branch fields that swallowed later callback assignments, direct/multiline `cfg_attr`/raw-string path-attributed nested callback implementation modules, visibility- and scoped-visibility nested modules, sibling callback helper modules, braced sibling-helper imports, split `Some(` callback assignments, raw-identifier non-wake scheduler methods, and raw-pointer scheduler aliases, function-item mutators, raw-pointer field-borrow/`std::mem::take` field mutations, raw-pointer field alias mutation independent of pointer variable names, arbitrary test-module naming in production-source erasure, whole-scheduler replacement bypasses, module-alias helper imports, production `include!` implementation files, `std`/`core` include aliases and path-qualified include calls, nested-braced `crate::vm::{jit::{callbacks...}}` use trees, direct `addr_of_mut!` pointers to scheduler fields, grouped and late root-grouped bare parent-module helper imports, and parenthesized raw scheduler-field places. Current production callbacks already publish transitions; the gap was gate coverage. | Mechanical source-contract proof extends `vm_jit_callback_boundary_001_source_guards_callback_abi_and_transition_publication` to scan every callback module derived from callback re-export source modules in `callbacks/mod.rs`, plus `vm/jit/context.rs`, `vm/jit/extern_call.rs`, and `vm/jit/frame.rs`. Source-set proofs `vm_jit_callback_boundary_001_source_set_tracks_callback_exports_062`, `vm_jit_callback_boundary_001_source_set_tracks_private_reexport_modules_062`, `vm_jit_callback_boundary_001_source_set_rejects_unresolved_context_fn_aliases_062`, `vm_jit_callback_boundary_001_source_set_tracks_multiline_context_fn_assignments_062`, `vm_jit_callback_boundary_001_source_set_keeps_scanning_after_none_fn_fields_062`, `vm_jit_callback_boundary_001_source_set_scans_nested_reexport_modules_062`, `vm_jit_callback_boundary_001_source_set_scans_sibling_helper_modules_062`, `vm_jit_callback_boundary_001_source_set_scans_late_root_grouped_parent_modules_062`, and `vm_jit_callback_boundary_001_source_set_scans_include_implementation_files_062` verify scanner coverage for exported callback modules, private re-exports, multiline assignments including split `Some(...)`, `None` branches, production-visible multiline `cfg_attr` raw-string path modules, direct, braced, nested-braced, raw-identifier, qualified, grouped, module-aliased, grouped bare parent-module, and late root-grouped parent-module sibling helpers, production `include!` implementation files including `std` aliases and path-qualified calls, and every resolved `JitContext` function pointer. The mutator fact list covers raw wake/block/spawn/schedule methods, raw identifiers, raw non-wake methods, raw-pointer scheduler aliases, direct scheduler wait-map/fiber-vector/current-fiber/blocked-count/runqueue mutations, `current` field take/replace and scheduler function-item mutator references, whole-field replacement of `Scheduler`, `ready_queue`, and `fibers`, mutable scheduler aliases, mutable field borrows, `addr_of_mut!` scheduler-field pointers including parenthesized raw places, and test-module erasure for any `#[cfg(test)] mod <name>`. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-main cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_jit_callback_boundary_001 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture` (21 tests); selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-SOURCE-CONTRACT-CFG-TEST-SPLIT-062 | Closed, 2026-06-22 | Source-contract tests must inspect production source through one syntax-aware production view, not by splitting at textual `#[cfg(test)]` markers or by hand-rolling partial test-module scanners at each gate. | Remaining VM source-contract gates used `include_str!(...).split("#[cfg(test)]").next()`. A production comment or raw string containing that text could hide later production code from absence checks such as discarded runtime-transition errors, and a real test module could also hide production code after the first test module. Follow-up audit found the first shared helper recognized only exact `#[cfg(test)] mod` shapes and missed cfg expressions such as `#[cfg(all(test, feature = "..."))]`, stacked attributes, and visibility-qualified test modules; scheduler and JIT callback gates also retained dead local scanner forks after delegating to the shared helper. | Failing-first proofs `source_contract_production_view_062_preserves_textual_cfg_test_markers`, `source_contract_production_view_062_erases_named_test_modules`, and `source_contract_production_view_062_erases_cfg_test_expression_modules` reproduced the textual marker, named-module, cfg-expression, stacked-attribute, and visibility-qualified module cases. The fix adds a shared `crate::source_contract::production_source_without_test_modules_062` helper that blanks real braced cfg-test module spans while preserving line offsets and ignoring comments, strings, chars, and raw strings; all remaining `vo-vm` split-based source-contract reads now use the shared helper, and the redundant local scanner forks were removed. | Focused validation passed `cargo test -p vo-vm source_contract_production_view_062 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, `cargo test -p vo-vm vm_scheduler_wake_boundary_013 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, and `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_jit_callback_boundary_001 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening` and `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-SOURCE-CONTRACT-CROSS-CRATE-VIEW-062 | Closed, 2026-06-22 | VM-adjacent source-contract gates in `vo-vm`, `vo-jit`, and `vo-runtime` must share one production-source view so comments, raw strings, or real test modules cannot hide later production code from contract scans. | After the VM helper fix, `vo-jit` and `vo-runtime` still used `.split("#[cfg(test)]").next()` or `.find("#[cfg(test)]")` in source-contract tests for helper registration, materialized-frame restore, prepared slow-path refresh, queue/select metadata lowering, JIT ABI wrapper panic paths, FFI/map/container contracts, and raw object mutation contracts. Those gates could still miss production code after a textual cfg marker or after a real test module. | Failing-first red-team proof found residual textual truncation in `vo-jit` and `vo-runtime`. The fix moves the production view into a shared `vo-source-contract` test-support crate, makes the VM wrapper delegate to that crate, migrates all `vo-jit` and `vo-runtime` textual truncation users to `production_source_without_test_modules_062`, and adds crate-wide regression scans `source_contract_tests_do_not_use_textual_cfg_test_truncation_062` to `vo-vm`, `vo-jit`, and `vo-runtime`. | Focused validation passed `cargo test -p vo-source-contract -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, `cargo test -p vo-vm source_contract -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, `cargo test -p vo-jit source_contract_tests_do_not_use_textual_cfg_test_truncation_062 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, `cargo test -p vo-runtime source_contract_tests_do_not_use_textual_cfg_test_truncation_062 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, full `cargo test -p vo-jit -- --test-threads=1`, and full `cargo test -p vo-runtime -- --test-threads=1`; selected by `vm-production` through the direct `cargo-test-vo-source-contract` task plus the VM/JIT/runtime hardening and contract crate tests, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-SOURCE-CONTRACT-GATE-SELECTION-062 | Closed, 2026-06-22 | A shared VM readiness proof crate must have its own explicit task and changed-prefix route; compiling it as another crate's dev-dependency is not enough evidence that its unit proofs are selected by `contract` or `vm-production`. | The shared `vo-source-contract` crate held the production-view parser proofs, but `vm-production` selected only VM/JIT/runtime crate tests. Those consumers compiled the helper where used, but did not run `cargo test -p vo-source-contract`, and `eng/ci.toml` had no `lang/crates/vo-source-contract/**` changed-prefix route. The previous cross-crate closure evidence therefore overclaimed gate selection for the helper crate's own tests. | Failing-first proof updated `vo-dev`'s VM hardening fact source to require `cargo-test-vo-source-contract`; strict task lint then failed with `eng/tasks.toml missing required VM hardening task cargo-test-vo-source-contract`. The fix adds `cargo-test-vo-source-contract`, includes it in `vm-hardening`, routes `lang/crates/vo-source-contract/**` to that task in changed-mode CI, and adds `lint_vm_readiness_changed_prefixes_requires_source_contract_route_062` plus `lint_vm_readiness_changed_prefixes_requires_source_contract_task_062`. | Focused validation passed `cargo test -p vo-source-contract -- --test-threads=1`, `cargo test -p vo-dev source_contract -- --test-threads=1`, and `cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- lint tasks --strict`; selected by `contract` and `vm-production` through `vm-hardening`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-SOURCE-CONTRACT-GUARD-VARIANTS-062 | Closed, 2026-06-22 | The guard that bans textual cfg-test truncation must reject call-shape, marker-composition, and source-tree module variants, not only exact `.split("#[cfg(test)]")` / `.find("#[cfg(test)]")` fragments. | The first shared guard checked only exact string fragments. Red-team reruns found bypasses through spaced/contextual/raw/escaped marker strings, `split_once`, `splitn`, `rsplitn`, turbofish and UFCS forms, `const` / `let` / `static` / `fn` marker facts, parenthesized/destructured/aliased/module-path/raw-identifier bindings, raw-identifier marker functions, `concat!`, all `stringify!` delimiter forms, block/if marker expressions, marker method calls, argument-taking helper calls, `cfg_attr(..., cfg(test))` marker strings, and cfg expressions such as `#[cfg(all(test, feature = "jit"))]`. without `test`, and raw-identifier `mod r#tests` modules as production-visible, lacked a proof that `#[cfg(not(test))]` modules remain production, scanned macro templates as production module declarations/call sites, missed production path-attributed modules outside `src`, globally excluded files that were both production-reachable and cfg-test path aliases, including in-src default and (`source_contract_production_view_062_erases_cfg_not_not_test_modules`, `..._preserves_cfg_not_test_modules`, `..._erases_raw_identifier_test_modules`, `..._erases_conjunctive_test_only_cfg_attrs`, `..._erases_conditional_cfg_attr_test_only_items`, `..._erases_macro_rules_bodies`), and source-tree variants (`source_contract_production_tree_062_excludes_out_of_line_cfg_test_modules`, The fix parses complete call argument lists after optional turbofish for one owned cfg-test truncation/locator API set, resolves marker facts by fixed point, decodes and concatenates string literals, recognizes all `stringify!` delimiters, models cfg expressions, cfg_attr implication clauses, and stacked attrs with a shared production cfg constraint view, erases macro templates from production scans and module discovery, resolves nested inline path attrs from the inline namespace, lets production module edges override cfg-test aliases to the same file through fixed-point production reachability and keeps path modules and literal production `include!` files, inside or outside `src`, only when a production edge reaches them, keeps call-argument binding checks expression-shaped to avoid closure false positives, and routes JIT offset/source-contract scans through the shared source-tree production view. | Focused validation passed full `vo-source-contract` test binary (74 tests), `cargo test -p vo-jit runtime_abi_manifest_covers_all_generated_jit_context_offsets -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, `cargo test -p vo-runtime source_contract_tests_do_not_use_textual_cfg_test_truncation_062 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, `cargo test -p vo-vm source_contract_tests_do_not_use_textual_cfg_test_truncation_062 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, `cargo test -p vo-jit source_contract_tests_do_not_use_textual_cfg_test_truncation_062 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, `cargo fmt --all --check`, `docs_lint`, strict task lint, and strict language manifest lint; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vo-source-contract`, `runtime-contract`, VM hardening, and JIT contract tasks, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-SOURCE-CONTRACT-CFG-ATTR-ITEM-VIEW-062 | Closed, 2026-06-22 | The shared production source view must erase every item that cannot compile when `test=false`, and the source tree must include production path-attributed modules outside `src` while excluding every possible cfg-test module file. | Final red-team found `cfg_attr(all(), cfg(test))` modules, `#[cfg(test)]` non-module items, `cfg_attr(test, path = "...")` test modules, and production `#[path = "../outside.rs"] mod outside;` modules were outside the helper's original module-only/default-src model. | Failing-first proofs `source_contract_production_view_062_erases_cfg_attr_cfg_test_modules`, `source_contract_production_view_062_erases_cfg_test_non_module_items`, `source_contract_production_tree_062_excludes_cfg_attr_path_attr_cfg_test_modules`, `source_contract_production_tree_062_excludes_cfg_attr_cfg_test_modules`, and `source_contract_production_tree_062_includes_production_path_attr_modules_outside_src`. The fix routes all attr stacks through one `cfg` / `cfg_attr` fact interpreter, erases cfg-test items generically, excludes all active test path candidates, follows production path modules through a de-duplicated source queue, and drops path modules inside or outside `src` when they are reachable only from cfg-test files or inline cfg-test modules. | Focused validation passed `cargo test -p vo-source-contract -- --test-threads=1` (74 tests; current aggregate proof); selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vo-source-contract`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-SCHED-SOURCE-SCANNER-TOKEN-COMMENTS-062 | Closed, 2026-06-22 | Raw scheduler wake ownership scans must treat Rust comments as token whitespace and must scan the shared production source tree, including production path-attributed modules. | Final runtime-boundary red-team found `.try_wake_fiber /* comment */ (` and `Scheduler::wake_queue_waiter /* comment */ (` bypassed the compact pattern scanner, and a production `#[path = "../..."]` module could sit outside the local `src` recursion. | Failing-first proof extends `vm_scheduler_wake_boundary_013_scanner_covers_sender_closed_ufcs_and_queue_mutation_062` with comment-separated method and UFCS wake calls. The fix replaces local source recursion with `vo_source_contract::production_sources_without_test_modules_062` and replaces byte compaction with `compact_rust_source_for_contract_062`, which skips comments as token whitespace. | Focused validation passed `cargo test -p vo-vm vm_scheduler_wake_boundary_013 -- --test-threads=1` and `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_scheduler_wake_boundary_013 -- --test-threads=1`; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-JIT-CALLBACK-NESTED-REEXPORT-GATE-062 | Closed, 2026-06-22 | JIT callback boundary source scans must cover the implementation body behind a callback export, including nested modules re-exported by the scanned module root. | Final JIT red-team found `callbacks/defer.rs`-style modules could re-export `inner::jit_defer_push` while the scanner read only `defer.rs`, leaving `defer/inner.rs` outside scheduler-mutator and callback ABI source checks. | Failing-first proof `vm_jit_callback_boundary_001_source_set_scans_nested_reexport_modules_062` created a re-exporting callback module with the scheduler mutator in the nested implementation file. The fix reads each callback source root plus its same-stem child production source tree and uses the shared comment-aware compact scanner. | Focused validation passed `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_jit_callback_boundary_001 -- --test-threads=1`; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-JIT-CONTEXT-OFFSET-ALIAS-GATE-062 | Closed, 2026-06-22 | JIT runtime ABI offset coverage must treat `JitContext` aliases as the same ABI authority, so alias-qualified `OFFSET_*` constants cannot bypass the manifest proof. | Final JIT ABI red-team found the manifest proof scanned only literal `JitContext::OFFSET_`, missing `use vo_runtime::jit_api::JitContext as RuntimeCtx; RuntimeCtx::OFFSET_*`, multiline braced imports, nested `vo_runtime::{jit_api::{JitContext as RuntimeCtx}}` use-tree imports, nested `vo_runtime::{jit_api::{self as api}}` module aliases, `pub use` aliases, nested `super::RuntimeCtx as NestedCtx` aliases, braced `super::{RuntimeCtx as NestedCtx}` aliases, multiline type aliases derived from those imports, cross-file parent re-export aliases used by child modules, `use vo_runtime::jit_api as api; type ModuleCtx = api::JitContext; ModuleCtx::OFFSET_*`, `use vo_runtime as rt; type RootCtx = rt::jit_api::JitContext; RootCtx::OFFSET_*`, braced `use vo_runtime::{self as rt};` root aliases, and comment/string mentions that could over-report doc-only fields. | Failing-first proofs `runtime_abi_offset_scanner_covers_jit_context_aliases_062`, `runtime_abi_offset_scanner_covers_cross_file_jit_context_aliases_062`, and `runtime_abi_offset_scanner_ignores_comments_and_strings_062` cover import aliases, module aliases, nested `jit_api::{self as api}` aliases, crate-root module aliases, multiline braced imports, nested use-tree imports, public re-export aliases, nested, braced-super, `crate::`, and `self::` aliases, line-local, module-qualified, multiline, scoped-visibility, and visibility-qualified type aliases, parent/child source-set alias propagation, and comment/string erasure. The fix derives a fixed-point qualifier set across the whole production source set, normalizes `vo_runtime::jit_api`, nested `jit_api::{self as ...}`, and `vo_runtime` root aliases to `JitContext`, routes braced and nested use-tree imports through one alias collector, collects every compact-source `OFFSET_*` use, then compares the collected field names with the runtime ABI manifest. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-main cargo test -p vo-jit runtime_abi_offset_scanner_covers_jit_context_aliases_062 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, `... runtime_abi_offset_scanner_covers_cross_file_jit_context_aliases_062 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, `... runtime_abi_offset_scanner_ignores_comments_and_strings_062 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, and `cargo test -p vo-jit runtime_abi_manifest_covers_all_generated_jit_context_offsets -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `vm-production` through `jit-contract`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-SOURCE-CONTRACT-TRACKED-HELPER-062 | Closed, 2026-06-22 | VM source-contract helper modules that are required by test builds must be tracked deliverable source, so clean checkouts and final evidence gates do not depend on local untracked files. | The new `#[cfg(test)] pub(crate) mod source_contract;` declaration in `vo-vm/src/lib.rs` depended on `vo-vm/src/source_contract.rs`, but the helper file was still `??` untracked. A clean tracked checkout would fail `cargo test -p vo-vm`, while the final evidence preflight would reject the local source state before writing current `contract` / `vm-production` evidence. | Failing-first proof `git ls-files --error-unmatch lang/crates/vo-vm/src/source_contract.rs` failed with the file unknown to git. The fix stages the new helper so the source-contract module is part of the tracked source state instead of an ambient local file. | Focused validation passed `git ls-files --error-unmatch lang/crates/vo-vm/src/source_contract.rs`, plus `cargo test -p vo-vm --lib -- --test-threads=1` and `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit --lib -- --test-threads=1`; final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-JIT-CONTEXT-OFFSET-SCAN-062 | Closed, 2026-06-22 | The JIT ABI manifest proof must cover every production `JitContext::OFFSET_*` use under `vo-jit/src`, not a hand-curated subset of lowering files. | `runtime_abi_manifest_covers_all_generated_jit_context_offsets` scanned a fixed include list and missed production lowering files such as `call_helpers/dynamic/closure.rs`, `call_helpers/dynamic/iface.rs`, `call_helpers/prepared.rs`, and sibling helper modules that use `JitContext::OFFSET_*`. A future callback offset could therefore drift outside `jit_context_abi_fields()` while the manifest proof still passed. | Red-team proof used `rg` to show omitted production offset users. The fix replaces the hand-written include list with a recursive `src/**/*.rs` reader, applies `vo_source_contract::production_source_without_test_modules_062` to each file, and keeps the manifest check against `jit_context_abi_fields()` as the single ABI fact source. | Focused validation passed `cargo test -p vo-jit runtime_abi_manifest_covers_all_generated_jit_context_offsets -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `vm-production` through `jit-contract` / `cargo-test-jit`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-FRAME-SCAN-SLOTS-LOCAL-SHAPE-062 | Closed, 2026-06-22 | VM frame setup and JIT frame materialization must reject functions whose `param_slots` or `gc_scan_slots` exceed `local_slots` before zeroing stack slots, mutating caller scan state, publishing JIT extern suspend payloads, pushing frames, running defers, or filling dynamic-call caches. | Red-team found `Fiber::try_push_borrowed_call_frame` still asserted when `scan_slots > local_slots`, and JIT prepared-call materialization could zero using `gc_scan_slots` before any safe frame-shape rejection. Later frame red-team found static defer invocation needed the same shared frame-shape fact before stack mutation, and JIT `CallExtern -> CallClosure` could publish `fiber.jit_extern_suspend` before validating the closure target's callee frame shape, allowing the wait transition to materialize frames before the malformed replay call rejected. Verified modules should not reach this shape, but hardening paths must convert metadata drift into structured VM/JIT errors rather than panics or partial publication. | Failing-first proofs `borrowed_call_frame_rejects_scan_slots_beyond_locals_without_panic_062`, `vm_jit_prepared_call_rejects_scan_slots_beyond_locals_before_zeroing_062`, `vm_jit_regular_call_rejects_scan_slots_beyond_locals_before_frame_push_062`, `vm_extern_replay_call_frame_shape_062_rejects_scan_slots_beyond_locals_before_frame_publication`, `vm_defer_static_frame_shape_062_rejects_scan_slots_beyond_locals_before_stack_mutation`, `vm_jit_extern_suspend_062_typed_replay_args_rejects_scan_slots_beyond_locals_before_payload_publication`, `vm_jit_extern_suspend_062_rejects_scan_slots_beyond_locals_before_suspend_publication`, and `vm_jit_extern_suspend_062_validates_callclosure_payload_before_suspend_publication`; the fix replaces the borrowed-frame assert with a structured `FiberCapacityError::StackSlots` return, makes function arg-shape and typed extern replay arg validation consume `validate_call_frame_shape`, and adds shared frame-shape preflight before extern replay, JIT extern suspend publication, prepared JIT, regular JIT, or defer frame publication/zeroing. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-main cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit scan_slots_beyond_locals -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture` (12 tests), `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_jit_extern_suspend_062 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, `cargo test -p vo-vm borrowed_call_frame_rejects_scan_slots_beyond_locals_without_panic_062 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, and `cargo test -p vo-vm vm_call_iface_contract_061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening` and `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-SOURCE-CONTRACT-INCLUDE-VIEW-062 | Closed, 2026-06-22 | The shared production source tree must follow every production `include!` file that rustc sees, and must not include files reachable only from cfg-test items or shadowed include macros. | Red-team found `production_include_files_062` scanned raw root source, so cfg-test-only module/function includes could enter the production tree, while legal `include! { ... }` and `include! [ ... ]` macro delimiter forms were missed. Later red-team found comment-wrapped `concat!`, bracket-delimited `concat!`, `env!("CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR", "...")`, char-literal separators, local `macro_rules! include`, `#[macro_export]` child-module include macros, `#[macro_use]` inline child-module include macros, `std`/`core` include aliases, path-qualified `std::include!` under a local bare `include` macro, post-declaration `#[macro_export] macro_rules! include` shadowing, and `r`-prefixed include aliases such as `rust_include!` were still outside the same include fact source. | Failing-first proofs `source_contract_production_tree_062_includes_production_include_files`, `source_contract_production_tree_062_excludes_cfg_test_include_files`, `source_contract_production_tree_062_ignores_shadowed_include_macro_files`, `source_contract_production_tree_062_tracks_std_include_alias_names`, `source_contract_production_tree_062_scans_std_include_alias_invocations`, and `source_contract_production_tree_062_scans_known_include_alias_invocations`; the fix scans includes from `production_source_without_test_modules_062`, parses include arguments through the shared macro-delimiter matcher, decodes literal/char/env concat arguments, tracks `std`/`core` include aliases as builtin include names, treats path-qualified builtin includes separately from locally shadowed bare includes, lets `r`-prefixed identifiers fall through raw-string detection into identifier scanning, exposes `production_include_files_from_production_source_062` so production source and original macro-scope facts stay unified, and carries production `macro_rules!` scope including local, crate-root `macro_export`, and `macro_use` shadowing so shadowed `include!` calls do not add files rustc never includes. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-main cargo test -p vo-source-contract -- --test-threads=1` (74 tests); selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vo-source-contract`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-SCHED-DESTRUCTURE-PREFIX-ALIAS-062 | Closed, 2026-06-22 | Scheduler free helpers must not hide `ready_queue` ownership behind destructured aliases, including binding-prefix forms such as `ref mut ready_queue`. | Runtime-boundary red-team found `let Scheduler { ref mut ready_queue, .. } = *scheduler; ready_queue.clear()` bypassed the destructuring scanner because the compact token before `ready_queue` was still an identifier character. A raw-identifier macro-forwarding probe was also checked and was already covered by the existing identifier scanner. | Failing-first proof extends `vm_scheduler_wake_boundary_013_scanner_rejects_scheduler_file_free_helpers_062`; the fix makes the destructured-field parser treat `ref`, `mut`, and `ref mut` binding prefixes as field boundaries instead of adding call-site guards. | Focused validation passed `cargo test -p vo-vm vm_scheduler_wake_boundary_013_scanner_rejects_scheduler_file_free_helpers_062 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-SCHED-ALIAS-MACRO-SCANNER-062 | Closed, 2026-06-22 | Scheduler source-contract scanners must resolve local aliases of `Scheduler` and of inherent raw-wake wrapper methods before deciding whether a free helper or macro can mutate scheduler state. | Follow-up runtime-boundary red-team found `use crate::scheduler::Scheduler as S; let S { ref mut ready_queue, .. } = *scheduler;` bypassed destructuring scans, and `call_sched!(vm.scheduler, raw_wake_alias, fid)` bypassed macro-forward scans even when `raw_wake_alias` was already known to forward to `try_wake_fiber`. The next red-team passes found root-grouped, nested-grouped, raw-identifier, `self::`, `super::`, and type aliases of `Scheduler`, nested same-name `Scheduler` owner spoofing through scoped-visibility and attribute-prefixed modules, nested same-name `Vm` / `VmState` owner spoofing in runtime-boundary allowlists, `mod/**/owner_spoof` and visibility-comment module declarations, raw-identifier raw-wake macro names, macro aliases/reexports, methods that forward raw wake through a local macro body, macro-to-macro raw-wake wrappers, grouped macro aliases after earlier grouped items, and grouped scheduler aliases after earlier use-tree items were still outside the same fact source. | Failing-first proofs extended `vm_scheduler_wake_boundary_013_scanner_rejects_scheduler_file_free_helpers_062`, `vm_scheduler_wake_boundary_013_scanner_rejects_non_applier_runtime_helpers_062`, and `vm_scheduler_wake_boundary_013_scanner_tracks_inherent_raw_wake_aliases_062`; the fix adds source-local `Scheduler` alias discovery for direct, grouped, nested-grouped, raw-identifier, `self::`, `super::`, and type aliases anywhere in a use-tree, deduplicates destructured ready-queue evidence by source location, rejects nested-module same-name owner spoofing for `Scheduler`, `Vm`, and `VmState` across scoped visibility, same-line attributes, and comment-token module spellings, derives raw-wake alias methods through the same global production macro fact source used by the gate, collects raw-wake macro definitions as a fixed point across macro bodies, and propagates direct, grouped, raw, and re-exported macro aliases into macro-forward scanning. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-main cargo test -p vo-vm vm_scheduler_wake_boundary_013 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture` (8 tests), including root/nested-grouped/raw/self/super/type alias, late grouped alias, scoped, attribute-prefixed, and comment-token nested owner-spoof, `Vm` / `VmState` owner spoof, raw macro name, macro wrapper, grouped macro alias, macro alias, and macro-body raw-wake proofs; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-JIT-CALLBACK-GROUPED-HELPER-SOURCE-062 | Closed, 2026-06-22 | JIT callback boundary scans must include helper modules reachable through crate-qualified grouped imports, not only direct `super::` or direct fully qualified helper paths. | JIT red-team found `use crate::vm::jit::callbacks::{dangerous as d, nested::{wake}};` could call a sibling helper whose scheduler mutation never entered the callback source set. | Failing-first proof extends `vm_jit_callback_boundary_001_source_set_scans_sibling_helper_modules_062`; the fix routes `use crate::vm::jit::callbacks::...` through the same module-name extractor as `use super::...`, covering grouped, aliased, and nested imports. | Focused validation passed `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_jit_callback_boundary_001_source_set_scans_sibling_helper_modules_062 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-JIT-CALLBACK-SOURCE-SET-ALIAS-INCLUDE-062 | Closed, 2026-06-22 | JIT callback source-set construction must follow the same production module/include graph for helper bodies regardless of whether a callback imports helpers through `callbacks as cb`, `super as scb`, grouped or nested crate paths, nested-braced `crate::vm::{jit::{callbacks...}}` use trees, visibility-qualified `use`, or `include!`. | Follow-up JIT/runtime-boundary red-team found `use crate::vm::jit::callbacks as cb; cb::helper::wake()`, `use super as scb; scb::helper::wake()`, root-grouped `use crate::{vm::jit::callbacks::helper::wake};`, and `include!("helper.inc")` implementations could leave helper scheduler mutations outside the callback boundary scan. The next passes found comment-wrapped `include!` tokens, comment-wrapped or bracket-delimited `concat!` paths, `concat!(env!("CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR"), "...")`, `env!("CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR", "...")`, char-literal path separators, `std` include aliases, path-qualified `std::include!`, locally shadowed `include!`, `pub(crate) use super::helper::wake;`, `use crate::vm::{jit::callbacks::helper::wake};`, `use crate::vm::{jit::callbacks as cb}; cb::helper::wake()`, `use crate::vm::{jit::{callbacks::helper::wake}};`, and `use crate::vm::{jit::{callbacks as cb}}; cb::helper::wake()` helper imports were not all included by the shared source set, so callback helper bodies could still disappear from the boundary scan. | Failing-first proofs extended `vm_jit_callback_boundary_001_source_set_scans_sibling_helper_modules_062`, `vm_jit_callback_boundary_001_source_set_scans_include_implementation_files_062`, and `vm_jit_callback_boundary_001_source_set_ignores_shadowed_include_implementation_files_062`; the fix adds callbacks-module alias discovery for direct, braced, grouped-at-`jit`, nested `crate::vm`, and nested-braced `crate::vm` aliases, root/nested-grouped callback path scanning, visibility-prefix handling for use statements, and reuses the shared production include graph through `production_include_files_from_production_source_062` so production include calls and original macro-scope facts stay unified. The shared include graph skips Rust trivia around `include!` and `concat!`, accepts all Rust macro delimiters, decodes literal, char, env, std/core alias, and path-qualified include arguments, and ignores shadowed `include!` macros. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-main cargo test -p vo-source-contract -- --test-threads=1` (74 tests) and `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-main cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_jit_callback_boundary_001 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture` (20 tests), plus the exact alias/include proof filters; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vo-source-contract` and `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-JIT-CALLBACK-RAW-FIELD-POINTER-062 | Closed, 2026-06-22 | JIT callbacks must not take raw pointers directly to scheduler internals, because raw field pointers bypass the callback transition-publication boundary even when later mutation happens through an alias. | Follow-up runtime-boundary red-team found `core::ptr::addr_of_mut!(vm.scheduler.ready_queue)` and `core::ptr::addr_of_mut!((*ctx_ref.vm).scheduler.fibers)` were not caught by the existing raw alias scanner. The next pass found raw-identifier field paths such as `(*ctx_ref.vm).r#scheduler.r#ready_queue` were a spelling bypass of the same pointer leak, and the latest JIT red-team found parenthesized raw places such as `addr_of_mut!(((*raw_sched)).ready_queue)` and `&raw mut ((*raw_sched)).fibers`. | Failing-first proof extends `vm_jit_callback_boundary_001_scanner_rejects_scheduler_mutators_062` and adds `vm_jit_callback_boundary_001_scanner_rejects_parenthesized_raw_field_places_062`; the fix treats `addr_of_mut!` of scheduler sensitive fields, including raw-identifier scheduler/field spellings and parenthesized raw pointer places, as raw scheduler authority leakage in the callback mutator scanner. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-main cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_jit_callback_boundary_001 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture` (20 tests); selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-CALL-FRAME-SHAPE-ENTRYPOINTS-062 | Closed, 2026-06-22 | All borrowed VM/JIT call entrypoints must consume one call-frame shape fact source so malformed `param_slots` or `gc_scan_slots` drift becomes a structured VM/JIT error before frame or suspend publication, not a language StackOverflow trap. | Follow-up frame-call red-team found the JIT materialization path had a shape preflight, but interpreter `Call`, `CallClosure`, `CallIface`, and direct `dispatch_jit_call` still relied on `try_push_borrowed_call_frame` and translated the resulting capacity error into StackOverflow/Panic. The next passes found `FrameCallBuilder::call_extern_replay_closure`, static defer invocation, and JIT extern `CallClosure` suspend publication had sibling paths that could reach stack/window/frame or suspend mutation without proving the shared shape contract first. | Failing-first proofs `vm_call_rejects_scan_slots_beyond_locals_before_stack_overflow_trap_062`, `vm_call_closure_rejects_scan_slots_beyond_locals_before_stack_overflow_trap_062`, `vm_call_iface_rejects_scan_slots_beyond_locals_before_ic_mutation_062`, `vm_jit_dispatch_rejects_scan_slots_beyond_locals_before_stack_overflow_trap_062`, `vm_extern_replay_call_frame_shape_062_rejects_scan_slots_beyond_locals_before_frame_publication`, `vm_defer_static_frame_shape_062_rejects_scan_slots_beyond_locals_before_stack_mutation`, and the `vm_jit_extern_suspend_062_*` proof set; the fix moves call-frame shape validation into `frame_call`, makes function arg-shape and typed extern replay arg validation consume that fact source, and routes interpreter, closure/interface, extern replay closure, JIT extern suspend setup, static defer invocation, JIT dispatch, materialization entrypoints, and prepared callback entrypoints through it before publication. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-main cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit scan_slots_beyond_locals -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture` (12 tests), `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_jit_extern_suspend_062 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture` (3 tests), `cargo test -p vo-vm vm_extern_replay_call_frame_shape_062 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, and `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_jit_prepared_call_frame_shape_062 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture` (2 tests); selected by VM hardening and JIT hardening gates, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-JIT-PREPARED-CALL-FRAME-SHAPE-062 | Closed, 2026-06-22 | JIT prepared closure/interface callbacks must prove callee frame shape before invoking `push_frame_fn` or copying user/receiver arguments into the callee frame. | Follow-up frame-call red-team found `jit_prepare_closure_call` could accept a malformed closure target with `param_slots > local_slots` and call `push_frame_fn` before the later VM materialization guard; iface variants were rechecked and are now covered directly by the same shared validator rather than relying on receiver/callsite preconditions. | Failing-first proof `vm_jit_prepared_call_frame_shape_062_closure_rejects_param_slots_beyond_locals_before_frame_push` returned `Ok` before the fix; iface proof `vm_jit_prepared_call_frame_shape_062_iface_rejects_scan_slots_beyond_locals_before_frame_push` protects the sibling path. The fix calls `validate_call_frame_shape` in both prepared callback entrypoints before frame push. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-main cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_jit_prepared_call_frame_shape_062 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture` (2 tests), and the iface proof is also selected by `scan_slots_beyond_locals`; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-JIT-CALL-RETURN-WINDOW-062 | Closed, 2026-06-22 | JIT prepared and regular VM-call materialization must reject caller return windows outside the actual caller frame before pushing a callee frame or publishing materialized resume frames. | JIT/ABI red-team found `setup_prepared_call` and `setup_regular_call` validated callee frame shape but not `call_ret_reg + callee_ret_slots <= caller.local_slots`; a follow-up found prepared calls validated against the stale materialized frame before `resume_stack` materialization, while the actual caller after materialization is the innermost resume point. The next passes found prepared closure/interface callbacks could still call `push_frame_fn` before proving the caller return window, and regular-call materialization with a non-empty `resume_stack` could publish frames and clear resume roots before a later return-window error. | Failing-first proofs `vm_jit_prepared_call_rejects_return_window_beyond_caller_locals_before_frame_push_062`, `vm_jit_regular_call_rejects_return_window_beyond_caller_locals_before_frame_push_062`, `vm_jit_prepared_call_rejects_return_window_beyond_caller_locals_against_materialized_resume_caller_062`, `vm_jit_regular_call_rejects_return_window_beyond_caller_locals_against_materialized_resume_caller_transactionally_062`, `vm_jit_prepared_call_return_window_beyond_caller_locals_062_closure_rejects_before_frame_push`, and `vm_jit_prepared_call_return_window_beyond_caller_locals_062_iface_rejects_before_frame_push`; the fix adds a shared `validate_call_return_window` fact source, makes prepared and regular materialization choose the innermost resume point as caller when `resume_stack` is non-empty, and routes prepared closure/interface callbacks through the same return-window preflight before `push_frame_fn`, argument copies, or resume-stack materialization. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-main cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit return_window_beyond_caller_locals -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture` (6 tests); selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-JIT-PUSH-FRAME-PREFLIGHT-062 | Closed, 2026-06-22 | `jit_push_frame` must prove the callee frame shape and caller return window before reserving stack space or publishing `fiber.sp`, `ctx.jit_bp`, or `ctx.fiber_sp`. | Round 25 frame-call red-team found `jit_push_frame` accepted `func_id`, `ret_reg`, and `ret_slots` but ignored them, reserving stack and publishing the callback frame before `jit_push_resume_point` or later materialization could validate the shared frame and return-window contracts. A malformed JIT ABI call could therefore expose an uncommitted frame/root window before the structured error. | Failing-first proofs `vm_jit_resume_point_contract_062_push_frame_rejects_frame_shape_before_stack_publication` and `vm_jit_resume_point_contract_062_push_frame_rejects_return_window_before_stack_publication` assert null return, unchanged `fiber.sp`, unchanged `ctx.jit_bp` / `ctx.fiber_sp`, and an infra error before publication. The fix converts return-window widths before publication, routes `jit_push_frame` through `validate_call_frame_shape` and `validate_call_return_window` with `ctx.current_func_id` as caller, and only reserves/publishes after those shared validators pass. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-main cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_jit_resume_point_contract_062_push_frame -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture` (2 tests) and the full `vm_jit_resume_point_contract_062` filter (6 tests); selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-GOISLAND-NONDOMINATING-BRANCH-VARIANTS-062 | Closed, 2026-06-22 | GoIsland source-order proofs must reject validator tokens inside obvious compile-time-false branches, not only `if false`, closure, async, or nested function bodies. | Interpreter/frame red-team found `if cfg!(any()) { validators }` and `match false { true => validators, false => {} }` could satisfy the compact token-order proof even though the validators do not dominate `commit_go_spawn` or `commit_go_island_commands`. | Failing-first proof `vm_goisland_object_kind_002_source_contract_rejects_cfg_false_spoofed_validators`; the fix extends `compact_source_without_non_dominating_blocks_062` to remove those compile-time-false blocks before checking validator-before-publication order. | Focused validation passed `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_goisland_object_kind_002_source_contract_rejects_cfg_false_spoofed_validators -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by VM/JIT hardening gates, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-OSR-REQUIRED-LOOP-CASE-RETENTION-062 | Closed, 2026-06-22 | Readiness-critical OSR loop-entry language proofs must be required by ID and path, not only shape-validated if a matching manifest entry happens to exist. | `VM_READINESS_OSR_LOOP_CONTRACT_CASES_061` enforced `osr-only`, `contract` tags, and `jit_loop_entries_min` only after a case was found in the manifest. The required-case list protected the HTTP JIT/OSR pair but did not protect `bugs.2026-06-15-osr-prepared-call-stack` or the three dynamic OSR loop cases from deletion or renaming. | Failing-first deletion-style proof `vm_readiness_required_cases_include_osr_loop_contract_set_062` removes `dyn.osr-dynamic-integer-key-contract` from a full required-case fixture and expects strict validation to fail. The fix adds all OSR loop-entry contract cases to `VM_READINESS_REQUIRED_CASES_062` with exact paths, preserving the existing shape validator as the contract owner for matrix/tags/loop-entry expectations. | Focused validation passed `cargo test -p vo-dev vm_readiness_required_cases -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture` and `cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- test lint --suite lang --strict`; selected by `vm-production` through `vo-dev-contract`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-DEFER-CLOSURE-TARGET-PUBLISH-062 | Closed, 2026-06-22 | Closure-form defer registration must validate and canonicalize non-null closure targets before publishing a `DeferEntry` or any GC-visible defer root, while preserving nil deferred function semantics for unwind-time panic/recover. | Interpreter `exec_defer_push` and JIT `jit_defer_push` read the closure slot and immediately stored it in `defer_stack`; the verifier proved only `GcRef` layout, so a valid non-closure heap object could become a defer root and fail later during unwind instead of being rejected before state publication. The first fix over-tightened nil closure refs by validating `0` at registration, which bypassed the existing recoverable nil deferred function path. Follow-up red-team found the source-order proofs used raw `.find`, so comments or strings could spoof validator-before-publication evidence. | Failing-first behavior proofs `vm_defer_closure_kind_062_rejects_non_closure_before_defer_publication` and `vm_jit_defer_closure_kind_062_rejects_non_closure_before_defer_publication` returned `Ok` before the fix; nil preservation proofs `vm_defer_closure_kind_062_preserves_nil_defer_registration_for_recover` and `vm_jit_defer_closure_kind_062_preserves_nil_defer_registration_for_recover` caught the over-tightening as a null-header crash. The fix makes interpreter defer registration receive the module fact source, validates non-null refs through shared `validate_closure_target`, stores the canonical closure ref, mirrors the same validation in the JIT callback, and leaves null closure refs registered for unwind-time `NilFuncCall`. Source-contract proofs `vm_defer_closure_kind_062_source_order_ignores_comment_spoofed_validator` and `vm_jit_defer_closure_kind_062_source_order_ignores_comment_spoofed_validator` now use compact production source so comments/strings cannot satisfy validator ordering. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-main cargo test -p vo-vm vm_defer_closure_kind_062 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-main cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_jit_defer_closure_kind_062 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, earlier `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_jit_callback_abi_defer_push -- --test-threads=1`, and `./d.py test both tests/lang/cases/runtime/defer/defer_nil_func_recover.vo`; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening`, `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`, and runtime language gates, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-DYNAMIC-CALL-JIT-FRAME-DEPS-062 | Closed, 2026-06-22 | Dynamic call opcode semantic rows must declare every JIT frame callback reachable from their emitted routes, so per-op ABI/dependency tooling does not rely on another opcode to cover the same callback. | `CallClosure` and `CallIface` rows declared prepare/resume/IC dependencies but omitted frame callbacks that their lowering can reach through prepared-call, IC-hit, non-OK slow-path, and stack-overflow routes: `push_frame_fn`, `pop_frame_fn`, and `stack_overflow_fn`. Global `JitContext` validation still filled these callbacks, but per-op semantic dependency gates could miss the route-level ABI requirement. | Failing-first proof `dynamic_call_semantic_dependencies_cover_reachable_frame_callbacks_062` rejected the missing callbacks. The fix adds `push_frame_fn`, `pop_frame_fn`, and `stack_overflow_fn` to `DEP_CALL_CLOSURE` and `DEP_CALL_IFACE`, keeping `prepare_*`, `push_resume_point_fn`, and `ic_table` in the same semantic dependency fact source. | Focused validation passed `cargo test -p vo-jit dynamic_call_semantic_dependencies_cover_reachable_frame_callbacks_062`, `cargo test -p vo-jit semantics::tests`, and `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_jit_callback_boundary_001`; selected by `vm-production` through `jit-contract` / `cargo-test-jit`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-DYNAMIC-CALL-JIT-RUNTIME-TRAP-DEPS-062 | Closed, 2026-06-22 | Dynamic call opcode semantic rows must declare every runtime helper reachable from nil closure/interface lowering, while preserving that their main call route is VM call materialization rather than a pure runtime-trap opcode. | `CallClosure` nil-closure lowering and `CallIface` nil-interface lowering both emit `vo_runtime_trap`, but `DEP_CALL_CLOSURE` and `DEP_CALL_IFACE` declared only prepare/frame/IC dependencies. The old single-value `TrapPolicy::VmSideExit` also made it awkward for contract tooling to distinguish dynamic call materialization from the runtime-trap exit branch. | Failing-first proof `dynamic_call_semantic_dependencies_cover_reachable_runtime_traps_062` scans the lowering sources for `emit_runtime_trap` and requires both dynamic-call rows to declare `RuntimeHelper("vo_runtime_trap")`. The fix adds that helper to both dependency rows and introduces `TrapPolicy::VmSideExitOrRuntimeTrap`, with contract-graph mapping to `PanicPolicy::VmSideExitOrRuntimeTrap`, so the row records both route classes without a side table. | Focused validation passed `cargo test -p vo-jit dynamic_call_semantic_dependencies_cover_reachable_runtime_traps_062 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, `cargo test -p vo-jit semantics::tests -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, and `cargo test -p vo-jit contract_graph::tests -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `vm-production` through `jit-contract` / `cargo-test-jit`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-TASK-GROUP-INCLUDED-IN-REVERSE-LINKS-062 | Closed, 2026-06-22 | Gate metadata must exactly describe direct task-group composition: `[[group]].included_in` must equal the direct parent groups derived from `[groups]`. | `vm-production` actually includes `jit-contract`, `stdlib-contract`, `app-contract`, `lang-main`, `lang-backends`, `stress`, `benchmarks`, and other contract groups, but several child metadata rows omitted `vm-production`, `pr`, or `test`; after the first fix, `docs` over-reported `vm-production` even though production reaches docs through `docs-contract`. Gate execution was correct, but audit/readiness metadata could under- or over-report which groups are selected by the production gate. | Mechanical proofs `lint_group_metadata_included_in_must_match_parent_groups_062` and `lint_group_metadata_included_in_rejects_extra_parent_links_062` reject missing and extra parent links. The fix derives direct parent links from `[groups]` during strict task lint and updates `eng/tasks.toml` `included_in` metadata to match the actual group graph exactly. | Focused validation passed `cargo test -p vo-dev lint_group_metadata_included_in`, `cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- lint tasks --strict`, and `cargo test -p vo-dev vm_readiness`; selected by `vm-production` through `vo-dev-contract`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
