| VM-DOCS-SIGNOFF-HISTORICAL-WORDING-062 | Closed, 2026-06-22 | Historical final-gate evidence must not read as current production-readiness restoration while any current active blocker row remains open. | After signoff was withdrawn for the 2026-06-22 source state, the readiness doc still used present-tense final-gate wording that could be read as current signoff restoration; docs lint did not reject that wording when active blockers were open. | Failing-first `node scripts/ci/docs_lint.mjs` rejected the sentinel startup row state, then follow-up docs-lint self-test rejected stale signoff-restoration wording while blockers remain open. The fix rewrites the passage as historical evidence and extends docs-lint to reject signoff-restoration wording under open blockers. | Focused validation passed `node scripts/ci/docs_lint.mjs` and `cargo test -p vo-dev docs_contract`; selected by `vm-production` through `docs-contract`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-JIT-FRAME-MATERIALIZATION-BULK-COPY-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | Full-function JIT frame materialization must keep memory-only frame slots as frame storage, while the SSA prefix remains compiler-owned; legal large stack-array frames must compile or cleanly report VM/JIT errors without Cranelift entity-limit or verifier-internal failures. | `big-array::jit` generated many stack-array `IndexCheck` slow paths. Each slow path materialized the frame by inline-copying every `memory_only_start..local_count` slot from native argument storage to `fiber.stack`, producing O(frame-size * slow-path-count) Cranelift IR and hitting the backend 24-bit entity limit before a deliverable VM result. | Proofs `big-array::jit`, `vm_jit_frame_materialization_061_uses_bulk_memory_suffix_copy`, `vm_jit_frame_slot_copy_helper_061_has_raw_frame_memmove_semantics`, and helper import/ABI manifest coverage. The fix routes the memory-only suffix through one raw frame memmove helper while preserving SSA-prefix spills in JIT-owned IR, and `big-array` is tagged `jit` and `contract`. | Focused validation passed the runtime helper proof, the JIT source-contract proof, helper manifest/import proofs, `big_array.vo --targets jit`, and release `big_array.vo --tags jit --targets vm,jit,osr`; selected by `jit-contract` and runtime language contract gates through the manifest update. Final `contract` and `vm-production` gates still require rerun from this source state. |
| VM-CALLEXTERN-REPLAY-EXPLICIT-RECEIVER-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | Extern `CallClosure` replay argument metadata must treat an explicit dynamic receiver prefix as frame-owned receiver slots, while using one method-table transfer fact source for receiver metadata and `FunctionDef.param_types` for user argument metadata. | OSR dynamic protocol calls could replay a closure with receiver+args while the callee `param_types` described only user parameters. The first fix accepted the prefix but skipped concrete receiver transfer validation, leaving a boundary split from GoIsland receiver transfer synthesis. Follow-ups found `param_types = []` plus explicit receiver could validate only by slot metadata when the suffix was scalar, and could still allow GC-visible suffix slots with no transfer metadata owner. | Proof tests `vm_extern_replay_transfer_contract_061_accepts_explicit_receiver_prefix`, `vm_extern_replay_transfer_contract_061_validates_synthesized_receiver_prefix`, `vm_extern_replay_transfer_contract_061_validates_receiver_in_inclusive_metadata`, `vm_extern_replay_transfer_contract_061_validates_receiver_with_empty_param_types_and_scalar_suffix`, and `vm_extern_replay_transfer_contract_061_rejects_empty_param_types_with_gcref_suffix`; fix makes `ExternReplayTransferPlan` synthesize receiver transfer metadata through `direct_method_receiver_transfer_plan` for explicit receiver prefixes, allows only scalar suffixes without transfer metadata, and preserves receiver-inclusive metadata compatibility. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-vm frame_call::tests::vm_extern_replay_transfer_contract_061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture` with 14 proof tests, plus OSR regressions `custom_dyn_attr.vo` and `dyn_access_interface_field.vo`; selected by `vm-production` through VM hardening and language OSR gates, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-EMPTY-IFACE-CONCRETE-ITAB-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | Empty-interface arguments and extern returns may carry a concrete nonzero itab as long as the data slot is valid for the concrete value kind; method-set validation belongs only to non-empty interfaces. | VM replay validation and runtime FFI return-shape verification rejected nonzero itabs for expected empty-interface slots, causing valid dynamic interface results to fail at the boundary. | Proof tests `vm_extern_replay_transfer_contract_061_accepts_empty_interface_arg_with_concrete_itab` and `ffi_resolved_return_shape_accepts_empty_interface_with_concrete_itab_061`; fix accepts concrete itabs after canonical data-object validation when the expected interface has no methods. | Focused validation passed the two proof tests and `dyn_access_interface_field.vo --targets osr`; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening`, `runtime-contract`, and language OSR gates, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-DYNAMIC-TARGET-PREP-STORAGE-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | Dynamic write preparation must normalize source values to the target storage contract in one owner before struct fields, arrays, maps, or slice/index writes publish data. | Dynamic field/map/index writes used separate ad hoc paths: one-slot aggregate boxes could be copied as boxed `GcRef`s instead of unboxed value slots, and integer map/index keys rejected compatible integer variants instead of normalizing to target key storage. | Proof tests `dynamic_target_integer_coercion_normalizes_to_storage_kind_061` and `dynamic_target_preparation_unboxes_aggregate_boxes_before_slot_fast_path_061`; fix routes dynamic struct/map/index writes through `prepare_dynamic_value_for_target`, adds integer-kind normalization, and unboxes aggregate boxes before the one-slot fast path. | Focused validation passed the two runtime proofs plus `dyn_set_from_get.vo`, `dyn_set_index_map_key_validation.vo`, and `int_index_variants.vo` on `vm,osr`; selected by `vm-production` through `runtime-contract` and default language VM/OSR gates, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-DYNAMIC-PACKED-SIGNED-SEQUENCE-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | Dynamic array/slice reads and boxed-array aggregate unboxing must materialize logical element values, not raw packed storage bytes, before boxing to `any` or copying into target aggregates. | Dynamic boxed array unboxing and dynamic array/slice indexing used `get_n`, zero-extending packed `int8`/`int16`/`int32` storage where normal VM/JIT element loads sign-extend signed narrow values. | Proof test `dynamic_sequence_reads_use_logical_packed_element_slots_061` plus language case `dynamic_packed_signed_array.vo`; fix centralizes dynamic sequence reads through `read_array_elem_logical_slots` and `read_slice_elem_logical_slots`, using existing `get_auto` for single-slot packed elements and preserving `get_n` for multi-slot raw aggregates. | Focused validation passed `cargo test -p vo-runtime _061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, `dynamic_packed_signed_array.vo` on `vm`, `jit`, and `osr`, and the hot-loop packed-array path inside `osr_dynamic_set_from_get_contract.vo`; selected by `runtime-contract` and language gates, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-DYNAMIC-OSR-LOOP-COVERAGE-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | Dynamic OSR readiness regressions must prove the tested operation actually enters loop JIT, not merely that an `osr` target job was selected. | The three dynamic blocker cases were selected by `--targets osr`, but their source bodies were straight-line, so the `osr` target could pass without executing the dynamic operation inside loop JIT. Follow-up red-team also found the first OSR proof shape leaked into plain `jit` and the invalid-key panic branch sat outside the hot loop. | Proof cases `osr_dynamic_set_from_get_contract.vo`, `osr_dynamic_integer_key_contract.vo`, and `osr_dynamic_protocol_index_contract.vo`; fix adds `jit_loop_entries_min` to manifest expectations, propagates it through `vo-dev test plan` and `vo-test`, enforces it against `RunObservation.jit_loop_entries`, makes the three cases `matrix = "osr-only"`, requires that matrix in strict manifest lint, and moves the invalid-key panic proof inside the hot loop. | Focused validation passed `cargo test -p vo-dev vm_readiness_osr`, `cargo test -p vo-test tests::vm_test_runner_expect_parses_loop_entry_contract_061`, `vo-dev test lint --suite lang --strict`, and all three new OSR language cases with `jit_loop_entries_min = 1`; selected by filtered `--tags jit --targets osr` and by production language gates, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-OSR-PREPARED-CALL-STACK-CONTRACT-SELECTION-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | The prepared-call stack-growth OSR regression must be selected as an executable OSR contract and must prove loop JIT entry. | Gate red-team found `bugs.2026-06-15-osr-prepared-call-stack` still looked like a regression-only native case: it lacked `contract` tagging and `jit_loop_entries_min`, so runtime contract selection could pass without proving the stack-refresh path executed inside loop JIT. | Proof case `bugs.2026-06-15-osr-prepared-call-stack`; fix changes the case to `matrix = "osr-only"`, adds `contract`, records `expect = { kind = "pass", jit_loop_entries_min = 1 }`, and includes the case in `VM_READINESS_OSR_LOOP_CONTRACT_CASES_061` so strict manifest lint keeps the contract shape. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- test lint --suite lang --strict` and `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 ./d.py test osr tests/lang/cases/bugs/2026_06_15_osr_prepared_call_stack.vo`; selected by `vm-production` through the OSR language gates, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-JIT-CALLEXTERN-OSR-STACK-REFRESH-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | Any JIT/OSR direct-helper extern call that may reallocate the fiber stack must refresh compiler-owned stack-base variables before copying helper return slots back to VM locals. | Shared `emit_call_extern` copied return slots through `IrEmitter::write_var` immediately after `vo_call_extern`; for `LoopCompiler`, `write_var` uses cached `locals_ptr_var`, which can be stale if the helper grows the fiber stack. | Mechanical proof `vm_osr_call_extern_stack_061_refreshes_before_return_copyback`; fix calls `emitter.refresh_stack_base_after_reallocation()` in the shared CallExtern lowering after the checked helper returns and before return-slot copyback, preserving the full-JIT no-op implementation and centralizing the OSR refresh in the existing `StackRefresh` owner. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-jit call_helpers::externs::tests::vm_osr_call_extern_stack_061_refreshes_before_return_copyback -- --exact --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `vm-production` through `jit-contract` / `cargo-test-jit`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-DYNAMIC-ASSIGN-BRANCH-CONDITION-061 | Audit-filtered with proof, 2026-06-21; final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence | Dynamic-assignment error branches must present scalar branch conditions to the verifier/JIT branch contract even when the source condition is an interface nil discriminator. | Red-team audit flagged `dyn_assign.rs` sites that call `emit_jump(Opcode::JumpIfNot, err_reg)` on an `Interface0` slot, but `FuncBuilder::emit_jump` already canonicalizes non-`Value` branch conditions through a generated scalar `NeI` condition before bytecode emission. | Proof test `dynamic_assignment_error_branches_verify_scalar_conditions_061`; no source semantic change was needed because `FuncBuilder::canonical_jump_condition` is the existing owner. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-codegen dynamic_assignment_error_branches_verify_scalar_conditions_061 --test integration -- --exact --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `vm-production` through `codegen-contract`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-SPAWN-CALL-RAW-TRANSFER-BOUNDARY-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | `Vm::spawn_call` must canonicalize raw GC-visible argument slots and, when transfer metadata exists, validate heap object kind, pointer metadata, and interface data/itab shape before publishing a new fiber frame. | `spawn_call` previously checked only raw GC membership from `slot_types`, then copied caller-provided slots into the new fiber. Typed `GcRef` parameters could receive the wrong heap object kind, valid interior refs stayed non-canonical in the spawned frame, and an empty-`param_types` direct-method receiver path could still accept a GC-visible suffix without transfer metadata. | Proof tests `spawn_call_transfer_contract_061_rejects_wrong_object_kind_before_enqueue`, `spawn_call_transfer_contract_061_stores_canonical_gcref_args`, and `spawn_call_transfer_contract_061_rejects_empty_param_types_with_gcref_suffix`; fix copies args into a VM-owned buffer, canonicalizes by slot metadata, derives transfer facts from `FunctionDef.param_types` plus method receiver metadata when available, rejects GC-visible suffixes without transfer metadata, and copies only validated canonical slots into the fiber. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-vm spawn_call_transfer_contract_061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `vm-production` through VM hardening and runtime contract gates, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-METHOD-EXPRESSION-WRAPPER-RECEIVER-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | Direct value-receiver method-expression transfer metadata must have one method-table fact source even when interface dispatch registers a generated `$iface` wrapper instead of the original method function id. | Codegen records value receiver methods in `NamedTypeMeta.methods` using the `$iface` wrapper func id, while direct method-expression replay/GoIsland paths ask `direct_method_receiver_transfer_plan` about the original value method func id. The previous lookup therefore missed receiver transfer metadata for the original func. | Proof test `vm_method_expression_receiver_metadata_061_uses_iface_wrapper_method_entry`; fix makes `direct_method_receiver_transfer_plan` accept a method-table entry for the exact method name when that entry points to the corresponding `$iface` wrapper, while preserving exact func-id matching as the primary path. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-vm exec::island::tests::vm_method_expression_receiver_metadata_061_uses_iface_wrapper_method_entry -- --exact --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `vm-production` through VM hardening and language dynamic method gates, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-PROMOTED-METHOD-BOXED-OUTER-RTTID-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | Promoted method wrappers must publish receiver transfer metadata for the boxed outer receiver layout they actually capture and replay, not for the embedded field type that selected the promoted method. | VM replay and `spawn_call` were hardened to require a pointer-to-outer named receiver for `$promoted` wrappers, but codegen still emitted the wrapper's capture/receiver metadata from the embedded method owner. That left direct-method transfer validation with no unified RTTID fact source for boxed outer receivers. | Proof test `vm_promoted_method_receiver_metadata_061_uses_boxed_outer_receiver_pointer`; source fix interns `RuntimeType::Pointer(ValueRttid::new(outer_rttid, outer_kind))` while generating promoted wrappers, using the outer named type as the metadata owner. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-vm exec::island::tests::vm_promoted_method_receiver_metadata_061_uses_boxed_outer_receiver_pointer -- --exact --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, wasm regressions `dyn_named_embed_protocol.vo` and `dyn_protocol_embed.vo`, and `app-contract` with `986 passed, 0 failed`; selected by `vm-production` through VM hardening, language dynamic gates, and `app-contract`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-SPAWNFIBER-MISSING-RECEIVER-PAYLOAD-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | Remote `SpawnFiber` payloads for direct-method frames must reject missing receiver/capture slots as a structured spawn error before a fiber is published. | A malformed remote payload for a direct method could unpack with zero captures and zero args, allocate a closure, then reach `build_closure_fiber_from_args_ptr(...).expect(...)` and panic on the callee arg-slot count instead of returning a VM-owned boundary error. | Proof test `vm_island_spawn_unpack_txn_061_rejects_missing_method_receiver_before_enqueue`; fix routes closure-fiber materialization through the fallible helper and maps the error to `SpawnFiberError`, preserving no-enqueue/no-live-endpoint behavior for malformed payloads. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-vm vm_island_spawn_unpack_txn_061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `vm-production` through VM hardening and runtime-boundary gates, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-INTERFACE-AGGREGATE-DATA-NONNULL-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | Interface values carrying concrete struct or array values must carry a non-null data object at every VM/runtime boundary; `slot1 == 0` is nil only for nil-able/reference-shaped values, not for aggregate value boxes. | VM extern replay and runtime FFI return validation treated `data_is_gc_ref(slot0) && slot1 == 0` as valid for all value kinds, and dynamic aggregate unboxing could read null aggregate boxes as zero slots. | Proof tests `vm_extern_replay_transfer_contract_061_rejects_null_struct_interface_data`, `vm_extern_replay_transfer_contract_061_rejects_null_array_interface_data`, `ffi_resolved_return_shape_rejects_null_struct_or_array_interface_box_061`, and source-contract coverage in `dynamic_target_preparation_unboxes_aggregate_boxes_before_slot_fast_path_061`; fixes reject null struct/array data refs before replay frame push, extern return commit, or dynamic aggregate slot reads. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-vm frame_call::tests::vm_extern_replay_transfer_contract_061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture` and `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-runtime _061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `vm-production` through VM hardening and runtime contract gates, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-FFI-RETURN-CANONICAL-DATA-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | FFI return-shape verification must commit canonical object references back to VM-visible return slots, including interface data slots for aggregate boxes. | Runtime FFI return validation canonicalized interior `GcRef` values for checking, but left the provider-written raw interior reference in the return window. Interface aggregate returns could therefore pass validation while publishing non-canonical `Interface1` data to the caller frame. | Proof test `ffi_resolved_return_shape_canonicalizes_interface_data_slot_061`; fix makes `verify_return_shape` and interface data validation write canonical refs back into declared return slots through one return-window writer after successful canonicalization. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-runtime _061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `vm-production` through `runtime-contract` / `cargo-test-runtime`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-STUDIO-WASM-VERIFIED-BYTECODE-GATE-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | Studio wasm serialized bytecode must be decoded through one verifier-owned helper, and app-contract/changed-mode gates must execute that proof on a host runner. | The wasm32-only unit `studio_serialized_module_gate_rejects_invalid_bytecode` was not selected by existing app-contract tasks, and `StudioVoVm::withExterns` still called raw `Module::deserialize` directly before creating a VM. The cache helpers had verifier coverage, but the VM construction path kept a bytecode-validation fork outside the gate. | Proof tests `studio_wasm_source_contract_061_validates_serialized_module_cache_before_reuse`, `studio_wasm_source_contract_061_rejects_raw_deserialize_bypass`, `lint_app_contract_requires_studio_wasm_source_contract_task_061`, and `lint_vm_readiness_changed_prefixes_requires_studio_wasm_source_contract_route_061`; fix routes `withExterns` through `decode_verified_module`, adds `cargo-test-studio-wasm-source-contract` to `app-contract`, and routes `apps/studio/**` changed-mode coverage to the same task. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-dev studio_wasm_source_contract -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, `cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- lint tasks --strict`, and `cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- task run cargo-test-studio-wasm-source-contract`; selected by `vm-production` through `app-contract`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-WASM-OS-SENTINEL-ERROR-INTERFACE-METADATA-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | Wasm `os` sentinel errors must use the same boxed `errors.Error` interface-pair authority as native/runtime sentinel errors before stdlib code receives them through `error` values. | `os_get_errors` returned raw `(ValueKind::String, string_ref)` pairs directly, bypassing `errors.Error` construction and interface metadata. That made wasm-only sentinel constants look like string-shaped interfaces instead of concrete `error` values. | Source-contract proof `wasm_os_error_interfaces_use_runtime_boxing_contract_061`; fix initializes the wasm sentinel-error cache with `create_error(call, msg)` and returns cached interface pairs through `ret_interface_pair`. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-web-runtime-wasm wasm_os_error_interfaces_use_runtime_boxing_contract_061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, wasm stdlib regressions `filepath_basic.vo`, `http_client_test.vo`, `phase2_packages.vo`, and `stdlib_phase15.vo`, plus `app-contract` with `986 passed, 0 failed`; selected by `vm-production` through `app-contract` and wasm language gates, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-DYNAMIC-MAP-INTERFACE-CONTRACT-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | Dynamic writes to `map[K]I` where `I` is a non-empty interface must preserve concrete itab/data ownership and reject values that do not implement `I`. | The proof case for dynamic map interface values had drifted into expecting `a~>a = "xxxxx"` to succeed for `map[string]error`, masking the actual VM contract that strings do not implement `error` and must be rejected before map publication. | Behavior proof `dyn_access_map_interface_val.vo`; fix updates the proof to assert the mismatched string dynamic write panics, then writes `errors.New("err_replaced")` and reads it back through the `error` interface. The manifest case now carries `contract` and records the non-empty-interface map assignment reason. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 ./d.py test both tests/lang/cases/dyn/dyn_access_map_interface_val.vo`, `./d.py test wasm tests/lang/cases/dyn/dyn_access_map_interface_val.vo`, and `app-contract` with `986 passed, 0 failed`; selected by `vm-production` through dynamic language contract and wasm gates, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-WASM-RUNTIME-SOURCE-CONTRACT-GATE-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | App-level readiness gates must execute the host-runnable vo-web runtime-wasm source-contract tests whenever wasm runtime sources or app-contract are in scope. | The wasm `os` sentinel-error source proof lived in `vo-web/runtime-wasm`, but `app-contract` and the vo-web changed-prefix route selected only wasm language and web hardening tasks. A future wasm runtime metadata fork could therefore miss the host source-contract proof while app-contract still passed. | Proof tests `lint_app_contract_requires_web_runtime_wasm_source_contract_task_061` and `lint_vm_readiness_changed_prefixes_requires_web_runtime_wasm_contract_route_061`; fix adds `cargo-test-web-runtime-wasm` to `app-contract`, to the app-contract group metadata, and to the `lang/crates/vo-web/**` changed-prefix task list. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-gatefix cargo test -p vo-dev web_runtime_wasm -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-gatefix cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- lint tasks --strict`, and `cargo fmt --all -- --check`; selected by `vm-production` through `app-contract`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-SAME-ISLAND-WAKEFIBER-ACTIVATION-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | A same-island queue waiter wake is single-use across both ordinary `WakeCommand` and same-island `IslandCommand::WakeFiber` representations before any local fiber is made runnable. | Runtime transition preflight de-duplicated `transition.wakes` only. Duplicate same-island `WakeFiber` commands, or a `WakeCommand` plus same-island `WakeFiber` for the same waiter, could each preflight while the fiber was blocked; the first apply made it runnable and the later duplicate failed after mutating scheduler state. | Behavior proofs `vm_same_island_wake_fiber_activation_061_rejects_duplicate_commands_before_wake` and `vm_same_island_wake_fiber_activation_061_rejects_mixed_wake_before_wake`; fix derives the same `WakeActivationKey` for local `WakeFiber` island commands and folds it into the existing transition-level wake activation set before per-effect preflight. | Failing-first proofs reproduced the late wake mutation, then focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-vm vm_same_island_wake_fiber_activation_061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-SPAWNFIBER-ENDPOINT-ROLLBACK-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | Remote `SpawnFiber` payload handling must treat endpoint handle resolution, closure materialization, and transition publication as one endpoint-registry transaction. | `handle_spawn_fiber` restored the endpoint registry when unpacking or queue-handle resolution failed, but the snapshot was scoped inside the unpack block. If unpack registered a remote endpoint proxy and later closure-fiber build failed, the error returned with the endpoint still live even though no fiber was spawned. A follow-up full `cargo-test-vm` run caught that the handle-resolution source-contract still required direct snapshot consumption on early restore paths. | Behavior proofs `vm_island_spawn_unpack_txn_061_build_failure_restores_endpoint_registry` and `vm_island_spawn_unpack_txn_003_handle_error_restores_endpoint_registry`; fix moves the endpoint-registry snapshot to the outer spawn transaction and restores it on unpack failure, queue-handle resolution failure, closure-fiber build failure, or runtime transition failure, dirtying GC roots after manual restore. The early-return restore paths consume the snapshot directly instead of cloning it, preserving the single rollback owner shape expected by the source contract. | Failing-first proof reproduced a live leaked endpoint after post-unpack arg-slot failure, then focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-vm vm_island_spawn_unpack_txn_061_build_failure_restores_endpoint_registry -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-vm vm_island_spawn_unpack_txn_061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, and `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-vm vm_island_spawn_unpack_txn_003_handle_error_restores_endpoint_registry -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm` and `cargo-test-vm-hardening`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-WASM-EXT-BRIDGE-PARAM-KINDS-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | Wasm extension bridge externs with input slots must declare exact parameter shape and one `ExtSlotKind` for every input slot before the bridge can encode a JS call. | `register_wasm_ext_bridges` recorded whatever `ExternDef.param_kinds` contained, while `ResolvedExternTable` accepted empty `param_kinds` as a generic ABI. A wasm-extension extern declared with one or more exact input slots, or with variadic params, could resolve and then call JS with an empty input vector. | Proof tests `resolve_module_externs_rejects_wasm_extension_bridge_missing_param_kinds_061` and `resolve_module_externs_rejects_wasm_extension_bridge_variadic_params_061`; fix makes `ExternRegistry::resolve_module_externs` enforce source-specific bridge ABI: exact params are required and `param_kinds.len()` must equal the exact input slot count. | Failing-first proofs reproduced both accepted malformed bridge ABIs, then focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-runtime wasm_extension_bridge -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `vm-production` through `runtime-contract` / `cargo-test-runtime`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-REMOTE-WAKEFIBER-ACTIVATION-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | Remote queue waiter wakes must be single-use across both explicit remote `IslandCommand::WakeFiber` commands and ordinary wake effects before any remote command is published. | Runtime transition preflight only de-duplicated ordinary wake effects and same-island `WakeFiber` commands. Duplicate remote `WakeFiber` commands, or a remote `WakeCommand` plus an explicit remote `WakeFiber` for the same waiter, could both pass preflight and leave the duplicate to fail after command publication ownership had already forked. | Behavior proofs `vm_remote_wake_fiber_activation_061_rejects_duplicate_commands_before_publish` and `vm_remote_wake_fiber_activation_061_rejects_mixed_wake_before_publish`; fix derives one `WakeActivationKey` for remote and same-island `WakeFiber` commands and folds all wake representations into the transition-level activation set. | Failing-first proofs reproduced accepted duplicate remote activations, then focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-failing-remote cargo test -p vo-vm vm_remote_wake_fiber_activation_061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-REMOTE-ENDPOINT-RESPONSE-ACTIVATION-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | Endpoint response activations must be single-use across closed-waiter wake effects and explicit remote endpoint-response commands before response publication. | Remote endpoint responses and closed sender/receiver wakes used separate preflight shapes, so a transition could combine a closed wake with an explicit remote response, or duplicate remote responses, for the same endpoint wait id and pass preflight before one late apply path rejected the stale activation. | Behavior proofs `vm_remote_endpoint_response_activation_061_rejects_mixed_closed_wake_before_publish` and `vm_remote_endpoint_response_activation_061_rejects_duplicate_commands_before_publish`; fix derives endpoint-response activation keys for remote waiter wakes and remote endpoint-response island commands through the existing response-source owner. | Failing-first proofs reproduced accepted duplicate endpoint-response activations, then focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-failing-remote-endpoint cargo test -p vo-vm vm_remote_endpoint_response_activation_061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-WASM-EXT-BRIDGE-DISPATCH-ABI-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | Wasm extension bridge providers may be called only through resolved extern metadata whose exact parameter shape and slot-kind vector still match the bridge ABI at dispatch time. | After resolution-time validation, low-level `ExternRegistry::call` could still dispatch a `WasmExtensionBridge` registered source without any resolved ABI metadata, and `call_resolved` trusted the caller-provided `ResolvedExtern` even if its public fields were mutated after a legal resolve. | Proof tests `wasm_extension_bridge_call_061_requires_resolved_abi_before_provider` and `wasm_extension_bridge_call_061_revalidates_resolved_param_kinds_before_provider`; fix rejects unresolved wasm bridge dispatch and revalidates resolved bridge params/param_kinds before invoking the provider. | Failing-first proofs showed both malformed dispatch paths ran the provider, then focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-failing-ffi cargo test -p vo-runtime wasm_extension_bridge_call_061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `vm-production` through `runtime-contract` / `cargo-test-runtime`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-DEFER-IFACE-WRAPPER-RECEIVER-META-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | Deferred interface method wrappers must include receiver interface metadata in their cache identity, because `CallIfaceLayout` is a callsite fact and same-named methods on different interfaces are not interchangeable. | The deferred interface wrapper cache keyed only on method name, method index, parameter slot layout, and return slot layout. In the HTTP regression, `io/fs.File.Close` generated `Close$defer_iface_0...` first and `net.Conn.Close` reused it, so `CallIface` expected `io/fs.File` while the dynamic receiver carried a `net.Conn` itab. | Failing proof case `bugs.2026-01-31-jit-http-server-client` reproduced `Close$defer_iface_0_pvoid_r2_3` with `callsite_interface=io/fs.File` and a `net.Conn` itab; source-contract proof `defer_iface_wrapper_cache_key_includes_receiver_interface_meta_061` asserts distinct receiver interfaces with same `Close` shape produce distinct wrappers with distinct `CallIfaceLayout.iface_meta_id`. The fix adds `iface_meta_id` to the defer-interface wrapper cache name. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-codegen defer_iface_wrapper_cache_key_includes_receiver_interface_meta_061 --test integration -- --exact --test-threads=1 --nocapture` and `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo run -q -p vo-test -- run-plan-job /private/tmp/volang-http-job.json` with `[VO:OK]`; selected by `vm-production` through codegen contract and the HTTP JIT contract language gate, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-REMOTE-ISLAND-COMMAND-SHAPE-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | Remote island command publication must preflight command shape and source identity before staging or publishing to the outbound transport. | Remote `IslandCommandEffect` preflight validated only the target route. A forged remote `WakeFiber` could name a different waiter island or tunnel an endpoint waiter, and forged remote endpoint requests/responses could claim a third-party `from_island`, creating outbound commands or pending response obligations the current VM did not own. | Behavior proofs `vm_remote_wake_fiber_shape_061_rejects_forged_waiter_before_publish` and `vm_remote_endpoint_effect_shape_061_rejects_forged_source_before_publish`; fix routes every remote effect through `preflight_remote_island_command_effect`, sharing one owner for target waiter identity, endpoint-wait exclusion, source island, and pending-response shape. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-vm _061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-REMOTE-OPAQUE-WAKE-IDENTITY-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | Cross-island wake and endpoint response identities must carry canonical opaque fiber keys with a nonzero generation before outbound publication or pending-response accounting. | Remote `WakeFiber`, remote endpoint `Send`/`Recv` requests, explicit remote endpoint responses, and synthesized closed endpoint wake responses accepted raw scheduler slot ids with generation `0`. The target side would reject the stale identity later, but the source VM could already publish an invalid command or create a pending response obligation. | Behavior proofs `vm_remote_wake_fiber_shape_061_rejects_raw_slot_identity_before_publish`, `vm_remote_endpoint_request_shape_061_rejects_raw_slot_response_identity_before_publish`, and `vm_remote_endpoint_response_shape_061_rejects_raw_slot_response_identity_before_publish`; fix adds a shared canonical opaque-key validator and routes remote WakeFiber, endpoint request activation, endpoint response command, and closed-wake response preflight through it before staging or publish. | Focused validation passed the three proof filters plus `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-vm _061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture` and `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit _061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening` and `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-SELECT-WAKE-PAYLOAD-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | A select waiter may be woken only with the `SelectWokenResult` matching its select kind: recv requires a layout-checked `Recv` payload, and send requires `SendAccepted`. | `validate_queue_wake_payload` validated malformed recv payloads only when a payload was present and did not require `SendAccepted` for select-send. Forged local transition wakes could therefore make select recv/send fibers runnable without the selected outcome materialized. | Behavior proofs `vm_select_recv_wake_contract_061_rejects_missing_payload_before_wake` and `vm_select_send_wake_contract_061_rejects_missing_send_accepted_before_wake`; fix makes queue-wake payload validation reject missing or mismatched select outcomes before scheduler mutation. | Focused validation passed both proof filters and `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-vm _061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-CLOSED-QUEUE-WAKE-SOURCE-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | Local closed receiver/sender wakes for non-endpoint queue waiters must prove the referenced local queue is actually closed before making a fiber runnable. | A forged local `ClosedSender` wake for a select-send waiter on an open queue bypassed the select-send `SendAccepted` payload contract because `ClosedSender` carried no select result and preflight checked only waiter/fiber/select identity. The selected fiber could become runnable while the source queue was still open. | Behavior proof `vm_select_closed_sender_wake_061_rejects_open_queue_before_wake`; fix makes local closed receiver/sender preflight validate the queue handle and `queue::is_closed` before scheduler wake ownership checks. | Focused validation passed the proof plus `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-vm _061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture` and `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit _061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening` and `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-WAKEFIBER-SELECT-WAKE-PAYLOAD-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | `WakeFiber` / direct `IslandWake` bridges cannot represent select outcomes; select receive data and select send acceptance must stay on wake forms that carry `SelectWokenResult`. | The first select-recv payload fix covered `WakeCommand` validation, but red-team found same-island `WakeFiber`, remote `WakeFiber`, and direct `RuntimeCommandTarget::IslandWake` paths could still construct identity-valid select receive or send wakes with no selected outcome. | Behavior proofs `vm_wake_fiber_shape_061_rejects_select_recv_without_payload_before_wake_or_publish`, `vm_wake_fiber_shape_061_rejects_select_send_without_payload_before_wake_or_publish`, `vm_island_wake_command_061_rejects_select_recv_without_payload_before_wake`, and `vm_island_wake_command_061_rejects_select_send_without_payload_before_wake`; fix rejects every select waiter in same-island, remote, and direct WakeFiber/IslandWake command preflight. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-vm vm_wake_fiber_shape_061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture` and `cargo test -p vo-vm vm_island_wake_command_061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-REMOTE-SELECT-WAKE-PAYLOAD-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | Remote select wakes produced from `RuntimeTransition.wakes` must not publish through `IslandCommand::WakeFiber`, because that transport command has no payload/result field for either selected receive data or send acceptance. | A remote select-recv wake could carry `SelectWokenResult::Recv`, pass payload validation, and then be split into a payload-less `WakeFiber`; a remote select-send wake could carry `SendAccepted` and be split the same way, allowing the target side to later materialize the wrong select outcome. | Behavior proofs `vm_remote_select_recv_wake_061_rejects_unrepresentable_payload_before_publish` and `vm_remote_select_send_wake_061_rejects_unrepresentable_payload_before_publish`; fix rejects all remote select waiter wakes in `preflight_queue_waiter_wake` before split/publish, keeping select outcome ownership local to representations that can carry it. | Failing-first proofs first showed `apply_runtime_transition` returned success and staged payload-less outbound wakes, then focused validation passed both proof filters under `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2`; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-ENDPOINT-REQUEST-ACTIVATION-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | Remote endpoint request activations must carry a nonzero waiter identity and be single-use within a transition before pending-response accounting is incremented or an outbound request is published. | Remote endpoint request preflight validated route and source shape but did not de-duplicate Send/Recv activation keys, and did not reject zero waiter identities before commit-time pending-response mutation. | Behavior proof `vm_remote_endpoint_request_activation_061_rejects_duplicate_or_missing_wait_identity`; fix derives one activation key from `(endpoint_id, fiber_key, wait_id)` for remote Send/Recv endpoint requests and rejects duplicates or identityless requests during transition preflight. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-vm vm_remote_endpoint_request_activation_061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-ENDPOINT-REQUEST-TARGET-IDENTITY-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | Incoming endpoint `Send`/`Recv` requests at the home island must prove the requester response identity is nonzero and generation-bearing before registering queue waiters or publishing rejection responses. | Source-side endpoint request publication rejected raw or zero response identity, but the target-side handler still accepted externally delivered `EndpointRequest::{Send,Recv}` with a raw scheduler slot or zero wait id. Empty recv and blocking send paths could register poisoned endpoint waiters in the home queue before any response path rejected them later. | Behavior proof `vm_endpoint_request_target_061_rejects_raw_or_zero_response_identity_before_registering_waiter`; fix routes target-side preflight and handler entry through the shared canonical opaque-key validator for `Send`/`Recv`, while leaving `Close`/`Transfer` identityless. | Focused validation passed the proof plus `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-vm _061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-SAME-ISLAND-ENDPOINT-REQUEST-SHAPE-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | Same-island endpoint request effects must use the same source and pending-response envelope contract as remote endpoint requests before pending-response accounting or endpoint queue mutation. | Remote `EndpointRequest` preflight enforced `from_island == current` plus `pending_response == true` for `Send`/`Recv` and `false` for `Close`/`Transfer`, but the same-island branch skipped those shape checks while `apply_island_command_effect` trusted the bit directly and the handler authorized by payload `from_island`. A forged same-island request could impersonate an endpoint peer, mutate transfer/close state, or register send/recv waiters with mismatched pending-response accounting. | Behavior proofs `vm_same_island_endpoint_request_shape_061_requires_pending_response_before_waiter`, `vm_same_island_endpoint_request_shape_061_rejects_spurious_pending_response`, `vm_same_island_endpoint_request_shape_061_rejects_forged_source_before_transfer`, and `vm_same_island_endpoint_request_shape_061_rejects_forged_source_before_waiter`; fix adds shared pending-response and same-island source validators before endpoint request preflight or dispatch. | Failing-first proofs showed malformed same-island envelopes and forged sources applied successfully, then focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-vm same_island_endpoint_request_shape_061 --quiet`; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-DIRECT-ISLAND-ENDPOINT-SOURCE-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | Direct island command ingress must bind an authenticated transport source to endpoint request/response payload source before endpoint authorization or mutation. | The VM boundary effect path now owned endpoint request source, but direct ingress from in-thread transport, external transport frames, or `push_island_command` still delivered a naked `IslandCommand`. The target handler authorized by payload `from_island`, so a forged direct request could impersonate an authorized endpoint peer before transfer, recv, send, close, or response handling. | Behavior proofs `vm_direct_endpoint_request_ingress_061_rejects_forged_transfer_source_before_peer_mutation` and `vm_direct_endpoint_request_ingress_061_rejects_forged_recv_source_before_waiter`; fix adds `IslandCommandEnvelope`, carries source through in-thread transport and external transport frames, makes VM command queues/outbound queues source-aware, and rejects endpoint request/response commands whose envelope source differs from payload `from_island`. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-vm vm_direct_endpoint_request_ingress_061 --quiet`, `cargo test -p vo-runtime island_transport --quiet`, `cargo test -p vo-app-runtime push_targeted_inbound_island_frame --quiet`, and `cargo check -p vo-web`; selected by `vm-production` through VM hardening, runtime contract, runtime-surface, and app-contract frame gates, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-JIT-OUTBOUND-ENVELOPE-PROOF-GATE-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | JIT callback hardening proofs must consume the canonical outbound island command envelope and prove its source island, not match the retired naked `IslandCommand` queue shape. | The direct-ingress source fix changed VM outbound publication to `IslandCommandEnvelope`, but several JIT callback proof fixtures still matched outbound queue entries as raw commands. That made `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit` fail at compile time and left the JIT GoIsland/QueueSend proof surface outside the new source-envelope contract. | Mechanical proof fixture updates in the existing JIT GoIsland and QueueSend 061 tests now assert the outbound `source_island_id` and match `envelope.command` for `SpawnFiber` / `EndpointRequest`. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit _061 --quiet -- --test-threads=1` with 79 proofs; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-DIRECT-ENDPOINT-RESPONSE-FIXTURE-SOURCE-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | Endpoint response command-queue proofs must inject responses through the authenticated source envelope matching payload `from_island`. | Full `vo-vm` validation found `command_queue_endpoint_response_wakes_blocked_fiber` still used local-source `push_island_command` while claiming `from_island = 9`; the new direct-ingress source gate correctly rejected the fixture before wake. | The proof fixture now uses `push_island_command_from(9, EndpointResponse { from_island: 9, ... })`, preserving the legal remote-response wake contract under the new envelope owner. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-vm command_queue_endpoint_response_wakes_blocked_fiber --quiet` and full `cargo test -p vo-vm --quiet -- --test-threads=1`; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-APP-RUNTIME-ISLAND-FRAME-SOURCE-PROOF-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | `vo-app-runtime` frame adapters must preserve nonzero source-envelope identity on inbound frames and must encode VM-owned outbound envelope source identity. | Test/gate red-team found existing app-runtime frame proofs used only `source = 0` `Shutdown` frames and the readiness row cited the wrong `app-contract` path. Source forwarded `source_island_id`, but a regression that dropped or substituted the source would not fail the selected runtime-surface tests. | Proofs `push_targeted_inbound_island_frame_preserves_source_envelope_061` and `drain_outbound_island_frames_preserves_source_envelope_061`; fix adds a nonzero-source endpoint response behavior proof and an outbound source-contract proof over `drain_outbound_island_frames`. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-app-runtime source_envelope_061 --quiet` and full `cargo test -p vo-app-runtime --quiet`; selected by `vm-production` through `runtime-surface-contract` / `cargo-test-vo-app-runtime`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-WEB-ISLAND-FRAME-SOURCE-PROOF-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | `vo-web` island frame adapters must forward decoded transport source into VM ingress and encode outbound frames from VM-owned envelope source identity. | Test/gate red-team found `vo-web` had compile coverage and app-contract routing but no selected `vm_` proof for source-envelope preservation, so `pushIslandCommand` or `takeOutboundCommands` could drop or forge source ids while still compiling. | Source-contract proof `vm_web_island_frame_source_envelope_061_source_contract` locks `push_targeted_island_command_from(source_island_id, target_island_id, cmd)` and outbound `encode_island_transport_frame(..., envelope.source_island_id, &envelope.command)`. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-web vm_web_island_frame_source_envelope_061 --quiet` and `cargo test -p vo-web vm_ --quiet`; selected by `vm-production` through `app-contract` / `cargo-test-web-hardening`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-SAME-ISLAND-ENDPOINT-RESPONSE-SOURCE-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | Same-island endpoint response effects must prove payload `from_island` is the current island before any local wake, root effect, pending-response accounting, or endpoint response dispatch can commit. | Runtime/wake red-team found `preflight_same_island_endpoint_response_command` treated forged `from_island != current` as no-op when endpoint response authorization returned false. A transition containing a valid local wake plus a forged same-island endpoint response could wake the local waiter first, then fail later in direct dispatch source checking. | Behavior proof `vm_same_island_endpoint_response_source_061_rejects_before_local_wake`; fix adds same-island EndpointResponse source preflight before authorization/no-op handling. | Failing-first proof first returned the later dispatch error after local wake staging; after the fix, focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-vm vm_same_island_endpoint_response_source_061 --quiet -- --test-threads=1` and the adjacent stale-response preflight proof; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-SAME-ISLAND-CLOSED-ENDPOINT-WAKE-SOURCE-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | Same-island closed endpoint wakes for local endpoint waiters must preflight endpoint-response source authority before any earlier wake in the transition can commit. | GC/root red-team found `preflight_endpoint_response_for_waiter` accepted local endpoint closed wakes based on `can_accept_endpoint_response` alone. For a live remote proxy whose home island is foreign, a transition could commit an earlier local queue wake and only then fail when `apply_endpoint_response_for_waiter` rejected the unauthorized source. | Behavior proof `vm_same_island_closed_endpoint_wake_source_061_rejects_foreign_live_remote_before_local_wake`; fix adds one same-island endpoint wake source preflight helper that mirrors the apply-time foreign live remote / foreign tombstone rejection while preserving the local closed-response tombstone exception. | Failing-first proof first reached the later endpoint wake rejection after local wake staging; after the fix, focused validation passed the new proof plus existing same-island closed endpoint replay proofs; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-SAME-ISLAND-NONREQUEST-PENDING-SHAPE-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | Same-island island commands may create pending response obligations only for `EndpointRequest::{Send, Recv}`; `EndpointResponse`, `WakeFiber`, and `Shutdown` must reject `pending_response=true` before dispatch or wake. | Runtime/wake red-team found same-island `EndpointResponse` and `WakeFiber` effects skipped pending-response shape validation, then gate red-team found the new `Shutdown` branch had source coverage but no named proof. Malformed effects could increment `pending_island_responses`, and WakeFiber could also wake a local fiber, even though no response obligation exists for those command shapes. | Behavior/source-contract proofs `vm_same_island_endpoint_response_shape_061_rejects_spurious_pending_response_before_dispatch`, `vm_same_island_wake_fiber_shape_061_rejects_spurious_pending_response_before_wake`, and `vm_same_island_shutdown_shape_061_rejects_spurious_pending_response_before_dispatch`; fix adds shared same-island non-request pending-response validation before dispatch. | Failing-first proofs accepted the malformed response/wake effects; after the fix and shutdown proof, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-vm spurious_pending_response --quiet -- --test-threads=1` passed, and same-island endpoint request shape proofs still passed; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-REMOTE-ENDPOINT-RESPONSE-SOURCE-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | A remote endpoint response effect must be authorized by the local endpoint registry before response publication, not merely claim the current island as `from_island`. | Remote endpoint response preflight checked route/source shape but did not prove the local island was the registered response source for the endpoint. A forged response could claim `from_island == current` without local endpoint authority. | Behavior proof `vm_remote_endpoint_response_source_061_requires_local_authority_before_publish`; fix reuses `endpoint_response_from_authorized_source` in both remote endpoint-response effects and waiter response preflight before route publication. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-vm vm_remote_endpoint_response_source_061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-REMOTE-ENDPOINT-RESPONSE-SHAPE-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | Remote endpoint responses that resume a fiber must carry nonzero fiber and endpoint-wait identity before outbound publication; only `EndpointResponseKind::Closed` is identityless. | Explicit remote `EndpointResponse` commands could pass source authority and route preflight with `fiber_key = 0` and `wait_id = 0`, publishing a response shape the target endpoint-response path cannot match to a pending wait. | Behavior proof `vm_remote_endpoint_response_shape_061_rejects_missing_wait_identity_before_publish`; fix makes remote endpoint-response preflight reject identityless non-`Closed` responses before route/publish. | Failing-first proof showed an authorized identityless response published successfully, then focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-vm vm_remote_endpoint_response_shape_061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-REMOTE-CLOSED-WAKE-RESPONSE-SHAPE-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | Remote closed receiver/sender wakes may synthesize endpoint responses only for endpoint waiters with a nonzero endpoint wait id; simple/select queue waiters have no endpoint-response resume slot to target. | Remote closed receiver/sender wakes for simple queue waiters with `endpoint_wait_id == 0` checked endpoint id, source authority, and route, then split into outbound `EndpointResponse` commands with `wait_id = 0`, a shape the target endpoint-response path can only reject. | Behavior proof `vm_remote_closed_non_endpoint_wake_061_rejects_unrepresentable_response_before_publish`; fix rejects remote closed receiver/sender wakes after the local-wake branch unless the waiter has already gone through endpoint-wait preflight. | Failing-first proof showed both authorized receiver and sender non-endpoint closed wakes published successfully, then focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-vm vm_remote_closed_non_endpoint_wake_061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-OSR-BORROW-ROLLBACK-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | Every runtime-transition rejection path, including OSR borrow-lease rejection, must restore transition rollback state and clear the current fiber's remote endpoint wait before returning. | `apply_runtime_transition` checked `jit_osr_borrow_lease_depth` before the preflight rollback path. If a transition had already installed endpoint-transfer state and set a remote endpoint wait, the early JIT error left both mutations live even though the transition did not commit. | Behavior proof `vm_osr_borrow_lease_rejection_061_restores_rollback_before_return`; fix funnels the OSR early rejection and normal preflight errors through one `reject_runtime_transition_before_commit` helper that restores rollback and current remote endpoint wait. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_osr_borrow_lease_rejection_061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-JIT-DYNAMIC-IC-OWNER-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | JIT dynamic-call inline-cache entries are owned by the caller/callsite that populated them; table hash collisions must miss and re-enter the VM-owned validation path. | JIT `CallIface` dynamic IC entries were addressed only by table slot plus `(itab_id, method_idx, slot0)`. Two callsites that hashed to the same `DynCallIC` slot could share a cached target, letting the later `CallIface` fast path skip the callsite `iface_meta_id` validation performed by the interpreter and JIT prepare callback. Follow-up red-team found the source-contract proof relied too much on string shape for the hit branch. | Source-contract proofs `vm_jit_dynamic_ic_owner_key_061_site_owns_hit_and_update` and `vm_jit_dynamic_ic_owner_key_061_generated_hit_condition_binds_owner_key`, plus runtime proof `dyn_call_ic_owner_key_061_distinguishes_same_table_slot_callsites`; fix adds `DynCallIC::owner_key(caller_func_id, callsite_pc)`, stores it on miss update, requires owner/key/extra to match before any dynamic IC hit, and factors the generated hit condition into one IR-tested helper. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-jit vm_jit_dynamic_ic_owner_key_061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture` and `cargo test -p vo-runtime dyn_call_ic_owner_key_061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-jit` and `runtime-contract`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-JIT-PENDING-SELECT-ROLLBACK-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | JIT pending runtime transitions may pre-cancel select sibling waiters only as rollback-owned precommit state; if later staging rejects or discards the transition, selected queue state, sibling queue state, and the selected fiber's registered queues must be restored. | `push_pending_runtime_transition` immediately called `cancel_select_sibling_waiters_for_transition`, which used the committed wake path and cleared every registered select queue. A late remote endpoint close reservation failure restored only the queue-close rollback, and `ExecResult::CallClosure` / policy-filtered pending drops cleared pending transitions without restoring rollback at all. | Behavior proofs `vm_pending_select_sibling_cancel_rollback_061_restores_failed_remote_close` and `vm_pending_select_sibling_cancel_rollback_061_restores_callclosure_discard`; fix snapshots affected select fiber state and registered local queue states into `RuntimeRollback::SelectWaiters`, makes pending pre-cancel remove only sibling waiters while keeping the selected activation for preflight, and restores pending rollback for both staging rejection and discard paths. | Focused validation passed both proofs and `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit _061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-WASM-EXT-BRIDGE-CONTEXT-ABI-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | Wasm extension bridge dispatch must bind the resolved extern name and slot-kind vector to the current extern call context, not to mutable process-global extern-id state. | `vo-web` stored bridge name/param-kinds in a thread-local `extern_id` table and cleared/rebuilt it on every `register_wasm_ext_bridges` call. Creating or loading a second VM could clobber the table for a still-live first VM, so the first VM's later bridge call could encode and dispatch with the second VM's ABI. | Runtime proof `wasm_extension_bridge_call_061_passes_resolved_abi_to_provider_context` and source-contract proof `wasm_extension_bridge_dispatch_061_uses_resolved_context_abi_not_global_side_table`; fix binds resolved bridge ABI into `ExternCallContext` during `call_resolved` and removes the global `EXTERN_ID_TO_INFO` table from wasm bridge dispatch. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-runtime wasm_extension_bridge_call_061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture` and `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-web-runtime-wasm wasm_extension_bridge_dispatch_061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `runtime-contract`, `app-contract`, and `vm-production`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-EXTERN-NONOK-RETURN-TXN-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | Extern return slots are committed only by `ExternResult::Ok`; all non-Ok and post-call contract-error paths must roll back provider writes before the current frame can yield, block, wait, or surface an infrastructure error. | `ExternRegistry::call_resolved` verified declared return shape only for `Ok`, while release builds relied on debug-only VM checks. A provider could write an invalid GC-shaped return slot and then return `Yield`, `Block`, host wait, replay wait, or a post-call contract error, leaving malformed data in a GC-visible caller frame. | Proof tests `ffi_resolved_non_ok_return_slots_rollback_before_yield_061` and `ffi_resolved_contract_error_rolls_back_return_slots_061`; fix snapshots the declared return window at the runtime FFI boundary, commits only after `Ok` return-shape verification, and restores the snapshot for non-Ok results and post-call errors. | Focused clean-target validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target cargo test -p vo-runtime ffi::tests::ffi_resolved_non_ok_return_slots_rollback_before_yield_061 -- --exact --test-threads=1 --nocapture` and `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target cargo test -p vo-runtime ffi::tests::ffi_resolved_contract_error_rolls_back_return_slots_061 -- --exact --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `vm-production` through `runtime-contract` / `cargo-test-runtime`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-ENDPOINT-REQUEST-RESPONSE-ROOTS-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | Live endpoint request handling that consumes queue state or publishes endpoint responses must mark the VM root set dirty through the same runtime transition that publishes the response. | Same-island endpoint `Recv` could drain a GC-bearing buffered payload and emit only an endpoint response with no local wake, but the enclosing transition declared `GcRootEffect::None`, leaving already-committed queue/response state outside the dirty-root contract. | Proof test `vm_endpoint_recv_gc_payload_marks_roots_dirty_061`; fix changes the live endpoint request response transition in `handle_endpoint_request_command` to `GcRootEffect::AllRootsDirty`, keeping rollback-on-apply-failure behavior intact. | Focused clean-target validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target cargo test -p vo-vm vm::island_shared::tests::vm_endpoint_recv_gc_payload_marks_roots_dirty_061 -- --exact --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, plus existing guards `vm_gc_transition_root_001_interpreter_recv_endpoint_responses_dirty_current_fiber` and `vm_gc_transition_none_live_002_interpreter_recv_data_transitions_dirty_current_fiber`; selected by `vm-production` through `runtime-contract` / `cargo-test-vm` and `vm-hardening` / `cargo-test-vm-hardening`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-REMOTE-SEND-TRANSFER-ROLLBACK-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | Queue-transfer preparation that publishes local endpoint state before a cross-island send or spawn must carry one rollback object through the runtime transition that publishes the remote command. | `QueueTransferCommit` only recorded a boolean. Interpreter `RemoteSend` discarded it, interpreter `GoIsland` ignored it, and JIT paths used it only for terminal-policy classification. A late remote reservation failure could leave newly installed `HomeInfo` and endpoint-registry entries published even though no send/spawn command reached the remote island. | Proof test `vm_remote_send_transfer_txn_061_late_publish_failure_rolls_back_local_endpoint_prepare`; source-contract tests `vm_queue_transfer_commit_061_interpreter_paths_attach_runtime_rollback`, `vm_jit_queue_send_transfer_commit_061_source_attaches_runtime_rollback`, and `vm_jit_goisland_transfer_commit_061_source_attaches_runtime_rollback`. The fix makes `QueueTransferCommit` snapshot local `HomeInfo` plus endpoint registry before mutation, adds `RuntimeRollback::EndpointTransfer`, and attaches that rollback in interpreter/JIT QueueSend and GoIsland transition builders. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target cargo test -p vo-vm 061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, explicit JIT 061 source-contract tests under `--features jit`, and existing 006 queue/go-island transfer transaction regressions; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening` and `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-TRANSFER-COMMIT-CONSUMER-COVERAGE-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | Every path that commits queue-transfer local endpoint state must either convert `QueueTransferCommit` into runtime rollback or restore the same commit manually on the enclosing transaction failure. | The first rollback fix covered interpreter/JIT `RemoteSend` and `GoIsland`, but red-team audit found two remaining consumers still used commit-dropping helpers: `RemoteDirect` queue send prepared nested payload ports before returning `RemoteRecvData`, and endpoint `Recv` response packing prepared buffered payload ports before a later response publish failure. Both could leave payload-port `HomeInfo` and endpoint-registry entries live after the response command was not delivered. | Proof tests `vm_remote_direct_transfer_txn_061_late_response_failure_rolls_back_payload_home_info` and `vm_endpoint_recv_transfer_txn_061_late_response_failure_rolls_back_payload_home_info`. The fix makes RemoteDirect merge local-queue rollback with endpoint-transfer rollback, splits endpoint recv preflight into validation-only plus pack-time commit, and adds `QueueTransferCommit::absorb` / `restore_committed_local_endpoint_state` so the endpoint handler owns one rollback object. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target cargo test -p vo-vm 061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture` and `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit 061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening` and `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-COMPOSITE-ROLLBACK-AUX-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | Composite runtime rollback must be a transparent rollback owner for every auxiliary mutation attached after composition, including stack-slot and select-state snapshots. | `RuntimeRollback::combine` could merge local queue rollback with endpoint-transfer rollback, but later calls to `push_stack_slot` or `set_select_state` became no-ops on the composite wrapper. Select remote endpoint paths that combined rollback objects could therefore lose current-frame and select-state restoration if transition publication failed. | Proof test `vm_composite_rollback_061_forwards_frame_and_select_state_rollback`; fix makes `RuntimeRollback::Composite` forward `push_stack_slot` and `set_select_state` to each child rollback, preserving the single rollback object while keeping restoration ownership in the existing variant implementations. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target cargo test -p vo-vm runtime_boundary::tests::vm_composite_rollback_061_forwards_frame_and_select_state_rollback -- --exact --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-SAME-ISLAND-ENDPOINT-RESPONSE-PREFLIGHT-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | Same-island endpoint responses must be preflighted before earlier effects in the same runtime transition can wake or mutate local fibers. | `preflight_island_command_effect` skipped every same-island `EndpointResponse`, so a transition containing an ordinary local wake followed by a stale same-island endpoint response could wake the local queue waiter before `apply_endpoint_response` rejected the stale endpoint wait id. | Proof test `vm_same_island_endpoint_response_preflight_061_rejects_before_local_wake`; fix routes same-island endpoint responses through the same response-source and pending-response validation used by runtime command application, while leaving unauthorized responses as no-op-compatible during preflight. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target2 cargo test -p vo-vm runtime_boundary::tests::vm_same_island_endpoint_response_preflight_061_rejects_before_local_wake -- --exact --test-threads=1 --nocapture` and `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target cargo test -p vo-vm 061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-JIT-REMOTE-ENDPOINT-ROLLBACK-COVERAGE-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | JIT queue/select remote endpoint callbacks must forward rollback returned by interpreter-domain execution into the pending runtime transition before the VM boundary applier publishes island commands. | JIT source already forwarded the rollback on these paths, but the gate surface did not have a focused contract proving both QueueSend `RemoteRecvData` and Select `RemoteSendAck`/`RemoteRecvData` preserve that bridge. That left future callback edits free to drop rollback while the interpreter tests remained green. | Source-contract proofs `vm_jit_queue_send_remote_recv_data_rollback_061_source_attaches_runtime_rollback` and `vm_jit_select_remote_endpoint_rollback_061_source_attaches_runtime_rollback`; tests enumerate the JIT callback arms and assert rollback is attached before `commit_*_transition`. | Focused validation passed both JIT proof tests and `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit 061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-JIT-ISLANDNEW-ABI-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | JIT runtime helpers that write through raw out pointers must validate the pointer before invoking any VM-visible side-effect callback. | `vo_island_new` called `create_island_fn` before checking `out`, so a malformed JIT helper ABI could create and register an island, then crash or corrupt memory while writing the handle. | Mechanical source-contract proof `vm_jit_island_new_abi_061_validates_out_before_create_callback`; fix rejects null `ctx/out` before the create callback, using `JIT_INFRA_ERROR_INVALID_CALLBACK_STATE` for invalid out pointers. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target cargo test -p vo-runtime 061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `vm-production` through `runtime-contract` / `cargo-test-runtime`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-JIT-SLICENEW-ABI-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | JIT allocation helpers that return status plus an out pointer must validate the out pointer before constructing GC-visible runtime objects. | `vo_slice_new_checked` called `slice::create_checked` before checking `out`, so a malformed JIT helper ABI could allocate slice state and then fail while publishing the handle. | Mechanical source-contract proof `vm_jit_slice_new_checked_abi_061_validates_out_before_constructor`; fix mirrors the queue helper by rejecting null `gc/out` with the existing allocation overflow status before invoking the slice constructor. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target cargo test -p vo-runtime 061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `vm-production` through `runtime-contract` / `cargo-test-runtime`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-ENDPOINT-RESPONSE-ACTIVATION-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | A local endpoint response obligation is single-use across all RuntimeTransition representations, including closed queue wakes and same-island endpoint response commands. | Transition preflight de-duplicated ordinary wake activations but did not unify endpoint-response activations across `WakeCommand::queue_closed_receiver/sender` and same-island `IslandCommand::EndpointResponse`. Both forms could preflight against the same pending response, then the first apply consumed the obligation and the second failed after local wake mutation. | Behavior proof `vm_endpoint_response_activation_061_rejects_cross_form_duplicate_before_commit`; fix adds one local endpoint response activation key source covering endpoint closed wakes and authorized same-island response commands before per-effect preflight. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target cargo test -p vo-vm 061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit 061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, and `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target cargo test -p vo-vm endpoint_response -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening` and `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-SAME-ISLAND-WAKEFIBER-PREFLIGHT-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | Same-island `WakeFiber` island commands must be proven acceptable before any earlier local wake in the same transition can commit. | Same-island `IslandCommand::WakeFiber` was accepted during preflight and then rejected during apply when the waiter was endpoint-shaped or otherwise failed queue identity checks, leaving earlier local wakes committed. | Behavior proof `vm_same_island_wake_fiber_command_061_rejects_before_local_wake`; fix preflights same-island `WakeFiber` against the same queue-wake identity, endpoint-wait exclusion, generation, and blocked-fiber checks used by runtime wake application. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target cargo test -p vo-vm 061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit 061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, and `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target cargo test -p vo-vm endpoint_response -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening` and `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
