| VM-JIT-IFACEASSERT-ABI-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | JIT helpers that write assertion results through raw destination pointers must validate those pointers before interface matching, itab-cache lookup, or result publication. | `vo_iface_assert` could evaluate interface satisfaction and enter successful assertion publishing before proving `dst` was non-null. | Mechanical source-contract proof `vm_jit_iface_assert_abi_061_validates_dst_before_match_or_write`; fix rejects null `ctx/dst` before interface matching and reports invalid dst through `JIT_INFRA_ERROR_INVALID_CALLBACK_STATE`. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target cargo test -p vo-runtime 061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `vm-production` through `runtime-contract` / `cargo-test-runtime`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-JIT-IFACEASSERT-LAYOUT-ABI-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | `IfaceAssert` result publication must derive result width from `IfaceAssertLayout` metadata before helper raw out writes or JIT local writeback, not from encoded flags alone. | Follow-up JIT/ABI red-team found `IfaceAssert` verifier and semantic rows required `IfaceAssertLayout`, but JIT lowering still allocated/read result buffers from `inst.flags >> 3` and `vo_iface_assert` wrote ok/default/result slots before validating current-PC metadata. Malformed helper ABI drift could therefore publish raw result slots inconsistent with the per-PC layout contract. | Failing-first proof `vm_jit_iface_assert_layout_abi_061_rejects_width_drift_before_out_write`; mechanical source-contract proof `jit_iface_assert_lowering_uses_metadata_layout_061`. The fix adds `IfaceAssertLayout` metadata decoding/access, makes JIT lowering size and write back result buffers from metadata, and makes `vo_iface_assert` validate flags-derived result width against current-PC metadata before interface lookup or `dst` writes. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-runtime vm_jit_iface_assert_layout_abi_061_rejects_width_drift_before_out_write -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-jit jit_iface_assert_lowering_uses_metadata_layout_061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-runtime 061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture` (14/14), `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-jit interface -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, and `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-jit semantics -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `vm-production` through `runtime-contract` / `cargo-test-runtime` and `jit-contract` / `cargo-test-jit`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-IFACEASSERT-RESULT-ROOT-LAYOUT-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | `IfaceAssertLayout` metadata must match the asserted target's real result slot layout, including GC root kind, before verifier acceptance and JIT metadata checks. | Follow-up GC/root red-team found `verify_iface_assert_contract` checked only encoded result width. A malformed module could declare `Value` metadata for a `String` assertion result, or `[Value, Value]` for interface-to-interface assertion output, leaving GC-bearing result slots invisible to root scanning while still satisfying width checks. | Failing-first verifier proofs `module_verifier_rejects_iface_assert_value_result_root_layout_drift_061` and `module_verifier_rejects_iface_assert_interface_result_root_layout_drift_061`; JIT metadata test `rejects_iface_assert_missing_or_mismatched_result_layout` now observes the same source contract before destination-shape fallback. The fix makes the module verifier derive the target layout from `RuntimeType` / interface assertion kind and reject metadata drift before local layout checks. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-common-core iface_assert_ -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-common-core verifier -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-jit interface -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo check -p vo-vm --features jit`, and `cargo fmt --check`; selected by `vm-production` through `common-core-contract` / `cargo-test-common-core` and by `jit-contract` / `cargo-test-jit`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-IFACEASSERT-HASOK-OUTPUT-TXN-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | `IfaceAssert` comma-ok output slots are one transaction: success result and ok flag commit only after all result materialization succeeds, and failure/error paths leave the output window unchanged except for the documented failed-assertion default result. | Follow-up runtime-boundary red-team found interpreter and JIT helper paths wrote the ok slot before success result materialization could fail, so malformed metadata could publish `ok=true` without a valid result. | Failing-first proofs `exec_iface_assert_has_ok_does_not_write_ok_before_success_materialization_061` and `vm_jit_iface_assert_has_ok_does_not_write_ok_before_success_materialization_061`; fix materializes success slots into bounded local buffers and commits result plus ok only after the whole success value is proven. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit 061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture` and `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-runtime 061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm`, `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`, and `runtime-contract` / `cargo-test-runtime`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-IFACEASSIGN-DEST-RAW-PAIR-ROOT-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | `IfaceAssign` destination slots must be verified as the canonical interface pair root layout, not merely a same-width raw `[Value, Value]` window. | Follow-up verifier/frame-call red-team found `verify_iface_assign_contract` accepted raw two-slot value layouts for interface destinations, allowing interface metadata/data slots to evade the interface-pair root contract. | Failing-first proof `module_verifier_rejects_iface_assign_raw_pair_destination_root_layout_061`; fix routes destination validation through the shared `verify_interface_pair` contract before assignment metadata can be accepted. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-common-core verifier -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `vm-production` through `common-core-contract` / `cargo-test-common-core`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-IFACEASSERT-TARGET-MODE-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | `IfaceAssert` target mode must agree with target metadata: interface-to-interface assertions require target interface metadata, and concrete assertions must derive result layout from a non-interface runtime type. | Follow-up verifier/JIT audit found concrete assertions could target an interface runtime type, while interface assertions could omit target interface metadata. That split let verifier/JIT layout checks reason from flags or destination shape instead of the asserted target. | Failing-first proofs `module_verifier_rejects_iface_assert_missing_target_interface_meta_061` and `module_verifier_rejects_iface_assert_concrete_kind_interface_target_061`; mechanical source proof `vm_jit_iface_assert_target_layout_abi_061_validates_target_layout_before_out_write`. The fix derives expected result layout from target mode in both module verification and the JIT helper's current-PC ABI validation. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-common-core verifier -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-runtime 061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, and `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-jit interface -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-common-core`, `cargo-test-runtime`, and `cargo-test-jit`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-JIT-QUEUE-SELECT-LAYOUT-WIDTH-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | JIT QueueSend/QueueRecv/SelectSend/SelectRecv helper ABI widths must come from `QueueLayout` metadata and be revalidated at the VM callback boundary before raw slot access or select-case mutation. | Follow-up JIT/OSR/extern red-team found lowering still passed encoded flags as helper widths while verifier required `QueueLayout`; callbacks also reached raw buffers or select mutation before proving helper width matched metadata. | Mechanical source-contract proofs `jit_queue_select_lowering_uses_metadata_layout_061`, `vm_jit_queue_callbacks_validate_metadata_width_before_raw_access_061`, and `vm_jit_select_callbacks_validate_metadata_width_before_case_mutation_061`; fix adds one metadata access fact source for queue element slots, uses it in lowering, and validates metadata width in queue/select callbacks before raw spans or case updates. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-jit queue_select -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit 061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-jit semantics -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, and `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo check -p vo-vm --features jit`; selected by `vm-production` through `jit-contract` / `cargo-test-jit` and `vm-hardening` / `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-STORAGE-RAW-PAIR-ROOT-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | Raw `[Value, Value]` storage metadata must not be accepted as compatible with `[Interface0, Interface1]` local roots; container/indexed storage copies must preserve exact root-kind layout. | Round-2 verifier red-team found `verify_storage_layout_compatible` still accepted raw `Value` metadata backed by structural interface-pair locals. `MapGet` and same-family storage paths could therefore copy raw map/indexed values into locals scanned as interface roots. | Failing-first proof `module_verifier_rejects_map_get_raw_value_layout_into_interface_destination_061`; fix removes the raw-value/interface-pair compatibility bypass, leaving structural interface validation as a shape check before exact slot-layout rejection. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-common-core module_verifier_rejects_map_get_raw_value_layout_into_interface_destination_061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture` and full `cargo test -p vo-common-core`; selected by `vm-production` through `common-core-contract` / `cargo-test-common-core`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-JIT-IFACEASSERT-FLAGS-WIDTH-ABI-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | `IfaceAssert` helper flags, target-derived layout, `IfaceAssertLayout` metadata, and JIT lowering scratch/readback width must agree before output writes. | Round-2 GC/root red-team found `vo_iface_assert` validated metadata against target layout but still used encoded `flags >> 3` for ok-slot placement and success/failure writes. A helper ABI call whose target layout and metadata agreed but flags width drifted could write ok/result outside the metadata-owned scratch layout. | Failing-first proof `vm_jit_iface_assert_flags_width_abi_061_rejects_flags_drift_before_out_write`; fix adds the shared `iface_assert_result_slots_from_flags` fact source, tightens `pack_iface_assert_flags` for interface assertions, and makes verifier/runtime validation reject flags/result-layout drift before helper writes. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-runtime vm_jit_iface_assert_flags_width_abi_061_rejects_flags_drift_before_out_write -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, `cargo test -p vo-runtime 061`, full `cargo test -p vo-common-core`, `cargo test -p vo-jit interface`, `cargo test -p vo-jit semantics`, and `cargo check -p vo-vm --features jit`; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-runtime`, `cargo-test-common-core`, and `cargo-test-jit`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-CODEGEN-CALLIFACE-LAYOUT-PROOF-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | Codegen proof tests for dynamic/interface calls must assert the current `CallIfaceLayout` contract instead of stale `CallLayout` metadata for `CallIface`. | Focused codegen widening found two integration tests failed while source emitted precise `CallIfaceLayout { iface_meta_id, arg_layout, ret_layout }`. The proof assertions still required the older `CallLayout` variant, making `cargo-test-codegen` a false blocker. | Source-contract tests `discarded_dynamic_calls_encode_callee_return_layout` and `scheduled_interface_wrappers_encode_callee_return_layout_but_discard_results` now accept `CallIfaceLayout` for `CallIface` and keep `CallLayout` for `CallClosure`. | Full validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-codegen -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `vm-production` and `contract` through `codegen-contract` / `cargo-test-codegen`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-IFACEASSERT-LOAD-FIXTURE-FLAGS-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | VM-load malformed-module proofs must encode `IfaceAssert` with legal packed flags when the proof target is missing metadata; otherwise the shared flags-width verifier masks the intended metadata contract. | The final `contract` gate exposed a stale VM negative fixture: `malformed_iface_assert_metadata_is_vm_error_instead_of_panic` used interface assertion flags with zero target slots, so the verifier correctly rejected the packed flags before reaching the missing `IfaceAssertLayout` path the test claimed to prove. | The fixture now uses `pack_iface_assert_flags(1, false, 2)` so it exercises the missing-layout VM-load rejection under the current shared flags contract. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-vm vm::tests::malformed_iface_assert_metadata_is_vm_error_instead_of_panic -- --exact --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `vm-production` and `contract` through `runtime-contract` / `cargo-test-vm`, final gates not yet rerun. |
| VM-JIT-MAP-RAW-BUFFER-ABI-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | JIT map helpers with sentinel error channels must validate non-empty raw key/value/iterator buffers before raw reads, raw writes, barriers, iterator mutation, or map mutation. | `vo_map_get`, `vo_map_set`, `vo_map_delete`, `vo_map_iter_init`, and `vo_map_iter_next` trusted raw buffer pointers before `from_raw_parts`, `write_bytes`, `copy_nonoverlapping`, typed barriers, or map/iterator mutation. | Mechanical source-contract proof `vm_jit_map_helpers_abi_061_validate_raw_buffers_before_access`; fix adds shared raw in/out/inout buffer validators and routes invalid buffers to the existing sentinel error channel with `JIT_INFRA_ERROR_INVALID_CALLBACK_STATE`. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target cargo test -p vo-runtime 061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `vm-production` through `runtime-contract` / `cargo-test-runtime`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-DYN-METHOD-CONTRACT-SELECTION-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | Proof cases cited as contract evidence must be selected by the corresponding contract gate, not only by broad `vm-production` language sweeps or focused ad-hoc commands. | The runtime-boundary repair record named `./d.py test both tests/lang/cases/dyn/dyn_method_value_receiver_multi_slot.vo` as proof, but `vo-test-runtime-language-contract` selects runtime-owned cases tagged `contract`; `dyn.dyn-method-value-receiver-multi-slot` had only `dyn`, `method`, and `regression` tags. | Manifest update adds the `contract` tag to `dyn.dyn-method-value-receiver-multi-slot`. | Selection validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- test plan --suite lang --owner runtime --tags contract --targets vm,jit,osr --release --format json`, showing `dyn.dyn-method-value-receiver-multi-slot::{vm,jit,osr}` selected. After VM-PTRNEW-CONSTANT-FACT-061 closed, focused execution passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- test run --suite lang --path tests/lang/cases/dyn/dyn_method_value_receiver_multi_slot.vo --targets vm,jit,osr --release --jobs 1` (3/3); final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-PTRNEW-CONSTANT-FACT-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | Every verifier check that consumes a runtime metadata register must register that slot as a constant-fact consumer, so fact analysis cannot depend on whether another metadata-consuming opcode appears in the same function. | The 060 PtrNew hardening required `PtrNew.b` to be a reachable constant, but `tracked_constant_slots` did not include `Opcode::PtrNew`. Functions with only `PtrNew` skipped constant-fact checking entirely, while functions that also used another tracked metadata opcode ran the analysis and reported the PtrNew metadata register as untracked. | Proof test `dynamic_method_value_receiver_ptr_new_metadata_fact_is_tracked_061`; fix adds `Opcode::PtrNew => inst.b` to the shared constant-fact tracked-slot source. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target cargo test -p vo-codegen --test integration dynamic_method_value_receiver_ptr_new_metadata_fact_is_tracked_061 -- --exact --test-threads=1 --nocapture` and the dyn method language proof above; the PtrNew Rust proof is selected by `vm-production` and `contract` through `codegen-contract` / `cargo-test-codegen`, while the dyn language proof is selected through `runtime-contract` / `vo-test-runtime-language-contract`; final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-JIT-IFACE-TO-IFACE-ABI-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | The runtime helper ABI manifest, JIT semantic row, and lowering descriptor must agree that `vo_iface_to_iface` reports failure through the `JIT_HELPER_U64_ERROR` sentinel, not a raw `u64` success value. | Red-team JIT/ABI audit found `vo_iface_to_iface` was declared as `RawU64` / no-panic even though the helper returns `JIT_HELPER_U64_ERROR` for invalid interface conversion state and the lowering already branches on that sentinel. The mismatch left contract graph and semantic policy free to treat a sentinel as an ordinary slot0 value. | Mechanical source-contract proof `vm_jit_iface_to_iface_abi_061_declares_sentinel_error_channel`; fix changes the runtime helper ABI manifest to `U64ErrorSentinel` / `ReturnsStatusOrSentinel`, adds the `IfaceAssign` lowering descriptor, and updates the `IfaceAssign` semantic row to `U64JitErrorSentinel`. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target cargo test -p vo-runtime jit_api::tests::vm_jit_iface_to_iface_abi_061_declares_sentinel_error_channel -- --exact --nocapture`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target cargo test -p vo-jit semantics::tests::runtime_helper_lowering_descriptors_match_abi_and_opcode_policy -- --exact --nocapture`, and full `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- task run vm-hardening`; selected by `vm-production` through `runtime-contract` / `cargo-test-runtime` and `vm-hardening` / `cargo-test-jit-hardening`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-EXTERN-REPLAY-TRANSFER-VALIDATION-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | Extern `CallClosure` replay must validate transfer-typed GC/interface arguments against the target closure's `FunctionDef.param_types` and runtime object kind before publishing an interpreter or JIT replay frame. | Red-team interpreter/verifier audit found extern replay payload validation accepted GC-visible slots by slot shape alone. A closure target with missing `param_types`, or a `String` transfer parameter backed by a slice/map/channel object, could be replayed into a frame before the shared transfer contract had proven object kind and interface ownership. | Failing-first proofs `vm_extern_replay_transfer_contract_061_rejects_missing_param_types_before_frame_push` and `vm_extern_replay_transfer_contract_061_rejects_wrong_object_kind_before_frame_push`; fix routes direct replay and typed JIT suspend replay through one `validate_extern_replay_transfer_args` helper using the target module, target `FunctionDef`, GC, and `ItabCache`. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target cargo test -p vo-vm vm_extern_replay_transfer_contract_061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture` and full `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- task run vm-hardening`; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening` and `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-EXTERN-REPLAY-INTERFACE-DATA-VALIDATION-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | Extern replay interface arguments must bind slot1 heap data to the concrete RTTID/kind encoded in slot0 before frame publication. | Follow-up interpreter/frame-call red-team found the new replay transfer gate proved interface slot0 RTTID/itab ownership but did not validate slot1's GC object header. A forged interface could advertise a named `String` value and valid itab in slot0 while supplying a slice/array/map object in slot1; replay then accepted the pair before the callee frame owned GC-visible data. | Failing-first proof `vm_extern_replay_transfer_contract_061_rejects_interface_slot1_heap_kind_before_frame_push`; fix extends the shared replay transfer validator so interface data refs are canonicalized, checked against the expected heap header kind, checked for struct/pointer meta id, and checked for array length/element metadata/physical element bytes from the canonical RTTID layout. | Focused exact proof validation passed, `cargo test -p vo-vm` passed 417 tests, and `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit` passed 596 tests in the clean target directory; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening` and `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-EXTERN-REPLAY-ARRAY-DATA-BOX-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | Extern replay interface data validation must accept the VM's canonical flattened value-slot box representation for fixed-array values, not only heap `Array` headers. | Red-team frame-call audit found the first interface-data replay guard falsely rejected array values carried in struct-backed value-slot boxes, even though that is the canonical frame/transfer representation for flattened array payloads. | Failing-first proof `vm_extern_replay_transfer_contract_061_accepts_interface_array_value_slot_box`; follow-up proof `vm_extern_replay_transfer_contract_061_rejects_interface_array_value_slot_box_slot_count_drift` guards undersized value-slot boxes. The fix routes interface array data through `validate_extern_replay_interface_array_data`, accepting either heap arrays with matching element metadata or struct value-slot boxes whose `StructMeta.slot_types` and allocation slot count exactly match `Module::slot_layout_for_value_rttid`. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-vm vm_extern_replay_transfer_contract_061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture` (6/6); selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening` and `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-EXTERN-REPLAY-INTERFACE-DATA-SIZE-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | Extern replay interface struct/pointer/array data boxes must prove allocation slot count against the canonical runtime layout before a replay frame can publish slot1. | The initial interface-data guard checked header kind/meta but still accepted undersized struct-backed data boxes, allowing a forged interface value to enter a replay frame with fewer GC-visible slots than the canonical type layout requires. | Failing-first proofs `vm_extern_replay_transfer_contract_061_rejects_interface_struct_data_slot_count_drift` and `vm_extern_replay_transfer_contract_061_rejects_interface_array_value_slot_box_slot_count_drift`; fix shares slot-width validation for struct, pointer, and struct-backed array value boxes and rejects allocation `data_size` drift before `typed_extern_replay_args` or `FrameCallBuilder` can change frames. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-vm vm_extern_replay_transfer_contract_061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture` (6/6); selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening` and `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-JIT-RUNTIME-PANIC-SENTINEL-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | A user/runtime panic must remain a panic even when its user argument equals the JIT infra-error sentinel value; the sentinel is meaningful only on infra-error result paths. | JIT transition handling checked `runtime_trap_arg0 == JIT_INFRA_ERROR_SENTINEL` before classifying `JitResult::Panic`, so a legitimate runtime panic whose payload argument matched the sentinel was misreported as a JIT infrastructure error. | Failing-first proof `vm_jit_runtime_panic_061_keeps_user_arg_equal_to_infra_sentinel`; fix classifies `JitResult::Panic` before the infra sentinel guard while preserving sentinel handling for non-panic non-Ok results. | Focused validation passed `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_jit_runtime_panic_061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture` and the existing infra-sentinel guard `vm_jit_call_request_abi_018_call_result_honors_infra_error_sentinel`; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-EXTERN-RETURN-INTERFACE-DATA-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | Runtime FFI interface return verification must validate slot1 data object kind, metadata, and allocation width against the concrete RTTID encoded in slot0 before return slots are committed. | Follow-up FFI audit found `verify_return_interface_metadata` validated interface/itab identity but not the returned interface data object, so a provider could return slot0 for `String` and slot1 for a slice/array/map object. The same path also treated interface meta id 0 as empty by id rather than by the interface meta's method set. | Failing-first proofs `ffi_resolved_return_shape_rejects_wrong_kind_interface_data_061`, `ffi_resolved_return_shape_rejects_wrong_meta_interface_data_061`, `ffi_resolved_return_shape_rejects_struct_interface_data_slot_count_drift_061`, and `ffi_resolved_return_shape_rejects_array_value_slot_box_count_drift_061`; fix validates interface slot1 through shared kind/meta/width rules for fixed heap objects, structs, pointers, heap arrays, and struct-backed array value boxes, and treats an interface as empty only when its `InterfaceMeta.methods` is empty. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-runtime ffi_resolved_return_shape_rejects -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture` (8/8); selected by `vm-production` through `runtime-contract` / `cargo-test-runtime`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-CALLIFACE-CALLSITE-ITAB-061 | Closed, 2026-06-22 | `CallIface` callsites must carry the expected interface metadata id as the same callsite fact source as arg/return layout, and interpreter/JIT dispatch must reject invalid itabs or malformed frame windows before IC mutation or frame push. | Red-team interpreter/JIT audit found `CallIface` validated the receiver and target shape but did not bind the dynamic itab to the interface expected by the callsite. A same-receiver, same-signature itab for a different interface could drive the wrong method vector through the inline cache. Follow-up red-team found the interpreter filled the `CallIface` inline cache before the final borrowed-return register preflight, so a malformed hand-built call with return-offset overflow or final borrowed-frame capacity failure could publish an IC entry before returning a structured error. | Failing-first proofs `vm_call_iface_contract_061_rejects_foreign_same_receiver_same_shape_itab_before_frame_push`, `vm_call_iface_contract_061_rejects_return_offset_overflow_before_ic_mutation`, `vm_call_iface_contract_061_rejects_frame_capacity_before_ic_mutation`, and `vm_call_iface_contract_061_jit_prepare_rejects_foreign_same_receiver_same_shape_itab_before_frame_push`; fix adds `JitInstructionMetadata::CallIfaceLayout { iface_meta_id, arg_layout, ret_layout }`, routes codegen, serialization, verifier, JIT semantic metadata requirements, contract graph, interpreter dispatch, and JIT prepared iface calls through that single metadata source, validates itab `iface_meta_id` before IC allocation or frame publication, and keeps interpreter IC fill after return-register preflight and after successful borrowed-frame publication. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-main cargo test -p vo-vm vm_call_iface_contract_061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture` (3 tests), plus earlier `cargo test -p vo-vm call_iface -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_call_iface_contract_061_jit_prepare_rejects_foreign_same_receiver_same_shape_itab_before_frame_push -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, `cargo test -p vo-common-core call_iface -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, `cargo test -p vo-common-core serialize::tests::test_serialize_deserialize_precise_jit_layout_metadata -- --exact --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo check -p vo-vm --features jit`, and `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo check -p vo-codegen`; selected by `vm-production` through common-core, codegen, VM hardening, and JIT hardening tasks, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-DOCS-LINT-VO-DEV-TIMEOUT-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | Docs lint must bound every synchronous `vo-dev` query it performs while resolving VM production final selectors and task plans, so readiness gates fail with a diagnostic instead of hanging indefinitely. | Red-team gate-coverage audit found `scripts/ci/docs_lint.mjs` called `cargo run -q -p vo-dev` with `spawnSync` and no timeout for final selector and task-plan resolution. If `vo-dev` hung, docs lint could hang while trying to prove signoff evidence. | Mechanical source-contract proof `lintVmProductionVoDevSpawnTimeoutContract`; fix routes both `vo-dev` JSON calls through `runVoDevJson`, with default 10-minute timeout, `VO_DEV_DOCS_LINT_VO_DEV_TIMEOUT_MS` override validation, and explicit `ETIMEDOUT` diagnostics. | Focused validation passed `node --check scripts/ci/docs_lint.mjs` and full `node scripts/ci/docs_lint.mjs`, which executes the self-test through the docs-lint gate; final production gates not yet run from this source state. |
| VM-ENDPOINT-RESPONSE-COMMAND-SOURCE-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | Direct `RuntimeCommand::endpoint_response` and `endpoint_closed_response` commands must carry the response source island and validate it against the endpoint home/tombstone authority before waiter resume, pending-count decrement, remote-proxy close, or tombstone publication. | Red-team runtime-boundary audit found the direct command bridge could synthesize endpoint responses without a source island. Closed responses checked only command self-consistency, so a non-home island could tombstone a live remote endpoint if it reached the bridge directly. | Failing-first proofs `vm_endpoint_response_command_bridge_061_rejects_non_home_closed_response_before_tombstone` and `vm_endpoint_response_command_bridge_061_rejects_non_home_targeted_response_before_waiter_resume`; fix carries `from_island` through `RuntimeCommandTarget` and `SourceWakeToken`, uses the shared endpoint response authorization helper for targeted and closed responses, and lets same-island closed queue wakes install a rollback-protected source tombstone before consuming the same command bridge. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-vm vm_endpoint_response_command_bridge_061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture` (2/2) and `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-vm endpoint_response -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture` (26/26); selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-JIT-MAPGET-NIL-ABI-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | JIT map helper nil/default-value fast paths must validate raw ABI key/value slot counts against current-PC `MapGet` metadata before writing through raw output buffers. | Red-team JIT helper audit found `vo_map_get` validated raw buffer non-nullness, but when `m == 0` it zeroed `val_slots` words before consulting current-PC metadata or map layout. Malformed JIT/helper ABI drift could therefore pass a one-slot output buffer with `val_slots=2` and clobber the adjacent slot. | Failing-first proof `vm_jit_map_get_nil_abi_061_rejects_value_width_drift_before_zeroing`; fix adds `validate_jit_map_key_value_abi_slots_for_current_pc` and calls it before the nil-map zeroing path, while keeping non-nil map object metadata/layout validation intact. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-runtime vm_jit_map_get_nil_abi_061_rejects_value_width_drift_before_zeroing -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-runtime 061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture` (12/12), `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo check -p vo-runtime`, and `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-jit map -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture` (10/10); selected by `vm-production` through `runtime-contract` / `cargo-test-runtime` and `jit-contract` / `cargo-test-jit`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-MAP-DEFAULT-OUTPUT-ABI-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | Interpreter and JIT map default-output paths must validate encoded/helper key-value slot counts against the per-PC map metadata before publishing zero/default key, value, or ok slots. | Follow-up GC/root red-team found the `MapGet` interpreter nil-map path, the `MapIterNext` interpreter nil/default iterator path, and the `vo_map_iter_next` JIT helper nil iterator path could publish zero/default outputs before proving the encoded/helper ABI widths matched current metadata. JIT lowering also derived `MapIterNext` helper widths from instruction flags while verifier semantics used `JitInstructionMetadata::MapIterNext`. | Failing-first proofs `exec_map_get_nil_rejects_value_width_drift_before_stack_write_061`, `exec_map_iter_next_nil_rejects_value_width_drift_before_stack_write_061`, and `vm_jit_map_iter_next_nil_abi_061_rejects_value_width_drift_before_zeroing`; mechanical source-contract proof `jit_map_iter_next_lowering_uses_metadata_layout_061`. The fix adds a shared interpreter expected-layout slot check, calls `validate_jit_map_key_value_abi_slots_for_current_pc` before `vo_map_iter_next` default writes, and routes JIT `MapIterNext` lowering through the same per-PC metadata layout as verifier/runtime helper checks. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-vm nil_rejects_value_width_drift -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-runtime vm_jit_map_iter_next_nil_abi_061_rejects_value_width_drift_before_zeroing -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-jit jit_map_iter_next_lowering_uses_metadata_layout_061 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, and `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo fmt --check`; selected by `vm-production` through `runtime-contract` / `cargo-test-vm` and `cargo-test-runtime`, and through `jit-contract` / `cargo-test-jit`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-INTERFACE-RETURN-FIXTURE-METADATA-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | Contract tests and codegen fixture registrations that model interface return pairs must use `ReturnShape.interface_metas`; slots-only `Interface0`/`Interface1` shapes are invalid except in tests that explicitly prove rejection. | Red-team test/gate audit found stale fixtures in `vo-codegen` and runtime FFI tests still constructed interface returns with `ReturnShape::with_slot_types` or the layout-only variable-return helper. Those fixtures no longer represented the production contract and could mask missing interface metadata coverage. | Source-contract fixture updates route the `variable_ret_externs_are_keyed_by_precise_return_layout_058`, `variable_ret_dynamic_externs_preserve_exact_parameter_abi_058`, and `ffi_resolved_return_shape_rejects_invalid_interface_gc_data_return` proofs through `ReturnShape::try_with_slot_types_and_interface_metas` and an explicit empty-interface module. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-codegen variable_ret_externs_are_keyed_by_precise_return_layout_058 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-round2 cargo test -p vo-codegen variable_ret_dynamic_externs_preserve_exact_parameter_abi_058 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, and the FFI return-shape proof group above; selected by `vm-production` through `codegen-contract` / `cargo-test-codegen` and `runtime-contract` / `cargo-test-runtime`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-HARDENING-FIXTURE-CONTRACT-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | VM hardening tests must construct modules, waiters, and JIT resume points that satisfy current verifier/runtime contracts, so gate failures distinguish product regressions from stale proof fixtures. | After the 061 fixes, `vm-hardening` exposed stale fixtures: interface receiver tests used noncanonical RTTIDs or missing interface metadata, runtime-boundary wake tests created a fresh queue waiter instead of reusing the registered waiter identity, and JIT special-call tests declared resume return windows that no longer matched function metadata. | Mechanical gate proof was the failing `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- task run vm-hardening`; closure updates the fixtures to use canonical named runtime types/interface metadata, reuse the exact `QueueWaiter` registration, and encode legal return slots/windows for nested JIT materialization. | Focused validation passed the affected groups (`cargo test -p vo-common-core 058`, `cargo test -p vo-jit verifier::tests::interface::rejects`, `cargo test -p vo-vm vm_closure_call_signature_002_call_iface_rejects`, `cargo test -p vo-vm vm_resume_owner_002_closed_sender_wake_replays_via_runtime_boundary`, and `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_gc_full_jit_special`), then full `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- task run vm-hardening` passed; final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-EXTERN-INTERFACE-RETURN-EXPECTED-IFACE-ABI-060 | Closed, 2026-06-19 | Interface return/call metadata must carry enough static interface identity to prove that a raw `(slot0, slot1)` pair's itab belongs to the interface expected by the extern return or `CallIface` callsite. | `ReturnShape` recorded only `Interface0/Interface1` slots, not the expected `InterfaceMeta` id; follow-up audit found slots-only serialized shapes could still carry interface metadata, and same-shaped method vectors could still bind the wrong signature. | Proof tests `return_shape_constructor_rejects_invalid_interface_metadata_060`, `deserialize_rejects_extern_return_shape_interface_metadata_drift_060`, `deserialize_rejects_extern_return_shape_slots_only_interface_metadata_060`, `module_verifier_rejects_extern_return_shape_slots_only_interface_metadata_060`, `declared_extern_rejects_same_name_different_return_interface_metadata_060`, `defer_extern_wrapper_cache_key_does_not_bypass_return_interface_metadata_060`, `vm_iface_assign_rejects_itab_method_name_mismatch_060`, `itab_cache_rejects_signature_mismatch_060`, `ffi_expected_interface_itab_methods_rejects_signature_mismatch_060`, `ffi_resolved_return_shape_rejects_slots_only_interface_metadata_060`, and `ffi_resolved_return_shape_rejects_wrong_signature_itab_060`; fix adds `ReturnShape.interface_metas`, load/verifier validation, exact extern/wrapper merge checks, dynamic extern expected-interface registrations, shared itab method/signature validation, and FFI return verification that rejects slots-only metadata and wrong-interface or wrong-signature itabs before result slots enter VM frames. | Compiled by `cargo test -p vo-common-core --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-codegen --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-runtime --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-vm --no-run`, and `cargo check -p vo-jit`; focused proof execution still hangs at test-binary startup and is not counted as passed. |
| VM-GC-TRANSITION-NONE-LIVE-002 | Closed, 2026-06-16 | A live current fiber that crosses a runtime transition after changing stack, global, defer, or replay roots must mark the affected roots dirty, or stronger, before GC can observe them. | `ExecResult::Transition(_)` suppresses the outer current-fiber dirty mark, and some live-fiber transitions still used `GcRootEffect::None`, including remote recv-data and JIT pending queue/select/go-island transitions. | Failing source-contract tests `vm_gc_transition_none_live_002_interpreter_recv_data_transitions_dirty_current_fiber`, `vm_gc_transition_none_live_002_jit_queue_send_remote_recv_data_marks_current_fiber_dirty`, and `vm_gc_transition_none_live_002_jit_select_remote_recv_data_marks_current_fiber_dirty`; fix changes recv-data endpoint response transitions to `GcRootEffect::CurrentFiberDirty` and marks JIT go-island pending commands all-roots dirty. | Passed focused `vo-vm` and `vo-vm --features jit` regressions; selected by `vm_` hardening in `vm-production`. |
| VM-PENDING-TRANSITION-ROOTS-002 | Closed, 2026-06-16 | Every root-bearing pending runtime payload must either be impossible to observe during GC or be scanned by the precise root scanner. | `VmState.pending_runtime_transitions` can hold spawned fibers, island commands, and wake payloads, but the GC root snapshot does not scan that vector; safety relied on an implicit "pending is drained before GC" invariant. | Failing proof test `vm_pending_transition_roots_002_gc_entry_rejects_unattached_pending_transition`; fix adds a JIT GC-entry assertion that pending runtime transitions must be attached or discarded before any VM GC step can run. | Passed focused `vo-vm --features jit` regression; selected by `vm_` hardening and covered by `gc-contract` through `cargo-test-gc-vm-jit`. |
| VM-PENDING-TERMINAL-TXN-002 | Closed, 2026-06-16 | Pending JIT runtime side effects must have an explicit transaction class: already-committed compensation may commit on terminal results, while uncommitted scheduler-visible effects must discard as a unit. | `attach_pending_runtime_transitions` merged and applied pending spawns/island commands/wakes across `Panic`, `JitError`, and `Interrupted`; tests also locked in side-effect survival for terminal outcomes, while runtime-boundary docs described terminal discard for uncommitted pending effects. | Failing tests `vm_pending_terminal_txn_002_jit_error_discards_uncommitted_spawn`, `vm_pending_terminal_txn_002_jit_error_discards_uncommitted_island_command`, and source-contract test `vm_pending_terminal_txn_002_pending_effects_declare_terminal_policy`; fix adds `PendingTransitionTerminalPolicy`, keeps `go`/island effects committed for language panic, discards pure effects on JIT infra terminal results, and preserves queue/select compensation effects on terminal results. | Passed focused `vo-vm --features jit` tests and existing queue/go parity regressions; selected by `vm_` hardening and `runtime-contract`. |
| VM-CLOSURE-CALL-SIGNATURE-002 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | Interpreter and JIT closure/interface calls must validate the same runtime target, object kind, canonical reference, allocated object shape, capture-count metadata, and call-shape metadata before frame setup. | Interpreter `CallClosure`/`CallIface` validated local call-site layout but did not prove the resolved closure or itab target shape matched encoded args/returns before pushing a frame; extern replay closure validation and JIT prepared closure callbacks also trusted closure header capture count and allocation width before proving they matched the target `FunctionDef`. | Proof tests `vm_closure_call_signature_002_call_closure_*`, `vm_closure_call_signature_002_call_iface_*`, `vm_closure_call_signature_002_jit_prepare_*`, `vm_closure_call_signature_002_extern_replay_rejects_capture_count_metadata_drift_before_frame_push`, `vm_closure_call_signature_002_extern_replay_rejects_closure_allocation_drift_before_frame_push`, `vm_closure_call_signature_002_jit_prepare_closure_call_rejects_capture_count_metadata_drift_before_frame_push`, and `vm_closure_call_signature_002_jit_prepare_closure_call_rejects_closure_allocation_drift_before_frame_push`; fix routes dynamic calls and JIT prepared closure callbacks through shared shape validators and makes `validate_closure_target` prove closure allocation slots, header slots, canonical kind, function id, and runtime capture count against the `FunctionDef` capture shape before frame setup. | Compiled by `cargo test -p vo-vm --no-run` and `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit --no-run`; selected by `cargo-test-vm-hardening` / `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`, but execution is blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-DEFER-CLOSURE-KIND-002 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | Deferred closure replay must validate object kind, canonical reference, function id, layout, and runtime target arg shape before frame setup. | `DeferPush` stores a raw closure ref and caller-derived arg layout; unwind replay previously validated kind only, then trusted recorded arg slots against the target frame shape. | Proof tests `vm_defer_closure_kind_002_rejects_non_closure_gcref_before_header_read` and `vm_defer_closure_shape_002_rejects_arg_slot_shape_drift_before_frame_push`; fix shares `validate_closure_target` and `validate_closure_arg_shape`. | Compiled by `cargo test -p vo-vm --no-run` and selected by `cargo-test-vm-hardening`; execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-GOISLAND-OBJECT-KIND-002 | Closed, 2026-06-22 | `GoIsland`/`GoStart` must validate island/closure object kinds and closure target arg shape before reading headers or publishing spawned frames; interpreter/JIT paths must share helper contracts. | Interpreter and JIT object-kind validation paths diverged, JIT proof tests were not selected by the `vm_` hardening filter, and closure spawn could build a frame before checking target arg slots. Follow-up source-contract audits found the shared-validator proof used raw `.contains`, so comments or test-only text could spoof interpreter/JIT GoIsland validator coverage, then found the JIT proof used `validate_closure_go_abi` as the terminal instead of the actual publication points and could count unreachable dead-branch validator tokens. | Proof tests `vm_goisland_object_kind_002_interpreter_*`, `vm_goisland_object_kind_002_paths_share_frame_call_validators`, `vm_goisland_object_kind_002_source_contract_ignores_comment_spoofed_validators`, `vm_goisland_object_kind_002_source_contract_rejects_jit_publication_before_validators`, `vm_goisland_object_kind_002_source_contract_rejects_non_dominating_validator_tokens`, `vm_goisland_object_kind_002_source_contract_rejects_closure_body_spoofed_validators`, `vm_goisland_object_kind_002_source_contract_rejects_async_body_spoofed_validators`, `vm_goisland_object_kind_002_source_contract_rejects_nested_fn_spoofed_validators`, `vm_goisland_object_kind_002_jit_*`, and `vm_closure_spawn_shape_002_rejects_arg_slot_shape_drift_before_frame_push`; fix routes closure spawn through shared closure validators, keeps interpreter/JIT tests under blocker-prefixed `vm_` names, and makes the source proof slice the actual interpreter/JIT GoIsland paths through compact production source, excluding unreachable `if false`, closure-body, async-body, and nested-function validator blocks, before checking validators before `exec_go_island`, `commit_go_spawn`, and `commit_go_island_commands`. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-main cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_goisland_object_kind_002 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture` (16 tests); selected by VM hardening and JIT hardening gates, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-GOSTART-CLOSURE-SIGNATURE-003 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | `GoStart` closure targets must pass the shared closure target validator before any closure header read or frame setup. | Interpreter `GoStart` canonicalized and kind-checked locally, then read `closure::func_id` before proving allocation width, header slot count, and capture count matched the target `FunctionDef`. | Proof test `vm_gostart_closure_signature_003_rejects_header_slot_drift_before_raw_read`; fix routes the closure branch through `validate_closure_target` and `try_build_validated_closure_fiber_from_args_ptr`. | Compiles under `cargo test -p vo-vm --no-run`; selected by `cargo-test-vm-hardening`; execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-WAKE-REGISTRATION-002 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | Queue, select, island, host, and I/O wake commands must carry or reconstruct a validated source, wake key, registration token, queue identity, case kind, operation identity, and target-island ownership before mutating scheduler state. | Queue wake registration was fixed at zero, `apply_runtime_wake` did not validate the envelope, island wake lacked registration and accepted a waiter for the wrong target island, select waiter identity only proved nested `select_id/case_index` without top-level queue/kind consistency, select cancellation removed every waiter with the same per-fiber `select_id`, registration token wrap could produce zero, simple queue waiters could wake a fiber after it re-blocked on another queue, and legacy identity-less simple waiters could still wake identity-bearing current simple or select waits. | Proof tests `vm_wake_registration_002_queue_wake_rejects_zero_registration`, `vm_wake_registration_002_island_wake_command_requires_registration`, `vm_wake_registration_002_island_wake_rejects_wrong_target_island`, `vm_wake_registration_002_select_wake_rejects_queue_or_kind_identity_mismatch`, `vm_wake_registration_002_wait_registration_generator_skips_zero_on_wrap`, `vm_wake_registration_002_simple_queue_wake_rejects_stale_queue_identity`, `vm_wake_registration_002_simple_queue_wake_rejects_legacy_identityless_waiter`, `vm_wake_registration_002_legacy_simple_wake_rejects_select_blocked_fiber`, `vm_wake_registration_002_island_wake_rejects_legacy_simple_select_waiter`, `vm_wake_registration_002_simple_queue_registration_includes_queue_identity`, and `vm_wake_registration_002_select_cancel_keeps_other_fiber_same_select_id_waiters`; fix derives nonzero registrations from full wake identity, carries simple/select queue/kind through waiter storage, fiber wait state, scheduler validation, island encoding, and runtime-boundary registration hashing, rejects wrong-island island wakes, validates top-level/nested select waiter identity consistency, and keys select cancellation by `fiber_key + select_id`. | Compiled by `cargo test -p vo-vm --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-runtime --lib --no-run`, and selected by VM/runtime gates; execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-SELECT-WOKEN-MATERIALIZATION-003 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | Once a select wake is accepted, the selected communication result must be owned by that fiber; later queue mutations must not revoke the selected case, drain its recv payload, or leave stale waiters after a terminal selected case. | `SelectState` stored only `woken_index`; a woken send could become `SendOnClosed` if another fiber closed the queue before resume, a woken recv reread shared queue state and could lose the direct-send payload, malformed woken queue validation canceled registered waiters before validating the selected queue handle, and closed selected send paths cleared select state without canceling other registered waiters. | Proof tests `vm_select_woken_materialization_003_send_accepted_survives_later_close`, `vm_select_woken_materialization_003_recv_uses_materialized_payload_when_queue_is_empty`, `vm_select_woken_validation_003_invalid_queue_preserves_registered_waiters`, and `vm_select_woken_closed_send_003_cancels_other_registered_waiters`; fix carries `SelectWokenResult` through `WakeCommand`, materializes select recv payloads out of the direct-send buffer with slot metadata, treats accepted select sends as complete, validates woken cases before canceling other waiters, and cancels other waiters before returning `SendOnClosed` for a closed selected send. | Compiled by `cargo test -p vo-vm --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit --no-run`, and `cargo test -p vo-runtime --lib --no-run`; selected by VM hardening gates, execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-SELECT-WOKEN-PREFLIGHT-TXN-003 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | Select recv payload metadata must be proven before the queue state machine consumes the waiting receiver or moves the direct-send payload. | `queue_send_core` and the home-island endpoint send path materialized `SelectWokenResult::Recv` only after `send_or_block_resolved` popped the receiver and direct-send payload; malformed payload slot metadata could return `Malformed` after queue mutation. | Proof test `vm_select_woken_materialization_003_preflight_preserves_receiver_on_payload_metadata_error`; fix splits select recv slot-type derivation into a reusable fact source, preflights local select recv payload width before mutation, and shares the same preflight in endpoint send handling. | Compiled by `cargo test -p vo-runtime --lib --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-vm --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-web --no-run`, and `cargo check -p vo-web --target wasm32-unknown-unknown`; selected by VM and app contract gates, execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-GC-SELECT-WOKEN-PAYLOAD-ROOT-003 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | Materialized select recv payloads stored in `SelectState` must remain precise GC roots until they are copied into the fiber stack. | The new `SelectWokenResult::Recv` owned direct-send payload slots on a blocked/runnable fiber, but `collect_fiber_roots` scanned only registered select queues, not the materialized payload. | Proof test `vm_gc_select_woken_payload_root_003_collects_materialized_recv_payload`; fix records payload slot types with `SelectWokenResult::Recv` and scans them via `collect_slots_by_types`. | Compiled by `cargo test -p vo-vm --no-run` and `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit --no-run`; selected by VM/GC hardening gates, execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-GC-SELECT-WOKEN-ARRAY-ROOT-003 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | Materialized select recv payload slot metadata must be precise for inline arrays as well as structs and interfaces. | The select-woken payload fact source treated `ValueKind::Array` through the generic GC-ref fallback, so array scalar slots could be recorded as `GcRef` and later scanned as invalid roots. | Proof test `vm_gc_select_woken_payload_root_003_array_slots_are_precise`; fix derives array payload slot types recursively from `RuntimeType` metadata, including nested structs, named arrays, and tuples. | Compiled by `cargo test -p vo-vm --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-web --no-run`, and `cargo check -p vo-web --target wasm32-unknown-unknown`; selected by VM/GC and app contract gates, execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-SELECT-WOKEN-NOSTD-COMPILE-003 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | VM select wake materialization must compile on both native/std and browser/no_std targets that share the queue executor. | `select_woken_recv_payload` used the `vec!` macro while only importing `Vec` under `alloc`, so native VM gates compiled but `vo-web` and wasm/no_std builds failed. | Mechanical source-contract failure: `cargo test -p vo-web --no-run` reported `cannot find macro vec in this scope`; fix imports `alloc::vec` alongside `alloc::vec::Vec` for no_std. | Passed `cargo test -p vo-web --no-run`, `cargo check -p vo-web --target wasm32-unknown-unknown`, `cargo test -p vo-vm --no-run`, and `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit --no-run`; selected by `vm-production` and `app-contract` through web hardening and wasm gates. |
| VM-RESUME-OWNER-002 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | Resume/replay PC ownership must have one runtime-boundary owner; scheduler and helper paths must not directly patch frame PCs except through that owner. | Queue replay/block, remote recv block, select block, closed sender wake, endpoint closed-send ack, JIT bridge transition defaults, and interpreter extern replay blocks used local PC rewrites or `pc:0` sentinels instead of a single explicit policy. | Proof tests `vm_arch_boundary_fact_sources_001_resume_policy_owner_is_explicit`, `vm_resume_owner_002_preserve_policy_does_not_touch_frame_pc`, `vm_resume_owner_002_closed_sender_wake_replays_via_runtime_boundary`, and `vm_extern_replay_pc_owner_002_replay_blocks_use_runtime_transition_resume`; source scan shows no direct `pc -= 1` / `checked_sub(1)` frame rewrites and no `pc:0` resume sentinels. | Compiled by `cargo test -p vo-vm --no-run` and `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit --no-run`; execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-EXTERN-REPLAY-VALIDATION-003 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | Extern replay state and caller frame PC may be consumed, opened, or rewritten only after `CallExtern` id, resolved entry, arg/ret frame ranges, and closure replay target shape are validated. | Interpreter `CallExtern` consumed resume tokens and opened closure replay extern scope before validating the extern call shape, and `prepare_extern_closure_replay_call` rewrote the current frame PC before validating closure kind and replay arg shape. | Proof tests `vm_extern_replay_validation_003_invalid_closure_does_not_rewrite_pc` and `vm_extern_replay_validation_003_call_extern_takes_resume_tokens_after_validation`; fix validates closure replay args before PC rewrite and moves resume token `take()` plus `snapshot_for_extern` calls after `CallExtern` validation. | Compiled by `cargo test -p vo-vm --no-run`; selected by `cargo-test-vm-hardening`, execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-JIT-EXTERN-RET-ABI-002 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | The exported JIT extern-call ABI must honor distinct `args` and `ret` buffers. | Public JIT API exposes independent `args` and `ret` pointers, but the VM callback treated `args` as the return/scratch buffer while first-party lowering aliased them. | Proof test `vm_jit_extern_ret_abi_002_honors_distinct_args_and_ret_buffers`; fix gives VM callback and `vo-jit` lowering separate arg/ret buffers and copies only validated return slots to `ret`. | Compiled by `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit --no-run` and `cargo test -p vo-jit --no-run`; selected by VM/JIT contract gates, execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-GC-SELECT-BLOCK-DIRTY-002 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | Any `SelectExec` block path that registers queue roots in `fiber.select_state` must mark current-fiber roots dirty before the runtime boundary can observe GC. | Interpreter `SelectExec` registered `registered_queues` then returned `RuntimeTransition::Block(Queue)` with `GcRootEffect::None`; transition results skip the generic current-fiber dirty compensation. | Proof test `vm_gc_select_block_dirty_002_select_block_transition_dirties_registered_queue_roots`; fix changes the select block transition to `GcRootEffect::CurrentFiberDirty`. | Compiled by `cargo test -p vo-vm --no-run` and selected by `cargo-test-vm-hardening`; execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-JIT-SELECT-BLOCK-DIRTY-002 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | JIT/OSR `SelectExec` and queue wait bridges that register wait roots must dirty the current fiber before publishing a queue block boundary, and adapters must preserve the full runtime transition. | JIT `SelectExec` returned bare `JitResult::WaitQueue`; the bridge decoded `GcRootEffect::CurrentFiberDirty` but exec/OSR adapters collapsed it back to `ExecResult::Block` / `OsrResult::WaitQueue`, losing the dirty-root effect. | Proof tests `vm_jit_select_block_dirty_002_wait_queue_bridge_dirties_current_fiber_roots`, `vm_jit_select_block_dirty_002_exec_adapter_preserves_wait_queue_gc_transition`, and `vm_jit_select_block_dirty_002_osr_adapter_preserves_wait_queue_gc_transition`; fix makes the shared JIT queue wait bridge and OSR adapter preserve `RuntimeTransition`. | Compiled by `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit --no-run`; selected by `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`, execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-GC-QUEUE-WAIT-ROOT-002 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | A fiber blocked on a simple queue wait must scan any queue handle it stores for stale-wake identity, and any transition that publishes that state must dirty the current fiber. | The new `fiber.queue_wait_state` stored `queue_ref/kind` for simple waits, but `collect_fiber_roots` did not scan it; `QueueAction::ReplayThenBlock` also published that state with `GcRootEffect::None`. | Proof tests `gc_root_matrix_scans_returns_defers_panic_sentinel_endpoints_and_selects` and `vm_gc_queue_wait_state_002_replay_block_dirties_queue_wait_root`; fix makes `QueueWaitState.queue_ref` a typed `GcRef`, scans it in fiber roots, and marks queue replay-block transitions current-fiber dirty. | Compiled by `cargo test -p vo-vm --no-run` and `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit --no-run`; selected by `cargo-test-vm-hardening`, execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-GC-QUEUE-PAYLOAD-ROOT-003 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | Local queue buffered payloads and waiting-sender payloads must keep nested roots precise when the element type is an inline fixed array. | The scanner routed queue payloads through `trace_value_slots_by_meta`, but readiness lacked a root-survival proof for real queue-owned `[N]T` payloads in both buffer and waiting-sender storage. | Proof tests `vm_gc_queue_payload_root_003_buffered_array_payload_roots_survive_scan` and `vm_gc_queue_payload_root_003_waiting_sender_array_payload_roots_survive_scan`; closure adds module-backed queue payload root proofs for buffered and waiting-sender paths. | Compiles under `cargo test -p vo-runtime vm_gc_queue_payload_root_003 --no-run`; selected by runtime and VM production gates; execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-JIT-CLOSURE-CANON-002 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | JIT prepared closure calls must store the canonical closure object in callee slot0, matching interpreter and extern replay semantics. | The JIT callback canonicalized `closure_ref` for object validation but passed the raw possibly-interior reference into `closure::call_layout`, so callee slot0 could receive an interior pointer. | Proof test `vm_jit_closure_canon_002_prepare_closure_call_stores_canonical_slot0`; fix calls `closure::call_layout` with the canonical closure ref. | Compiled by `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit --no-run`; selected by `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`, execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-GOISLAND-REMOTE-SHAPE-002 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | Remote `GoIsland` must validate closure target arg shape before queue-transfer preparation, endpoint mutation, packing, or outbound island effects. | The remote-island path validated island/closure object kind but reached queue-transfer preparation and spawn packing before comparing encoded arg slots with the closure target. | Proof test `vm_goisland_remote_shape_002_rejects_arg_shape_drift_before_island_effects`; fix validates `GoIsland` arg shape before both local spawn and remote transfer paths. | Compiled by `cargo test -p vo-vm --no-run` and `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit --no-run`; selected by VM hardening gates, execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-DEFER-STATIC-SHAPE-002 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | Static defer replay must validate recorded arg layout against the target function before reserving stack or pushing a frame. | Closure defer replay had target shape validation, but static defer replay trusted `entry.arg_layout.arg_slots()` after resolving the function id. | Proof test `vm_defer_static_shape_002_rejects_arg_slot_shape_drift_before_frame_push`; fix uses shared function arg-shape validation before static defer frame setup. | Compiled by `cargo test -p vo-vm --no-run`; selected by `cargo-test-vm-hardening`, execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-JIT-PREPARED-CALL-ABI-002 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | JIT prepared-call callbacks must validate all ABI-owned pointers, including the `PreparedCall *out` buffer, before any success-path frame push or write. | Closure/interface prepared-call callbacks validated args and return buffers but could dereference a null `out` pointer after successful shape validation. | Proof tests `vm_jit_prepared_call_abi_002_prepare_closure_call_rejects_null_out_before_frame_push` and `vm_jit_prepared_call_abi_002_prepare_iface_call_rejects_null_out_before_frame_push`; fix folds `out` validation into shared prepared-call shape validation. | Compiled by `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit --no-run`; selected by `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`, execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-GC-LOCAL-RECV-WAKE-DIRTY-002 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | Local queue/select wake transitions that copy GC-bearing payloads into the current fiber stack must mark current-fiber roots dirty before publishing wake effects. | Interpreter and JIT local recv wake paths could write a payload into the current stack, publish a queue/select wake transition, and return with `GcRootEffect::None`. | Proof tests `vm_gc_local_recv_wake_dirty_002_interpreter_queue_wake_transition_dirties_current_fiber`, `vm_gc_local_recv_wake_dirty_002_jit_queue_wake_transition_dirties_current_fiber`, and `vm_gc_local_recv_wake_dirty_002_jit_select_wake_transition_dirties_current_fiber`; fix makes those wake transitions `GcRootEffect::CurrentFiberDirty`. | Compiled by `cargo test -p vo-vm --no-run` and `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit --no-run`; execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-GC-JIT-SELECT-CONTINUE-DIRTY-002 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | JIT `SelectExec` paths that complete locally and return directly to JIT after writing current-fiber stack slots must dirty current-fiber roots before GC can observe stale stack metadata. | The JIT `SelectExec` `Continue` arm can cover a woken recv that copies GC-bearing payload slots into the current fiber stack, then returns bare `JitResult::Ok` without a runtime transition or dirty-root effect. | Proof test `vm_gc_jit_select_continue_dirty_002_jit_select_continue_marks_current_fiber_dirty`; fix marks `FiberId::from_raw(fiber.id)` dirty before returning `JitResult::Ok` from the Continue arm. | Compiled by `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit --no-run`; selected by `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`, execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-GOISLAND-TRANSFER-TXN-002 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | GoIsland queue-handle transfer preparation must be all-or-nothing: metadata/layout validation must complete before queue home info, endpoint registry, peers, or island effects are mutated. | Recursive transfer walking installed local queue `HomeInfo` or staged remote endpoint-transfer effects before later fields or args could fail sendability/layout validation. | Proof test `vm_goisland_transfer_txn_002_preflight_prevents_partial_queue_publication`; fix splits queue-handle transfer into a pure validation pass followed by a commit pass. | Compiled by `cargo test -p vo-vm --no-run` and `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit --no-run`; execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-QUEUE-TRANSFER-KIND-TXN-003 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | Queue transfer validation must prove both that a GC ref is a queue object and that its runtime `QueueKind` matches the encoded `Channel`/`Port` value metadata before any commit pass runs. | Transfer preflight accepted any queue object for queue-typed metadata; commit then called `install_home_info` on a `Chan` value whose metadata claimed `Port`, panicking after earlier valid ports or remote receivers could be consumed. | Proof tests `vm_goisland_transfer_txn_003_rejects_queue_kind_drift_without_partial_publish` and `vm_queue_remote_direct_txn_003_rejects_queue_kind_drift_before_receiver_consumption`; fix validates `queue_state::kind(handle)` against `QueueKind::from_value_kind(metadata_kind)` in the shared transfer walker. | Compiled by `cargo test -p vo-vm --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-runtime --lib --no-run`, and selected by VM/runtime gates; execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-QUEUE-TRANSFER-CONTAINER-KIND-TXN-004 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | Queue transfer validation must prove container object header kind before reading `Slice`, `Array`, `Map`, or `Pointer` layout fields. | Transfer walking raw-cast slice/array/map refs before checking object kind, so metadata drift could silently decode the wrong layout or panic after earlier transfer state had been published. | Proof tests `vm_goisland_transfer_txn_004_rejects_container_kind_drift_without_partial_publish` and `vm_queue_remote_direct_txn_004_rejects_container_kind_drift_before_receiver_consumption`; fix adds a shared `validate_transfer_object_ref` helper before container and pointer layout reads. | Compiled by `cargo test -p vo-vm --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-web --no-run`, and `cargo check -p vo-web --target wasm32-unknown-unknown`; selected by VM/runtime and app contract gates, execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-GOISLAND-CAPTURE-BOX-TXN-005 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | GoIsland capture transfer and packing must validate capture-box object kind, metadata, header slot count, and allocated slot count before raw-reading boxed capture slots. | `prepare_queue_handles_for_transfer_pass` and closure packing read capture boxes directly from `GoIslandResult.capture_data`; malformed capture boxes could panic or read the wrong layout before transfer validation rejected them, and same-kind capture boxes with forged header slots could still drift from their actual GC allocation width. | Proof tests `vm_goisland_transfer_txn_005_rejects_malformed_capture_box_before_raw_read` and `vm_goisland_transfer_txn_005_rejects_capture_box_allocation_drift_before_raw_read`; fix exposes the runtime capture-box metadata fact source, validates capture boxes through the same fixed-object transfer layout helper used by container transfer preflight, and makes `pack_closure_for_island` fallible. | Compiled by `cargo test -p vo-runtime --lib --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-vm --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-jit --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-web --no-run`, and `cargo check -p vo-web --target wasm32-unknown-unknown`; selected by VM/JIT/runtime and app contract gates, execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-QUEUE-TRANSFER-INLINE-ARRAY-TXN-005 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | Queue transfer walking and GoIsland spawn packing for fixed arrays must use the same inline array runtime layout as transport packing, including named-array underlying layouts, and must recurse through every flattened element before any commit pass can publish queue handles. | `ValueKind::Array` in the transfer walker treated the value as a heap array object stored in slot 0, while transport packing treats fixed arrays as flattened inline slots when runtime type metadata proves the layout; nested queue handles after slot 0, and named fixed-array payloads that require `NamedTypeMeta`, could be skipped during preflight or diverge between preflight and pack/unpack. | Proof tests `vm_queue_transfer_inline_array_txn_005_walks_all_flattened_elements` and `vm_queue_transfer_inline_array_txn_005_resolves_named_array_layout`; fix derives inline array length, element metadata, and element slot count from `StructMeta + NamedTypeMeta + RuntimeType`, recurses over each flattened element, passes named type metadata through queue, endpoint, interpreter GoIsland, JIT GoIsland, and island spawn encode/decode paths, and keeps the heap-array fallback only when fixed-array layout cannot be proven from the available transfer metadata. | Compiled by `cargo test -p vo-runtime --lib --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-vm --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-jit --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-web --no-run`, and `cargo check -p vo-web --target wasm32-unknown-unknown`; selected by VM hardening and app contract gates; execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-QUEUE-TRANSFER-INLINE-ARRAY-TXN-006 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | Fixed-array transfer walking and runtime pack/unpack must require the exact physical slot width derived from `RuntimeType`; no trailing slots may be ignored by prefix-only scans or serialization. | The inline fixed-array transfer walker and `pack_array_value_inline` / `unpack_array_value_inline` accepted buffers with at least the expected slot count, then scanned or serialized only the prefix; a queue-bearing trailing slot could bypass transfer preflight or disappear across island transport. | Proof tests `vm_queue_transfer_inline_array_txn_006_rejects_physical_slot_width_drift` and `pack_inline_array_value_rejects_physical_slot_width_drift`; fix makes the transfer walker and runtime pack/unpack require `slots.len() == expected_slots`, keeping fixed-array layout ownership in `RuntimeType` instead of local prefix assumptions. | Compiled by `cargo test -p vo-runtime --lib --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-vm --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-jit --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-web --no-run`, and `cargo check -p vo-web --target wasm32-unknown-unknown`; selected by VM hardening, runtime, JIT, web, and app contract gates; execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-QUEUE-TRANSFER-INLINE-ARRAY-TXN-007 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | Sequence elements whose metadata is a fixed array must derive byte width and slot width from `RuntimeType`, not from encoded `elem_bytes`. | Direct inline arrays required exact physical width, but slices/heap arrays with element metadata `[N]T` still let `elem_bytes` define how many slots to scan, so `[1]Port` with `elem_bytes = 1` could bypass canonical queue-handle scanning. | Proof test `vm_queue_transfer_inline_array_txn_007_rejects_sequence_elem_byte_width_drift`; fix makes VM transfer sequence walking compute array element slots/bytes from the same `RuntimeType` layout used for direct inline arrays. | Compiled by `cargo test -p vo-vm --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-runtime --lib --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-jit --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-web --no-run`, and `cargo check -p vo-web --target wasm32-unknown-unknown`; selected by VM/runtime/app contract gates; execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-QUEUE-TRANSFER-POINTER-LAYOUT-008 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | Pointer transfer must distinguish the pointer slot type from the heap object it points at: the slot is `Pointer`, while the pointee object is validated as the target `Struct` layout. | The queue-transfer walker validated a non-null pointer's heap object with expected kind `Pointer`, rejecting legitimate struct-backed pointees and keeping a semantic split from `pack_pointer`, which encodes pointees as `Struct`. | Proof test `vm_queue_transfer_pointer_layout_008_accepts_struct_backed_pointer_transfer`; fix validates pointee refs with `ValueKind::Struct` and the target `StructMeta` slot width before recursively walking fields. | Compiles under `cargo test -p vo-vm --no-run`; selected by VM hardening filters; execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-TRANSFER-TYPE-LAYOUT-009 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | Function `TransferType` entries must derive `ValueMeta` and slot width from the encoded `ValueRttid`; kind tags alone are not a valid transfer contract. | The module verifier only compared `meta_raw` and `rttid_raw` kind tags, allowing same-kind metadata/rttid drift or slot-count drift to reach GoStart/GoIsland/spawn transfer paths as split facts. | Proof tests `vm_transfer_type_layout_009_module_verifier_rejects_meta_rttid_drift` and `vm_transfer_type_layout_009_module_verifier_rejects_slot_drift`; fix moves rttid slot-width derivation onto `Module`, then validates transfer meta and slots against `Module::canonical_value_meta_for_value_rttid` and `Module::slot_count_for_value_rttid`. | Compiles under `cargo test -p vo-common-core vm_transfer_type_layout_009 --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-vm --no-run`, and `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit --no-run`; selected by VM hardening filters; execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-TRANSFER-TYPE-LAYOUT-010 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | Function parameter `TransferType` entries must derive the complete slot layout from `ValueRttid` and match the callee frame parameter slot layout, not just total slot width. | The verifier accepted same-width drift such as `param_types[0]=Int64` for a GC-scanned frame parameter slot, which could materialize scalar transport data into root-scanned slots or skip queue-handle preparation for root-bearing values. | Proof test `vm_transfer_type_layout_010_rejects_param_slot_layout_drift`; fix adds `Module::slot_layout_for_value_rttid` and validates the concatenated `param_types` layout against the function frame parameter layout. | Compiles under `cargo test -p vo-common-core vm_transfer_type_layout_010 --no-run`; selected by common-core and VM hardening filters; execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
