| VM-TRANSFER-TYPE-LAYOUT-011 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | Pointer transfer metadata must resolve to a real pointee `StructMeta` layout or reject; unresolved and non-struct pointees must never alias `Pointer(meta 0)`. | `Module::canonical_pointer_meta_id`, runtime pack metadata, and VM transfer metadata all had `unwrap_or(0)` fallbacks, so `*non-struct` could become `Pointer(meta 0)` and walk the wrong struct layout. | Proof test `vm_transfer_type_layout_011_rejects_pointer_to_non_struct_rttid`; fix removes the pointer meta-zero fallback from module metadata derivation, runtime pack metadata, and VM queue-transfer metadata. | Compiles under `cargo test -p vo-common-core vm_transfer_type_layout_011 --no-run` and downstream focused VM/JIT no-run checks; selected by common-core and VM hardening filters; execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-QUEUE-NEW-TYPE-LAYOUT-009 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | Queue creation must store element metadata that is canonical for the element `ValueRttid`, and encoded element slot width must match the module type table for interpreter and JIT paths. | `QueueNew` verifier checked opcode/JIT metadata width, but runtime creation still trusted the stack `elem_type` word; the JIT helper also accepted a naked `gc` pointer and therefore could not validate against the module type table. | Proof tests `vm_queue_new_type_layout_009_rejects_meta_rttid_drift_before_create`, `vm_queue_new_type_layout_009_rejects_elem_slot_drift_before_create`, `vm_jit_queue_new_type_layout_009_helper_uses_module_backed_validation`, and `vm_jit_queue_new_type_layout_009_lowering_passes_context_to_checked_helper`; fix routes interpreter and JIT QueueNew through module-backed queue element layout validation and changes JIT lowering to pass `JitContext`. | Compiles under `cargo test -p vo-runtime vm_queue_new_type_layout_009 --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-runtime vm_jit_queue_new_type_layout_009 --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-jit vm_jit_queue_new_type_layout_009 --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-vm --no-run`, and `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit --no-run`; selected by VM/runtime/JIT hardening filters; execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-JIT-QUEUE-NEW-CHECKED-ABI-010 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | The JIT `vo_queue_new_checked` helper must validate raw ABI arguments before constructing internal enums or narrowing widths, and return a status code instead of panicking or truncating. | The helper passed `kind as u16` into panicking `QueueKind::from_raw` and narrowed `elem_slots as u16` before module-backed validation, so malformed JIT ABI calls could panic or allocate a queue after truncating an over-wide slot count. | Proof test `vm_jit_queue_new_checked_abi_010_rejects_kind_and_slot_narrowing_before_constructor`; fix matches raw queue kind explicitly and uses `u16::try_from(elem_slots)` before calling `create_checked_with_module`. | Compiles under `cargo test -p vo-runtime vm_jit_queue_new_checked_abi_010 --no-run`; selected by runtime and VM production gates; execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-ISLAND-SPAWN-CAPTURE-ROOT-005 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | Island spawn capture boxes must preserve precise GC root scanning for flattened value-slot payloads, including fixed arrays; GoIsland source capture normalization and destination spawn unpack must share the same flattened value-slot contract. | Spawn unpack allocated array capture boxes as `Struct(0)` and stored `transfer_type.slots` raw flattened slots, so GC scanned with `struct_metas[0]` and could miss later roots. Source GoIsland capture prepare/pack also assumed capture refs were raw boxes and could read actual heap `Array` headers instead of flattened elements. | Proof tests `vm_island_spawn_capture_root_005_array_value_slot_box_scans_all_slots`, `vm_island_spawn_capture_root_005_unpack_allocates_value_slot_capture_box`, and `vm_island_spawn_capture_root_005_heap_array_capture_prepare_scans_all_slots`; fix adds GC value-slot objects scanned by `ValueMeta`, uses them for spawn-unpacked captures, normalizes GoIsland capture values before packing, and removes the stale heap-box spawn encoder bypass. | Compiles under `cargo test -p vo-runtime vm_island_spawn_capture_root_005 --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-vm vm_island_spawn_capture_root_005 --no-run`, and `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_island_spawn_capture_root_005 --no-run`; selected by VM/runtime hardening filters through `vm_` proof names and runtime tests; execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-VALUE-SLOT-CLONE-LIFECYCLE-006 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | GC clone, object sizing, and range-index removal must preserve the value-slot-object marker and use allocation data size instead of interpreting zero-slot fixed-array boxes as heap arrays. | Value-slot objects reuse `ValueKind::Array` metadata for flattened capture payloads; `ptr_clone` and object sizing could treat `slots == 0` as an `ArrayHeader` object and clones could lose the value-slot scan marker. | Proof tests `vm_value_slot_clone_lifecycle_006_ptr_clone_preserves_value_slot_scan_layout` and `vm_value_slot_clone_lifecycle_006_zero_slot_array_box_size_uses_header_slots`; fix preserves the marker during `ptr_clone` and records allocation data size as the source of truth for sweep/range-index bookkeeping. | Compiles under `cargo test -p vo-runtime vm_value_slot_clone_lifecycle_006 --no-run`; selected by runtime and VM production gates; execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-TRANSFER-RTTID-KIND-DRIFT-010 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | `ValueRttid` kind tags must match the runtime type table before transfer metadata or slot layout is derived. | `Module::canonical_value_meta_for_value_rttid` and `slot_layout_for_value_rttid` could derive layout from the runtime type row while trusting a forged `ValueRttid` kind tag, reopening kind drift below the verifier's explicit reference checks. | Proof tests `vm_transfer_rttid_kind_drift_010_rejects_basic_kind_tag_drift` and `vm_transfer_rttid_kind_drift_010_rejects_array_tag_to_non_array_runtime_type`; fix makes the shared `Module` transfer metadata/layout helpers reject runtime-type kind drift before returning canonical facts. | Compiles under `cargo test -p vo-common-core vm_transfer_rttid_kind_drift_010 --no-run`; selected by common-core and VM hardening filters; execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-BOXED-ARRAY-CAPTURE-TRANSFER-006 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | GoIsland capture normalization must accept codegen's synthetic struct boxes for fixed-array captures only when their `StructMeta.slot_types` exactly match the array's flattened `RuntimeType` layout. | Fixed-array method/closure captures can be boxed as synthetic structs, while the source capture walker only accepted value-slot boxes, actual heap arrays, or legacy capture boxes, leaving a shape split between codegen and island transfer. | Proof test `vm_boxed_array_capture_transfer_006_accepts_synthetic_struct_array_capture_box`; fix derives full slot layout from `ValueRttid` and validates synthetic struct boxes before reading slots or packing. | Compiles under `cargo test -p vo-vm vm_boxed_array_capture_transfer_006 --no-run`; selected by VM hardening filters; execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-NESTED-POINTER-FIELD-TRANSFER-012 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | Struct field transfer and pack recursion must derive each field's canonical `ValueMeta` from `ValueRttid`, including pointer pointee metadata. | Struct field recursion used local kind branches and fell back to `ValueMeta::new(0, field_kind)` for non-struct/non-array fields, so `*Inner` inside a transferred struct could validate or pack against `Struct(0)` instead of the canonical pointee `StructMeta`. | Proof tests `vm_nested_pointer_field_transfer_012_uses_canonical_pointee_meta` and `vm_transfer_nested_pointer_meta_012_pack_struct_field_uses_canonical_pointer_meta`; fix routes VM queue-transfer field recursion and runtime struct packing through the shared RTTID-to-metadata resolver. | Compiles under `cargo test -p vo-vm vm_nested_pointer_field_transfer_012 --no-run` and `cargo test -p vo-runtime vm_transfer_nested_pointer_meta_012 --no-run`; selected by VM/runtime hardening filters; execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-GOSTART-CLOSURE-SHAPE-006 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | `GoStart` closure spawn must validate target argument slot shape through the same helper used by other closure-spawn paths before frame creation. | The closure branch validated the object target, then called the inner `try_build_validated_closure_fiber_from_args_ptr` helper without running `validate_closure_arg_shape`, allowing encoded arg-slot drift to create a malformed child frame. | Proof test `vm_gostart_closure_shape_006_rejects_arg_slot_drift_before_spawn`; fix routes interpreter `GoStart` through `try_build_closure_fiber_from_args_ptr`. | Compiles under `cargo test -p vo-vm vm_gostart_closure_shape_006 --no-run`; selected by VM hardening filters; execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-GOSTART-STATIC-SHAPE-006 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | `GoStart` static-function spawn must validate encoded argument slot shape through the shared frame-call helper before creating the child fiber. | The static branch checked only `arg_start + arg_count <= slots.len()` before `Fiber::new`, `try_push_frame`, and `copy_within`, so bytecode with too few or too many argument slots could create a malformed spawned frame. | Proof test `vm_gostart_static_shape_006_rejects_arg_slot_drift_before_spawn`; fix routes the static branch through `validate_function_arg_shape` before frame allocation or stack copy. | Compiles under `cargo test -p vo-vm vm_gostart_static_shape_006 --no-run`; selected by VM hardening filters; execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-JIT-SELECT-CALLBACK-ABI-006 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | JIT select callbacks must validate register and element-slot widths before recording select cases. | `jit_select_send` and `jit_select_recv` bounded element slots but narrowed queue/value/destination registers with `as u16`, so over-wide registers could alias lower slots and be recorded as valid cases. | Proof tests `vm_jit_select_callback_abi_006_rejects_send_register_width_drift` and `vm_jit_select_callback_abi_006_rejects_recv_register_width_drift`; fix uses explicit `try_from` validation for registers and element slots before mutating `SelectState`. | Compiles under `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_jit_select_callback_abi_006 --no-run`; selected by JIT hardening filters; execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-JIT-CALL-REQUEST-ABI-006 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | `vo_set_call_request` must validate every narrowed ABI field before publishing call-request state in `JitContext`. | The helper wrote `arg_start`, `ret_slots`, `ret_reg`, and `call_kind` with truncating casts; malformed JIT output could publish a partially valid request that VM materialization would later trust. | Mechanical source-contract test `vm_jit_call_request_abi_006_validates_narrow_fields_before_publishing`; fix validates all narrow fields with `try_from` before writing any request field, and records JIT infra error on failure. | Compiles under `cargo test -p vo-runtime vm_jit_call_request_abi_006 --no-run`; selected by runtime/JIT production gates; execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-JIT-RESUME-POINT-ABI-006 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | `jit_push_resume_point` must validate return register/slot widths before appending resume-stack state. | The callback narrowed `ret_reg` and `ret_slots` with `as u16` while pushing a resume point, so an invalid ABI call could persist a truncated return destination. | Proof test `vm_jit_resume_point_abi_006_rejects_ret_register_width_drift_before_push`; fix validates both fields before borrowing the fiber or appending to `resume_stack`. | Compiles under `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_jit_resume_point_abi_006 --no-run`; selected by JIT hardening filters; execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-EXTERN-PROVIDER-PANIC-BOUNDARY-006 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | Internal Rust extern providers must not unwind across the runtime/JIT extern boundary; provider panics become `ExternContractError`. | `ExternRegistry::call_with_resolved_effects` invoked `RegisteredFn::Internal` directly, so provider or context-helper panics could escape through VM/JIT extern-call paths instead of being reported as contract errors. | Proof test `vm_extern_provider_panic_boundary_006_returns_contract_error`; fix wraps internal provider calls in `catch_unwind` under `std` and leaves C extension ABI requirements explicit. | Compiles under `cargo test -p vo-runtime vm_extern_provider_panic_boundary_006 --no-run`; selected by runtime/extern contract gates; execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-QUEUE-TRANSFER-SAME-KIND-LAYOUT-TXN-006 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | Queue transfer validation must prove same-kind container object metadata, allocation slot count, slice element-boundary alignment, and sequence element width before reading `Pointer`, `Slice`, heap `Array`, or `Map` layouts or iterating map entries. | The previous object-kind check rejected wrong-kind containers but still accepted right-kind malformed objects; a `Pointer`, `Slice`, `Array`, or `Map` with the expected `ValueKind` but wrong slots, wrong `ValueMeta`, missing map backing storage, invalid slice backing array, misaligned slice data pointer, or impossible queue-bearing element width could reach raw slot/header/data reads after earlier transfer fields had been validated. | Proof tests `vm_goisland_transfer_txn_006_rejects_same_kind_container_layout_drift_before_raw_read`, `vm_goisland_transfer_txn_006_rejects_misaligned_slice_data_pointer_before_raw_read`, `vm_queue_remote_direct_txn_005_rejects_same_kind_container_layout_drift_before_receiver_consumption`, and `vm_queue_remote_direct_txn_005_rejects_misaligned_slice_data_pointer_before_receiver_consumption`; fix records GC allocation data size as a runtime fact source, adds shared transfer layout validators for fixed objects and heap arrays, routes Pointer/Slice/Array/Map transfer walking through those validators, and rejects misaligned, zero-width, or width-drift sequence elements that may contain queue handles. | Compiled by `cargo test -p vo-runtime --lib --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-vm --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-jit --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-web --no-run`, and `cargo check -p vo-web --target wasm32-unknown-unknown`; `node scripts/ci/docs_lint.mjs`, `cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- lint tasks --strict`, `task plan vm-production`, and `task plan app-contract` pass/confirm selection; execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-ISLAND-SPAWN-UNPACK-TXN-003 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | Island spawn payload unpack must publish endpoint registry roots transactionally: failed queue-handle unpack must restore registry state, while successful unpack must dirty roots before GC can observe new endpoint handles. | `handle_spawn_fiber` unpacked captures/args through a raw queue-handle resolver; a valid earlier handle could register a remote proxy, then a later invalid handle returned early without registry rollback or all-roots dirtying. | Proof test `vm_island_spawn_unpack_txn_003_handle_error_restores_endpoint_registry`; fix snapshots `EndpointRegistry` before spawn unpack and restores it on handle validation failure, while preserving the success-path `mark_gc_all_roots_dirty`. | Compiled by `cargo test -p vo-vm --no-run`; selected by `cargo-test-vm-hardening`, execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-ISLAND-SPAWN-UNPACK-TXN-004 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | Island spawn payload unpack must validate header counts, chunk bounds, receiver `ValueMeta`, receiver `ValueRttid`, and non-zero-slot chunk presence before allocating closure captures, resolving queue handles, or spawning a fiber. | `handle_spawn_fiber` trusted spawn header counts and `unpack_spawn_payload` defaulted missing transfer metadata/chunks, sliced chunks by unchecked lengths, and unpacked against only `ValueMeta`, allowing count drift, panics, zero-value materialization, or wire shape drift before closure creation. | Proof tests `vm_island_spawn_unpack_txn_004_rejects_capture_count_drift_before_closure_create`, `vm_island_spawn_unpack_txn_004_rejects_arg_rttid_drift_before_closure_create`, and `vm_island_spawn_unpack_txn_004_rejects_zero_length_arg_chunk_before_closure_create`; fix makes spawn header decode and payload unpack checked/Result-based, rejects count/bounds/layout/chunk-presence drift, and runs the no_std expected-layout validator with expected `ValueRttid` before unpack. | Compiles under `cargo test -p vo-vm --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-runtime --lib --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-web --no-run`, and `cargo check -p vo-web --target wasm32-unknown-unknown`; selected by VM/runtime/app gates; execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-TRANSPORT-UNPACK-TXN-002 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | Transport unpack must publish endpoint-registry handles all-or-nothing: if any nested handle is rejected, no earlier handle from the same message may remain registered, and per-message cache reuse must not bypass full handle metadata validation. | Recursive unpack registered a valid first endpoint before a later nested queue handle could fail validation, leaving a half-published remote proxy in `EndpointRegistry` after the message was rejected; same-message queue-handle cache reuse was keyed only by endpoint id and could hide later metadata drift for that endpoint. | Proof tests `vm_transport_handle_validation_002_unpack_error_rolls_back_new_endpoint_handles` and `vm_transport_handle_validation_002_unpack_cache_revalidates_same_endpoint_metadata`; fix snapshots `EndpointRegistry` before unpack, restores it on any queue-handle validation error, and only reuses cached unpacked handles when the complete `QueueHandleInfo` matches. | Compiled by `cargo test -p vo-vm --no-run`; selected by `cargo-test-vm-hardening`, execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-TRANSPORT-UNPACK-LAYOUT-006 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | Cross-island transport unpack must decode against the receiver's static `ValueMeta`/`ValueRttid`/`RuntimeType` contract before writing GC-visible slots or allocating scanned heap sequences; wire tags, nested rttids, raw-byte sequence markers, and `elem_bytes` cannot redefine root layout. | Transport unpack previously trusted the encoded value kind and sequence `elem_bytes`; later audit found nested Slice/Array/Pointer/Map expected rttids were not enforced, zero-slot payloads bypassed envelope validation, root-bearing sequence elements could request raw-byte encoding, and forged queue-handle element metadata could be registered. | Proof tests `vm_remote_recv_unpack_layout_006_rejects_wire_kind_drift_before_recv_slots`, `vm_remote_recv_unpack_layout_007_rejects_slice_elem_rttid_drift_before_recv_slots`, `vm_transport_unpack_layout_006_rejects_sequence_fixed_array_byte_width_drift`, `vm_transport_zero_slot_001_rejects_wire_kind_drift`, `vm_transport_queue_handle_layout_006_rejects_forged_elem_metadata_before_register`, `unpack_expected_value_rejects_wire_kind_drift_for_gc_root_slot`, `unpack_slice_rejects_root_elem_byte_width_drift`, `unpack_slice_rejects_fixed_array_elem_byte_width_drift`, `validate_packed_slice_rejects_expected_elem_rttid_drift`, and `validate_packed_slice_rejects_raw_bytes_for_root_bearing_elements`; fix adds a no_std-safe expected-layout validator to runtime pack/unpack, routes VM transport through receiver `ValueMeta` plus `ValueRttid`, validates zero-slot envelopes, struct fields/inline arrays/sequences and queue handles recursively, rejects raw bytes for root-bearing elements, and maps malformed payloads to transaction-clean `MalformedPayload` errors. | Compiled by `cargo test -p vo-runtime --lib --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-vm --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-jit --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-web --no-run`, and `cargo check -p vo-web --target wasm32-unknown-unknown`; selected by VM/runtime/app contract gates; execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-QUEUE-HANDLE-VALIDATION-002 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | VM queue operations and transfer walkers must validate non-null queue handles as canonical base refs whose object header kind is `Channel` or `Port` before any `QueueData` decode. | Queue send/recv/close/len/cap/select/transfer paths accepted any `GcRef`-typed slot and could read `QueueData` from a string, closure, interior pointer, or other non-queue object; the select woken resume path and JIT QueueLen/QueueCap lowering still had raw helper/header-read bypasses after the first fix. | Proof tests `vm_queue_handle_validation_002_queue_ops_reject_non_queue_gcref`, `vm_queue_handle_validation_002_select_rejects_non_queue_gcref`, `vm_queue_handle_validation_002_select_woken_rejects_non_queue_gcref`, `vm_queue_handle_validation_002_transfer_rejects_non_queue_gcref_without_partial_publish`, `vm_queue_handle_validation_002_jit_queue_get_rejects_non_queue_gcref`, and `vm_queue_handle_validation_002_jit_queue_len_cap_use_checked_callbacks`; fix adds shared `validate_queue_handle` and routes queue ops, queue len/cap, select ready/registration/woken completion, transport live-endpoint checks, queue-transfer recursion, and JIT QueueLen/QueueCap callback wrappers through it. | Compiled by `cargo test -p vo-vm --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit --no-run`, and `cargo test -p vo-jit --no-run`; selected by `cargo-test-vm-hardening`, `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`, and `cargo-test-jit-hardening`; execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-JIT-QUEUE-RECV-REMOTE-REPLAY-003 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | Interpreter/JIT queue replay helpers must validate callback buffers, queue handles, and queue element slot width before consuming pending remote recv/send replay state. | The JIT remote-replay branch called `take_remote_recv_response()` before validating `chan` as a queue handle, the interpreter remote recv replay consumed the response before queue-handle validation, and JIT QueueSend/QueueRecv did not compare callback slot widths with queue metadata before replay/enqueue ownership changes. | Proof tests `vm_jit_queue_recv_remote_replay_003_rejects_invalid_handle_before_remote_replay_consumption`, `vm_jit_queue_recv_remote_replay_003_rejects_elem_slot_drift_before_replay_consumption`, `vm_jit_queue_send_remote_replay_003_rejects_invalid_callback_before_remote_send_closed_consumption`, `vm_jit_queue_send_callback_layout_003_rejects_elem_slot_drift_before_enqueue`, and `vm_queue_recv_remote_replay_003_source_validates_handle_before_response_take`; fix validates callback buffers, queue handles, and queue element slots before replay ownership transfer, remote-send closed consumption, or enqueue. | Compiles under `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit --no-run` and `cargo test -p vo-vm --no-run`; selected by VM/JIT hardening gates; execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-JIT-QUEUE-RECV-NIL-001 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | JIT queue recv on a nil channel with no pending remote replay must block like the interpreter path; nil recv is not an infrastructure error. | `jit_queue_recv` validated `chan` before calling `queue_recv_core`, so nil recv returned `JitError` while interpreter `queue_recv_core` returns `WouldBlock { waiter: None }`. | Proof test `vm_jit_queue_recv_nil_001_blocks_like_interpreter`; fix lets nil/no-replay recv reach `queue_recv_core`, while remote replay still requires a validated queue handle before consuming replay data. | Compiles under `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_jit_queue_recv_nil_001 --no-run`; selected by VM JIT hardening filters; execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-CONTRACT-HARDENING-GATE-001 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | The composed `contract` gate must select blocker-named VM, JIT, and common-core hardening tasks, not only the broader runtime/JIT crate tests. | `vm-production` selected `vm-hardening`, but `contract` did not, so final contract runs could omit `cargo-test-common-core-hardening`, `cargo-test-jit-hardening`, `cargo-test-vm-hardening`, and `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`. | Proof artifact `cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- task plan contract --format json` must include the VM hardening tasks; fix adds `vm-hardening` to the `contract` group and group metadata in `eng/tasks.toml`. | Validated by `cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- task plan contract --format json` and `cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- lint tasks --strict`; execution of selected Rust tests remains blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-RUNTIME-LANGUAGE-CONTRACT-GATE-006 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | The composed runtime contract gate must select VM/JIT/OSR language cases tagged as runtime contracts, including panic-surface cases that prove dynamic runtime behavior. | `runtime-contract` covered Rust runtime checks but did not select manifest language cases owned by `runtime` and tagged for contract coverage, so final contract planning could miss VM/JIT/OSR regressions such as dynamic unhashable panic behavior. | Proof artifacts `cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- task plan contract --format json` includes `vo-test-runtime-language-contract`, and `cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- test plan --suite lang --owner runtime --tags contract --targets vm,jit,osr --release --format json` selects the tagged runtime panic cases over VM/JIT/OSR. | Validated by `cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- lint tasks --strict`, `cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- task plan contract --format json`, and the focused `test plan` command; execution remains blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-QUEUE-REMOTE-DIRECT-TXN-002 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | Local-home sends that will wake a remote endpoint receiver must validate cross-island payload transfer and endpoint home metadata before consuming the waiting receiver or moving queue state. | `queue_send_core` called `send_or_block_resolved` first; if the result was `RemoteDirect`, payload transfer validation or missing `HomeInfo` detection could fail after the receiver had been popped and the payload moved. | Proof tests `vm_queue_remote_direct_txn_002_preflight_preserves_waiting_receiver_on_transfer_error`, `vm_queue_remote_direct_txn_002_select_preflight_preserves_waiting_receiver_on_transfer_error`, `vm_queue_remote_direct_txn_002_missing_home_info_preserves_waiting_receiver_without_panic`, and `vm_queue_remote_direct_txn_002_select_missing_home_info_preserves_waiting_receiver`; fix exposes the next remote-direct receiver from the runtime queue state and runs HomeInfo plus validation-only transfer preflight before mutating queue state. | Compiled by `cargo test -p vo-vm --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit --no-run`, and `cargo test -p vo-runtime --lib --no-run`; selected by `cargo-test-vm-hardening`, execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-ENDPOINT-SEND-REMOTE-DIRECT-TXN-004 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | Home-island endpoint sends that will direct-deliver to a remote endpoint receiver must run the same validation-only transfer preflight before consuming the waiting receiver or moving the payload. | `handle_endpoint_request_inner` only preflighted local select recv materialization; the remote-direct endpoint-send branch packed transfer payloads after `send_or_block_resolved` had popped the remote receiver, and metadata failures panicked or aborted after queue mutation. | Proof test `vm_endpoint_send_remote_direct_txn_004_preflight_preserves_receiver_on_transfer_error`; fix peeks the front remote receiver and validates cross-island transfer before entering the queue state machine. | Compiled by `cargo test -p vo-vm --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-web --no-run`, and `cargo check -p vo-web --target wasm32-unknown-unknown`; selected by VM/runtime and app contract gates, execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-ENDPOINT-SEND-REMOTE-DIRECT-TXN-005 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | Endpoint `Send` must treat nested transport-unpack handle registration and remote-direct transfer validation as one transaction. | Endpoint `Send` unpacked payloads and could register nested remote queue handles before later validation rejected the send, leaving endpoint-registry state published even though the payload was not accepted. | Proof test `vm_endpoint_send_remote_direct_txn_005_source_rolls_back_endpoint_registry_on_send_reject`; fix snapshots the endpoint registry before endpoint-send unpack and restores it on unpack, typed-barrier, remote-direct transfer, or select-payload validation rejection. | Compiled by `cargo test -p vo-vm --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-web --no-run`, and `cargo check -p vo-web --target wasm32-unknown-unknown`; selected by VM/app contract gates; execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-ENDPOINT-RECV-REMOTE-DIRECT-TXN-004 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | Home-island endpoint receives for remote waiters must validate cross-island payload materialization before consuming buffered values, popping waiting senders, or sending sender acknowledgements; validation failure must not be encoded as a real channel close. | The endpoint recv branch called `recv_or_block` first, then packed the payload through fallible queue-transfer preparation; malformed or non-sendable payload metadata could panic after the buffered value was consumed and sender wake state had changed. The first preflight fix responded with closed `RecvData`, which preserved home queue state but incorrectly poisoned the remote proxy as closed. | Proof tests `vm_endpoint_recv_remote_direct_txn_004_preflight_preserves_buffer_on_transfer_error`, `vm_endpoint_recv_remote_direct_txn_004_preflight_preserves_waiting_sender_on_transfer_error`, and `vm_endpoint_recv_remote_direct_txn_004_rejected_response_does_not_write_recv_slots`; fix peeks the payload that `recv_or_block` would consume, runs validation-only transfer preflight before entering the queue state machine, and replies with non-terminal `RecvError` so the remote wait is released without closing the proxy or materializing a fake closed recv result. | Compiled by `cargo test -p vo-runtime --lib --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-vm --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-jit --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-web --no-run`, and `cargo check -p vo-web --target wasm32-unknown-unknown`; selected by VM/runtime and app contract gates; execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-WAKE-REMOTE-ENDPOINT-002 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | Local receive, select receive, and close paths that must answer remote endpoint waiters must prove endpoint `HomeInfo` is present before consuming waiters, payloads, or closed-response obligations. | `QueueRecv`/`SelectRecv` could pop a remote endpoint sender and only then fail while building the send ack for missing `HomeInfo`; `QueueClose` could mark the queue closed and drain endpoint waiters before discovering it could not send closed responses. | Proof tests `vm_wake_remote_endpoint_002_queue_recv_missing_home_info_preserves_waiting_sender`, `vm_wake_remote_endpoint_002_select_recv_missing_home_info_preserves_waiting_sender`, and `vm_queue_close_endpoint_waiter_missing_home_info_preserves_waiters`; fix peeks endpoint senders before recv, checks endpoint waiters before close, and rejects missing `HomeInfo` before queue state is mutated. | Compiled by `cargo test -p vo-vm --no-run` and `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit --no-run`; selected by `cargo-test-vm-hardening`, execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-JIT-CLOSURE-IC-VALIDATION-002 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | JIT `CallClosure` must not read closure object headers or take an IC hit before VM-owned object-kind, canonicalization, and call-shape validation. | The closure IC hit path loaded `func_id` directly from `closure_ref` and could branch to the direct JIT call before the prepare callback validated the callee object. | Proof test `vm_jit_closure_ic_validation_002_closure_lowering_has_no_header_read_fast_path`; fix removes the closure header-derived IC hit path and routes closure calls through the prepared-call callback fact source. | Compiled by `cargo test -p vo-jit --no-run` and `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit --no-run`; execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-IO-WAKE-KEY-002 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | I/O ready commands must cross the runtime boundary with a complete wait key containing token, fiber wake key, and wait registration, not a raw token alone. | `RuntimeCommand::io_ready(token)` carried no `wake_key` or registration and relied on scheduler-side `io_waiters` rejection as a second line of defense. | Proof test `vm_io_wake_key_002_runtime_command_rejects_mutated_wake_key_or_registration`; fix introduces `IoWaitKey`, has polling resolve the full key before building `RuntimeCommand::io_ready`, and validates source, wake key, registration, and token before calling `scheduler.wake_io`. | Compiled by `cargo test -p vo-vm --no-run`; selected by `cargo-test-vm-hardening`, execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-WASM-FETCH-WAKE-KEY-002 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | WASM host replay wakes must use the complete `HostWaitKey`, not a legacy token lookup, so source, generation, and registration mismatches are rejected by the scheduler. | Browser fetch Promise completion woke replay waiters through a token-keyed compatibility adapter after the Promise resolved; the first closure proof lived only behind `cargo check` and was not selected by a Rust unit-test gate. | Proof test `vm_wasm_fetch_wake_key_002_async_fetch_wakes_with_host_wait_key`; fix resolves the full replay `HostWaitKey` before awaiting the Promise, calls `vm.wake_host_event(key)`, adds `cargo-test-web-hardening`, and routes it through `app-contract` and `vm-production`. | Compiles under `cargo test -p vo-web --no-run` and `cargo check -p vo-web --target wasm32-unknown-unknown`; `cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- task plan app-contract --format json` and `task plan vm-production` select `cargo-test-web-hardening`; actual `cargo test -p vo-web vm_wasm_fetch_wake_key_002_async_fetch_wakes_with_host_wait_key -- --exact --nocapture` hangs after `Running unittests`, so execution remains blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-DIRECT-METHOD-CAPTURE-PROTOCOL-006 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | Direct method-value closures spawned across islands must preserve receiver captures as raw receiver slots while still packing/unpacking them with precise transfer metadata and queue-handle validation. | Runtime dynamic method values can create closures over the original method function with `recv_slots > 0`; those captures are raw receiver slots consumed by `closure::call_layout`, but the island spawn payload protocol treated captures as typed boxed values that unpack into capture boxes. Multi-slot receivers also created a count mismatch between typed transfer values and closure capture slots, and the follow-up audit found a suffix-based `$iface` heuristic that could accidentally box a raw one-slot receiver. | Proof tests `vm_direct_method_capture_protocol_006_unpack_preserves_raw_receiver_slots`, `vm_direct_method_capture_protocol_006_transfers_raw_receiver_slots`, and `vm_iface_wrapper_suffix_authority_006_keeps_suffixed_one_slot_struct_receiver_raw`; fix extends the spawn payload header with raw receiver-capture slot count, derives direct-method receiver `TransferType` from `NamedTypeMeta.methods` and module RTTID/layout helpers, packs the receiver as one typed value, unpacks it back into raw closure capture slots, and uses explicit method metadata rather than function-name suffixes for boxed interface wrappers. | Compiles under `cargo test -p vo-runtime vm_direct_method_capture_protocol_006 --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-vm vm_direct_method_capture_protocol_006 --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_direct_method_capture_protocol_006 --no-run`, and focused VM/common-core `--no-run` proof builds; selected by runtime/VM production gates; execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-IFACE-WRAPPER-SUFFIX-AUTHORITY-006 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | Interface wrapper receiver boxing must derive from serialized, verified method ABI metadata, not a function-name suffix. | `direct_method_receiver_transfer_plan` treated any method target whose function name ended in `$iface` as a boxed one-slot wrapper; a legitimate raw receiver target with that suffix could be transferred as a boxed `GcRef`, while bytecode had no verifier-owned fact proving the target frame actually used the boxed ABI. | Proof tests `vm_iface_wrapper_suffix_authority_006_rejects_boxed_receiver_without_gcref_abi` and `vm_iface_wrapper_suffix_authority_006_keeps_suffixed_one_slot_struct_receiver_raw`; fix adds `MethodInfo.receiver_is_iface_boxed`, serializes it, has codegen set it for real boxed value-receiver interface wrappers, and has the verifier reject metadata whose target function does not have a one-slot `GcRef` receiver ABI. | Compiles under `cargo test -p vo-common-core vm_iface_wrapper_suffix_authority_006 --no-run` and VM hardening builds; selected by common-core and VM hardening filters; execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-GOISLAND-TRANSFER-PACKABILITY-TXN-006 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | GoIsland and remote queue send transfer preflight must prove the entire payload is packable before publishing endpoint/home state, not only queue-handle leaves. | The queue-transfer walkers preflighted queue handles and some container layouts, but non-queue root-bearing values such as malformed later strings could be validated only when the payload was packed after local endpoint state was already installed. | Proof test `vm_goisland_transfer_txn_006_rejects_malformed_later_string_before_endpoint_publication`; fix routes direct receiver captures, captures, args, and remote-send elements through a full value-layout preflight that validates strings, slices, arrays, maps, structs, and scalar slot widths before the commit pass can publish endpoint state. | Compiles under `cargo test -p vo-vm vm_goisland_transfer_txn_006_rejects_malformed_later_string_before_endpoint_publication --no-run`; selected by VM hardening filters; execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-JIT-WAITQUEUE-MATERIALIZE-006 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | JIT `WaitQueue` materialization failure must roll back every registered simple, select, and remote wait it opened before returning an infrastructure error. | `handle_wait_queue_transition` could fail while materializing a queue wait frame after registering simple or select waiters, leaving stale queue wait state and remote endpoint wait state attached to the fiber. | Proof tests `vm_jit_waitqueue_materialize_006_cleans_simple_and_remote_wait_state_on_failure` and `vm_jit_waitqueue_materialize_006_cleans_select_waiters_on_failure`; fix centralizes failed-materialization cleanup in `cleanup_unmaterialized_wait_queue_state`, canceling simple waiters, select waiters, select state, and remote endpoint wait state before returning the materialization error. | Compiles under `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_jit_waitqueue_materialize_006 --no-run`; selected by `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`; execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-JIT-WAITQUEUE-PENDING-ENDPOINT-CLEANUP-007 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | A pending endpoint request with `pending_response=true` must not be terminal-committed after failed JIT wait materialization clears the fiber-side remote endpoint wait state. | JIT remote queue send can build a `CommitOnAnyTerminal` pending transition when payload transfer has already published local endpoint state. If `WaitQueue` materialization then fails, cleanup clears `fiber.remote_endpoint_wait` but the terminal attach path could still publish the endpoint send request, creating a response obligation no fiber can accept. | Proof test `vm_jit_waitqueue_pending_endpoint_cleanup_007_drops_unpaired_response_obligation_on_failure`; fix filters pending-response island commands out of pending runtime transitions during unmaterialized wait cleanup while retaining non-response endpoint transfer effects. | Compiles under `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_jit_waitqueue_pending_endpoint_cleanup_007 --no-run`; selected by `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`; execution blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002. |
| VM-CONTRACT-TASK-TIMEOUT-006 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | `contract` and `vm-production` must reject selected gate tasks that can hang without a bounded timeout. | Timeouts were present on many concrete tasks, but no lint contract prevented a future selected task from entering either gate without `timeout_sec`. | Source-contract `lint_selected_gate_tasks_have_timeouts` walks concrete tasks selected by `contract` and `vm-production` and rejects any task without `timeout_sec`; proof commands are `cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- lint tasks --strict` and `cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- task plan vm-production --format json`. | Lint and plan commands pass; proof-test execution remains blocked by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002, but hangs are now bounded at the task-graph layer. |
| VM-ENDPOINT-DIRECT-PREFLIGHT-012 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | Local port sends that complete an endpoint waiter directly must validate `HomeInfo` and transfer packability before consuming the waiting receiver, regardless of whether the endpoint waiter lives on the same island. | `next_remote_direct_receiver` filtered same-island waiters out of preflight while `send_or_block_resolved` classified `endpoint_wait_id != 0` receivers as `RemoteDirect`; malformed same-island endpoint sends could pop the receiver before missing-`HomeInfo` or packability validation failed, including through the JIT callback path. | Failing-first proofs `vm_endpoint_direct_preflight_012_same_island_receiver_missing_home_info_preserves_waiter`, `vm_endpoint_direct_preflight_012_same_island_receiver_transfer_error_preserves_waiter`, `vm_endpoint_direct_preflight_012_same_island_select_missing_home_info_preserves_waiter`, `vm_endpoint_direct_preflight_012_same_island_select_transfer_error_preserves_waiter`, and `vm_endpoint_direct_preflight_012_jit_same_island_missing_home_info_preserves_waiter`; fix makes the preflight helper treat `endpoint_wait_id != 0` as endpoint-direct ownership. | Passed focused `vo-vm` and `vo-vm --features jit` proof filters; `vm_`-prefixed proofs are selected by VM hardening gates in `vm-production`. |
| VM-STDLIB-CONTRACT-CI-012 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | The task and CI graph must treat `lang/stdlib/**` as first-class stdlib source, matching `vo-stdlib` embed freshness and routing stdlib changes through stdlib and WASM contract gates. | `vo-dev` runner freshness and Cargo rebuilds knew `lang/stdlib` was source input, but `eng/tasks.toml` omitted it from `cargo-test-stdlib` and `cargo-test-web-runtime-wasm`, and `eng/ci.toml` lacked a `lang/stdlib/**` changed-prefix route. | Source-contract tests `lint_stdlib_embedded_source_inputs_requires_source_tree`, `lint_stdlib_embedded_source_inputs_accepts_embedded_source_tree`, `lint_vm_readiness_changed_prefixes_requires_stdlib_source_prefix`, and `lint_vm_readiness_changed_prefixes_accepts_stdlib_contract_route`; failing-first strict lint reported `task cargo-test-stdlib must include input lang/stdlib/**`. | Passed `vo-dev` lint proof filters and `cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- lint tasks --strict`; `lang/stdlib/**` now selects `cargo-test-stdlib`, `cargo-test-web-runtime-wasm`, `vo-test-runtime-contract`, and `vo-test-wasm`. |
| VM-ENDPOINT-SENDER-PREFLIGHT-012 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | Local recv/select-recv/JIT recv paths that must acknowledge an endpoint sender must validate endpoint response metadata before consuming the sender payload or writing destination slots, regardless of island locality. | `next_recv_remote_endpoint_sender` filtered endpoint senders by `sender.island_id != local_island`, while `queue_sender_ack_or_wake` routes all `endpoint_wait_id != 0` senders through endpoint responses. Same-island endpoint sender recv could write the received value and pop the sender before missing `HomeInfo` was reported. | Failing-first proofs `vm_endpoint_sender_preflight_012_same_island_queue_recv_missing_home_info_preserves_sender`, `vm_endpoint_sender_preflight_012_same_island_select_recv_missing_home_info_preserves_sender`, and `vm_endpoint_sender_preflight_012_jit_same_island_recv_missing_home_info_preserves_sender`; fix renames the fact source to `next_recv_endpoint_sender` and keys it only on `endpoint_wait_id != 0`. | Passed focused `vo-vm` and `vo-vm --features jit` proof filters; `vm_`-prefixed proofs are selected by VM hardening gates. |
| VM-JIT-BRIDGE-SUSPEND-DIRTY-012 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | Any JIT bridge side exit, yield, I/O wait, host-event wait, or replay block may follow compiled root mutation and must dirty the current fiber before the runtime boundary can observe GC state. | `bridge_runtime_transition` used `GcRootEffect::None` for `TimesliceExpired`, `WaitIo`, `HostEvent`, and `HostEventReplay`; `ExecResult::Transition` intentionally does not add the generic current-fiber dirty compensation. | Failing-first proof `vm_jit_bridge_suspend_dirty_012_side_exit_transitions_dirty_current_fiber_roots`; fix makes bridge-created runtime transitions default to `GcRootEffect::CurrentFiberDirty`, matching the already-explicit queue wait bridge. | Passed focused `vo-vm --features jit` proof filter; selected by `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`. |
| VM-JIT-EXTERN-HARDENING-GATE-012 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | JIT extern ABI, suspend, terminal mapping, and provider-drift proof tests must be selected by the VM JIT hardening task that gates `vm-production`. | Several JIT extern tests were named `jit_call_extern_*` or `jit_callback_abi_call_extern_*`, so `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit` (`cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_`) skipped them; plain `cargo-test-vm` cannot cover `#[cfg(feature = "jit")]` internals. | Source-contract lint `lint_vm_jit_hardening_test_prefixes_*` rejects unprefixed JIT extern hardening tests; failing-first strict lint reported `JIT extern hardening test jit_call_extern_maps_terminal_and_scheduler_results must start with vm_`; tests were renamed to `vm_jit_extern_*`. | Passed `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_jit_extern -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` (12/12) and `cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- lint tasks --strict`. |
| VM-GATE-TASK-TIMEOUT-012 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | Every concrete task selected by `contract` and `vm-production` must have a task-level timeout, not only tasks recognized as Rust or language tests. | `task_runner` waits indefinitely when `timeout_sec` is absent; strict lint only enforced timeouts for `cargo test` and language test-run tasks, leaving selected tasks such as `test-data-lint`, `docs-lint`, `eng-lint-release`, `eng-lint-benchmarks`, and `wasm-check` unbounded. | Source-contract tests `lint_selected_gate_tasks_have_timeouts_rejects_non_test_task_without_timeout` and `lint_selected_gate_tasks_have_timeouts_accepts_bounded_gate_tasks`; failing-first strict lint reported `contract selected task test-data-lint has no timeout_sec`. | Passed `cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- lint tasks --strict`; `vm-production` and `contract` task plans still select the expected proof gates and now only select bounded concrete tasks. |
| VM-EXTERN-REPLAY-DIRTY-012 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | Interpreter extern replay waits that may follow extern-mutated return slots must dirty current-fiber roots before blocking at the runtime boundary. | `CallExtern` mapped `HostEventWaitAndReplay` and `WaitIo` to `RuntimeTransition` with `GcRootEffect::None`; extern providers can write root-bearing return slots before those transitions, and `ExecResult::Transition` does not apply the generic dirty-current-fiber compensation. | Source-contract test `vm_extern_replay_dirty_012_replay_wait_transitions_dirty_current_fiber`; failing-first focused run reported the replay wait branch could preserve extern-mutated roots without dirtying them. | Passed focused `vo-vm` and `vo-vm --features jit` proof filters; selected by `cargo-test-vm-hardening` and `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit` through `vm_`. |
| VM-ENDPOINT-RECV-PACK-PREFLIGHT-012 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | Endpoint recv responses must prepare or reject queue-transfer payload serialization before consuming buffered values or waiting senders, even when the endpoint waiter is on the home island. | `preflight_endpoint_recv_value_for_waiter` returned early for `target.island_id == home_island`, but same-island endpoint waiters still dispatch through serialized `EndpointResponseKind::RecvData`; local port payloads without `HomeInfo` or invalid root-bearing payload metadata could panic after `recv_or_block` removed queue state. | Failing-first tests `vm_endpoint_recv_pack_preflight_012_same_island_buffered_port_prepares_before_pack` and `vm_endpoint_recv_pack_preflight_012_same_island_waiting_sender_error_preserves_sender`; fix derives one serialization fact source from `endpoint_wait_id != 0 || target.island_id != home_island` and prepares payloads before queue mutation. | Passed focused proof filter and both non-JIT/JIT `vm_` hardening selectors; selected by VM hardening gates. |
| VM-JIT-CALLBACK-ABI-HARDENING-GATE-012 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | JIT queue/defer callback ABI guard proofs must be selected by `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`, not left behind unprefixed test names. | Queue and defer callback ABI tests were named `jit_callback_abi_*`, so the JIT hardening selector `vm_` skipped null-buffer and width-overflow guard proofs even though those callbacks cross raw ABI pointers. | Source-contract lint now scans `vo-vm/src/vm/jit/extern_call.rs` plus `vo-vm/src/vm/jit/callbacks/*.rs` and rejects unprefixed JIT hardening tests; failing-first strict lint reported the unselected defer callback ABI test. | Passed `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_jit_callback_abi -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` (6/6), full JIT `vm_` hardening, `cargo test -p vo-dev -- --test-threads=1`, and strict task lint. |
| VM-VO-DEV-CONTRACT-GATE-012 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | The gate-policy proofs that certify `contract` and `vm-production` must themselves be selected by those composed gates and by changed-file routing for `cmd/vo-dev/**`. | Closure evidence relied on manually running `cargo test -p vo-dev` and `vo-dev lint tasks --strict`, but `contract` and `vm-production` did not include a vo-dev contract group, and `cmd/vo-dev/**` lacked an explicit readiness changed-prefix route. | Source-contract tests `lint_vo_dev_contract_requires_group_in_contract`, `lint_vo_dev_contract_requires_group_in_vm_production`, `lint_vo_dev_contract_requires_policy_tests`, `lint_vo_dev_contract_accepts_group_ownership`, and `lint_vm_readiness_changed_prefixes_requires_vo_dev_contract_route`; failing-first strict lint reported `contract must include vo-dev-contract`. | Passed full `cargo test -p vo-dev -- --test-threads=1`, strict task lint, and `task plan contract` / `task plan vm-production` showing `eng-lint-tasks` and `cargo-test-vo-dev` selected. |
| VM-JIT-RAW-ABI-PREFLIGHT-012 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | JIT prepared-call, go, and go-island callbacks must validate raw ABI buffers before publishing traps, prepared-call sentinels, frames, spawns, pending transitions, or island effects. | Nil closure/interface/island early-trap branches ran before shared ABI shape validators; malformed raw ABI inputs such as null `out` or non-empty null `args_ptr` could mutate trap state before returning an infrastructure error. | Failing-first tests `vm_jit_go_callback_abi_012_nil_closure_rejects_null_non_empty_args_before_trap` and `vm_jit_goisland_callback_abi_012_nil_closure_rejects_null_non_empty_args_before_trap`; prepared-call tests `vm_jit_prepared_call_abi_012_nil_closure_rejects_null_out_before_trap` and `vm_jit_prepared_call_abi_012_nil_iface_rejects_null_out_before_trap`; fix moves raw ABI preflight ahead of nil trap branches. | Passed focused `callback_abi_012` and `vm_jit_prepared_call_abi_012` filters, plus current full `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_ -- --test-threads=1` (303/303). |
| VM-JIT-HARDENING-TREE-SELECTION-012 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | All VM-owned JIT readiness tests behind `--features jit` must be selected by `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`. | Bridge/OSR/transition and callback tests named `jit_*`, `osr_*`, or callback-local names were skipped by the `vm_` selector even though they certify JIT bridge transport, OSR trap handling, transition error reporting, callback frame-elision, queue panic avoidance, and select width-drift contracts. | Source-contract lint now recursively scans `vo-vm/src/vm/jit/**/*.rs`, rejects `#[test] fn jit_*`/`osr_*` names, and requires every `jit/callbacks` test to start with `vm_`; failing-first strict lint rejected `jit_host_replay_transport_decodes_to_runtime_transition`. | Passed `cargo test -p vo-dev lint_vm_jit_hardening_test_prefixes -- --nocapture --test-threads=1`, strict task lint, and full JIT `vm_` hardening. |
| VM-RUNTIME-CHANGED-MODE-HARDENING-012 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | Runtime queue/pack/GC source changes that can affect VM boundary contracts must route through VM hardening proofs in changed-mode CI. | `lang/crates/vo-runtime/**` selected runtime and GC tests but not `cargo-test-vm-hardening` / `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`, while endpoint and packability fixes can live in runtime fact sources and be proved by VM hardening tests. | Source-contract test `lint_vm_readiness_changed_prefixes_requires_runtime_vm_hardening_route`; fix adds both VM hardening tasks to the runtime changed-prefix route. | Passed `cargo test -p vo-dev lint_vm_readiness_changed_prefixes -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` and strict task lint. |
| VM-ENG-CONTRACT-CHANGED-MODE-012 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | Engineering task/CI manifest changes must run the vo-dev contract proofs that certify gate composition, timeouts, and changed-prefix routing. | `eng/tasks.toml` and `eng/ci.toml` could fall back to generic changed-mode tasks without `cargo-test-vo-dev`, so task-graph or route regressions could bypass the source-contract tests that guard `contract` and `vm-production`. | Source-contract tests `lint_vm_readiness_changed_prefixes_requires_eng_task_manifest_route` and `lint_vm_readiness_changed_prefixes_requires_eng_ci_manifest_route`; fix adds explicit known prefixes for both manifests selecting `eng-lint-tasks` and `cargo-test-vo-dev`. | Passed `cargo test -p vo-dev lint_vm_readiness_changed_prefixes -- --nocapture --test-threads=1`, strict task lint, and `task plan vm-production --format json`. |
| VM-JIT-TREE-TEST-SELECTION-013 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | Every VM-owned `#[test]` under `vo-vm/src/vm/jit/**/*.rs` must be selected by the JIT hardening gate. | The 012 lint still allowed ordinary non-`vm_` JIT-tree test names such as context, invoke, materialization, frame, side-exit, and GC materialization proofs, so `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit` could skip them even though it is the production JIT selector. | Failing-first lint proof `lint_vm_jit_hardening_test_prefixes_rejects_unselected_plain_jit_tree_tests`; fix makes the recursive lint require every JIT-tree test to start with `vm_` and renames the remaining plain/`gc_` tests to `vm_*` / `vm_gc_*`. | Passed `cargo test -p vo-dev lint_vm_jit_hardening_test_prefixes -- --nocapture --test-threads=1`, full `cargo test -p vo-dev -- --test-threads=1` (56/56), and full JIT `vm_` hardening (303/303). |
| VM-JIT-SELECT-CALLBACK-ABI-013 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | JIT select execution must validate the raw `result_reg` width before mutating select state or writing the result slot. | `jit_select_exec` accepted a `u32` result register and cast it to `u16` when calling the interpreter-owned select executor; oversized registers could truncate, write the wrong slot, clear select state, or panic before returning a contract error. | Failing-first proof `vm_jit_select_callback_abi_013_rejects_exec_result_register_width_drift_before_select_mutation`; fix routes `result_reg` through `validate_select_reg` before extracting VM/fiber state. | Passed focused `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_jit_select_callback_abi_013 -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` and full JIT `vm_` hardening. |
| VM-JIT-PREPARED-RESUME-PC-013 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | JIT prepared closure/interface callbacks must validate `caller_resume_pc` before nil trap publication. | Nil closure and nil interface early-trap branches used `caller_resume_pc.saturating_sub(1)`, so `caller_resume_pc == 0` became a user/runtime trap at pc 0 instead of a strict callback contract error, bypassing the checked callsite validation used by non-nil paths. | Failing-first proofs `vm_jit_prepared_call_abi_013_nil_closure_rejects_zero_resume_pc_before_trap` and `vm_jit_prepared_call_abi_013_nil_iface_rejects_zero_resume_pc_before_trap`; fix shares a checked resume-PC helper before nil trap branches. | Passed focused `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_jit_prepared_call_abi_013 -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` and full JIT `vm_` hardening. |
| VM-JIT-DEFER-CALLBACK-ABI-013 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | JIT defer callbacks must validate raw `arg_start` width before deriving defer arg layout or pushing defer entries. | `jit_defer_push` accepted `arg_start: u32` and cast it to `u16` for `DeferArgLayout`; an oversized value could truncate to a valid caller slot, allocate args, and mutate `fiber.defer_stack` instead of returning a callback ABI error. | Failing-first proof `vm_jit_callback_abi_defer_push_rejects_arg_start_width_drift_before_defer_entry`; fix validates `arg_start` through `validate_callback_slot_count` before layout derivation. | Passed focused defer ABI proofs (3/3) and full JIT `vm_` hardening (303/303). |
| VM-ENDPOINT-REQUEST-OWNERSHIP-013 | Closed, 2026-06-17 | Endpoint request publication and pending-response accounting must be owned by `RuntimeTransition` / `IslandCommandEffect`, not exposed as direct `VmState` request helpers or arbitrary public island-command publication. | Public `VmState::send_endpoint_*_request` helpers could increment `pending_island_responses` and publish endpoint requests outside the runtime-boundary commit/discard protocol; after deleting those helpers, public raw `send_to_island` / `try_send_to_island` / `outbound_commands` could still publish `IslandCommand::EndpointRequest` without incrementing pending response obligations, and `vo-web` still read the raw queue directly. | Failing-first source-contract proof `vm_endpoint_request_publication_013_request_publishers_are_boundary_owned`; fix deletes the unused endpoint request helpers, makes raw island-command routing/output queue crate-owned, and routes public callers through `Vm::take_outbound_commands` / `Vm::has_outbound_commands`. | Passed focused `cargo test -p vo-vm vm_endpoint_request_publication_013 -- --nocapture --test-threads=1`, non-JIT `vm_` hardening (179/179), JIT `vm_` hardening (304/304), and `cargo check -p vo-web --target wasm32-unknown-unknown`. |
| VM-RUNTIME-PUBLIC-STATE-OWNERSHIP-013 | Closed, 2026-06-18 | External crates must use semantic `Vm` APIs for host-event polling, output sink setup, extern registry access, and loaded-module inspection instead of reaching into `Scheduler`, `VmState`, or mutable module storage; raw scheduler wake appliers remain VM boundary-owned. | Red-team source-contract rerun found `Vm.state` still exposed `pending_island_responses` and raw island publication helpers, `Vm.scheduler` still exposed raw host-event, I/O, and queue wake appliers, and external crates still accessed `vm.scheduler` / `vm.state` directly; the proof also guards `Vm.module` against public mutable storage around `load()` / `finish_load()` invariants. | Failing-first source-contract proof `vm_endpoint_request_publication_013_request_publishers_are_boundary_owned`, plus scheduler ownership proof `vm_scheduler_wake_boundary_013_raw_wake_appliers_are_vm_boundary_owned`; fix makes `Vm.state`, `Vm.scheduler`, and `Vm.module` crate-owned, routes external callers through `Vm::take_pending_host_events`, `Vm::host_event_key`, `Vm::set_output_sink`, and `Vm::extern_registry_mut`, and leaves raw scheduler wake appliers crate-owned. | Passed focused ownership proof, non-JIT `vm_` hardening (179/179), JIT `vm_` hardening (304/304), `cargo check -p vo-vm --features jit`, `cargo check -p vo-app-runtime`, `cargo check -p vo-web --target wasm32-unknown-unknown`, and `cargo test -p vo-engine strict_jit_extern_not_registered_fails_fast --features jit -- --nocapture --test-threads=1`. |
| VM-ISLAND-IDENTITY-SURFACE-015 | Closed, 2026-06-18 | Targeted island transport frames must adopt or reject the VM island identity through one VM-owned semantic API, not through external duplicated `current_island_id == 0` checks or a public raw setter. | The final `contract` rerun exposed that `vo-app-runtime` and `vo-web` still owned the adopt/reject/push sequence and called a public raw island-id setter before pushing a decoded frame. | Source-contract proof `vm_endpoint_request_publication_013_request_publishers_are_boundary_owned` requires `Vm::push_targeted_island_command`; fix routes both embedders through that API, makes `adopt_island_id` private, and leaves mismatch reporting structured at the app/web boundary. | Passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-fix-targeted-island cargo test -p vo-vm vm_endpoint_request_publication_013 -- --nocapture --test-threads=1`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-fix-targeted-app cargo test -p vo-app-runtime push_targeted_inbound_island_frame -- --nocapture --test-threads=1`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-fix-targeted-web cargo check -p vo-web --target wasm32-unknown-unknown`, and source grep confirming no production `set_island_id` surface remains. |
| VM-JIT-MANAGER-SURFACE-014 | Closed, 2026-06-18 | Strict JIT manager state and resolved extern provider authority must be owned by VM load/runtime contracts, not replaceable through public VM fields, public manager modules, or debug hooks after load. | `vo_vm::vm` still re-exported `JitManager`, `VmJitState` was public, `Vm.jit` was public, and a public debug registry replacement hook could swap the loaded registry; a follow-up audit also found `pub mod jit_mgr` still exposed `vo_vm::vm::jit_mgr::JitManager` after the field was hidden. Separately, provider identities were allocated from a per-registry counter, so a replacement registry could collide with resolved extern authority and run the wrong provider. | Failing-first proofs `resolved_call_rejects_provider_identity_drift_after_registry_replacement`, `lint_vm_jit_manager_surface_rejects_public_jit_state`, `cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- lint all` rejecting `pub mod jit_mgr`, and `strict_jit_extern_not_registered_fails_fast`; fix moves provider identity allocation to one global authority, hides `JitManager`/`VmJitState`/`Vm.jit` behind VM-owned constructors and init methods, makes `jit_mgr` private while re-exporting only public JIT config/observation types, removes the registry replacement hook, and makes the engine proof use load-time unregistered extern resolution. | Passed the focused runtime proof, `cargo test -p vo-dev lint_vm_jit_manager_surface`, `cargo test -p vo-engine strict_jit_extern_not_registered_fails_fast --features jit`, `cargo check -p vo-vm --features jit`, public-surface grep, and full `cargo test -p vo-dev -- --test-threads=1`. |
| VM-RUNTIME-SURFACE-GATE-014 | Closed, 2026-06-18 | `contract`, `vm-production`, and changed-mode CI must select external embedder checks for VM public surface changes that touch app runtime or native engine/JIT execution. | The public VM surface fix touched `vo-app-runtime` and `vo-engine`, but the composed gates did not own dedicated app-runtime/engine checks and changed-prefix routing had no `lang/crates/vo-app-runtime/**` or `lang/crates/vo-engine/**` VM readiness route. Follow-up 043 found app-runtime behavior proofs also needed first-class gate ownership, not only compile checks. | Source-contract tests `lint_runtime_surface_contract_*`, `lint_vm_readiness_changed_prefixes_requires_app_runtime_surface_route`, and `lint_vm_readiness_changed_prefixes_requires_engine_surface_route`; fix adds `runtime-surface-contract`, tasks `cargo-check-vo-app-runtime`, `cargo-test-vo-app-runtime`, and `cargo-test-vo-engine`, selects the group from `contract` and `vm-production`, and routes both prefixes through `eng/ci.toml`. | Passed the focused vo-dev policy tests, strict task lint, `task plan contract --format json`, `task plan vm-production --format json`, focused/full `vo-app-runtime` tests, `cargo check -p vo-app-runtime`, and the focused `vo-engine` JIT proof. |
| VM-JIT-MANAGER-MODULE-015 | Closed, 2026-06-18 | The JIT manager module itself must remain VM-owned; public APIs may expose only semantic JIT configuration and statistics types. | After `Vm.jit` and `VmJitState` became private, `pub mod jit_mgr` still let external crates name `vo_vm::vm::jit_mgr::JitManager`; `vo-engine` used the same public module path for `JitSideExitReason`, proving the module had escaped. | Failing-first `vo-dev lint tasks --strict` rejected `pub mod jit_mgr`; fix makes `jit_mgr` private, re-exports only `JitConfig`, `JitExecutionStats`, `JitSideExitReason`, and `JitSideExitReasonStats`, and updates `vo-engine` to use `vo_vm::JitSideExitReason`. | Passed `cargo test -p vo-dev lint_vm_jit_manager_surface`, `cargo check -p vo-vm --features jit`, and `cargo test -p vo-engine strict_jit_extern_not_registered_fails_fast --features jit`; final signoff remains blocked until `contract` and `vm-production` pass. |
| VM-JIT-VERIFIER-ORDER-015 | Closed, 2026-06-18 | Per-function strict JIT verification must preserve shared structural error ownership while keeping JIT metadata-domain diagnostics precise. | The final `contract` gate exposed that `verify_jit_metadata` could report strict JIT metadata errors before shared missing-function facts for static `GoStart` / `DeferPush`; a naive common-first order then let common metadata invariants mask strict `ElemLayout` diagnostics. | Existing proof `rejects_static_go_and_defer_missing_function` failed in `cargo-test-jit`; fix makes `verify_jit_metadata` arbitrate common structural errors ahead of strict metadata errors while preserving strict metadata ownership for layout-kind and ElemLayout drift. The collection proof fixture was narrowed so it tests only ElemLayout inconsistency instead of mixing in an unrelated slice-slot shape error. | Passed focused `cargo test -p vo-jit rejects_static_go_and_defer_missing_function -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` and full `cargo test -p vo-jit` (213/213). |
| VM-READINESS-LEDGER-STARTUP-HANG-014 | Closed, 2026-06-18 | Readiness docs must keep exactly one current status row for an open blocker and must not preserve stale active-blocker language after later proof narrows the blocker. | `VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002` had two current rows before `### Superseded`, then red-team found stale current-state hang prose in the 2026-06-17 follow-up checklist after the active row had narrowed the blocker to local default-target artifacts plus unexecuted final gates. | Failing-first docs lint rules rejected stale active focused-hang language, duplicate current rows, and stale execution-hang prose in the follow-up active checklist; fix narrows the active row, removes the duplicate current row, aligns follow-up evidence to the narrowed state, and keeps signoff withdrawn. | Passed `node scripts/ci/docs_lint.mjs`; final signoff remains blocked until `contract` and `vm-production` pass. |
| VM-QUEUE-INTERIOR-POINTER-TRANSFER-016 | Closed, 2026-06-18 | Queue-transfer pointer payload validation must accept aligned interior pointers whose pointee range is inside the allocated object and reject only malformed pointee ranges. | The transfer preflight reused object-base validation for pointer values, so valid `*T` values pointing at struct fields were rejected before island queue transfer, breaking OSR parity for pointer-bearing payloads. | Failing-first proof `vm_queue_transfer_pointer_layout_008_accepts_interior_struct_pointee_transfer`; fix teaches `Gc` to expose allocation data ranges and makes pointer transfer validate the pointee range/slot alignment instead of requiring the pointer value itself to be an object base. | Passed focused `cargo test -p vo-vm vm_queue_transfer_pointer_layout_008 -- --test-threads=1` and OSR language case `2026_01_28_1406_island_pointer_transfer.vo`. |
| VM-EMPTY-STRUCT-SEQUENCE-LAYOUT-016 | Closed, 2026-06-18 | Runtime sequence packing and VM transfer preflight must share one logical-slot/physical-byte layout fact source, including zero-byte empty structs with one logical value slot. | `pack_slice` and queue-transfer sequence validation derived element width separately; empty `struct{}` slices carried one logical `Value` slot but zero physical bytes, causing pack validation to panic with `expected 8, got 0`. | Failing-first runtime proof `empty_struct_slice_round_trips_zero_byte_sequence_with_exact_metadata`; fix adds shared `sequence_element_layout` metadata and routes runtime pack/unpack plus VM transfer preflight through it. | Passed focused `cargo test -p vo-runtime empty_struct_slice_round_trips_zero_byte_sequence_with_exact_metadata -- --test-threads=1`, `cargo test -p vo-runtime pack::tests -- --test-threads=1`, and the empty-struct slice language case on `vm`, `jit`, and `osr`. |
| VM-SPAWN-PAYLOAD-DIAGNOSTIC-016 | Closed, 2026-06-18 | Island spawn payload decode/unpack failures must propagate as structured VM errors and restore transactional endpoint state instead of being silently swallowed into scheduler timeouts. | `handle_spawn_fiber` returned `()` and discarded unpack/layout errors after partially preparing endpoint handles, so malformed spawn payloads could surface only as later timeouts. | Proofs `vm_island_spawn_unpack_txn_003_handle_error_restores_endpoint_registry` and malformed payload assertions now require `SpawnFiberError`; fix returns `Result`, restores endpoint registry snapshots on failures, and propagates the error through `process_island_commands`, runtime-boundary command effects, and island thread diagnostics. | Passed focused `cargo test -p vo-vm vm_island_spawn_unpack_txn_ -- --test-threads=1`; the empty-struct slice regression first produced the structured unpack error before the metadata identity fix and then passed. |
| VM-ANON-STRUCT-META-IDENTITY-016 | Closed, 2026-06-18 | Struct metadata identity for structurally identical anonymous structs must be canonical enough that pack/unpack RTTID metadata agrees across producer and consumer functions. | Struct meta registration keyed only by `TypeKey`; structurally identical anonymous `struct{}` types could receive distinct `StructMeta` ids, so a slice created in one function packed with one RTTID and a spawn receiver expecting another failed with `LayoutMismatch`. | Failing-first codegen proof `anonymous_empty_struct_slice_reuses_single_struct_meta_identity`; fix reuses equivalent anonymous `StructMeta` records by field layout/name/type/tag/slot metadata before allocating a new meta id. | Passed focused `cargo test -p vo-codegen anonymous_empty_struct_slice_reuses_single_struct_meta_identity -- --test-threads=1` and the empty-struct slice language case on `vm`, `jit`, and `osr`. |
| VM-JIT-GOSTART-YIELD-017 | Closed, 2026-06-18 | JIT `GoStart`/`GoIsland` callbacks must publish spawn/island side effects and then suspend at an observable yield boundary before generated code continues into later scheduler-visible bytecode. | `GoStart` committed the spawn as a pending transition but returned `JitResult::Ok`, so JIT code could continue into `SelectExec`; a later block boundary merged away the intended yield ordering and the trampoline select regression deadlocked. | Failing-first engine proof `vm_jit_trampoline_select_017_compile_path_runs_pending_spawn_select_wake` and VM callback proofs `vm_jit_gostart_pending_001_*`; fix records `JitExternSuspend::Yield { resume_pc }` and returns `JitResult::ExternSuspend` after committed go transitions. | Passed focused `cargo test -p vo-engine vm_jit_trampoline_select_017_compile_path_runs_pending_spawn_select_wake -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_jit_gostart_pending_001 -- --test-threads=1`, and `./d.py test jit tests/lang/cases/jit/2026_01_29_jit_trampoline_select.vo`; selected by `cargo-test-vo-engine`, `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`, and `vo-test-jit-contract` in `contract` / `vm-production`. |
| VM-SELECT-CASE-COUNT-017 | Closed, 2026-06-18 | `SelectBegin.a` must declare the number of communication cases, default must be represented only by the default flag, and `SelectExec` must return the original source case index for dispatch. | `SelectBegin` accepted extra `SelectSend`/`SelectRecv` entries, waiter registration truncated internal indexes through `idx as u16`, and codegen counted default clauses even though default emits no waiter; additionally the VM/JIT callback path ignored the encoded source case index and exposed internal communication-case indexes. | Failing-first proofs `vm_select_case_contract_017_rejects_extra_case_beyond_select_begin_count`, `vm_select_case_contract_017_rejects_case_beyond_declared_select_begin_count`, and `vm_select_case_contract_017_returns_source_case_index_with_default_middle`; fix makes `SelectState.expected_cases` a `u16`, verifier/VM reject over-declared cases, codegen emits communication-case counts, and `SelectCase.result_index` owns bytecode-visible result dispatch. | Passed focused `cargo test -p vo-common-core vm_select_case_contract_017 -- --test-threads=1`, `cargo test -p vo-vm vm_select_case_contract_017 -- --test-threads=1`, `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_select_case_contract_017 -- --test-threads=1`, and the JIT trampoline language regression; selected by `common-core-contract`, `runtime-contract`, `vm-hardening`, and `jit-contract` in `contract` / `vm-production`. |
| VM-SELECT-SOURCE-INDEX-017 | Closed, 2026-06-18 | `SelectSend.c` / `SelectRecv.c` are source case indexes, not communication-case ordinals; verifier, VM, and full-function JIT select sync must preserve that meaning end to end. | The shared verifier counted communication cases but accepted out-of-domain or duplicate source indexes, and full-function JIT precise select sync reconstructed recv cases from vector ordinal instead of the encoded source index. | Failing-first verifier proof `vm_select_case_contract_017_rejects_source_case_index_outside_domain`, duplicate-index proof `vm_select_case_contract_017_rejects_duplicate_source_case_index`, mechanical JIT contract `vm_select_source_case_sync_contract_017_recv_carries_source_case_index`, and language regression `2026_02_18_jit_select_default_middle_recv_value.vo`; fix validates source-index domain/uniqueness and carries `inst.c` into `SelectSyncCase::Recv`. | Passed focused `cargo test -p vo-common-core vm_select_case_contract_017 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, `cargo test -p vo-jit -- --test-threads=1`, `cargo test -p vo-vm vm_select_case_contract_017 -- --test-threads=1`, `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_select_case_contract_017 -- --test-threads=1`, and `./d.py test jit tests/lang/cases/jit/2026_02_18_jit_select_default_middle_recv_value.vo`; selected by `common-core-contract`, `cargo-test-jit`, `vm-hardening`, `vm-hardening-jit`, and `vo-test-jit-contract`. |
| VM-PACK-POINTER-SLOT-WIDTH-017 | Closed, 2026-06-18 | Struct slot counts used by GC allocation, pointer packing, and packed pointer validation must fit the VM/GC `u16` object slot width; no path may silently truncate wider metadata or wire payloads. | `StructMeta::slot_count()` and `unpack_pointer` truncated `slot_types.len()` / wire slot counts to `u16`; `validate_module_gc_layout` and `validate_packed_value` accepted 65536-slot struct layouts, allowing validation to pass before allocation wrote a different slot width. | Failing-first proofs `vm_pack_pointer_slot_contract_017_rejects_struct_meta_above_gc_width` and `vm_pack_pointer_slot_contract_017_rejects_pointer_payload_above_gc_width`; fix rejects oversized slot layouts in common verifier and packed payload validation, checks `StructMeta::slot_count()`, and makes `unpack_pointer` use a checked `u16` conversion. | Passed focused `cargo test -p vo-common-core contract_017 -- --test-threads=1`, `cargo test -p vo-runtime vm_pack_pointer_slot_contract_017_rejects_pointer_payload_above_gc_width -- --test-threads=1`, and `cargo test -p vo-runtime pack::tests -- --test-threads=1`; selected by `common-core-contract`, `runtime-contract`, `gc-contract`, and `vm-hardening` in `contract` / `vm-production`. |
| VM-PACK-ARRAY-SLOT-WIDTH-017 | Closed, 2026-06-18 | Inline array value packing must encode nested element slot widths with the same width the runtime metadata model uses; pack must not truncate `usize` array element slot counts before validation/unpack. | `pack_array_value_inline` wrote `layout.elem_slots as u16`, while validator/unpack compared the narrowed field against full runtime layout metadata; a `[][1][65536]int64` payload packed by the runtime failed its own validator. | Failing-first runtime proof `vm_pack_array_slot_contract_017_round_trips_inline_array_elem_width_above_u16`; fix widens the inline-array `elem_slots` wire field to `u64` and routes pack, validation, and unpack through that shared width. | Passed focused `cargo test -p vo-runtime vm_pack_array_slot_contract_017_round_trips_inline_array_elem_width_above_u16 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture` and full `cargo test -p vo-runtime pack::tests -- --test-threads=1`; selected by `runtime-contract` and `vm-production` through `cargo-test-runtime`. |
| VM-SELECT-OPERAND-CALL-018 | Closed, 2026-06-18 | Select operand evaluation must not create an active outer `SelectState` before channel/value expressions run, because operand calls may execute nested selects on the same fiber. | Codegen emitted `SelectBegin` before evaluating case operands; a channel expression calling a function with its own select overwrote and cleared the fiber-level `select_state`, so the outer `SelectRecv` failed with `SelectRecv without active SelectBegin`. | Failing-first language regression `2026_02_18_select_operand_call_nested_select.vo` failed on VM and JIT; fix evaluates all select case operands in source order, stores a compact case plan, then emits `SelectBegin` and registers `SelectSend`/`SelectRecv` from the evaluated operands. | Passed `./d.py test both tests/lang/cases/runtime/scheduler/2026_02_18_select_operand_call_nested_select.vo`; selected by `vo-test-runtime-contract` / default VM+JIT language gates through the manifest. |
