| VM-SELECT-ZERO-SLOT-SEND-018 | Closed, 2026-06-18 | `SelectSend.flags` must encode the actual element slot count, including zero, and `QueueLayout` metadata is the layout authority. | The shared verifier rewrote `SelectSend.flags == 0` to one slot, unlike `QueueSend` and `SelectRecv`; zero-slot array sends compiled to valid metadata but were rejected before execution. | Failing-first verifier proof `vm_select_zero_slot_send_contract_018_accepts_empty_element_layout` and language regression `2026_02_18_select_send_zero_slot_array.vo`; fix removes the verifier rewrite and lets empty `QueueLayout` prove zero-slot sends. | Passed focused `cargo test -p vo-common-core vm_select_zero_slot_send_contract_018_accepts_empty_element_layout -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture` and `./d.py test both tests/lang/cases/runtime/scheduler/2026_02_18_select_send_zero_slot_array.vo`; selected by `common-core-contract` and runtime language gates. |
| VM-TYPE-SLOT-WIDTH-018 | Closed, 2026-06-18 | Type layout slot counting must use one non-lossy fact source and only narrow to `u16` at VM/frame metadata boundaries. | `type_slot_count` used `u16` addition/multiplication and cast array lengths to `u16`, while `type_slot_types` built the full layout; a struct with `[65536]int` could reach bytecode metadata with wrapped field widths before verifier rejection. | Failing-first compile regression `2026_02_18_slot_count_u16_limit.vo` first failed as generated invalid bytecode; fix adds `type_slot_count_usize`, makes `type_slot_count` a checked narrowing wrapper, and returns a codegen error before building struct metadata for oversized flattened layouts. | Passed `./d.py test compile tests/lang/cases/typechecker/2026_02_18_slot_count_u16_limit.vo`; selected by the manifest compile matrix and codegen/typechecker contract coverage. |
| VM-PACK-CANONICAL-MARKER-018 | Closed, 2026-06-18 | Packed nullable reference values must use canonical markers only: `0` for null, `1` for non-null, with fixed-array inline value marker `2` reserved for inline array values. | Packed slice, pointer, map, heap-array, and queue validation treated any nonzero marker as non-null, so malformed cross-island bytes with marker `3` validated and unpacked as ordinary references. | Failing-first runtime proof `vm_pack_marker_contract_018_rejects_noncanonical_reference_markers`; fix shares canonical marker validation across queue and nullable reference payloads and makes unpack reject noncanonical markers if validation is bypassed. | Passed `cargo test -p vo-runtime vm_pack_marker_contract_018_rejects_noncanonical_reference_markers -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `runtime-contract` and `vm-production` through `cargo-test-runtime`. |
| VM-JIT-SELECT-ZERO-SLOT-READ-018 | Closed, 2026-06-18 | JIT read-effect and liveness facts for `SelectSend` must derive the value width from `SelectSend.flags`, including zero, instead of inventing a minimum one-slot operand. | `RegisterCount::SelectSendElemSlots` normalized `flags == 0` to one value read, so a legal zero-slot send had a phantom value dependency in JIT effects and loop analysis. | Failing-first source-contract proof `vm_select_zero_slot_send_contract_018_jit_read_effects_skip_value_slots` first observed reads `[12, 13]` instead of `[12]`; fix makes the effect fact source use exact flags and updates the loop-analysis expectation. | Passed the focused `vo-jit` proof and `test_get_read_regs_queue_and_select_ops`; selected by `jit-contract` through `cargo-test-jit`. |
| VM-SELECT-PENDING-REGION-018 | Closed, 2026-06-18 | The verifier must keep every `SelectBegin` registration region contiguous until `SelectExec`; no non-select opcode may run while select registration is pending. | The shared verifier accepted `SelectBegin; LoadInt; SelectRecv; SelectExec`, allowing malformed bytecode to leave an active select open across arbitrary execution and potential nested select/call state. | Failing-first source-contract proof `vm_select_pending_region_contract_018_rejects_non_select_opcode_before_exec`; fix rejects non-select opcodes while a `SelectBegin` is pending. | Passed `cargo test -p vo-common-core vm_select_ -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `common-core-contract` and VM hardening selectors. |
| VM-SELECT-RECV-EXACT-LAYOUT-018 | Closed, 2026-06-18 | `SelectRecv` destinations must exactly match the queue element layout recorded in `QueueLayout`; structural compatibility is not enough for metadata-bearing multi-slot values. | `verify_select_recv_contract` used `verify_storage_layout_compatible`, so malformed bytecode could receive `[Value, Value]` queue metadata into an interface-pair destination. | Failing-first source-contract proof `vm_select_recv_layout_contract_018_rejects_structural_interface_destination`; fix routes `SelectRecv value` through exact local-layout matching. | Passed `cargo test -p vo-common-core vm_select_ -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `common-core-contract` and VM hardening selectors. |
| VM-CODEGEN-SLOT-AGGREGATE-018 | Closed, 2026-06-18 | Codegen must narrow aggregate slot widths at one checked boundary for struct metadata, function params/returns/locals, `SelectRecv` ABI flags, and dynamic closure/interface calls. | Struct offset accumulation, function frame high-water accounting, return layout sums, local allocation, `SelectRecv` flags, and dynamic `CallClosure`/`CallIface` arg/ret widths could panic, wrap, or cast oversized layouts after analysis had computed the non-lossy shape. | Failing-first compile regressions `2026_02_18_struct_total_slot_count_u16_limit.vo`, `2026_02_18_anonymous_struct_total_slot_count_u16_limit.vo`, `2026_02_18_function_param_total_slot_count_u16_limit.vo`, `2026_02_18_function_return_total_slot_count_u16_limit.vo`, `2026_02_18_function_local_total_slot_count_u16_limit.vo`, `2026_02_18_select_recv_abi_slot_count_limit.vo`, `2026_02_18_dynamic_call_slot_count_limit.vo`, and `2026_02_18_dynamic_iface_call_slot_count_limit.vo`. | Passed the eight-case compile proof suite through `./d.py test compile`; the compile regressions are selected by `vo-test-compile` through `compile-contract` in `contract` and through `lang-main` in `vm-production`. |
| VM-DOCS-LINT-LEDGER-SLICE-018 | Closed, 2026-06-18 | Docs lint must parse the active readiness ledger by Markdown heading boundaries and reject restored signoff whenever an open current blocker exists, even if table text mentions a heading literal. | `docs_lint.mjs` sliced active rows with raw `.split("### Superseded")`, so a literal `### Superseded` inside a table cell could hide later open rows from the signoff lint. | Source-contract self-test `selfTestVmProductionReadinessSignoffLint` builds the counterexample with an open `VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002` row after a literal heading mention; fix uses heading-line bounded sections and accepts only withdrawn signoff text while blockers remain open. | Passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-docs-lint ./d.py ci task docs-lint`; selected by `docs-contract` in `contract`. |
| VM-DOCS-CONTRACT-VMPROD-018 | Closed, 2026-06-18 | Readiness docs/signoff proofs must be selected by both `contract` and `vm-production`; no production signoff gate may bypass docs-contract lint. | `docs-contract` guarded the active-ledger/signoff lint in `contract`, but `vm-production` did not select it, so production-readiness execution could omit the document gate that rejects restored signoff while blockers remain open. | Source-contract proofs `lint_docs_contract_requires_group_in_contract` and `lint_docs_contract_requires_group_in_vm_production`; strict lint first failed with `vm-production must include docs-contract`. | Passed `cargo test -p vo-dev lint_docs_contract -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture` and `cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- lint tasks --strict`; `vm-production` now includes `docs-contract` and its `docs-lint` task. |
| VM-SELECT-WOKEN-PAYLOAD-CONTRACT-018 | Closed, 2026-06-18 | A materialized select recv wake payload must prove payload width and slot metadata match the selected queue element before stack mutation, waiter cancellation, or select-state clearing. | `complete_woken_case` trusted `SelectWokenResult::Recv` and wrote `data.iter().take(elem_slots)` without checking `slot_types`, so a width-drifted wake payload could partially overwrite stack slots and still complete the selected case. | Failing-first proof `vm_select_woken_payload_contract_018_rejects_width_drift_before_stack_write`; fix shares `validate_select_woken_recv_payload_contract` with queue/select ownership and restores the woken result before returning `Malformed` on drift. | Passed `cargo test -p vo-vm vm_select_woken_payload_contract_018 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by VM hardening gates through the `vm_` proof prefix. |
| VM-ENDPOINT-TRANSFER-OWNER-018 | Closed, 2026-06-18 | Endpoint `Transfer { new_peer }` at the home island must be authorized by the home island or an already registered peer before mutating endpoint peer ownership. | `handle_endpoint_request_command` accepted a transfer command from any `from_island`, allowing an unknown island that knew an endpoint id to add an arbitrary peer and receive later close/tombstone effects. | Failing-first proof `vm_endpoint_transfer_owner_018_rejects_unknown_peer_transfer`; fix makes home-peer mutation conditional on `from_island == current_island_id || home_info.peers.contains(&from_island)`. | Passed `cargo test -p vo-vm vm_endpoint_transfer_owner_018 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by VM hardening gates through the `vm_` proof prefix. |
| VM-HOST-WAKE-LEGACY-TOKEN-018 | Closed, 2026-06-18 | Host replay/timer wakes must carry the generation-bearing `HostWaitKey`; legacy raw-token adapters must not wake a later registration that reused the same token. | `wake_host_event_legacy_*_token` re-looked up the current waiter by raw token, so a stale external completion could wake a newer replay/timer registration with the same token after the original generation had already completed. | Failing-first proof `vm_host_wake_identity_018_legacy_replay_token_does_not_wake_new_registration`; fix leaves key-bearing `wake_host_event` as the wake authority and makes legacy raw-token adapters return `false`. | Passed `cargo test -p vo-vm vm_host_wake_identity_018 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by VM hardening gates through the `vm_` proof prefix. |
| VM-JIT-PREPARED-CALL-REQUEST-018 | Closed, 2026-06-18 | JIT prepared dynamic-call requests must preserve the full caller resume PC, and the bridge must honor a runtime helper infrastructure-error sentinel before decoding a call request. | `JitContext.call_arg_start` was a `u16` even though prepared calls used it to carry caller `resume_pc`; additionally `handle_jit_non_ok_transition` decoded `JitResult::Call` without first checking the helper's infra-error sentinel. | Failing-first proofs `vm_jit_call_request_abi_018_call_result_honors_infra_error_sentinel` and `vm_jit_prepared_call_request_abi_018_preserves_full_caller_resume_pc`; runtime ABI proof `vm_jit_call_request_abi_006_validates_narrow_fields_before_publishing` guards non-prepared narrowing. | Passed `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit call_request_abi_018 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture` and `cargo test -p vo-runtime vm_jit_call_request_abi_006_validates_narrow_fields_before_publishing -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by JIT VM hardening and runtime contract gates. |
| VM-EMBEDDED-IFACE-METHOD-VALUE-DEADPATH-018 | Closed, 2026-06-18 | Embedded-interface method-value wrappers must have one owner for capture-slot layout and capture transfer metadata. | Red-team found an unused `get_or_create_method_value_wrapper_embedded_iface` helper that could build a closure with capture slot metadata but no capture transfer metadata if it became reachable later; current source had no call sites, so the issue was a dead-path semantic fork rather than a reachable VM bug. | Source-contract cleanup deletes the dead helper and cache variant, leaving `wrapper.rs` as the method-value wrapper owner; `rg get_or_create_method_value_wrapper_embedded_iface` no longer finds a helper path. | Passed `cargo test -p vo-codegen method_value_wrapper_uses -- --test-threads=1`; selected by codegen contract coverage. |
| VM-CONTAINER-FACT-KIND-035 | Closed, 2026-06-19 | Verifier container facts must carry both layout and object kind, zero-slot operations must not kill facts they do not write, and static-call write kills must derive from the callee's real return shape. | The 034 layout facts still allowed known map values to satisfy queue ops and known queue values to satisfy map ops; zero-slot `SelectRecv` without `ok` killed facts for a destination it did not write; and static `Call` fact kills used packed caller flags instead of the callee `param_slots` / `ret_slots`. | Failing-first proofs `module_verifier_rejects_map_len_on_known_queue_035`, `module_verifier_rejects_map_iter_init_on_known_queue_035`, `module_verifier_rejects_queue_close_on_known_map_035`, `module_verifier_preserves_container_fact_across_zero_slot_select_recv_without_ok_035`, and `module_verifier_drops_container_fact_across_large_static_call_return_035`. | Passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-common-core-035-proof cargo test -p vo-common-core 035 -- --test-threads=1`; selected by `common-core-contract` through `cargo-test-common-core`. |
| VM-COLLECTION-LAYOUT-BOUNDARY-035 | Closed, 2026-06-19 | Map, queue, and select runtime operations must validate object slot width and precise slot layout against the module metadata before stack writes, waiter registration, remote replay, write barriers, or map/queue mutation. | VM collection helpers accepted runtime map/queue objects whose stored `ValueMeta` widths or layouts drifted from the instruction metadata; queue/select recv paths could write malformed payloads, select send could register malformed payloads, and map get/set could read or publish values before proving object metadata matched the bytecode contract. | Failing-first proofs `queue_send_core_rejects_payload_width_drift_035`, `exec_queue_recv_rejects_element_width_drift_before_stack_write_035`, `queue_send_core_rejects_payload_layout_drift_035`, `exec_queue_recv_rejects_element_layout_drift_before_stack_write_035`, `select_recv_rejects_queue_element_width_drift_before_stack_write_035`, `select_send_registration_rejects_queue_element_width_drift_035`, `select_recv_rejects_queue_element_layout_drift_before_stack_write_035`, `exec_map_get_rejects_runtime_value_width_drift_before_stack_write_035`, `exec_map_set_rejects_runtime_value_width_drift_before_mutation_035`, `exec_map_get_rejects_runtime_value_layout_drift_before_stack_write_035`, and `exec_map_set_rejects_runtime_value_layout_drift_before_mutation_035`. | Passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-vm-035-queue-proof cargo test -p vo-vm 035 -- --test-threads=1`; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm` and by VM contract coverage. |
| VM-JIT-MAPSET-BARRIER-ORDER-035 | Closed, 2026-06-19 | JIT map writes must run typed write barriers from map object metadata before publishing `set_checked`, matching the interpreter metadata owner. | `vo_map_set` called `map::set_checked` before the key/value barriers, so a GC interleave could observe the new map entry before the typed barriers recorded roots; the 034 interpreter fix removed flag-based barrier ownership but the JIT ABI helper still had publish-before-barrier order. | Source-contract proof `vo_map_set_barrier_order_035_barriers_before_set_checked`; fix moves typed barriers before `map::set_checked` and keeps map metadata as the barrier source. | Passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-runtime-035-jit-proof cargo test -p vo-runtime vo_map_set_barrier_order_035_barriers_before_set_checked -- --test-threads=1`; selected by `runtime-contract` and `vm-production` through `cargo-test-runtime`. |
| VM-FINAL-GATE-PLAN-SOURCE-035 | Closed, 2026-06-19 | Final signoff evidence must compare task evidence against the canonical `vo-dev` selector plan, including task dependencies, and final evidence headings must not be pinned to a historical date. | Docs-lint still parsed `eng/tasks.toml` in JavaScript, ignoring `[[task]].needs`, and recognized only `### Final Signoff Gate Evidence, 2026-06-18`; restored-signoff evidence could therefore diverge from `vo-dev task plan` or become stale-date special-cased. | Source-contract proofs `selfTestVmProductionSourceStateHash` with a 2026-06-19 final-evidence heading and `gate_plan_env_includes_resolved_task_dependencies_035`; fix injects canonical `contract` / `vm-production` task plans from `vo-dev task run`, falls back to `vo-dev task plan --format json`, removes the duplicate JS group parser, and validates any ISO final-evidence date. | Passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-docs-lint-035 node scripts/ci/docs_lint.mjs` and `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-vo-dev-035 cargo test -p vo-dev gate_plan_env_includes_resolved_task_dependencies_035 -- --test-threads=1`; selected by `docs-contract` and `vo-dev-contract` in `contract` / `vm-production`. |
| VM-MAP-HANDLE-KIND-036 | Closed, 2026-06-19 | Map operations must prove a non-nil handle canonicalizes to a `ValueKind::Map` object before reading `MapData`, iterator state, or map metadata. | Red-team audit found `MapLen`, `MapIterInit`, and `MapIterNext` could trust an unknown container fact or iterator map reference and reach map runtime reads with a non-map `GcRef`; JIT map helpers had the same handle-kind gap before metadata/layout checks. | Failing-first behavior and source-contract proofs `exec_map_len_rejects_non_map_gcref_before_mapdata_read_036`, `exec_map_iter_init_rejects_non_map_gcref_before_iterator_write_036`, `exec_map_iter_next_rejects_non_map_iterator_ref_before_stack_write_036`, `map_len_and_iter_init_use_checked_map_handle_contract_036`, and `jit_map_helpers_use_current_pc_layout_contract_036`. | `exec_map_*` helpers and JIT map ABI helpers now validate canonical map handles before `MapData`, iterator, layout, barrier, or mutation effects and return VM `JitError` / helper sentinels on invalid metadata. Passed focused `vo-vm` 036 and `vo-runtime` 036 proofs; selected by VM/runtime contract gates through the proof tests. |
| VM-JIT-CURRENT-PC-LAYOUT-036 | Closed, 2026-06-19 | JIT runtime helpers and callbacks must recover collection layouts from the current JIT function id plus runtime trap PC, not from packed widths or stale caller state. | Red-team audit found JIT queue callbacks carried only `elem_slots`, select callbacks used non-layout helpers, and map helpers accepted same-width key/value metadata drift; direct JIT-to-JIT calls also lacked a callee-owned `current_func_id` fact for helper metadata lookup. | Source-contract proofs `vm_jit_current_func_metadata_036_prologue_sets_current_func_id`, `vm_jit_queue_callbacks_use_pc_metadata_layout_contract_036`, `vm_jit_select_callbacks_use_pc_metadata_layout_contract_036`, and `jit_map_helpers_use_current_pc_layout_contract_036`. | JIT prologues publish `JitContext.current_func_id`, lowerings mark `runtime_trap_pc`, map helpers and queue/select callbacks recover per-PC `JitInstructionMetadata`, and missing/wrong metadata or same-width layout drift is rejected before enqueue, select registration, stack writes, barriers, or map mutation. Passed focused 036 proofs, 035 regressions, and no-run compile gates for `vo-vm --features jit`, `vo-jit`, and `vo-runtime --lib`; selected by JIT/VM/runtime contract gates. |
| VM-JIT-CALLER-METADATA-OWNER-037 | Closed, 2026-06-19 | Nested JIT call OK paths must restore the caller execution context, including `JitContext.current_func_id`, before caller helpers read per-PC metadata. | Red-team audit found direct static JIT calls, prepared closure/interface calls, and dynamic IC-hit calls restored `jit_bp`/`fiber_sp` after callee OK returns but left `current_func_id` set to the callee. A caller collection helper could then validate `(callee func id, caller pc)` metadata. | Failing-first source-contract proofs `vm_jit_current_func_metadata_037_direct_call_ok_paths_restore_caller_func_id`, `vm_jit_current_func_metadata_037_prepared_ok_path_restores_caller_func_id`, `vm_jit_current_func_metadata_037_ic_hit_ok_path_restores_caller_func_id`, and `vm_jit_current_func_metadata_037_restore_context_includes_current_func_id`. | JIT call helpers now share `restore_caller_execution_context`, restoring bp, sp, and current func id together on every JIT-to-JIT OK edge. Passed focused 037 JIT proofs and full `vo-jit`; selected by `jit-contract` / `cargo-test-jit`. |
| VM-JIT-PC-METADATA-STRICT-037 | Closed, 2026-06-19 | Production JIT contexts with real JIT tables must fail fast if `current_func_id` or `runtime_trap_pc` is unset before per-PC metadata lookup. | Red-team audit found map helpers and queue/select callbacks returned `Ok(None)` when metadata owner/PC was unset, which is acceptable only for no-table unit contexts, not production JIT execution. | Failing-first source-contract proofs `jit_map_helpers_require_current_pc_metadata_for_jit_tables_037` and `vm_jit_queue_layout_requires_current_pc_metadata_for_jit_tables_037`. | Metadata lookup now permits `Ok(None)` only for no-table direct unit contexts; contexts with `jit_func_count` or `direct_call_count` require `(current_func_id, runtime_trap_pc)` and otherwise report invalid metadata. Passed focused runtime/VM 037 proofs plus full `vo-runtime` and `vo-vm --features jit`; selected by runtime/VM/JIT contract gates. |
| VM-MAPITER-FACT-PRESERVE-037 | Closed, 2026-06-19 | The shared verifier must preserve a map iterator's captured key/value layout fact across `MapIterNext` cursor writes. | Red-team audit found `MapIterNext` wrote the iterator slot range and killed the known `MapIter` fact, so a second `MapIterNext` on the same iterator could use mismatched metadata and fall back to `Unknown`. | Failing-first proof `module_verifier_preserves_map_iter_fact_across_iter_next_037`. | Container-layout dataflow now treats `MapIterNext` cursor writes as preserving the iterator's captured map layout fact. Passed focused 037 and full `vo-common-core`; selected by `common-core-contract`. |
| VM-FINAL-EVIDENCE-TRACKED-037 | Closed, 2026-06-19 | Final signoff machine evidence must be tracked by Git, not merely present as local untracked JSON files. | Red-team docs/gate audit found docs-lint verified evidence schema/source-state/task arrays but read untracked `lang/docs/dev/vm-production-gate-evidence/*.json` files, allowing non-reproducible local evidence to satisfy restored signoff. | Failing-first `selfTestVmProductionSourceStateHash` case writes matching untracked evidence and requires rejection before `git add`, then acceptance only after tracking. | `hasTaskRunEvidence` now requires each evidence JSON to be a real tracked non-intent-to-add path before reading it. Passed `node scripts/ci/docs_lint.mjs`; selected by `docs-contract` in `contract` and `vm-production`. |
| VM-FINAL-EVIDENCE-CANONICAL-PLAN-038 | Closed, 2026-06-19 | Final signoff evidence must compare recorded task arrays to the canonical `vo-dev task plan`, not to ambient environment variables. | Red-team docs/gate audit found direct `node scripts/ci/docs_lint.mjs` could accept locally supplied `VO_DEV_EXPECTED_TASKS_*` values that shrank the required `contract` / `vm-production` task arrays. | Failing-first `selfTestVmProductionSourceStateHash` case sets subset ambient task-plan env values and requires rejection. | `docs_lint` now resolves the selector plan through `vo-dev task plan --format json`; `VO_DEV_EXPECTED_TASKS_*` is only accepted as a hint when it exactly matches the canonical plan. Passed `node scripts/ci/docs_lint.mjs`; selected by `docs-contract`. |
| VM-WORKFLOW-CHANGED-PREFIX-038 | Closed, 2026-06-19 | Workflow policy changes that affect production-readiness gates must select `docs-lint` in changed-mode through the CI route fact source. | Red-team gate audit found `.github/workflows/**` policy coverage relied on task input matching instead of an explicit `eng/ci.toml` changed-prefix route guarded by the VM readiness lint. | Failing-first proof `lint_vm_readiness_changed_prefixes_requires_production_readiness_workflow_route`. | `eng/ci.toml` now declares `.github/workflows/** -> docs-lint`, and `vo-dev lint tasks --strict` requires that route through `lint_vm_readiness_changed_prefixes`. Passed the focused vo-dev proof and strict task lint; selected by `vo-dev-contract`. |
| VM-SLOTSET-CONTAINER-FACT-KILL-038 | Closed, 2026-06-19 | Shared verifier container facts must be killed by dynamic local writes whose runtime index can overwrite a tracked container slot. | Red-team verifier audit found `SlotSet`/`SlotSetN` wrote stack locals in the interpreter but `instruction_writes_slot` treated them as no-ops for fact transfer, so a stale `MapNew`/`QueueNew` fact could survive an indexed write. | Failing-first proof `module_verifier_drops_container_fact_across_slot_set_dynamic_write_038`. | The verifier write model now treats dynamic `SlotSet` writes as possibly writing tracked slots at or after the base and lowers the fact to `Unknown` instead of preserving stale layout. Passed focused 038 and full `vo-common-core`; selected by `common-core-contract`. |
| VM-DYNAMIC-CALL-RET-OVERFLOW-038 | Closed, 2026-06-19 | Malformed dynamic call operands must produce structured verifier errors before fact analysis or call-shape validation can panic. | Red-team verifier audit found `packed_call_ret_start()` performed unchecked `b + arg_slots`; container fact analysis can call it before opcode contract validation, so malformed `CallClosure`/`CallIface` bytecode could panic in debug gates. | Failing-first proof `module_verifier_rejects_dynamic_call_ret_start_overflow_without_panic_038`. | Fact analysis now uses a checked optional return-start helper, and dynamic call contract reports `SlotRangeOverflow` through a shared checked helper before reading return layouts. Passed focused 038 and full `vo-common-core`; selected by `common-core-contract`. |
| VM-DYNAMIC-SLOT-SPAN-LAYOUT-039 | Closed, 2026-06-19 | Dynamic stack-array `SlotGet`/`SlotSet` must prove the whole bounds-checked stack-array span has the metadata element layout before interpreter or JIT execution. | Red-team GC/verifier audit found `SlotGet`/`SlotSet` verified only the base element and index slot, so a checked runtime index could read or write a `GcRef` through a statically `Value` slot outside the first element and break precise stack scanning. | Failing-first proofs `module_verifier_rejects_dynamic_slot_set_gc_layout_drift_039` and `module_verifier_rejects_dynamic_slot_get_gc_layout_drift_039`. | The shared verifier now derives an `IndexCheck(index, const_len)` dataflow fact and validates the repeated element layout across the whole checked local span. Passed focused `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-common-039-proof cargo test -p vo-common-core 039 -- --test-threads=1`; selected by `common-core-contract`. |
| VM-MAPITER-NEXT-OUTPUT-OWNERSHIP-039 | Closed, 2026-06-19 | `MapIterNext` key/value/ok outputs must have exact verifier-owned layouts and must not alias the opaque iterator state slots or each other. | Red-team verifier audit found `MapIterNext` did not validate the `ok` output slot and allowed key/value/ok output ranges to overlap hidden iterator state, letting runtime cursor writes and output writes invalidate the preserved iterator fact or static GC metadata. Follow-up red-team audit found `ok` could also overwrite the produced key or value output because runtime writes key/value first and `ok` last. | Failing-first proofs `module_verifier_rejects_map_iter_next_ok_slot_layout_drift_039`, `module_verifier_rejects_map_iter_next_ok_slot_aliases_iterator_039`, `module_verifier_rejects_map_iter_next_output_range_aliases_iterator_039`, `module_verifier_rejects_map_iter_next_ok_slot_aliases_key_output_039`, and `module_verifier_rejects_map_iter_next_ok_slot_aliases_value_output_039`. | The shared verifier now validates `ok` as `Value` and rejects key/value/ok overlap with `MAP_ITER_SLOT_TYPES` plus ok overlap with key/value outputs. Passed focused `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-common-039-proof cargo test -p vo-common-core 039 -- --test-threads=1`; selected by `common-core-contract`. |
| VM-FINAL-EVIDENCE-READINESS-DOC-TRACKED-039 | Closed, 2026-06-19 | Restored-signoff final evidence must be reproducible from tracked readiness docs as well as tracked machine evidence files. | Red-team docs/gate audit found final evidence could accept tracked `contract` / `vm-production` JSON while the readiness document containing the restored-signoff claim was itself untracked or locally divergent. | Failing-first `selfTestVmProductionSourceStateHash` case removes `lang/docs/dev/vm-production-readiness.md` from the index after writing otherwise matching evidence and requires rejection. | `hasVmProductionFinalGateEvidence` now requires the readiness doc itself to be tracked before accepting final evidence. Passed `node scripts/ci/docs_lint.mjs`; selected by `docs-contract` in `contract` and `vm-production`. |
| VM-PRODUCTION-WORKFLOW-SEMANTIC-LINT-039 | Closed, 2026-06-19 | Production-readiness workflow lint must inspect actual matrix selectors and executable scalar `run:` commands, not raw YAML text that comments, string literals, or disabled block-run control flow can spoof. | Red-team gate audit found comments containing `- contract`, `- vm-production`, and the expected `cargo run` lines could satisfy raw regex/string checks while the workflow executed no gate; local review found the same class with `echo "cargo run ..."` run commands; follow-up red-team audit found `run: | if false; then cargo run ...; fi` could satisfy line-based command checks. | Failing-first `selfTestProductionReadinessWorkflowLint` cases build comment-spoofed, string-spoofed, and block-run control-flow-spoofed workflows that do not execute the gate commands. | Docs-lint now strips YAML comments, reads selector entries, rejects block `run:` in the production-readiness workflow, and requires actual normalized scalar `cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- ... ${{ matrix.selector }}` command lines while rejecting hard-coded `vm-production` commands. Passed `node scripts/ci/docs_lint.mjs`; selected by `docs-contract`. |
| VM-INDEXCHECK-FACT-PRECISION-039 | Closed, 2026-06-19 | The verifier's checked-index fact source must preserve proofs across dynamic SlotSet writes that cannot write the index slot, while remaining conservative for unproven spans. | Red-team verifier audit found `IndexCheckAnalysis` reused the broad dynamic `SlotSet` may-write model that is appropriate for container facts, so a proven one-element `SlotSet` at base `r0` killed an index fact in `r2` even though runtime could not write `r2`. | Failing-first proof `module_verifier_preserves_index_check_fact_across_non_aliasing_slot_set_039`. | `IndexCheckAnalysis` now uses a span-aware write predicate for `SlotSet`/`SlotSetN`, preserving checked-index facts outside the proven stack-array write span and falling back to broad killing only when the span cannot be proven. Passed focused common-core 039 proof; selected by `common-core-contract`. |
| VM-JIT-SHARED-VERIFIER-FACT-SOURCE-039 | Closed, 2026-06-19 | JIT semantic rows and strict JIT entry proofs must express the shared verifier facts that their lowerings rely on, including checked stack-array spans and MapIterNext output ownership. | Red-team JIT audit found current execution still goes through the shared verifier, but `SlotGet`/`SlotSet`/`SlotGetN`/`SlotSetN` rows declared only generic metadata/local layout requirements, `MapIterNext` did not record the new output-ownership verifier contract, and the follow-up JIT audit found no strict-entry fixture proving `vo_jit::verify_module` rejects the new shared-verifier failures before lowering. | Failing-first proofs `vm_jit_semantic_rows_cover_shared_verifier_039_contracts`, `verify_module_rejects_checked_stack_array_span_drift_039`, and `verify_module_rejects_map_iter_next_output_alias_039`. | `VerifierRequirement` now includes `CheckedStackArraySpan` and `MapIterNextOutputOwnership`; the affected semantic rows declare those requirements through dedicated requirement sets, strict JIT verifier fixtures model the shared `IndexCheck` precondition instead of bypassing it, and `vo_jit::verify_module` rejects checked-span drift and MapIterNext output aliasing through the shared verifier. Passed focused `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-jit-039-proof cargo test -p vo-jit vm_jit_semantic_rows_cover_shared_verifier_039_contracts -- --test-threads=1`, focused strict-entry 039 tests, and full `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-jit-039-proof cargo test -p vo-jit -- --test-threads=1` (228/228); selected by `jit-contract`. |
| VM-FINAL-EVIDENCE-TRACKED-SOURCE-039 | Closed, 2026-06-19 | Final signoff source-state evidence must describe tracked deliverable source paths, not local untracked source files. | Red-team docs/gate audit found final evidence could hash an untracked workflow/source file, record matching tracked evidence JSON, and pass locally even though a clean checkout could not reproduce that source state. | Failing-first `selfTestVmProductionSourceStateHash` case writes an untracked `.github/workflows/production-readiness.yml` and matching evidence for that source state; vo-dev proof `vm_production_source_state_rejects_untracked_source_039` rejects the same class before evidence writing. | Docs-lint now rejects final evidence when any non-readiness/evidence source path is untracked, and `vo-dev task run` refuses to write final `contract` / `vm-production` evidence with untracked source paths. Passed `node scripts/ci/docs_lint.mjs` and focused `cargo test -p vo-dev vm_production_source_state_rejects_untracked_source_039`; selected by `docs-contract` and `vo-dev-contract`. |
| VM-PRODUCTION-WORKFLOW-JOB-BINDING-040 | Closed, 2026-06-19 | Production-readiness workflow lint must prove the same `readiness-gate` job owns the real `strategy.matrix.selector` values and unconditional fail-blocking gate steps. | Red-team gate audit found selector and run-command text could be split across jobs, the `readiness-gate` job / required run step could be skipped with `if:` or made non-blocking with `continue-on-error: true`, a job-level `continue-on-error: true` could make gate failure non-blocking, and a dummy same-job `selector:` outside `strategy.matrix` could spoof the selector coverage. | Failing-first `selfTestProductionReadinessWorkflowLint` cases cover job-level `if: false`, job-level `continue-on-error: true`, required step `if: false`, step `continue-on-error: true`, selector/command split across jobs, and dummy selector outside `strategy.matrix`. | Docs-lint now binds selectors specifically to `readiness-gate.strategy.matrix.selector`, binds required scalar run commands to the same job, rejects conditional or continue-on-error gate jobs, rejects required gate steps with `if:` or `continue-on-error`, and continues to reject block-run/string/comment spoofing. Passed `node scripts/ci/docs_lint.mjs`; selected by `docs-contract`. |
| VM-FINAL-EVIDENCE-ENV-OVERRIDE-PROOF-040 | Closed, 2026-06-19 | Final-evidence self-tests must prove ambient `VO_DEV_EXPECTED_TASKS_*` cannot shrink canonical task arrays without being masked by unrelated source-state mismatch. | Red-team gate audit found the ambient task-plan override self-test rewrote evidence after adding a workflow to the index but kept an older source-state token, so the rejection could be caused by source-state mismatch rather than the env/canonical mismatch. | Failing-first self-test shape recomputes a tracked-workflow source-state baseline, verifies canonical evidence passes, then writes matching-source subset evidence under subset `VO_DEV_EXPECTED_TASKS_*` env values and requires rejection. | `selfTestVmProductionSourceStateHash` now isolates the env override proof from source-state mismatch, so docs-lint rejects subset ambient plans only because `expectedSelectorTasks` requires the env hint to match canonical `vo-dev task plan`. Passed `node scripts/ci/docs_lint.mjs`; selected by `docs-contract`. |
| VM-FINAL-EVIDENCE-INTENT-TO-ADD-041 | Closed, 2026-06-19 | Final signoff source-state, readiness-doc, and gate-evidence checks must reject Git intent-to-add paths, because they are not reproducible tracked content. | Follow-up gate red-team audit found `git add -N` removes a new source file from `git ls-files --others` and makes `git ls-files --error-unmatch` succeed, while the index only records an empty placeholder. Matching final evidence could therefore claim uncommitted source, readiness-doc, or evidence JSON paths as tracked. | Failing-first proofs extend `selfTestVmProductionSourceStateHash` with matching-source intent-to-add evidence plus intent-to-add readiness-doc and task-run evidence cases, and add `vm_production_source_state_rejects_intent_to_add_source_041` for the `vo-dev task run` evidence writer. | Docs-lint now uses `git status --porcelain=v1 -z` for tracked evidence/doc checks; docs-lint and `vo-dev` use the same status-based source preflight and reject both `??` untracked paths and ` A` intent-to-add paths while preserving ordinary tracked working-tree edits for source-state hashing. Passed `node scripts/ci/docs_lint.mjs` and focused `cargo test -p vo-dev vm_production_source_state_rejects_intent_to_add_source_041`; selected by `docs-contract` and `vo-dev-contract`. |
| VM-PRODUCTION-WORKFLOW-EXECUTABLE-SHAPE-042 | Closed, 2026-06-19 | Production-readiness workflow lint must prove the `readiness-gate` matrix and scalar `run` steps are executable as written, including quoted YAML keys, untransformed selector matrix shape, and default shell execution. | Red-team gate audit found quoted YAML keys could hide job/step `if:` and `continue-on-error:`, `strategy.matrix.exclude` could remove a required selector after the lint saw the selector list, job or workflow-level `defaults.run.shell` could replace the shell for all gate steps, and step `shell:` could make a matching `run:` script return success without executing the gate command. | Failing-first `selfTestProductionReadinessWorkflowLint` cases cover quoted job-level `if`, quoted job-level and step-level `continue-on-error`, `matrix.exclude`, `matrix.include`, workflow-level shell defaults, job-level shell defaults, and required-step shell override. | Docs-lint now uses one YAML-key matcher for bare/single-quoted/double-quoted keys, restricts `readiness-gate.strategy.matrix` to exactly one `selector` key, rejects workflow/job defaults and run-step shell overrides, and keeps required scalar gate commands bound to unconditional fail-blocking steps. Passed `node scripts/ci/docs_lint.mjs`; selected by `docs-contract`. |
| VM-JIT-COMPILER-MODULE-SCOPE-042 | Closed, 2026-06-19 | A `JitCompiler`'s verified module token, function declarations, and compiled full/OSR cache must have the same module ownership boundary. | Red-team JIT audit found `verify_module_once` reverified a digest-mismatched module but the compiled-function and compiled-loop caches were keyed only by `func_id` / `(func_id, begin_pc)`, so a reused compiler could verify module B and return module A's old artifact. | Failing-first proofs `compile_rejects_module_scope_change_instead_of_reusing_cached_function_042` and `compile_loop_rejects_module_scope_change_instead_of_reusing_cached_loop_042`. | `JitCompiler::verify_module_once` now binds the compiler to the first verified module digest and returns `ModuleScopeChanged` on digest mismatch, keeping Cranelift declarations and compiled artifacts under the same module token. Passed both focused 042 JIT proofs; selected by `jit-contract`. |
| VM-HOST-WAKE-INGRESS-043 | Closed, 2026-06-19 | External host-event ingress across app-runtime, Studio wasm/web backend, `vo-web`, and legacy Playground must surface VM host-wake rejection; app-runtime mailbox pending keys may be consumed only after the VM accepts the wake. | Red-team runtime-boundary audits found app-runtime, Studio wasm, `vo-web`, and legacy Playground wake adapters ignored the `bool` returned by `Vm::wake_host_event` / `wake_host_event_with_data`; `AppSession::wake_host_event` also removed its mailbox dedup key before checking VM acceptance, and Studio web backend swallowed scheduled host-event failures with an empty catch. | Failing-first proofs `app_session_host_wake_rejection_does_not_consume_mailbox_key_043` and `handle_event_reports_rejected_host_wake_043`; source-contract tests `lint_runtime_surface_contract_requires_app_runtime_test`, `lint_vm_readiness_changed_prefixes_requires_app_runtime_surface_route`, and `selfTestStudioRuntimeSourceContractLint` / `lintStudioRuntimeSourceContracts` prove the behavior and adapter contracts are selected by their gates. | App-runtime wake APIs now return `SessionError::HostWakeRejected` and remove mailbox keys only after acceptance; event resume checks `wake_host_event_with_data`; Studio wasm maps rejection to JS errors; Studio web backend records scheduled wake failures through one GUI fatal path; `vo-web` island/async timer paths and legacy Playground report rejection instead of silently succeeding. Passed focused/full app-runtime tests, legacy Playground proof, `vo-web` wasm check, Studio wasm check, Studio build, full `vo-dev` tests, strict task lint, docs-lint, and `contract` / `vm-production` task plans showing `cargo-test-vo-app-runtime` selected. |
| VM-ENDPOINT-RESPONSE-REJECTION-043 | Closed, 2026-06-19 | Same-island endpoint response application must report rejected endpoint responses and preserve pending response obligations when wait id, source, or payload acceptance fails. | Red-team runtime-boundary audit found `apply_endpoint_response_for_waiter` discarded the `RuntimeCommandOutcome` returned by `RuntimeCommand::endpoint_response` for same-island endpoint waiters, so stale or wrong wait ids could return an outer success even though the endpoint response was not accepted. | Failing-first proof `vm_endpoint_runtime_wake_043_reports_rejected_same_island_endpoint_response`. | The same-island branch now requires `outcome.applied && outcome.payload_accepted`, returning `VmError` on rejection before decrementing or hiding pending endpoint response state. Passed the focused 043 proof and the same-island endpoint replay regression set. |
| VM-JIT-COMPILER-ENV-SCOPE-043 | Closed, 2026-06-19 | A reused `JitCompiler`'s compiled full/OSR caches must be bound to the resolved extern table and backend capability scope, not only to module digest and function ids. | Red-team JIT/ABI audit found the module-scope fix still allowed cache reuse when the same module compiled with a different `JitCompileEnv` resolved extern/provider identity or backend capability set. | Failing-first proofs `compile_rejects_env_scope_change_instead_of_reusing_cached_function_043` and `compile_loop_rejects_env_scope_change_instead_of_reusing_cached_loop_043`. | `JitCompiler` now binds the first `JitCompileEnv` scope, including resolved extern entries and backend caps, and returns `CompileEnvScopeChanged` before any full/OSR cache hit on mismatch. Passed the focused 043 env-scope proofs and the earlier 042 module-scope proofs. |
| VM-JIT-HELPER-DETAIL-ID-043 | Closed, 2026-06-19 | JIT helper invalid-metadata detail ids must be unique so runtime/JIT diagnostics identify the failing helper contract without ambiguity. | Red-team JIT/OSR audit found `JIT_HELPER_MAP_LEN_LAYOUT` and `JIT_HELPER_TYPED_WRITE_BARRIER_LAYOUT` both used detail id `105`, creating a diagnostic fact fork. | Failing-first source-contract proof `jit_helper_invalid_metadata_detail_ids_are_unique_043`. | `JIT_HELPER_TYPED_WRITE_BARRIER_LAYOUT` now has its own detail id, and the uniqueness proof enumerates helper invalid-metadata constants. Passed the focused uniqueness proof plus typed write-barrier and slice metadata drift regressions. |
| VM-JIT-EXTERN-REPLAY-LOGICAL-DEPTH-062 | Closed, 2026-06-23 | JIT extern replay scope identity must use logical direct-call depth, not only materialized VM frame count, so closure replay results survive JIT frame materialization. | A direct JIT-to-JIT callee executing `CallExtern` could return `CallClosure` before its logical call frame was materialized. The original replay scope used `fiber.frames.len()`, so after side-exit materialization the same extern saw a different depth, opened a nested replay scope, hid the cached closure result, and suspended again instead of completing. | Failing-first proof `vm_jit_extern_replay_scope_062_survives_direct_call_materialization_depth_change` reproduced replay returning `ExternSuspend` instead of `Ok` after the simulated materialized frame changed `fiber.frames.len()`. The fix routes JIT extern replay scope through `jit_extern_replay_frame_depth(fiber, ctx)`, using materialized frame count plus `JitContext.call_depth`, the existing direct-JIT logical-depth fact. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-jit-replay-proof cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_jit_extern_replay_scope_062_survives_direct_call_materialization_depth_change -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture` and `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-jit-replay-proof cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_jit_extern -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-JIT-EXTERN-REPLAY-CALL-DEPTH-GATE-062 | Closed, 2026-06-23 | Every generated direct JIT-to-JIT call path that can reach a callee extern must publish `JitContext.call_depth` for the duration of callee execution, because JIT extern replay scope now uses logical depth as part of its identity. | The first logical-depth proof manually set `ctx.call_depth = 1`, proving the replay identity formula but not proving the production lowerings actually maintain that fact for direct, prepared, and dynamic-IC direct calls. | Mechanical source-contract proofs `vm_jit_extern_replay_scope_062_direct_calls_publish_logical_call_depth`, `vm_jit_extern_replay_scope_062_prepared_calls_publish_logical_call_depth`, and `vm_jit_extern_replay_scope_062_ic_hit_publishes_logical_call_depth` require `emit_call_depth_enter` to dominate the direct JIT call and `emit_call_depth_leave` to run only after callee result capture. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-jit-replay-proof cargo test -p vo-jit vm_jit_extern_replay_scope_062 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture` (3/3); selected by `vm-production` through `jit-contract` / `cargo-test-jit`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-EXTERN-REPLAY-SETUP-STACK-OVERFLOW-SCOPE-062 | Closed, 2026-06-23 | `FrameChanged` from extern `CallClosure` replay setup must mean a replay frame and depth record were actually published; terminal setup failures must close the active extern replay scope before any recoverable trap can resume. | `call_extern_replay_closure` could hit stack-overflow `runtime_trap` before `closure_replay.push_depth`. A recoverable panic path can return `ExecResult::FrameChanged`, letting callers preserve an extern replay scope even though no replay frame/depth was published. | Mechanical source-contract proof `vm_extern_replay_setup_stack_overflow_062_closes_scope_before_runtime_trap` requires both stack-overflow `return runtime_trap(` exits in `call_extern_replay_closure` to call `self.fiber.closure_replay.finish_extern_terminal()` first. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-jit-replay-proof cargo test -p vo-vm vm_extern_replay_setup_stack_overflow_062 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-GC-STEP-EXTERN-SAFE-BOUNDARY-062 | Closed, 2026-06-23 | Collector advancement must require an explicit stable-root VM/test boundary; safe extern-provider contexts may allocate but must not expose a safe `Gc::step*` path while direct-JIT helper arguments or locals can still live only in native/JIT storage. | `ExternCallContext::gc()` returns a safe collector handle, and safe `Gc::step`, `step_with_root_state`, and `step_with_root_scanner` allowed an extern provider reached through a JIT direct helper to advance collection before the VM had materialized JIT frames and roots. | Proof `gc_step_boundary_062_step_apis_are_unsafe` locks the collector advancement APIs behind `unsafe`; the fix makes all three step APIs unsafe with explicit stable-root safety docs, leaves allocation reachable, and wraps only VM/test/perf harness call sites that own a root scanner or test root state. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-jit-replay-proof cargo test -p vo-runtime gc_step_boundary_062 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture` and full `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-jit-replay-proof cargo test -p vo-runtime -- --test-threads=1` (237 unit, 8 GC model, 3 GC perf); selected by `vm-production` through `runtime-contract` / `cargo-test-runtime`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-SOURCE-CONTRACT-MACRO-PROOF-VIEW-062 | Closed, 2026-06-23 | Source-contract proofs must use a fact view that matches the contract subject; macro-template contracts cannot rely on a production view that intentionally erases `macro_rules!` bodies. | Full runtime validation failed `raw_object_headers_as_mut_are_unsafe_public_primitives_058` after the shared production-source helper erased macro bodies by design. The product contract was still true, but the proof used the wrong source view and blocked runtime/production gates. | The proof now checks raw `objects/mod.rs` source with occurrence counts so the test's own sentinel strings cannot satisfy the assertion without the `impl_gc_object!` macro template still declaring `pub unsafe fn as_mut` and the write-barrier safety obligation. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-jit-replay-proof cargo test -p vo-runtime objects::tests::raw_object_headers_as_mut_are_unsafe_public_primitives_058 -- --exact --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, and full `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-jit-replay-proof cargo test -p vo-runtime -- --test-threads=1` passed; selected by `vm-production` through `runtime-contract` / `cargo-test-runtime`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-QUICKPLAY-HOST-WAKE-KEY-PACKAGE-062 | Closed, 2026-06-23 | Checked-in quickplay packages must use the current generation-bearing `HostWaitKey` host-wake contract, not the legacy token-only wake path. | The embedded `github.com/vo-lang/voplay` package inside `apps/studio/public/quickplay/blockkart/deps.json` still exposed `wakeHostEvent(token)` and scheduled pending host events by `ev.token`. Current VM/Studio wake APIs use the full encoded key; a packaged quickplay app could therefore carry an inert or stale host wake adapter while site gates passed artifact digest checks. | `scripts/ci/quickplay_validate.mjs` now validates the embedded `render_bootstrap.d.ts` and `render_bootstrap.js` contract, requiring `key` / `wakeHostEvent(key)` paths and rejecting token-only scheduling/wake strings. The checked-in package bytes and provenance digests were updated to use `ev.key` for timers/display-pulse waiters and `this.vm.wakeHostEvent(key)` for wake ingress. | Focused validation passed `node scripts/ci/quickplay_validate.mjs`; selected by `site` through the quickplay validation task, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-EXTERN-REPLAY-SETUP-FRAMECHANGED-062 | Closed, 2026-06-23 | Extern `CallClosure` replay setup callers may treat `ExecResult::FrameChanged` as a replay-frame publication only when the setup path actually published replay depth state. Runtime traps and recoverable unwind paths can also return `FrameChanged`, so PC commit and JIT replay side-exit publication must be driven by an explicit setup fact. | Follow-up frame-call/JIT red-team found the stack-overflow replay-scope fix still left a semantic fork: interpreter `prepare_extern_closure_replay_call` committed the caller replay PC for any `FrameChanged`, and the JIT extern-suspend bridge recorded `JitSideExitReason::Replay` for any `FrameChanged`, even when the result came from a pre-publication runtime trap rather than a replay frame. | Failing-first source-contract proofs `vm_extern_replay_setup_framechanged_062_commits_pc_only_after_replay_frame_publication` and `vm_jit_extern_suspend_framechanged_062_records_replay_only_after_frame_publication`; fix changes `prepare_typed_extern_closure_replay_call` into `prepare_typed_extern_closure_replay_setup`, returning `ExternReplaySetup { result, replay_frame_published }` from one owner that observes `closure_replay.depth_stack` growth, and makes interpreter/JIT consumers commit PC or record Replay only when that fact is true. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-jit-replay-proof cargo test -p vo-vm vm_extern_replay_setup -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture` and `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-jit-replay-proof cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_jit_extern_suspend -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening` and `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-APP-RUNTIME-HOST-REPLAY-MAILBOX-062 | Closed, 2026-06-23 | App-runtime pending host-event mailboxes must preserve source identity and replay identity for every non-GUI host replay event; only GUI replay waits are consumed by the dedicated replay-event path. | Runtime-boundary red-team found `record_pending_host_events` saved GUI replay keys separately, then filtered out every `event.replay` before queueing pending host events. Non-GUI replay waits such as fetch or extension replay could therefore disappear at the app-runtime boundary even though VM host waits were published correctly. The Studio wasm drain adapter also derived source from the encoded key and hardcoded `replay: false`, keeping the package adapter split alive. | Failing-first mailbox proof extends `record_pending_host_events_tracks_replay_and_dedups_non_replay` to include a fetch replay event. The fix makes `PendingHostEvent` carry `key`, `source`, `token`, `delay_ms`, and `replay`, filters only GUI replay keys out of the pending queue, and makes Studio wasm emit the VM-owned `source` and `replay` fields. | Focused validation passed the exact mailbox proof and full `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-app-runtime-proof cargo test -p vo-app-runtime -- --test-threads=1` (26/26); selected by `vm-production` through `runtime-surface-contract` / `cargo-test-vo-app-runtime`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-QUICKPLAY-PROJECT-DEPS-CONTRACT-062 | Closed, 2026-06-23 | Checked-in quickplay project dependency facts must be bound across the embedded `vo.mod`, `vo.lock`, deps package metadata, and each dependency `vo.web.json`; package freshness cannot rely only on artifact URL presence. | Runtime/gate red-team found `quickplay_validate.mjs` verified provenance and package digests but did not prove that BlockKart's embedded `vo.mod` and `vo.lock` still matched the embedded `github.com/vo-lang/voplay` dependency package. A stale deps module could therefore keep valid local artifact digests while drifting from the project lock's selected version, commit, source, or artifact digest facts. | `quickplay_validate.mjs` now parses the embedded project `vo.mod` and `vo.lock`, requires direct project requirements to match packaged dependency versions, requires every packaged dependency to be resolved in the lock with source and release-manifest digests, and binds each package `vo.web.json` module/version/commit plus locked artifact size/digest facts to the project lock. | Focused validation passed `node scripts/ci/quickplay_validate.mjs`; selected by `site` through `quickplay-validate`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-QUICKPLAY-HOST-WAKE-RENDERER-GRAPH-062 | Closed, 2026-06-23 | Quickplay host-wake package validation must inspect the renderer JavaScript module actually declared by `vo.web.json` and its local import graph, not a hand-picked implementation file that Studio may stop loading. | Gate red-team found the first HostWaitKey package proof hardcoded `js/dist/render_bootstrap.js`, while Studio loads the renderer from `vo.web.json` `extension.web.js_modules.renderer`. If the renderer entry changed or stopped importing the bootstrap bridge, the validator could still pass by checking a stale, unreachable file. | `quickplay_validate.mjs` now reads `vo.web.json`, resolves the declared renderer entry, walks static and dynamic relative JS imports in the embedded package, requires the import graph to reach `render_bootstrap.js`, and checks both stale token-only paths and current HostWaitKey paths in the reachable renderer graph. | Focused validation passed `node scripts/ci/quickplay_validate.mjs`; selected by `site` through `quickplay-validate`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
| VM-QUICKPLAY-VALIDATOR-CHANGED-MODE-062 | Closed, 2026-06-23 | Changes to quickplay validation policy must select `quickplay-validate` in changed-mode planning, otherwise the policy can change without executing against the checked-in quickplay package. | Gate-coverage red-team found `eng/ci.toml` and the mirrored vo-dev prefix contract routed `scripts/ci/**` changes to docs/CI self-check tasks but not `quickplay-validate`. A validator regression could therefore pass changed-mode PR planning while the package contract was never run. | Source-contract proof extends `lint_vm_readiness_changed_prefixes_requires_docs_lint_script_route_058` to reject removing `quickplay-validate` from the `scripts/ci/**` route. The fix adds `quickplay-validate` to both `eng/ci.toml` and `VM_READINESS_CHANGED_PREFIX_TASKS`. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-vo-dev-proof cargo test -p vo-dev lint_vm_readiness_changed_prefixes_requires_docs_lint_script_route_058 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-vo-dev-proof cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- lint tasks --strict`, and `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-vo-dev-proof cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- task plan pr --changed`, whose planned tasks include `quickplay-validate`; final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |

Rebuilding the same source in a clean target directory executed the test binary and listed all tests, narrowing the hang to a local artifact state rather than current source proof execution. | Failing-first reproduction: default-target `cargo test -p vo-dev -- --list` required interruption after `Running unittests src/main.rs`. Clean-target proof: `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-vo-dev-hang cargo test -p vo-dev -- --list`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-vo-dev-hang cargo test -p vo-dev lint_system::tests::lint_vm_hardening_accepts_gate_owned_hardening_selector_062 -- --exact --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, and `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-vo-dev-hang cargo test -p vo-dev lint_system::tests::lint_vm_readiness_changed_prefixes_requires_vm_production_analysis_scope_062 -- --exact --test-threads=1 --nocapture` all passed from clean target state. | The 2026-06-23 final `contract`, `vm-production`, `site`, and `release-verify` evidence remains valid only for its recorded historical source state. This row stays open for the current source state until the final selectors pass again and write matching evidence. |

Fresh final `contract`, `vm-production`, `site`, and `release-verify` evidence has not yet been recorded for this source state. |
| VM-FINAL-EVIDENCE-PROBE-IGNORE-GATE-062 | Closed, 2026-06-23 | The local-probe ignore policy used to keep final evidence source-state clean must itself be a tracked, executable contract rather than an undocumented workspace cleanup. | Gate-coverage red-team accepted the `.gitignore` cleanup behavior but found no durable proof that future edits would preserve the exact root probe/rmeta ignore rules needed to keep local red-team artifacts out of final source evidence. | Source-contract proof `vm_final_evidence_local_probe_source_062_gitignore_locks_probe_artifact_rules` checks the tracked `.gitignore` for `*.rmeta`, `/vo_*_probe`, `/vo_scheduler_*`, and `/vo_macro_hardcoded_raw_wake`. | Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-vo-dev-proof cargo test -p vo-dev vm_final_evidence_local_probe_source_062_gitignore_locks_probe_artifact_rules -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; selected by `vm-production` through `vo-dev-contract` / `cargo-test-vo-dev`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |
