The deliverable source state is now represented by the current source-state token and final evidence files. | Proof is the earlier final `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-contract-047-proof cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- task run contract` failure message plus the existing `vm_production_source_state_rejects_untracked_source_039` contract; closure is the successful final evidence writer path for all final selectors. | Passed final `contract`, `vm-production`, `site`, and `release-verify` selectors from source state `vm-production-current-source:b625c2ddd82eb7b3e0fb8e84397176edb590d072e1a3847fa170287ecaa00282`; `contract.json`, `vm-production.json`, `site.json`, and `release-verify.json` record exact task arrays and passed status. |

#### 2026-06-22 Supplemental Active Ledger Entries

- `VM-JIT-MATERIALIZE-RESUME-FRAME-SHAPE-062` is closed by the 2026-06-23 final selector evidence. JIT resume-frame
  materialization must consume the shared `validate_call_frame_shape` fact for
  every resume and existing materialized frame before publishing VM frames,
  advancing `fiber.sp`, or clearing `resume_stack`. The frame-call red-team
  found `materialize_jit_frames` rejected `gc_scan_slots > local_slots` but did
  not reject `param_slots > local_slots`, allowing a malformed resume
  `FunctionDef` to bypass the single frame-shape source. Failing-first proof
  `vm_jit_materialize_resume_frame_shape_062_rejects_param_slots_beyond_locals_transactionally`
  covers the transactional rejection: no panic, no frame publication, no stack
  growth, no entry-PC mutation, and no resume-root clearing. The fix routes
  resume frames, existing frames, and post-materialization invariants through
  `validate_call_frame_shape` instead of a local scan-slot-only check. Focused
  validation passed
  `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-main cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_jit_materialize_resume_frame_shape_062 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`,
  `... cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit frame_shape_062 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`
  (6 tests), and
  `... cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit scan_slots_beyond_locals -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`
  (12 tests). It is selected by `vm-production` through
  `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`; final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence.

- `VM-JIT-CALL-ENTRYPOINT-FRAME-SHAPE-062` is closed by the 2026-06-23 final selector evidence. VM public spawn entrypoints and
  JIT GoStart static callbacks must consume the same function argument-shape
  fact as interpreter frame calls, so `param_slots`, GC-visible argument slots,
  and call argument length cannot drift by entrypoint. Interpreter/JIT red-team
  found `jit_go_start`'s static-function path and `Vm::spawn_call` each kept
  local frame-shape checks, splitting ownership from
  `validate_function_arg_shape`. Failing-first proofs
  `vm_jit_go_start_frame_shape_062_static_path_uses_shared_validator_before_spawn`
  and `spawn_call_frame_shape_062_uses_shared_validator_before_enqueue` cover
  both paths before spawn publication. The fix routes both entrypoints through
  the shared validator instead of duplicating param/local/GC checks. Focused
  validation passed the exact GoStart and spawn proofs plus the broader
  `go_start` and `spawn_call` filters; selected by `vm-production` through
  `cargo-test-vm-hardening` and `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`, final gates have
  not yet run for this source state.

- `VM-JIT-RESUME-POINT-PUBLICATION-062` is closed by the 2026-06-23 final selector evidence. JIT non-OK propagation must
  validate the side-exiting callee frame shape, encoded return-slot count, and
  caller return window before publishing a `ResumePoint` into
  `fiber.resume_stack`; materialization should not be the first owner of those
  facts after roots have already been exposed. Interpreter/frame red-team found
  `jit_push_resume_point` validated only integer width before pushing the
  resume point, leaving callee func id, callee frame shape, ret-slot metadata,
  and caller return-window drift to fail later in materialization. Failing-first
  proofs
  `vm_jit_resume_point_contract_062_rejects_missing_func_before_resume_publication`,
  `vm_jit_resume_point_contract_062_rejects_frame_shape_before_resume_publication`,
  `vm_jit_resume_point_contract_062_rejects_ret_slot_mismatch_before_resume_publication`,
  and
  `vm_jit_resume_point_contract_062_rejects_return_window_before_resume_publication`
  require `JitError` with `resume_stack` unchanged. The fix validates against
  `ctx.module` and `ctx.current_func_id` through the shared
  `validate_call_frame_shape` / `validate_call_return_window` helpers before
  `resume_stack.push`. The next GC/root and frame-call red-team pass found the
  generic JIT non-OK slow path restored caller `jit_bp` and `fiber_sp` before
  publishing the resume point but left `ctx.current_func_id` pointing at the
  callee, so the prepublication return-window validator could read stale caller
  metadata. Failing-first source-contract proof
  `vm_jit_resume_point_contract_062_non_ok_slow_path_restores_current_func_id_before_push_resume`
  covers the ordering. The fix passes the saved caller function id into
  `NonOkSlowPathParams` and routes the slow path through the shared
  `restore_caller_execution_context` helper before the push-resume callsite.
  Focused validation passed
  `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-main cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_jit_resume_point_contract_062 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`
  (4 tests), the broader `resume_point` filter (6 tests),
  `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-main cargo test -p vo-jit vm_jit_resume_point_contract_062 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`,
  and
  `... cargo test -p vo-jit vm_jit_current_func_metadata_037 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`
  (4 tests); selected by `vm-production` through
  `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit` and `cargo-test-jit`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence.

- `VM-JIT-MATERIALIZE-BORROWED-RESTORE-PREFLIGHT-062` is closed by the 2026-06-23 final selector evidence. JIT frame
  materialization must validate borrowed-frame restore metadata and caller-zero
  ranges before publishing materialized frames, advancing `sp`, rewriting
  caller `pc`, or clearing `resume_stack`. GC/root red-team found the old
  `materialized_jit_frame_invariants` checked `caller_scan_slots_restore` only
  after non-empty resume publication, and zero-resume plus prepared-call
  empty-resume paths skipped that invariant entirely. Failing-first proofs
  `vm_jit_materialize_zero_resume_borrowed_restore_failure_is_transactional_062`,
  `vm_jit_materialize_resume_stack_borrowed_restore_failure_is_transactional_062`,
  and
  `vm_jit_prepared_call_empty_resume_existing_frame_scan_failure_is_transactional_062`
  reproduced the Ok path and post-error mutation. The fix extracts one
  materialized-frame invariant validator over candidate frames and future `sp`,
  runs it before empty/non-empty materialization commits, and makes prepared
  empty-resume calls preflight existing materialized frames before caller-PC or
  callee-frame publication. Focused validation passed the three exact proofs
  and `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit materialize -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`
  (41 tests); selected by `vm-production` through
  `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence.

- `VM-JIT-MATERIALIZE-RESTORE-SP-SCAN-EXTENT-062` is closed by the 2026-06-23 final selector evidence. A materialized child
  frame's `sp_restore` must be at or above the caller scan extent that will be
  restored after that child frame pops; current `fiber.sp` is not the right
  authority because borrowed parent frames may legitimately restore above the
  innermost frame's current `sp`. GC/root red-team found prepared-call
  empty-resume and resume-stack materialization could publish a child whose
  `sp_restore` was below the parent's scanned roots while the final stack
  extent still looked valid. Failing-first proofs
  `vm_jit_prepared_call_empty_resume_rejects_restore_sp_below_parent_scan_extent_062`
  and
  `vm_jit_materialize_resume_stack_rejects_restore_sp_below_caller_scan_extent_062`
  reproduce both entrypoints transactionally. The fix extends the shared
  materialized-frame invariant to compare each child restore point against the
  parent scan slots that will be visible after pop, while preserving the
  existing borrowed-parent-above-current-sp proof. Focused validation passed
  the exact `restore_sp_below` proofs and the full
  `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit materialize -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`
  filter (41 tests); selected by `vm-production` through
  `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence.

- `VM-SOURCE-CONTRACT-RAW-INCLUDE-ALIAS-062` is closed by the 2026-06-23 final selector evidence. The shared production source
  tree must treat Rust raw identifiers as the same include macro authority for
  `std` / `core` aliases and path-qualified builtin include calls. The GC/source
  red-team found `use r#std::r#include as r#jit_include; r#jit_include!(...)`
  and `std::r#include!(...)` under a local bare `include!` shadow could be
  missed by include graph construction. Failing-first proofs
  `source_contract_production_tree_062_scans_raw_identifier_include_aliases`
  and
  `source_contract_production_tree_062_scans_raw_path_qualified_include_under_shadow`
  cover both bypasses. The fix normalizes raw identifier prefixes in include
  alias discovery and reuses the shared Rust identifier token reader when
  scanning include invocations, keeping alias names, shadow checks, and
  path-qualified builtin calls on one fact source. Focused validation passed
  full `vo-source-contract` (74 tests); selected by `vm-production` through
  `cargo-test-vo-source-contract`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence.

- `VM-JIT-CONTEXT-RELATIVE-TYPE-ALIAS-062` is closed by the 2026-06-23 final selector evidence. JIT ABI offset manifest coverage
  must canonicalize `super::`, `crate::`, and `self::` type-alias targets that
  resolve to known `JitContext` qualifiers, including cross-file parent
  re-export aliases. The JIT/ABI red-team found `type SuperCtx =
  super::RuntimeCtx; SuperCtx::OFFSET_*` could evade the fixed-point qualifier
  set even after `RuntimeCtx` was known. Failing-first proof extends
  `runtime_abi_offset_scanner_covers_cross_file_jit_context_aliases_062`; the
  fix normalizes relative type-alias targets against the current known
  qualifier set before deciding whether to add the alias. Follow-up red-team
  found the broad fixture could be masked by earlier imports, so isolated
  fixtures now prove `use vo_runtime::{jit_api::{self}}; type Alias =
  jit_api::JitContext` and mixed nested `self as api, JitContext as Ctx` forms
  without any prior `jit_api` import. The fix compacts each use statement and
  derives bare, grouped, nested `self`, and late grouped `jit_api` module
  aliases from one statement-local fact source. The latest JIT/ABI red-team
  found comment trivia inside module paths and type-alias paths, such as
  `jit_api /* ... */ ::{self as api}` and
  `type Ctx = api /* ... */ :: JitContext`, could still bypass alias discovery
  even though offset scanning itself used the shared comment-aware source
  compactor. The fix now feeds use/type alias statements through the same
  source-contract compactor and teaches alias parsing the compact `as` form
  instead of maintaining a whitespace-only view. The latest JIT/ABI red-team
  found two remaining scanner forks: compact alias splitting cut inside alias
  names such as `BaseCtxasLocalCtx`, and raw line-ending checks dropped
  `type Ctx = api::JitContext; // trailing comment` before compaction could see
  the semicolon. The fix now recognizes compact `as` only when the left-hand
  side resolves to a known qualifier, and statement completion uses the shared
  compacted source view. The next JIT/ABI red-team pass found `self as` module
  aliases later in a braced import and same-line leading attributes/comments
  before `use` / `type` statements could still hide alias facts. The fix strips
  leading outer attributes from compact statements, detects statement starts
  after comment compaction, and collects `self as` items anywhere inside the
  `jit_api::{...}` body. The next JIT/ABI red-team pass found multiline outer
  attributes that close on the same line as the `use` or `type` item still
  defeated line-local statement starts. The fix now accumulates leading outer
  attributes into a logical Rust statement before applying the same compact
  start predicate to both `use` and `type` aliases. The next JIT/ABI red-team
  pass found root-grouped nested imports with a sibling before `jit_api`, such
  as `use vo_runtime::{gc, jit_api::{JitContext as Ctx}}`, were not handled by
  the prefix-only nested import parser. The fix now scans any `jit_api::{...}`
  body inside the compact use rest and routes it through the same braced
  `JitContext` alias collector. The next pass found the sibling root-group path
  item spelling `use vo_runtime::{gc, jit_api::JitContext as Ctx}` was the same
  ABI authority. The fix now also collects `jit_api::JitContext as ...` aliases
  anywhere inside the compact use rest, including raw-identifier
  `r#JitContext` path segments. Focused validation passed
  the full
  `runtime_abi` filter (4 tests), including the alias scanner, cross-file alias
  scanner, comment/string erasure, and manifest coverage proofs; selected by
  `vm-production` through `jit-contract`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence.

- `VM-JIT-CALLBACK-SOURCE-SET-ROOT-NESTED-062` is closed by the 2026-06-23 final selector evidence. JIT callback source-set
  construction must follow callback helper modules reached through
  root-grouped nested `crate::{vm::{jit::callbacks...}}` use trees and default
  child modules under path-attributed `mod.rs` implementations. The JIT
  source-set red-team found both forms could hide scheduler-mutating callback
  helpers outside the callback boundary scan. Failing-first proofs extend
  `vm_jit_callback_boundary_001_source_set_scans_sibling_helper_modules_062`
  and `vm_jit_callback_boundary_001_source_set_scans_nested_reexport_modules_062`.
  The fix routes root-grouped nested use trees through the existing nested
  callback-module extractor and treats `mod.rs` as a module root whose default
  child modules live in its parent directory. Focused validation passed
  `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_jit_callback_boundary_001 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`
  (20 tests); selected by `vm-production` through
  `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence.

- `VM-JIT-MATERIALIZE-ZERO-RESUME-FRAME-SHAPE-062` is closed by the 2026-06-23 final selector evidence. JIT frame
  materialization must validate the existing materialized frame shape even when
  `resume_stack` is empty before advancing `pc` / `fiber.sp` for VM takeover.
  The frame-call red-team found the empty-resume branch skipped the shared
  `validate_call_frame_shape` fact while the non-empty branch now consumed it.
  The next pass found the same branch also skipped the existing-frame scan
  extent preflight, so a current frame with `scan_slots` beyond the function's
  `gc_scan_slots` could still commit a new `pc` / `sp`.
  Failing-first proof
  `vm_jit_materialize_zero_resume_existing_frame_shape_062_rejects_param_slots_beyond_locals_transactionally`
  showed the old path returned `Ok(())` and committed state; follow-up proof
  `vm_jit_materialize_zero_resume_entry_scan_extent_failure_is_transactional_062`
  covers the scan-extent variant. The fix validates the entry frame shape and
  routes empty-resume takeover through `preflight_existing_frame_scan_extents`
  before ensuring capacity or publishing `pc` / `sp`. Focused validation passed
  both exact proofs, the `frame_shape_062` aggregate, and the focused scan
  extent proof;
  selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`, final
  gates have not yet run for this source state.

- `VM-SCHED-MODULE-ALIAS-TYPE-DESTRUCTURE-062` is closed by the 2026-06-23 final selector evidence. Scheduler source-contract scans
  must treat type aliases whose target is a local module alias of
  `crate::scheduler` as aliases of `Scheduler`, so free helpers cannot mutate
  `ready_queue` through destructuring outside inherent `impl Scheduler`.
  Runtime-boundary red-team found the first proof used `ModuleAliasS`, which
  was accidentally covered by the shorter alias `S{`; the isolated
  `RunQueueOwner = sched::Scheduler` proof failed first. The next pass added
  the grouped self-module alias form `use crate::scheduler::{self as
  grouped_sched}; type GroupedRunQueueOwner = grouped_sched::Scheduler;`. The
  follow-up late-position grouped variants were filtered as already covered by
  the compact use-tree scanner and remain in the proof fixture. The fix derives
  scheduler module aliases once, including direct/grouped raw and grouped
  `self as` forms, and lets type-alias discovery consume that fact source. The
  latest runtime-boundary red-team found alias-to-alias chains and late
  root-grouped module aliases could still fork the fact source:
  `type BaseRunQueueSource = Scheduler; type ChainedRunQueueOwner =
  BaseRunQueueSource` and `use crate::{other, scheduler::{self as
  hidden_sched}}; type HiddenRunQueueHolder = hidden_sched::Scheduler`. The
  fix computes scheduler type aliases to a fixed point and scans each grouped
  use statement for embedded scheduler module aliases instead of trusting
  suffix-shaped names. The latest runtime-boundary red-team found the same
  ownership facts could still be hidden behind `self::hidden_sched::Scheduler`
  targets and split scoped visibility such as `pub/**/(crate) mod spoof`, which
  avoided nested-module owner-spoof detection. The fix normalizes
  `self::`/`super::` before module-alias matching and strips `pub(...)` scope
  tokens after trivia before testing for nested `mod` declarations. The next
  runtime-boundary red-team pass found `crate::r#scheduler::Scheduler` raw
  module segments and module-alias-to-module-alias chains such as
  `use self::hidden_sched as hidden_sched_realias; type Owner =
  hidden_sched_realias::Scheduler` still forked the alias fact source. The fix
  treats raw module segments as canonical scheduler targets and computes
  scheduler module aliases to a fixed point before type-alias discovery
  consumes them. The next runtime-boundary red-team pass found parenthesized
  type-alias targets such as `type Owner = (crate::scheduler::Scheduler);` and
  multi-level relative targets such as `super::super::Scheduler` were valid Rust
  spellings but not normalized by the alias scanner. The fix now strips
  enclosing parentheses and loops over `self::` / `super::` prefixes before
  matching scheduler aliases. The next runtime-boundary red-team pass found
  relative module paths such as `super::scheduler::Scheduler` and type aliases
  with pre-`=` `where` clauses were still valid ways to spell the same owner
  type. The fix recognizes relative `scheduler::Scheduler` / raw-module targets
  after prefix normalization and parses type alias targets from the first `=`,
  including compacted `where` constraints. The next scheduler red-team pass
  found imports rooted through an already-known module alias, such as
  `use self::sched::Scheduler as HiddenS` and
  `use self::sched::{self as hidden_mod}`, could still hide owner facts. The
  fix derives Scheduler import aliases and grouped `self as` module aliases from
  the scheduler module-alias fixed point rather than only literal
  `scheduler::...` paths. The next scheduler red-team pass found non-leading
  grouped `Scheduler as ...` items and nested grouped `self::{sched::{self as
  hidden}}` forms were still valid module-alias-rooted spellings. The existing
  fixed-point collector now covers those proof fixtures through the same module
  alias fact source.
  Focused validation passed
  `cargo test -p vo-vm vm_scheduler_wake_boundary_013_scanner_rejects_scheduler_file_free_helpers_062 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`
  and the full `vm_scheduler_wake_boundary_013` set (8 tests); selected by
  `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence.

- `VM-JIT-CALLBACK-PARENT-ALIAS-RAW-POINTER-062` is closed by the 2026-06-23 final selector evidence. JIT callback
  boundary scans must include helpers reached through aliases of parent
  modules such as `crate::vm::jit as jit_mod` or `crate::vm as vm_mod`, and
  must reject second-order raw field pointers such as
  `addr_of_mut!((*raw_sched).ready_queue)` before mutation can hide behind a
  pointer variable without a field name. Follow-up red-team found bare parent
  imports (`use crate::vm::jit; jit::callbacks::...`), grouped bare parent
  imports (`use crate::vm::{jit, types}; jit::callbacks::...`), late
  root-grouped parent imports (`use crate::{types, vm::{jit as late_jit}}`),
  comment-trivia nested use trees, `use core::ptr::addr_of_mut as aom;
  aom!((*raw_sched).field)`, `r#addr_of_mut!((*raw_sched).field)`, `use
  core::ptr::{other, addr_of_mut as grouped_aom}`, raw aliases such as `as
  r#aom`, `&raw mut (*raw_sched).field`, parenthesized raw places such as
  `addr_of_mut!(((*raw_sched)).field)` / `&raw mut ((*raw_sched)).field`, and
  mutator methods such as `append`, `truncate`, and `drain` were the same
  boundary bypass class. The latest JIT callback red-team found immutable raw
  field pointers were the same scheduler-field escape hatch: `addr_of!` aliases
  and `&raw const ((*raw_sched)).field as *mut _` could hand a later mutation
  the same field pointer without matching the mut-only source pattern. The next
  JIT callback red-team found shared-reference field pointers cast through
  `&(*raw_sched).ready_queue as *const _ as *mut _` and
  `core::ptr::from_ref(&((*raw_sched)).fibers) as *mut _` were the same field
  pointer escape.
  Failing-first proofs extend
  `vm_jit_callback_boundary_001_source_set_scans_sibling_helper_modules_062`
  and add
  `vm_jit_callback_boundary_001_scanner_rejects_second_order_addr_of_mut_field_aliases_062`
  plus
  `vm_jit_callback_boundary_001_scanner_rejects_raw_pointer_field_mutator_method_variants_062`
  and
  `vm_jit_callback_boundary_001_scanner_rejects_parenthesized_raw_field_places_062`,
  plus
  `vm_jit_callback_boundary_001_source_set_scans_late_root_grouped_parent_modules_062`
  and
  `vm_jit_callback_boundary_001_scanner_rejects_raw_const_field_pointer_casts_062`.
  The fix derives bare, aliased, and grouped callback parent-module prefixes
  from the same grouped use-tree item fact source, feeds them into the same
  helper module source-set fact source, uses the shared comment-aware Rust
  compactor for nested callback use trees, derives `addr_of_mut` macro names
  from default/raw/import-alias spellings with token-boundary matching, keeps
  raw aliases intact, and scans `addr_of_mut!`, `addr_of!`, `&raw mut`, and
  `&raw const`, shared-reference casts, and `ptr::from_ref` field-pointer leaks,
  their parenthesized raw-place variants,
  plus expanded raw-field mutator methods for scheduler-sensitive fields. The
  next JIT callback red-team pass found aliased and turbofish `from_ref`
  calls, such as `use core::ptr::from_ref as fr; fr(&field) as *mut _` and
  `core::ptr::from_ref::<T>(&field) as *mut _`, still bypassed the same field
  pointer contract. The fix derives `ptr::from_ref` call heads, import aliases,
  and unaliased imports into one function-name fact source and parses both
  ordinary and turbofish calls before checking the mutable pointer cast. The
  next callback red-team pass found grouped unaliased imports such as
  `use core::ptr::{self, from_ref}; from_ref(&field) as *mut _` and raw-pointer
  method casts such as `ptr::from_ref(&field).cast_mut()` were still the same
  field-pointer escape. The fix treats any `core::ptr` / `std::ptr` grouped
  import containing `from_ref` as a bare `from_ref` fact and accepts `.cast_mut()`
  and `.cast::<T>() as *mut T` as mutable pointer casts. The next callback
  red-team pass found parenthesized receivers and cast chains such as
  `(ptr::from_ref(&field)).cast_mut()` and
  `ptr::from_ref(&field).cast::<T>().cast_mut()` were the same bypass class.
  The fix skips wrapping parentheses before cast recognition and accepts
  `.cast::<T>().cast_mut()` as a mutable pointer cast. The next callback
  red-team pass found root module aliases such as `use core::ptr as p;
  p::from_ref(&field).cast_mut()`, parenthesized intermediate casts, and
  two-step `let raw = ptr::from_ref(&field); raw.cast_mut()` were still raw
  scheduler-field pointer escapes. The fix derives `core::ptr` / `std::ptr`
  module aliases into `alias::from_ref` call heads and tightens the source
  contract to reject any `from_ref(&scheduler.field)` escape, independent of
  whether the mutable cast is chained in the same expression. The next callback
  red-team pass found `ptr::{self as alias}` module aliases, local function-item
  aliases like `let fr = core::ptr::from_ref::<T>; fr(&field)`, and
  parenthesized callees `(ptr::from_ref)(&field)` were still the same call-head
  discovery class. The fix now derives braced `ptr::{self as ...}` aliases,
  local `from_ref` function-item aliases to a fixed point, and skips
  parenthesized callee wrappers when locating a call.
  Focused validation passed the exact source-set proof, the second-order
  raw-pointer proof, the mutator-method proof, and the full 20-test callback
  boundary gate;
  selected
  by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`, final gates have
  not yet run for this source state.

- `VM-JIT-CONTEXT-ABSOLUTE-TYPE-ALIAS-062` is closed by the 2026-06-23 final selector evidence. JIT ABI offset manifest coverage
  must treat a leading-absolute Rust path as the same `JitContext` type source as
  `vo_runtime::jit_api::JitContext`, even when no local `jit_api` module alias is
  present to mask the gap. The Round 9 JIT/ABI red-team found
  `type AbsCtx = ::vo_runtime::jit_api::JitContext; AbsCtx::OFFSET_*` was missed
  in an isolated fixture. Failing-first proof extends
  `runtime_abi_offset_scanner_covers_jit_context_aliases_062` with an isolated
  absolute-path type alias. The fix strips a leading `::` before the existing
  `vo_runtime::jit_api::` canonicalization, keeping absolute and relative
  spellings on one alias fact source. Focused validation passed
  `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-main cargo test -p vo-jit runtime_abi -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`
  (4 tests); selected by `vm-production` through `jit-contract`, final gates have
  not yet run for this source state.

- `VM-JIT-CALLBACK-TYPED-RAW-POINTER-062` is closed by the 2026-06-23 final selector evidence. JIT callback boundary scans must
  reject raw scheduler-field pointer escapes even when Rust type inference or a
  local type annotation performs the raw-pointer coercion instead of an immediate
  `as *mut` cast. The Round 9 JIT/ABI red-team found
  `let p: *const Vec<_> = unsafe { &((*raw_sched)).fibers }; p.cast_mut()` could
  evade the shared-reference field-pointer proof. Failing-first proof extends
  `vm_jit_callback_boundary_001_scanner_rejects_raw_const_field_pointer_casts_062`
  with a typed raw-pointer coercion. The fix attributes `&scheduler.field`
  expressions back to the enclosing `let _: *const` / `let _: *mut` declaration
  before applying the existing scheduler-field escape rejection. Focused
  validation passed the exact proof and the full
  `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-main cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_jit_callback_boundary_001 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`
  gate (20 tests); selected by `vm-production` through
  `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence.

- `VM-JIT-CALLBACK-SCHEDULER-DESTRUCTURE-062` is closed by the 2026-06-23 final selector evidence. JIT callbacks must not bypass
  `RuntimeTransition` publication by taking a raw pointer to the whole scheduler,
  destructuring `Scheduler { ready_queue, .. }`, and mutating the destructured
  field alias. The Round 9 runtime-boundary red-team found that this path avoided
  both direct `.scheduler.ready_queue` detection and raw field-place detection.
  Failing-first proof extends
  `vm_jit_callback_boundary_001_scanner_rejects_raw_const_field_pointer_casts_062`
  with a whole-scheduler destructuring probe. The fix derives destructured
  aliases for scheduler-sensitive fields from `Scheduler { ... }` patterns and
  feeds those aliases back into the same field-mutator detector used for direct
  scheduler field references. Focused validation passed the exact proof and the
  full 20-test JIT callback boundary gate; selected by `vm-production` through
  `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence.

- `VM-JIT-SHADOW-CAPACITY-ROOTS-062` is closed by the 2026-06-23 final selector evidence. A JIT materialization capacity failure may
  enter the recoverable stack-overflow panic path only when all live JIT frames
  are materialized and therefore visible to the precise GC root scanner. The
  Round 9 GC/root red-team found `frame_materialize_exec_result` and the OSR
  adapter mapped `JitFrameMaterializeError::Capacity` to recoverable panic even
  when `fiber.resume_stack` still held unmaterialized shadow frames; GC scans
  `fiber.frames`, not `resume_stack`. Failing-first behavior proofs
  `vm_jit_materialize_capacity_with_shadow_frames_is_fatal_before_recoverable_panic_062`
  and
  `vm_osr_materialize_capacity_with_shadow_frames_is_fatal_before_recoverable_panic_062`
  reproduced `Panic` before the fix. The fix makes capacity failures with a
  non-empty `resume_stack` fatal `JitError`s with the shadow frames preserved,
  while retaining recoverable stack overflow only after roots are materialized.
  The Round 12 GC/root red-team found the same root-safety contract applied
  before a resume point is published: `jit_push_resume_point` could fail
  `try_reserve_call_frames` before `resume_stack.push` and return recoverable
  `Panic`, even though the current JIT frame was still not a VM-scannable root.
  Failing-first proof
  `vm_jit_shadow_capacity_roots_062_push_resume_point_capacity_before_publication_is_fatal`
  covers this producer-side capacity failure. The fix reports an infra
  `JitError` before publication rather than recording a recoverable
  stack-overflow panic. The Round 13 GC/root red-team found the same
  pre-publication root-safety issue one step earlier: `jit_push_frame` could
  fail stack reservation before any resume point or VM frame was published, and
  the non-OK slow path then converted the recorded runtime trap into
  recoverable `Panic`. Failing-first proofs
  `vm_jit_shadow_capacity_roots_062_push_frame_capacity_before_publication_is_fatal`
  and
  `vm_jit_shadow_capacity_roots_062_non_ok_push_frame_failure_is_fatal_before_resume_publication`
  cover the callback and lowering sides. The fix makes `jit_push_frame` capacity
  failure write an infra sentinel and return a null args pointer, makes non-OK
  lowering return `JitError` immediately on that null pointer before argument
  copy or resume publication, and removes the now-unused runtime-trap-to-panic
  helper from `vo-jit`. The Round 14 interpreter/frame red-team found
  `jit_prepare_closure_call` and `jit_prepare_iface_call` still classified a
  null `push_frame_fn` result as recoverable `Panic`, even when the callback had
  already set the infra sentinel for a pre-publication capacity failure.
  Failing-first proofs
  `vm_jit_shadow_capacity_roots_062_prepare_closure_null_push_frame_is_fatal`
  and
  `vm_jit_shadow_capacity_roots_062_prepare_iface_null_push_frame_is_fatal`
  cover valid prepared dynamic-call targets whose frame publication fails
  before any callee frame or resume point is visible to root scanning. The fix
  routes prepared callback null callee-args results through one
  fatal-infra helper, preserving an existing sentinel or writing
  `JIT_INFRA_ERROR_INVALID_CALLBACK_STATE` when the callback failed silently.
  Focused validation passed `bridge_result` (9 tests) and the full
  `materialize` filter (43 tests), plus
  `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-main cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_jit_shadow_capacity_roots_062 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`
  (4 tests)
  and
  `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-main cargo test -p vo-jit vm_jit_shadow_capacity_roots_062 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`;
  selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit` for the
  `vo-vm` proofs and through `jit-contract` / `cargo-test-jit` for the `vo-jit`
  source-contract proof. Final gates have not yet run for this source state.

- `VM-JIT-CONTEXT-RAW-ABSOLUTE-PRECISION-062` is closed by the 2026-06-23 final selector evidence. JIT ABI offset scans must
  canonicalize raw `r#JitContext` type-alias targets while rejecting unrelated
  local modules named `jit_api` that are not rooted in `vo_runtime`. The Round 10
  JIT/ABI red-team found `type Ctx = ::vo_runtime::jit_api::r#JitContext` was
  missed, and `use crate::{foo, jit_api, bar}; type Ctx = jit_api::JitContext`
  could falsely seed a runtime ABI alias. Failing-first proofs extend
  `runtime_abi_offset_scanner_covers_jit_context_aliases_062` with an isolated
  raw absolute target and add
  `runtime_abi_offset_scanner_ignores_unrelated_grouped_jit_api_imports_062`.
  The fix normalizes raw `JitContext` path segments after leading-absolute
  canonicalization and scopes unaliased `jit_api` module imports to
  `vo_runtime::jit_api` or `vo_runtime::{...}` use trees. Focused validation
  passed the full `runtime_abi` filter (5 tests); selected by `vm-production`
  through `jit-contract`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence.

- `VM-JIT-CALLBACK-DESTRUCTURE-POINTER-VARIANTS-062` is closed by the 2026-06-23 final selector evidence. JIT callback scheduler
  mutation scans must treat split raw-pointer declarations, raw-pointer type
  aliases, `ref` / `ref mut` destructuring, renamed destructuring, raw local
  aliases, and UFCS first-argument mutations as the same scheduler ownership
  bypass. The Round 10 runtime-boundary red-team found each form could evade the
  initial typed-pointer/destructure collector while still mutating
  `Scheduler::fibers` or `Scheduler::ready_queue` directly from a callback.
  Failing-first proof extends
  `vm_jit_callback_boundary_001_scanner_rejects_raw_const_field_pointer_casts_062`
  with split assignment, pointer type alias, `ref mut`, renamed `ref mut`, raw
  alias, and `VecDeque::push_back(ready_queue, fid)` probes. The fix derives raw
  pointer type aliases and declared raw-pointer bindings as scanner facts,
  attributes `&scheduler.field` assignments to those bindings, preserves raw
  destructured alias tokens, strips pattern binding prefixes before field/alias
  ownership checks, and detects UFCS mutator calls whose first argument is a
  destructured scheduler-field alias. Focused validation passed the exact proof
  and the full 20-test JIT callback boundary gate; selected by `vm-production`
  through `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence.

- `VM-JIT-CONTEXT-ABSOLUTE-USE-RAW-ROOT-PRECISION-062` is closed by the 2026-06-23 final selector evidence. JIT ABI offset scans
  must canonicalize the same runtime `JitContext` fact through leading-absolute
  imports, raw root segments, and raw `JitContext` identifiers while rejecting
  local relative paths that merely end in `JitContext`. The Round 11 JIT/ABI
  red-team found `use ::vo_runtime::jit_api::JitContext as Ctx`,
  `use ::vo_runtime::jit_api as api; type Ctx = api::JitContext`,
  `type Ctx = r#vo_runtime::jit_api::JitContext`, and
  `type Ctx = ::r#vo_runtime::jit_api::r#JitContext` could fork the scanner
  fact source, while `type LocalCtx = crate::local::JitContext` was a false
  positive. Failing-first proofs extend
  `runtime_abi_offset_scanner_covers_jit_context_aliases_062` and add
  `runtime_abi_offset_scanner_ignores_unrelated_relative_jitcontext_paths_062`.
  The Round 12 JIT/ABI red-team found raw-qualified offset uses such as
  `r#JitContext::OFFSET_*`, raw alias declarations referenced without `r#`,
  and nested `jit_api::{helpers::{...}, JitContext as ...}` / `self as ...`
  imports after an inner use tree still forked the same fact source. The fix
  normalizes leading `::` and raw runtime path spellings before import and
  type-alias discovery, expands offset scans over raw/non-raw qualifier
  spellings, matches `jit_api::{...}` imports by brace depth, and stops reducing
  multi-segment relative paths to a last-segment `JitContext` match unless the
  full target is already known. The Round 13 JIT/ABI red-team found the
  tightened module-alias target normalization lost cross-source module alias
  propagation (`use super::api as local_api; type Ctx =
  local_api::JitContext`) and grouped root aliases after sibling imports (`use
  vo_runtime::{gc, self as rt}; type Ctx = rt::jit_api::JitContext`). The proof
  now also covers qualified associated-const offset forms, raw-qualified offset
  aliases, raw alias declarations referenced both with and without `r#`, and
  negative lookalikes such as `crate::local::api::JitContext` and
  `vo_runtime::{not_jit_api::{...}}`. The fix derives `jit_api` module aliases
  as a whole-source fixed point, propagates relative module aliases across
  parent/child source sets, and treats compact grouped `selfas` items as
  root-module aliases without restoring the old last-segment false positive.
  The Round 14 JIT/ABI red-team found the same cross-source module alias fact
  could still be hidden behind crate-qualified paths such as
  `type Ctx = crate::api::JitContext` after a parent
  `pub use vo_runtime::jit_api as api`. The cross-file proof now covers both
  direct `crate::api::JitContext` type targets and `use crate::api as
  local_api` reexports; the fix normalizes `crate::` module-alias targets
  through the same path as `self::` and `super::`.
  The Round 15 JIT/ABI red-team found three final scanner edges: associated
  const uses through qualified paths such as
  `<vo_runtime::jit_api::JitContext>::OFFSET_*` and `<crate::RuntimeCtx>::...`,
  root-grouped nested bare `self` imports such as
  `use vo_runtime::{jit_api::{helpers::{H}, self}}`, and a precision bug where
  `use crate::local::{api as local_api}` could be promoted by the relative
  alias fixed point. The proof now covers qualified direct runtime paths,
  qualified relative aliases, nested bare-self module imports, and the local
  grouped-alias lookalike. The fix adds one qualified spelling fact source,
  parses `jit_api::{...}` use bodies by brace depth for bare `self`, and
  restricts relative alias propagation to anchored direct or exact root-group
  imports.
  The Round 16 JIT/ABI red-team found three final scanner edges: offset uses
  through module-alias paths such as `<api::JitContext>::OFFSET_*`, false
  positives for unrelated local paths ending in `JitContext` such as
  `crate::local::JitContext::OFFSET_*` or
  `use crate::local::JitContext as LocalCtx`, and top-level braced imports such
  as `use {vo_runtime::jit_api::JitContext as RuntimeCtx}`. The proof now
  covers module-alias direct and qualified offset paths, top-level braced
  runtime imports, and negative local direct/imported `JitContext` lookalikes.
  The fix derives `alias::JitContext` offset qualifiers from the same
  `vo_runtime::jit_api` module-alias fact source, tightens bare qualifier
  boundaries so path suffixes cannot match by last segment alone, and requires
  relative import aliases to target an already-known qualifier without an
  unknown intermediate module path.
  The Round 17 JIT/ABI and GC/root red-teams found remaining alias propagation
  variants: `use api::JitContext as ApiCtx`, `use api::{JitContext as ApiCtx}`,
  `use vo_runtime as rt; use rt::jit_api::JitContext as Ctx`, raw module aliases
  referenced through their unraw spelling, local `crate::local::jit_api::{self
  as local_api}` lookalikes, and nested module re-exports such as
  `mod bridge { pub use vo_runtime::jit_api as api; } bridge::api::JitContext`.
  The proof now covers those positive and negative forms. The fix makes import
  alias parsing operate on any known `JitContext` qualifier, inserts unraw
  spellings for raw module aliases, limits broad nested `jit_api::{self as ...}`
  harvesting to `vo_runtime`-rooted paths, and records compact nested-module
  runtime re-export aliases with their module path.
  The Round 18 JIT/ABI and GC/root red-teams found the last module-alias
  projection variants: direct `crate::api::JitContext::OFFSET_*` /
  `self::api::JitContext::OFFSET_*` uses, root-alias braced imports such as
  `use rt::{jit_api as api}`, top-level braced relative module imports such as
  `use {super::api as local_api}`, out-of-line `mod bridge;` child re-exports,
  and nested parent-root re-exports such as
  `mod bridge { pub use super::rt::jit_api as api; }`. The proof extends
  `runtime_abi_offset_scanner_covers_jit_context_aliases_062` and
  `runtime_abi_offset_scanner_covers_cross_file_jit_context_aliases_062` for
  those forms. The fix keeps one module-alias fact source by adding
  path-qualified spellings for known module aliases, recursively expanding
  top-level braced use trees through the same relative-alias collector,
  projecting known aliases through out-of-line module declarations, and
  extracting nested parent aliases from compact inline module bodies.
  The Round 19 JIT/ABI and GC/root red-teams found visibility-qualified module
  declarations, nested out-of-line module paths, relative root-alias braced
  imports, and parent-file alias false positives were still split across
  scanner facts: `pub mod bridge;`, `pub(crate) mod bridge;`,
  `outer::bridge::api::JitContext`, `use super::rt::{jit_api as api}`, and a
  local `mod unrelated; unrelated::api::JitContext` lookalike. Failing-first
  proof extends the same scanner tests and adds
  `runtime_abi_offset_scanner_ignores_parent_alias_on_local_out_of_line_modules_062`.
  The fix parses compact module declarations through one visibility-aware
  helper, makes inline nested re-export discovery and relative alias expansion
  converge to a fixed point, projects out-of-line module aliases by source-local
  ownership instead of blindly attaching parent aliases to every module, and
  prevents recursive projection from repeatedly adding a module segment already
  present in the alias path.
  The Round 20 JIT/ABI and GC/root red-teams found the remaining source-set
  ownership split: alias projection was still path-blind enough that an
  unrelated out-of-line source could lend a parent alias to a local module, and
  nested inline `mod outer { mod bridge; }` children plus inline default
  `pub use vo_runtime::jit_api;` re-exports were not represented by the same
  module-path fact. The proof extends the cross-file scanner with
  `outer::bridge::api::JitContext`, a default `bridge::jit_api::JitContext`
  re-export, and an unrelated sibling-source lookalike. The fix replaces the
  flat source-list scanner with a source-tree scanner keyed by module path,
  derives production module paths from actual `src/**.rs` locations, qualifies
  only aliases owned by the source that declared them, and deletes the old flat
  `*_from_sources_062` helper path so the runtime ABI manifest proof has one
  source-tree fact owner.
  The Round 21 JIT/ABI and GC/root red-teams found the source-tree fix still
  applied one global qualifier set to every source, so a runtime `api` alias in
  one module could falsely collect a sibling module's local `api::JitContext`
  lookalike while true sibling-relative paths such as
  `super::foo::api::JitContext::OFFSET_*` were missed. Failing-first proof
  extends the cross-file scanner with the sibling-relative positive case and
  extends the local out-of-line negative case with sibling-local `api` and
  `local_api` lookalikes. The fix derives visible module aliases and
  `JitContext` qualifiers per module path instead of per source set, inserts
  `crate::`, `self::`, and exact `super::...` spellings from the source tree's
  relative path, and makes grouped relative imports validate their target after
  applying the enclosing `super::` / `crate::` / `self::` prefix.
  The Round 22 JIT/ABI red-team found three remaining source-tree precision
  edges: source-tree mode still seeded bare `JitContext` in every source,
  nested inline modules could be flattened from `outer::inner::api` to
  root-level `inner::api`, and scoped `pub(in crate)` inline re-exports with
  later body items were not covered. Failing-first proof extends the same
  scanner tests with a bare local `struct JitContext` negative, a flattened
  nested-inline lookalike negative, and a scoped inline re-export positive. The
  fix removes the source-tree default bare `JitContext` seed, derives bare
  `JitContext` only from explicit runtime imports/aliases, keeps module-alias
  type targets as full qualifiers instead of folding them to a bare name, and
  makes compact inline-module declaration discovery operate only at the current
  module-body depth.
  The Round 23 JIT/ABI and GC/root red-teams found that the Round 22 bare-seed
  fix also removed source-tree coverage for legal absolute runtime spellings:
  `vo_runtime::jit_api::JitContext::OFFSET_*`,
  `<vo_runtime::jit_api::JitContext>::OFFSET_*`, and type aliases to
  `::vo_runtime::jit_api::JitContext` could false-green the production manifest
  scan. Failing-first proof
  `runtime_abi_offset_scanner_source_tree_covers_direct_runtime_jit_context_paths_062`
  initially returned an empty offset set. The fix seeds only the canonical
  absolute runtime `JitContext` path as a per-source qualifier, normalizes
  direct runtime type-alias targets to that canonical path, and derives raw and
  leading-`::` spellings from one helper without restoring global bare
  `JitContext` visibility or source-tree alias propagation.
  Focused validation passed
  `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-main cargo test -p vo-jit runtime_abi -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`
  (9 tests); selected by `vm-production` through `jit-contract`, final gates
  have not yet run for this source state.

- `VM-JIT-CALLBACK-GENERIC-ALIAS-TURBOFISH-062` is closed by the 2026-06-23 final selector evidence. JIT callback boundary
  scans must treat generic raw-pointer aliases, Scheduler import/type aliases,
  and turbofish mutator calls as the same scheduler ownership bypass as direct
  raw field pointers or direct `Scheduler { ready_queue, .. }` destructuring.
  The Round 11 runtime-boundary red-team found
  `type ConstPtr<T> = *const T; let p: ConstPtr<Vec<Fiber>> = &scheduler.fibers`,
  `use crate::scheduler::Scheduler as ImportS; let ImportS { ready_queue, .. }`,
  `type TypeS = crate::scheduler::Scheduler; let TypeS { ready_queue, .. }`,
  and `ready_queue.extend::<...>(...)` / `VecDeque::extend::<...>(ready_queue, ...)`
  could evade the callback mutator contract. Failing-first proof extends
  `vm_jit_callback_boundary_001_scanner_rejects_raw_const_field_pointer_casts_062`
  with isolated generic-pointer and destructuring probes. The follow-up local
  red-team pass found the aggregate destructuring count could hide raw-module,
  root-grouped, and module-alias Scheduler imports, and compacted source erased
  the space in `let ImportS { ... }` so the old token boundary skipped imported
  pattern names. The proof now includes isolated raw import, grouped import,
  raw type, module-alias type, and grouped-module-alias type destructuring
  probes. The Round 12 runtime-boundary and GC/root red-teams then found direct
  raw borrows such as `&raw mut vm.scheduler.ready_queue`, alias-to-alias
  transfers like `let rq = ready_queue; rq.push_back(...)`, free-function
  `std::mem::take/replace(ready_queue, ...)`, and queue order mutators such as
  `swap` / `rotate_left` / UFCS `rotate_right` were still the same callback
  boundary bypass. The fix makes raw pointer aliases match their generic base
  identifier, derives Scheduler module/import/type aliases to a fixed point,
  treats direct `.scheduler.<field>` raw borrows as scheduler-field pointer
  escapes, propagates destructured aliases through local alias transfers, lets
  the shared method/UFCS mutator parser skip turbofish arguments before checking
  the call, recognizes mutating `mem` free functions and queue order mutators,
  and treats compact `let` / `if let` / `while let` keyword prefixes as valid
  struct-pattern boundaries. The Round 13 runtime-boundary red-team found the
  same facts still needed fixed-point and normalization: parenthesized raw
  pointer aliases such as `type ConstPtr<T> = (*const T)`, alias-of-alias raw
  pointer typedefs declared before their base alias, typed destructured alias
  transfers (`let rq: &mut VecDeque<_> = ready_queue`), `&mut *ready_queue`
  transfers, free `std::mem::swap(ready_queue, ...)`, and mutable queue APIs
  `front_mut`, `back_mut`, `retain_mut`, and `make_contiguous`. The proof now
  includes isolated fixtures for those forms. The fix normalizes outer
  parentheses in pointer type targets, derives raw pointer type aliases to a
  fixed point, normalizes destructured alias-transfer RHS references, and keeps
  mutable queue accessors in the same mutator fact list as direct queue writes.
  The Round 14 runtime-boundary red-team found the remaining variants were the
  same ownership bypass behind item-prefix and argument-shape trivia:
  visibility- or attribute-prefixed raw-pointer type aliases, destructured
  alias transfers through parenthesized or single-tuple bindings, `mem::swap`
  with the scheduler alias in the second argument, turbofish and raw-identifier
  `swap`, and `mem::replace(&mut *ready_queue, ...)`. The proof now includes
  isolated fixtures for each form. The fix strips item attrs/visibility before
  raw-pointer typedef discovery, normalizes single binding/RHS tuple wrappers
  in alias transfers, and parses `mem::{take,replace,swap}` top-level argument
  positions instead of assuming the alias immediately follows the call open.
  The Round 15 runtime-boundary red-team found four final spelling families
  behind the same callback-boundary bypass: split shared-reference aliases later
  cast to mutable raw pointers, `NonNull::from(&scheduler_field).as_ptr()`,
  visibility- or attribute-prefixed `Scheduler` type aliases in destructuring
  patterns, pattern-wrapped destructured field aliases such as `rq @ _` and
  `(rq)`, and local function-item aliases for mutators such as
  `let push = VecDeque::push_back` or
  `let sw = std::mem::swap::<VecDeque<FiberId>>`. The proof now includes
  isolated fixtures for each form in
  `vm_jit_callback_boundary_001_scanner_rejects_raw_const_field_pointer_casts_062`
  and the callback boundary scanner. The fix keeps the facts unified by
  allowing compact `alias as *const ... as *mut ...` spelling in
  shared-reference alias casts, recognizing `NonNull::from` as the same
  pointer-from-reference source, stripping item prefixes before `Scheduler`
  type-alias discovery, normalizing pattern wrappers before destructured alias
  capture, and routing local mutator function-item aliases through the same
  top-level call-argument mutation checker used by free `mem` calls.
  The Round 16 runtime-boundary red-team found sibling provenance and mutable
  view escapes behind the same callback-boundary bypass: shared-reference field
  aliases copied to another alias, copied into typed raw-pointer bindings, or
  passed to `NonNull::from(alias).as_ptr()`, destructured `ready_queue` aliases
  converted to raw pointers with `as *mut` or `core::ptr::from_mut`, and
  mutable-view queue APIs such as `iter_mut`, `as_mut_slices`, and `range_mut`.
  The proof now includes isolated fixtures for those forms. The fix derives
  shared-reference field aliases to a local fixed point, treats alias-to-raw and
  pointer-from-reference conversions as scheduler-field authority escapes,
  permits compact `aliasas*mut` matching for destructured aliases, and keeps
  mutable-view APIs in the same mutator fact source as direct queue writes.
  The Round 17 runtime-boundary and GC/root red-teams found remaining
  provenance-chain variants: destructured aliases copied into typed raw-pointer
  bindings or passed to `NonNull::from(alias).as_ptr()`, shared-reference aliases
  copied through raw-pointer alias chains before `cast_mut`, Vec/slice mutable
  views such as `fibers.first_mut`, `fibers.as_mut_slice`, and
  `free_slots.split_at_mut`, local function-item alias chains such as
  `let pb = VecDeque::push_back; let call = pb`, and `core`/`std` root aliases
  feeding pointer macro imports. The proof now includes isolated fixtures for
  those forms, including
  `vm_jit_callback_boundary_001_scanner_rejects_core_std_root_alias_pointer_imports_062`.
  The fix expands raw-pointer and function-item aliases to a local fixed point,
  treats destructured alias raw binding and pointer-from-reference conversions
  as authority escapes, keeps Vec/slice mutable-view APIs in the mutator method
  fact source, and derives pointer macro aliases through root `core`/`std`
  module aliases.
  The Round 18 runtime-boundary and GC/root red-teams found the same bypass
  behind block-prefixed `let` statements, imported `mem` aliases, root
  `core`/`std` aliases for `ptr::from_ref` / `ptr::NonNull::from`, and
  additional Vec/slice mutable-view APIs. Failing-first proof extended
  `vm_jit_callback_boundary_001_scanner_rejects_raw_const_field_pointer_casts_062`
  with `unsafe { let raw_ready_ptr: *mut ... = ready_queue; ... }`,
  `unsafe { let push = VecDeque::push_back; ... }`,
  `use std::mem::{replace as rep, swap as sw}`, and
  `free_slots.reverse/sort_unstable/fill` probes, and extended
  `vm_jit_callback_boundary_001_scanner_rejects_core_std_root_alias_pointer_imports_062`
  with `use core as k; k::ptr::from_ref(...)`,
  `use k::ptr::{self as kptr}; kptr::from_ref(...)`, and
  `use std as s; s::ptr::NonNull::from(...)`. The typed raw-pointer block
  probe failed before the fix because raw-pointer provenance still required
  statement-start `let`; the root-alias pointer proof then passed once the
  assertion matched the scanner's compact normalized output. The fix reuses
  `compact_let_rest_062` for raw-pointer initialization and local function-item
  seeds, derives imported `mem` aliases into the same function-item mutator map,
  shares one `core_std_root_aliases_062` helper between pointer macro and
  pointer-from-reference scanners, and derives `from_ref` / `from_mut` /
  `NonNull::from` names from root and pointer-module aliases.
  The Round 19 runtime-boundary and GC/root red-teams found the same callback
  boundary bypass behind absolute-root aliases (`use ::core as c`),
  root-grouped use trees (`use c::{ptr as p}` and
  `use c::{ptr::addr_of_mut as aom}`), `std::mem as m` / `std as s`
  module-alias mutators, imported or type-aliased `NonNull`, Vec raw/mutator
  APIs (`as_mut_ptr`, `set_len`, `dedup`, `copy_within`, `fill_with`), and a
  local helper whose first `&mut` parameter is mutated before being called with
  a destructured scheduler-field alias. The proof now extends
  `vm_jit_callback_boundary_001_scanner_rejects_core_std_root_alias_pointer_imports_062`
  and `vm_jit_callback_boundary_001_scanner_rejects_raw_const_field_pointer_casts_062`
  with those trigger paths. The fix keeps the root alias helper as the shared
  source for pointer macro and pointer-from-reference names, builds one
  `mem`-mutator callee map for direct/root/module alias calls, adds
  `NonNull` import/type aliases to the pointer-from-reference name set, extends
  the scheduler mutator method table for raw Vec/slice mutators, and derives
  local helper mutators only when the helper body directly mutates its first
  parameter through the existing mutator rules.
  The Round 20 runtime-boundary and GC/root red-teams found the same callback
  boundary bypass behind root-grouped direct `ptr::from_mut` imports, nested
  grouped `std::{mem::{replace as rep, swap as sw}}` aliases, additional
  Vec/slice mutators such as `sort_by_key`, `sort_unstable_by_key`,
  `dedup_by_key`, and `splice`, `NonNull` alias chains, and local helper calls
  where the mutated scheduler-field alias was not the first helper argument or
  was reached through a closure/helper-body alias transfer. The proof extends
  `vm_jit_callback_boundary_001_scanner_rejects_core_std_root_alias_pointer_imports_062`
  and `vm_jit_callback_boundary_001_scanner_rejects_raw_const_field_pointer_casts_062`
  with those trigger paths. The fix keeps pointer-from-reference, `mem`
  mutator aliases, `NonNull` aliases, queue/storage mutator methods, and local
  helper mutators in shared fact builders, records exact mutated helper
  argument indices, follows simple body-local alias transfers and raw-pointer
  escapes, and covers closure helper calls through the same argument-index
  mutation checker.
  The Round 21 runtime-boundary and GC/root red-teams found the helper
  classifier still missed generic or `unsafe fn` helpers, expression-bodied and
  inferred-parameter closure helpers, helper bodies that mutate through
  `mem::{replace,swap}` or local function-item aliases, typed raw-pointer
  helper-body bindings, helpers returning raw pointers by typed return
  coercion, reverse-ordered `NonNull` type-alias chains, and root-alias nested
  grouped `mem` function imports. Failing-first proof extends the same callback
  scanner tests with each trigger path. The fix makes `NonNull` alias
  discovery a fixed point, lets helper parsing skip function generics and
  `unsafe` prefixes, parses block and expression closure bodies through the
  same parameter-index mutator analysis, reuses the shared `mem`,
  function-item, raw-binding, and pointer-from-mut fact builders inside helper
  bodies, treats raw-pointer helper returns as scheduler-field authority
  escapes, and derives nested grouped `mem` function aliases from `core` /
  `std` root aliases.
  The Round 22 runtime-boundary and GC/root red-teams found same-family helper
  bypasses behind callback-local `macro_rules!` forwarding, helper-to-helper
  calls, path-qualified associated helper calls, `move` closure helpers,
  `pub fn` helpers, and parenthesized raw-pointer helper returns followed by
  index assignment. Failing-first proof extends the callback scanner with each
  shape. The fix keeps one helper fact source by allowing `pub` helper prefixes,
  parsing `move` closures, propagating local-helper mutator argument indices
  through helper-to-helper calls to a fixed point, resolving path-qualified
  helper callees by their terminal method name, normalizing parenthesized raw
  return aliases, and adding a local `macro_rules!` parameter-mutator fact that
  connects macro call argument indices to destructured scheduler-field aliases.
  The Round 23 runtime-boundary and GC/root red-teams found the same callback
  contract could still be bypassed through block-local helper items reached
  from closures, local and associated helper function-item aliases, local
  receiver extension methods, non-parenthesized macro invocation delimiters,
  multi-arm macro expansions that mutate through UFCS or helper forwarding, and
  standard mutable-view APIs such as `IndexMut::index_mut`, `AsMut::as_mut`,
  `Option::get_or_insert*`, and map `entry`. The new proof variants were added
  to `vm_jit_callback_boundary_001_scanner_rejects_raw_const_field_pointer_casts_062`;
  the first focused run failed on
  `destructure_queue_closure_helper_chain_mutation_probe`. The fix keeps helper
  ownership in one fact graph by scanning nested block-local `fn` items, adding
  helper function-item aliases to the same fixed point, letting closure and
  macro bodies query local-helper facts, scanning every `macro_rules!` arm and
  all three invocation delimiters, deriving receiver-method mutators from
  helpers whose `&mut self`/arg0 is mutated, and extending the shared mutator
  method table for standard mutable-view APIs.
  The Round 24 runtime-boundary and GC/root red-teams found the same fact graph
  still missed split declaration/assignment aliases (`rq = ready_queue`),
  macro-to-macro forwarding, macro-body alias transfers, parenthesized macro
  receivers, raw-pointer escapes inside macro bodies, raw macro names, and
  generic or `as fn(...)` helper function-item aliases. Failing-first proof
  extended the same callback scanner table; the first focused run failed on
  `destructure_queue_split_assignment_alias_probe`, then the parenthesized
  macro receiver after the split-alias fix. The fix keeps ownership in the same
  transfer graph by parsing assignment alias transfers, normalizing
  function-item alias targets through turbofish and function-pointer coercions,
  deriving macro mutators to a fixed point across macro calls, analyzing macro
  bodies with `$param` substituted into the shared direct-alias mutator
  checker, and treating parenthesized alias receivers as the same direct field
  mutation fact.
  The Round 25 runtime-boundary, GC/root, and frame-call red-teams found the
  same callback-boundary fact graph still missed non-parenthesized raw field
  macro delimiters, match-bound destructured aliases, direct
  `addr_of_mut!(*ready_queue)` alias escapes, macro-forwarded variants of that
  escape, immediate closure calls that mutate destructured aliases, and
  scheduler-field mutator APIs such as `take_if` and `values_mut`.
  Failing-first proof extended the same scanner table; the first focused run
  failed on `destructure_queue_match_alias_transfer_probe`. The fix keeps the
  ownership source unified by making raw field pointer macro discovery
  delimiter-aware for `()`, `[]`, and `{}`, adding match alias transfer into
  the destructured-alias transfer graph, treating direct and macro-forwarded
  `addr_of_mut!(*alias)` as scheduler-field authority escape, analyzing
  immediate closure calls through the same parameter-to-argument mutation
  checker, and keeping `take_if` / `values_mut` in the shared mutator method
  table.
  Focused validation passed the exact proof and the full
  `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-main cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_jit_callback_boundary_001 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`
  gate (21 tests); selected by `vm-production` through
  `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence.

- `VM-JIT-CALLBACK-RETURNING-OUT-POINTER-062` is closed by the 2026-06-23 final selector evidence. The `ReturningJitResult` JIT
  callback wrapper may only route callbacks whose ABI policy is plain
  `JitResult`; callbacks with `JitResultWithOutPointer` or
  `PreparedCallOutPointer` require call-specific checked handling because the
  out pointer is part of the result contract. The Round 14 JIT/ABI red-team
  found `validate_callback_callsite` grouped all JitResult-returning policies,
  so a synthetic `ReturningJitResult` callsite over `PrepareClosureCallFn` or
  `QueueLenFn` would validate even though `emit_returning_jit_result_indirect_callback_call`
  returns the raw result immediately. Failing-first proof
  `callback_callsite_returning_jit_result_rejects_out_pointer_policies_062`
  covers both `PreparedCallOutPointer` and `JitResultWithOutPointer`. The fix
  separates checked JitResult-aware callbacks from returning callbacks:
  checked wrappers may handle all JitResult policies, while returning wrappers
  must match exactly `JitCallbackReturnPolicy::JitResult`. Focused validation
  passed
  `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-target-main cargo test -p vo-jit callback_abi -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`
  (5 tests); selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-jit`, final gates
  have not yet run for this source state.

### Superseded 2026-06-16 Closure Ledger

The following rows are the previous 2026-06-16 closure set. They remain useful
regression inventory, but they are no longer current signoff evidence because
the active blocker ledger above found broader contract gaps.

| ID | Status | Contract | Blocking Shape | First Proof Artifact | Closure Gate |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| VM-GC-TRANSITION-ROOT-001 | Closed, 2026-06-16 | Any queue/select recv path that writes GC-bearing values into the current fiber stack before returning `ExecResult::Transition` must mark the current fiber dirty, or stronger, before GC can observe roots. | `QueueRecv` and select recv can write a payload into the current fiber stack, then return `RemoteSendAck` with `GcRootEffect::None`; `ExecResult::Transition` does not get the outer current-fiber dirty compensation. | Failing VM/JIT regression where a remote sender payload containing a heap ref is received by queue and select recv, forced GC runs after the transition, and the payload root must survive. Add a source-contract test rejecting recv-produced `RemoteSendAck` transitions with `GcRootEffect::None`. | Pass focused interpreter and `--features jit` regressions, then ensure the checks are selected by `vm-production`. |
| VM-OSR-SLOWPATH-STACK-001 | Closed, 2026-06-16 | OSR code must refresh cached stack bases after every helper path that can grow or reallocate `fiber.stack` and before any caller-local spill or materialization. | Prepared-call trampoline and nested JIT non-OK slow paths spill caller SSA locals through the cached OSR locals pointer without first refreshing after possible callee-frame growth. | Failing OSR/JIT regression that forces dynamic closure/interface prepared-call trampoline or call-depth exhaustion after stack growth, then proves caller locals and roots match interpreter behavior. Add a source check that slow-path spill sites call `refresh_stack_base_after_reallocation()`. | Pass `cargo test -p vo-jit vm_osr_slowpath_stack_001`, `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_osr_slowpath_stack_001` if VM-side harness is needed, and the OSR language regression through `vm-production`. |
| VM-ENDPOINT-RESPONSE-COMMIT-001 | Closed, 2026-06-16 | Endpoint response object-state changes must commit only after endpoint id, response kind, wait id, fiber generation, and pending obligation validation accepts the response. | `SendAck { closed: true }` and `RecvData { closed: true }` close the remote proxy before `RuntimeCommand::endpoint_response` validation, so stale or wrong closed responses can poison future sends as `SendOnClosedChannel`. | Failing `island_shared` ingress test that registers a live remote proxy, injects a stale wait id or `pending_island_responses == 0` closed response through `handle_endpoint_response_command`, and proves both fiber accounting and `RemoteProxy.closed` remain unchanged. | Pass focused VM tests with and without `--features jit`; add a mechanical guard or helper contract so remote-proxy close is tied to accepted runtime command outcome. |
| VM-JIT-QUEUE-CLOSE-OSR-001 | Closed, 2026-06-16 | JIT callbacks running under an OSR borrow lease must publish endpoint close/tombstone effects through pending runtime transitions, not direct transition application. | `jit_queue_close` calls `finalize_closed_home_endpoint`, which directly calls `apply_runtime_transition` and ignores the result; under OSR the transition is rejected while the borrow lease is active, dropping endpoint tombstone and remote close effects. | Failing JIT/OSR test that closes a local endpoint in OSR and proves the endpoint tombstone plus outbound close response are still published after the boundary. Add a source check that JIT queue close does not call direct runtime-transition helpers. | Pass `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_jit_queue_close_osr_001` and route it through `vm-production`. |
| VM-OSR-PANIC-PENDING-001 | Closed, 2026-06-16 | Terminal OSR results must not discard or hide failures while attaching pending runtime transitions. | The OSR panic path ignores the result of `attach_pending_runtime_transitions(ExecResult::Panic)`, so a pending transition application failure can be swallowed before panic unwinding continues. | Failing unit that injects a pending transition which returns `ExecResult::JitError` when attached to an OSR panic result, and asserts the JIT error is propagated instead of continuing unwind. | Pass focused `vo-vm --features jit` regression and keep pending-transition terminal-result behavior covered by `vm-production`. |
| VM-PANIC-RECOVER-LOC-001 | Closed, 2026-06-16 | Recovering a panic must clear the consumed panic's source location so later independent panics report their own origin. | `take_recoverable_panic()` clears panic state and trap kind but leaves `panic_source_loc`; later panic capture is skipped because the old location is still present. | Failing VM or language regression where `defer recover()` consumes one panic, execution continues, then a second panic reports the second panic's file/pc instead of the first. | Pass focused panic/recover regression in VM or manifest-driven language tests; route through the normal VM/language production gate. |

### Signoff Reentry Criteria, 2026-06-16 Post-Audit

Production-readiness signoff can be restored only after all open rows above are
closed and the closure evidence is stronger than both the 2026-06-15 and
2026-06-16 source-audit checks that missed these paths:

- each blocker has a failing-first regression or a mechanical source-contract
  test that would have failed in the withdrawn state;
- each regression name carries the blocker ID and is selected by the
  `vm-production` route through `vo-dev`;
- runtime-boundary and GC dirty-root invariants have producer-enumeration tests
  for `GcRootEffect::None`, pending runtime payloads, terminal pending effects,
  rejected commands, and stale wake identities, not only happy-path regression
  coverage;
- VM object-header reads for closure, island, interface, and materialized JIT
  payloads are routed through shared validation helpers or covered by source
  contracts that list every raw access site;
- interpreter, JIT, OSR, and extern bridge parity is proved for each affected
  bytecode and ABI surface, including negative malformed-metadata cases;
- an adversarial source-audit pass runs after the fixes and before signoff, and
  any new finding becomes an active blocker instead of a historical note;
- the final `vm-production`, `contract`, `site`, and `release-verify` gates are
  rerun after the fixes land, and this document records the exact source-state
  evidence.

| VM-ENDPOINT-REQUEST-OWNER-019 | Closed, 2026-06-18; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/vm/island_shared.rs` | `vm_endpoint_request_owner_019_rejects_unknown_peer_close`, `vm_endpoint_request_owner_019_rejects_unknown_peer_send`, `vm_endpoint_request_owner_019_rejects_unknown_peer_recv`, `vm_endpoint_request_owner_019_unauthorized_send_closes_source_pending_wait`, `vm_endpoint_request_owner_019_unauthorized_recv_rejects_source_pending_wait`. | Failing-first proofs showed unknown islands could close, send to, or register recv waiters on live endpoints because only `Transfer` checked endpoint ownership; the red-team follow-up found unauthorized `Send`/`Recv` with a live source wait could be orphaned by a silent home-side return.
