| VM-STUDIO-GENERATED-ISLAND-WAKE-ABI-051 | Superseded by VM-STUDIO-WASM-BUILD-HERMETICITY-052; production signoff still pending final gates | `apps/studio/public/wasm/vo_studio_wasm.js`, `apps/studio/public/wasm/vo_studio_wasm.d.ts`, `scripts/ci/docs_lint.mjs` | Local ignored artifact patch only; not counted as production proof. | 051 wasm artifact audit found the local generated `VoVmIsland.wakeHostEvent` JS and `.d.ts` still discarded the Rust `bool` result. The local ignored artifact was updated for developer parity, but 052 red-team audit found `apps/studio/public/wasm/**` is declared untracked build output, so docs-lint must not treat those files as hermetic production proof. The source-level Studio wrapper contract remains `VM-STUDIO-WASM-WAKE-TYPE-048`; generated artifact ownership is closed by `VM-STUDIO-WASM-BUILD-HERMETICITY-052`. |
| VM-JIT-ARRAY-SLICE-BARRIER-ORDER-051 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-jit/src/translate/collections/array.rs`, `lang/crates/vo-jit/src/translate/collections/slice.rs`, `lang/crates/vo-jit/src/lib.rs` | `vm_jit_array_slice_set_typed_barriers_precede_stores_051`. | 051 runtime/GC audit found JIT `ArraySet` and `SliceSet` performed heap stores before the fallible typed GC barrier, unlike interpreter set paths. JIT lowering now runs the typed single-slot or multi-slot barrier before mutating the destination element, so helper failure leaves the heap unchanged. The proof failed first on store-before-barrier order and then passed; `contract` and `vm-production` plans select `cargo-test-jit`. |
| VM-PR-CHANGED-VO-DEV-GATE-051 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `eng/tasks.toml`, `cmd/vo-dev/src/lint_system.rs` | `lint_vo_dev_contract_requires_group_in_pr_051`, `vo-dev lint tasks --strict`. | 051 gate audit found `eng/ci.toml` routed gate-policy changes to `cargo-test-vo-dev`, but `task plan pr --changed` filtered that task out because the `pr` selector did not own `vo-dev-contract`. The `pr` group now includes `vo-dev-contract`, and task lint requires that ownership. The lint failed first on `pr must include vo-dev-contract`, then passed; `pr --changed`, `contract`, and `vm-production` plans select `cargo-test-vo-dev`. |
| VM-STUDIO-WASM-BUILD-HERMETICITY-052 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `eng/tasks.toml`, `scripts/ci/docs_lint.mjs`, `lang/docs/dev/vm-production-readiness.md` | `selfTestStudioWasmBuildOutputOwnershipContracts`, `node scripts/ci/docs_lint.mjs`. | 052 docs/gate red-team audit found docs-lint directly read ignored `apps/studio/public/wasm/vo_studio_wasm.*` build output and readiness called that output checked-in, so a clean checkout could fail while a dirty local tree passed. Docs-lint now checks only tracked source contracts and build-output ownership, rejects direct or split reads of ignored Studio wasm artifacts under `apps/studio/public/wasm/**`, and `studio-wasm-build` declares the generated wasm output directory it owns. The proof failed first on direct and split ignored artifact reads, then passed; `contract` and `vm-production` select `docs-lint`, while generated artifact materialization remains owned by the site/app build gates. |
| VM-RUNTIME-TYPED-BARRIER-WIDTH-ORDER-052 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-runtime/src/gc_types.rs`, `lang/crates/vo-runtime/src/builtins/builtin.rs`, `lang/crates/vo-runtime/src/builtins/dynamic.rs`, `lang/crates/vo-runtime/src/jit_api.rs` | `typed_write_barrier_by_meta_rejects_ref_and_interface_width_drift_052`, `builtin_copy_and_append_barrier_before_existing_array_mutation_052`, `dynamic_field_and_slice_set_barriers_precede_heap_mutation_052`, `jit_typed_barrier_helper_does_not_bypass_zero_slot_values_052`. | 052 GC/barrier red-team audit found `try_typed_write_barrier_by_meta` silently accepted wrong-width direct references and truncated interfaces, while `copy`, append-with-capacity, dynamic field set, and dynamic slice index set still mutated existing heap containers before fallible typed/range barriers. The by-meta barrier now rejects direct-ref and interface width drift, the JIT helper no longer accepts zero-slot values before by-meta validation, and the builtin/dynamic writers validate or barrier the source value before heap mutation. The focused proofs failed first on accepted zero-width refs and write-before-barrier order, then passed with `cargo test -p vo-runtime 052 -- --test-threads=1`; `contract` and `vm-production` plans select `cargo-test-runtime`. |
| VM-RUNTIME-TRANSITION-PREFLIGHT-053 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/{runtime_boundary.rs,scheduler.rs,fiber.rs,vm/types.rs}` | `vm_runtime_transition_preflights_all_wakes_before_commit_053`. | 053 runtime-boundary red-team audit found multi-effect transitions could commit an earlier wake before a later wake or route failed. `apply_runtime_transition` now preflights every resume policy, wake key, endpoint response, and island-route effect before committing scheduler-visible state, so a rejected transition leaves earlier wake targets blocked. The proof failed first with a half-committed wake, then passed; focused 051 queue-wake and endpoint-response source regressions also passed. |
| VM-ENDPOINT-CLOSED-RESPONSE-BRIDGE-053 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/{runtime_boundary.rs,vm/island_shared.rs}` | `endpoint_closed_response_uses_runtime_command_bridge_053`. | 053 scheduler audit found `EndpointResponseKind::Closed` still mutated endpoint/proxy state directly instead of going through the runtime command owner. Closed endpoint responses now use `RuntimeCommand::endpoint_closed_response`, which validates source, applies the remote-close/tombstone/all-roots-dirty effects in the boundary owner, and preserves the existing response-source authority checks. The source-contract proof failed first on direct mutation and then passed with existing closed-response owner tests. |
| VM-ARRAY-SLICE-NEW-METADATA-053 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-common-core/src/verifier.rs` | `module_verifier_rejects_array_new_metadata_layout_drift_053`, `module_verifier_rejects_slice_new_metadata_layout_drift_053`. | 053 JIT/GC audit found `ArrayNew` and `SliceNew` could seed an array header with runtime `ValueMeta` whose slot layout disagreed with the JIT `ElemLayout`, so later typed barriers could trust the wrong header metadata. The shared module verifier now requires the element metadata register to be a constant and rejects any runtime metadata layout that differs from the verified element layout, keeping allocation headers and lowering facts on one source. Both proofs failed first and then passed under `cargo test -p vo-common-core 053`. |
| VM-JIT-COPY-HELPER-BARRIER-053 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-runtime/src/jit_api.rs` | `jit_runtime_abi_does_not_export_module_blind_copy_helper_053`. | 053 GC audit found an exported JIT `vo_copy(dst, src)` helper that copied slice bytes without module or element metadata, creating a barrier bypass if any runtime-helper caller used it for root-bearing slices. Current JIT lowering does not call this helper, and language `vo_copy` remains owned by the checked builtin extern path, so the module-blind JIT runtime symbol and ABI row were removed instead of adding a second copy fact source. The proof failed first on the exported helper and then passed under `cargo test -p vo-runtime 053`. |
| VM-PTRSET-FFI-BARRIER-ORDER-053 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/exec/ptr.rs`, `lang/crates/vo-jit/src/translate/memory.rs`, `lang/crates/vo-runtime/src/ffi/containers.rs` | `ptr_set_write_barrier_precedes_heap_mutation_053`, `ptr_set_write_barrier_lowering_precedes_store_053`, `ffi_slice_and_array_set_barriers_precede_mutation_053`. | 053 GC audit found interpreter `PtrSet`, JIT `PtrSet`, and public FFI `VoSlice`/`VoArray` setters still executed root barriers after publishing heap writes. These paths now run the known-ref or element write barrier before the heap store, matching the typed array/slice/map/copy mutation contract. All three source-order proofs failed first and then passed under focused `vo-vm`, `vo-jit`, and `vo-runtime` 053 runs. |
| VM-BUILTIN-VARIADIC-DEFER-EXTERN-ABI-053 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-codegen/src/{context.rs,wrapper.rs}` | `defer_extern_wrapper_cache_key_includes_arg_count`, `vo-codegen extern`. | Follow-up extern validation found deferred wrappers for built-in variadic externs such as `vo_println` used the declared-exact extern registration path, so the first wrapper arity could become the global extern ABI and a second arity panicked under strict ABI merge. `CodegenContext` now keeps known built-in variadic externs as `ParamShape::CallSiteVariadic` while preserving exact call-site ABI for declared externs; the wrapper proof asserts `vo_println` does not capture one arity as `param_kinds`. The wider `cargo test -p vo-codegen extern -- --test-threads=1` filter failed first and then passed. |
| VM-FFI-SLICE-SET-BOUNDS-054 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-runtime/src/ffi/containers.rs` | `vo_slice_set_rejects_indices_outside_visible_len_before_mutation_054`. | 054 GC/FFI red-team audit found public `VoSlice::set` validated barrier ordering but still delegated to unchecked `slice::set`, allowing native FFI to mutate backing capacity outside the visible slice length. `VoSlice::set` now checks `idx < len` before any barrier or raw write, matching `VoArray::set` release-mode bounds ownership at the FFI boundary. The behavior proof failed first by mutating a `len=1, cap=2` backing slot, then passed with the focused runtime filter; `contract` and `vm-production` select `cargo-test-runtime`. |
| VM-JIT-CALL-REQUEST-HELPER-DEPENDENCY-054 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-jit/src/semantics/{runtime_policy.rs,tests.rs}` | `vm_call_request_rows_name_set_call_request_helper_054`, `fail_fast_policy_covers_metadata_helpers_callbacks_and_gc_frames`. | 054 JIT red-team audit found `Call`, `CallClosure`, and `CallIface` materialize VM call requests through the registered `vo_set_call_request` helper but their semantic rows only named `VmCallRequest`, so the contract graph understated a real runtime ABI dependency. The call-family dependency rows now name `RuntimeHelper("vo_set_call_request")` and their fail-fast policies include `MissingHelper`, keeping runtime dependency, ABI registration, and strict-JIT failure policy on one fact source. The dependency proof and fail-fast policy test failed first, then passed with focused `vo-jit` filters; `contract` and `vm-production` select `cargo-test-jit`. |
| VM-FFI-RAW-ACCESSOR-OWNERSHIP-055 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-runtime/src/{ffi/containers.rs,jit_api.rs,objects/array.rs,objects/slice.rs}` | `vo_slice_get_rejects_indices_outside_visible_len_055`, `jit_runtime_abi_does_not_export_module_blind_array_slice_element_helpers_055`, `raw_array_element_accessors_are_unsafe_public_primitives_055`, `raw_slice_element_accessors_are_unsafe_public_primitives_055`. | 055 GC/FFI red-team audit found `VoSlice::get` could read backing capacity outside visible slice length, public raw array/slice object accessors looked like safe APIs while relying on external bounds/barrier proofs, and JIT runtime ABI still exported module-blind array/slice element helpers. `VoSlice::get` now bounds-checks at the FFI surface, raw object element primitives are `unsafe`, and module-blind JIT element helpers were removed from symbol and ABI manifests. The proofs failed first, then passed with `cargo test -p vo-runtime 055 -- --test-threads=1`; downstream compile gates passed for `vo-stdlib`, `vo-web-runtime-wasm`, and `vo-vm` no-run after call sites declared their unsafe proof boundary. |
| VM-FFI-CONTRACT-GATE-055 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `eng/tasks.toml`, `eng/ci.toml`, `cmd/vo-dev/src/lint_system.rs` | `lint_ffi_contract_*_055`, `lint_vm_readiness_changed_prefixes_requires_ffi_macro_route_055`, `lint_vm_readiness_changed_prefixes_requires_vo_ext_route_055`. | 055 gate red-team audit found changes under `vo-ffi-macro` and `vo-ext` could reach readiness through stdlib consumers without a dedicated macro/SDK contract gate. `ffi-contract` now owns `cargo-test-ffi-macro` and `cargo-test-vo-ext`, is selected by `test`, `pr`, `contract`, and `vm-production`, and changed-mode routes `lang/crates/vo-ffi-macro/**` and `lang/crates/vo-ext/**` to those tasks. The lint proofs failed first on missing ownership/routing, then passed with focused `vo-dev` tests, `vo-dev lint tasks --strict`, `cargo test -p vo-ffi-macro`, and `cargo test -p vo-ext`. |
| VM-SELECT-DUPLICATE-CLOSE-WAKE-055 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/{runtime_boundary.rs,vm/mod.rs,vm/island_shared.rs,vm/jit/callbacks/queue.rs}` | `duplicate_select_wakes_reject_before_partial_apply_055`, `queue_close_wake_insertion_coalesces_duplicate_select_activation_055`. | 055 runtime/scheduler red-team audit found a select with duplicate cases on the same queue could register multiple waiters; queue close drained them into one transition, preflight accepted each against the pre-commit fiber state, and apply could wake the first case then fail on the second. Runtime transitions now reject duplicate select wakes before mutation, and queue-close wake construction coalesces by select activation through one shared helper used by interpreter close, JIT close, and island local close responses. Both proofs failed first, then passed with `cargo test -p vo-vm 055 -- --test-threads=1`; `cargo test -p vo-vm --no-run` also passed. |
| VM-FFI-RAW-BULK-COPY-056 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-runtime/src/objects/array.rs` | `raw_array_bulk_copy_is_unsafe_public_primitive_056`. | 056 GC/FFI red-team audit found `array::copy_range` remained a safe public raw heap-copy primitive after 055 made raw get/set element primitives unsafe. The function has no current production callers but still exposed unchecked bounds, metadata, and barrier ownership to safe downstream Rust. `array::copy_range` is now `pub unsafe fn`, and the source-contract proof failed first on the safe signature then passed with `cargo test -p vo-runtime 056 -- --test-threads=1`; `contract` and `vm-production` select `cargo-test-runtime`. |
| VM-SELECT-WAKE-SIBLING-CANCEL-056 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/runtime_boundary.rs` | `accepted_select_wake_cancels_sibling_waiters_056`. | 056 runtime/scheduler red-team audit found an accepted select wake made the fiber runnable but left sibling waiters registered until the select fiber resumed, leaving a window where another queue operation could consume a stale waiter and reject the transition. Runtime wake acceptance now cancels sibling select waiters at the boundary owner for normal, closed-receiver, and closed-sender wakes, and JIT pending queue transitions cancel sibling waiters before publishing already-committed queue side effects. The proof failed first with the sibling queue still holding one waiter, then passed with `cargo test -p vo-vm 056 -- --test-threads=1`; the VM hardening gates select these tests. |
| VM-JIT-PENDING-CLOSE-WAKE-MERGE-056 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/runtime_boundary.rs` | `vm_pending_runtime_transition_merge_coalesces_duplicate_select_close_wakes_056`. | 056 scheduler/JIT audit found close-wake coalescing happened when a single close transition was built, but JIT pending transition merge appended wake vectors directly and could recreate duplicate select close wakes after queue state was already mutated. Pending transition merge now preserves the same queue-close coalescing helper used by close construction, so multiple pending close effects for one select activation attach as one wake. The JIT-feature proof passed with `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_pending_runtime_transition_merge_coalesces_duplicate_select_close_wakes_056 -- --test-threads=1`; `contract` and `vm-production` select `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`. |
| VM-CALLCLOSURE-FRAME-PREFLIGHT-056 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/exec/call.rs` | `call_closure_without_active_frame_rejects_before_stack_read_056`. | 056 interpreter/frame-call red-team audit found `CallClosure` used `bp=0` and read the caller stack before confirming an active caller frame, unlike `Call` and `CallIface`. `exec_call_closure` now rejects missing caller frames before stack pointer or closure-slot reads. The proof failed first by returning a nil-call panic path, then passed with `cargo test -p vo-vm 056 -- --test-threads=1`; `contract` and `vm-production` select `cargo-test-vm`. |
| VM-FFI-WASM-SDK-GATE-056 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `eng/tasks.toml`, `eng/ci.toml`, `cmd/vo-dev/src/lint_system.rs` | `lint_ffi_contract_requires_vo_ext_wasm_check_056`, `cargo-check-vo-ext-wasm`. | 056 gate audit found `ffi-contract` covered native/default `vo-ext` but not the WASM static SDK feature path exposed by `export_extensions!`. `ffi-contract` now includes `cargo-check-vo-ext-wasm`, changed-mode routes both `vo-ffi-macro/**` and `vo-ext/**` to that check, and task lint rejects removing it. The lint proof failed first when the old contract was accepted, then passed with `cargo test -p vo-dev lint_ffi_contract -- --test-threads=1`; `cargo check -p vo-ext --target wasm32-unknown-unknown --no-default-features --features wasm`, `vo-dev lint tasks --strict`, and `task plan contract` / `task plan vm-production` also passed and selected the new task. |
| VM-SLICE-APPEND-METADATA-057 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-common-core/src/verifier.rs`, `lang/crates/vo-runtime/src/objects/slice.rs`, `lang/crates/vo-runtime/src/builtins/builtin.rs` | `module_verifier_rejects_slice_append_metadata_layout_drift_057`, `try_append_non_nil_uses_slice_elem_meta_not_caller_metadata_057`, `builtin_spread_append_derives_elem_meta_from_containers_057`. | 057 GC/metadata audit found `SliceAppend` and spread append still allowed caller-supplied metadata to diverge from the container or JIT element layout, which could skip root barriers or create backing arrays with the wrong scan metadata. The verifier now tracks and validates `SliceAppend` metadata constants against the JIT elem layout, non-nil `slice::try_append` derives metadata and byte width from the existing backing array, and `vo_slice_append_slice` derives source/destination layouts from containers while recording a contract violation on drift. Focused proofs passed with `cargo test -p vo-common-core module_verifier_rejects_slice_append_metadata_layout_drift_057 -- --test-threads=1` and `cargo test -p vo-runtime 057 -- --test-threads=1`; selected by common-core/runtime tasks in `vm-production`. |
| VM-SLICE-HEADER-INVARIANT-057 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-runtime/src/objects/slice.rs` | `slice_header_constructors_reject_len_beyond_capacity_057`. | 057 FFI/raw-accessor audit found safe slice header constructors could create `len > cap` or ranges beyond the backing array, undermining later visible-length checks. `from_array_range`, `from_array_range_with_cap`, and `with_new_len` now enforce `start <= len`, `length <= capacity`, and capacity within the backing range before publishing a slice header. The proof passed under `cargo test -p vo-runtime 057 -- --test-threads=1`; `contract` and `vm-production` select `cargo-test-runtime`. |
| VM-ISLAND-WAKE-SELECT-SIBLING-057 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/runtime_boundary.rs` | `vm_island_wake_cancels_select_sibling_waiters_057`. | 057 runtime-boundary audit found `RuntimeCommand::IslandWake` accepted select wakes through a side path that did not cancel sibling queue waiters, unlike normal runtime transition wakes. The IslandWake branch now cancels sibling select waiters after a wake is accepted by the scheduler. The proof passed with `cargo test -p vo-vm 057 -- --test-threads=1`, `cargo test -p vo-vm vm_island_wake_cancels_select_sibling_waiters_057 -- --test-threads=1`, and under the JIT-feature shard; selected by VM hardening gates through the `vm_` prefix. |
| VM-JIT-CALLBACK-FRAME-PREFLIGHT-057 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/vm/jit/callbacks/closure_call.rs`, `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/vm/jit/callbacks/goroutine.rs` | `vm_jit_prepared_call_abi_057_nil_closure_requires_active_caller_frame_before_trap`, `vm_jit_prepared_call_abi_057_nil_iface_requires_active_caller_frame_before_trap`, `vm_jit_go_callback_abi_057_nil_closure_requires_active_caller_frame_before_trap`, `vm_jit_goisland_callback_abi_057_nil_island_requires_active_caller_frame_before_trap`. | 057 JIT ABI audit found nil closure/interface/island fast-trap branches could run before validating that the callback had an active caller frame and readable callsite layout. Prepared-call and go callbacks now preflight caller frame/callsite metadata before classifying nil operands as language traps, so invalid callback state cannot be disguised as a user panic. The JIT-feature proof shard passed with `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit 057 -- --test-threads=1`; selected by `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`. |
| VM-JIT-QUEUE-ROUTE-PREFLIGHT-057 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/vm/jit/callbacks/queue.rs`, `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/exec/island.rs` | `vm_jit_queue_close_route_preflight_057_missing_peer_route_preserves_open_queue`, `vm_jit_remote_send_route_preflight_057_missing_home_route_preserves_payload_endpoint_state`. | 057 JIT/runtime-boundary audit found JIT queue close and remote-send callbacks could mutate queue or endpoint state before route preflight later rejected the pending transition. JIT close/send now preflight required island routes before queue mutation, and queue-transfer validation preflights transfer routes before endpoint commit. The JIT-feature proof shard passed with `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit 057 -- --test-threads=1`; selected by `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`. |
| VM-CALLIFACE-RECEIVER-LAYOUT-058 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-common-core/src/verifier.rs` | `vm_call_iface_receiver_layout_058_iface_assign_rejects_itab_receiver_layout_drift`. | 058 verifier audit found `IfaceAssign` could pair a concrete scalar source with an itab whose method target expected a `GcRef` receiver, letting `CallIface` copy raw scalar bits into a GC-scanned callee slot. The module verifier now checks every concrete `IfaceAssign` itab method receiver slot against the source `ValueKind` storage class before `Vm::load`. Focused proof passed with `cargo test -p vo-common-core vm_call_iface_receiver_layout_058 -- --test-threads=1`; the `vm_` prefix selects the proof in common-core hardening gates. |
| VM-DYNAMIC-CONTAINER-LAYOUT-058 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-runtime/src/builtins/dynamic.rs` | `dynamic_container_setters_use_container_layout_as_storage_fact_058`. | 058 GC/runtime audit found dynamic map and slice setters still used caller/base RTTID slot counts as write width, while barriers and later scans trusted container metadata. Dynamic map writes now validate RTTID metadata against `map::val_slots` and write that object-derived width, and dynamic slice writes validate RTTID metadata against backing-array `elem_bytes` before using the byte-width-aware slice primitive. Focused proof passed with `cargo test -p vo-runtime 058 -- --test-threads=1`; runtime gates select it. |
| VM-QUEUE-ROUTE-PREFLIGHT-058 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/exec/queue.rs`, `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/exec/select.rs`, `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/vm/mod.rs`, `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/vm/jit/callbacks/queue.rs` | `vm_queue_route_preflight_058_send_missing_receiver_route_preserves_queue_state`, `vm_queue_route_preflight_058_recv_missing_sender_route_preserves_queue_state`, `vm_queue_route_preflight_058_remote_close_missing_home_route_preserves_proxy`. | 058 runtime-boundary/JIT audit found interpreter queue send/recv/close, select immediate cases, and JIT recv response paths could consume waiters, write destinations, or mark remote proxies closed before route preflight rejected the resulting endpoint command. Queue route checks are now shared in `exec::queue`, run before queue/select mutation in interpreter and JIT paths, and preserve old HomeInfo error ownership. Focused proofs passed with `cargo test -p vo-vm 058 -- --test-threads=1` and `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit 058 -- --test-threads=1`; selected by VM hardening gates through `vm_`. |
| VM-GOISLAND-ROUTE-PREFLIGHT-058 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/vm/jit/callbacks/goroutine.rs` | `vm_jit_goisland_route_preflight_058_missing_target_route_preserves_no_pending_transition`. | 058 JIT audit found `jit_go_island` preflighted routes only when queue handles were transferred, so scalar or empty remote spawns could stage a pending spawn transition and suspend before discovering the target island route was missing. `jit_go_island` now validates the target route after island/closure ABI checks and before transfer preparation or command publication. Focused proof passed with `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_jit_goisland_route_preflight_058 -- --test-threads=1`; selected by the JIT VM hardening gate through `vm_`. |
| VM-ENDPOINT-REQUEST-ROUTE-PREFLIGHT-058 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/vm/island_shared.rs` | `vm_endpoint_request_route_preflight_058_recv_missing_requester_route_preserves_queue`, `vm_endpoint_request_route_preflight_058_send_missing_requester_route_preserves_queue`, `vm_endpoint_request_route_preflight_058_close_missing_peer_route_preserves_queue`. | 058 island ingress audit found endpoint requests could mutate the home queue inside `with_local_state` before route preflight rejected responses or close broadcasts. `handle_endpoint_request_command` now preflights requester, paired waiter, and close-peer response routes before entering the local queue mutation block, using the same local-wake vs endpoint-response distinction as `dispatch_response`. Focused proofs passed with `cargo test -p vo-vm vm_endpoint_request_route_preflight_058 -- --test-threads=1` and the same filter under `--features jit`; selected by VM hardening gates through `vm_`. |
| VM-MAP-RAW-SET-UNSAFE-058 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-runtime/src/objects/map.rs`, map mutation callers in runtime/VM/JIT/pack paths | `raw_map_set_checked_is_unsafe_public_primitive_058`. | 058 GC/barrier red-team audit found `map::set_checked` remained a public safe raw mutator even though it publishes key/value roots and cannot own the required write barriers. The raw insertion primitive is now `pub unsafe fn` with a documented barrier-or-initialization proof obligation, and all current callers declare the local proof chain before calling it. Focused proof passed with `cargo test -p vo-runtime raw_map_set_checked_is_unsafe_public_primitive_058 -- --test-threads=1`; runtime gates select it. |
| VM-RUNTIME-ISLAND-EFFECT-TXN-058 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/runtime_boundary.rs`, `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/vm/island_shared.rs`, `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/vm/mod.rs` | `vm_runtime_transition_preflights_same_island_endpoint_request_before_block_058`. | 058 runtime-boundary red-team audit found same-island endpoint request effects could pass only target-island preflight, then block the current fiber before the endpoint request handler discovered a missing response route. Runtime-transition preflight now delegates same-island endpoint requests to the endpoint request route checker before any boundary mutation, cross-island island effects use fallible send, and pending response counts are retained only after successful send/dispatch with rollback on same-island failure. Focused proof passed with `cargo test -p vo-vm vm_runtime_transition_preflights_same_island_endpoint_request_before_block_058 -- --test-threads=1`; VM hardening gates select it through the `vm_` prefix. |
| VM-WORKFLOW-CHANGED-SELF-CHECK-058 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `eng/ci.toml`, `cmd/vo-dev/src/lint_system.rs` | `lint_vm_readiness_changed_prefixes_requires_workflow_self_check_route_058`. | 058 gate audit found `.github/workflows/**` changed-mode routed only to `docs-lint`, so workflow changes could miss the CI self-check owner even though workflow files are `ci-self-check` inputs. The changed-prefix now selects both `docs-lint` and `ci-self-check`, and task lint rejects removing that route. Focused proof passed with `cargo test -p vo-dev lint_vm_readiness_changed_prefixes_requires_workflow_self_check_route_058 -- --test-threads=1`; `vm-production` and PR planning use the same changed-mode policy. |
| VM-DOCS-LINT-SCRIPT-ROUTE-058 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `eng/ci.toml`, `cmd/vo-dev/src/lint_system.rs`, `scripts/ci/docs_lint.mjs` | `lint_vm_readiness_changed_prefixes_requires_docs_lint_script_route_058`. | 058 test/gate audit found changes to `scripts/ci/docs_lint.mjs` or `docs_sync.mjs` could weaken readiness/docs contracts without selecting `docs-lint` in changed-mode, even though those scripts are docs-lint inputs. `scripts/ci/**` now routes to `docs-lint` and `ci-self-check`, with a vo-dev lint proof that rejects missing or docs-lint-free script routes. Focused proof passed with `cargo test -p vo-dev lint_vm_readiness_changed_prefixes_requires_docs_lint_script_route_058 -- --test-threads=1`; docs-lint also passed after docs-sync regenerated the Playground mirror. |
| VM-BYTECODE-RETURNSHAPE-SPEC-058 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/docs/spec/vm-bytecode.md`, `scripts/ci/docs_lint.mjs`, generated Playground bytecode spec mirror | `lintVmBytecodeSpecAbi` ReturnShape field/invariant checks. | 058 docs/ABI audit found the bytecode spec named `ReturnShape` but did not define its fields or invariants, leaving the fixed source contract under-documented. The spec now documents `ParamShape`, `ReturnShape { slots, kinds, slot_types }`, length invariants, and `Interface0`/`Interface1` adjacency, and docs-lint rejects incomplete ReturnShape prose. `node scripts/ci/docs_lint.mjs` passed after `node scripts/ci/docs_sync.mjs` updated generated docs; common-core ReturnShape proof tests also passed with `cargo test -p vo-common-core return_shape -- --test-threads=1`. |
| VM-EXTERN-RETURNSHAPE-CALLSITE-LAYOUT-058 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-common-core/src/verifier.rs`, `lang/crates/vo-codegen/src/context.rs`, `lang/crates/vo-codegen/src/expr/dyn_access.rs`, `lang/docs/spec/vm-bytecode.md` | `module_verifier_rejects_precise_call_extern_return_with_slots_only_shape_058`, `variable_ret_externs_are_keyed_by_precise_return_layout_058`. | Follow-up extern ABI audit found hand-authored bytecode could pair `ExternDef.returns = ReturnShape::slots(n)` with a `CallExternLayout.ret_layout` containing `GcRef` or interface slots, bypassing runtime/JIT post-call return validation. The verifier now rejects precise non-Value callsite returns unless `ReturnShape.slot_types` is present and exact, and `dyn_call`/`dyn_method` variable-return externs are keyed by full return layout rather than slot count alone. The verifier proof failed first by accepting the malformed module, then passed with `cargo test -p vo-common-core module_verifier_rejects_precise_call_extern_return_with_slots_only_shape_058 -- --test-threads=1`; the codegen proof passed with `cargo test -p vo-codegen variable_ret_externs_are_keyed_by_precise_return_layout_058 -- --test-threads=1`. |
| VM-GC-RAW-MUTATOR-UNSAFE-058 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-runtime/src/objects/{mod.rs,closure.rs,struct_ops.rs}`, closure/header mutation callers in runtime and VM tests | `raw_object_headers_as_mut_are_unsafe_public_primitives_058`, `raw_closure_set_capture_is_unsafe_public_primitive_058`, `raw_struct_set_field_is_unsafe_public_primitive_058`. | Follow-up GC audit found remaining safe public raw mutators (`impl_gc_object!::as_mut`, `closure::set_capture`, and `struct_ops::set_field`) could publish GC-visible roots without owning a write barrier or fresh-allocation scan proof. These primitives are now `unsafe` with explicit barrier-or-initialization obligations, and all current callers declare the local proof before mutation. Focused runtime proof passed with `cargo test -p vo-runtime 058 -- --test-threads=1`, and a VM closure fixture compiled and passed with `cargo test -p vo-vm vm_call_closure_canon_001_go_closure_fiber_stores_canonical_slot0_from_interior_ref -- --test-threads=1`; runtime/VM gates select these tests. |
| VM-RUNTIME-REMOTE-PUBLISH-TXN-058 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/runtime_boundary.rs`, `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/vm/types.rs` | `vm_runtime_transition_remote_endpoint_send_failure_is_transactional_058`, `vm_runtime_transition_remote_wake_send_failure_returns_error_058`, `vm_endpoint_request_publication_013_request_publishers_are_boundary_owned`. | Follow-up runtime-boundary audit found cross-island endpoint request publication could fail after the current fiber had been blocked and remote-wait state left behind, and remote wake helpers could still panic through `send_to_island`. Runtime transitions now publish remote island commands through a fallible phase before local resume/wake/block mutation, roll back the current remote endpoint wait on publication failure, and wake/endpoint-response helpers use the same fallible publication path. The panic helper `send_to_island` was removed. Both failing-first proofs passed with `cargo test -p vo-vm vm_runtime_transition_remote_ -- --test-threads=1`, and the existing publication source-contract proof passed. |
| VM-JIT-MATERIALIZE-CAPACITY-TXN-058 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/vm/jit/materialize.rs` | `vm_jit_prepared_call_materialize_capacity_failure_is_transactional_058`, `vm_jit_regular_call_materialize_capacity_failure_is_transactional_058`. | Follow-up JIT/frame audit found prepared and regular JIT call materialization could convert `resume_stack` into real frames or rewrite caller pc before discovering there was no room for the final callee `CallFrame`. JIT call setup now preflights `resume_stack.len() + 1` call frames and the callee stack window before materializing resume frames, and the zero-resume materializer preflights stack growth before rewriting the entry pc. Both proofs failed first with an extra materialized frame, then passed with `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit materialize_capacity_failure_is_transactional_058 -- --test-threads=1`; the JIT VM hardening gate selects them through `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`. |
| VM-JIT-REGULAR-CALL-RESOLVE-TXN-058 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/vm/jit/transition/call.rs` | `vm_jit_regular_call_resolve_failure_is_transactional_058`. | Follow-up JIT transition audit found full-function regular call side exits materialized caller resume frames and pushed the callee frame before `jit_mgr.resolve_call` could reject an unsupported or failed callee compilation. Regular and prepared call transitions now share a callee resolve preflight that runs before frame materialization, and side-exit statistics are recorded only after the transition commits successfully. The proof failed first with `frames.len()` growing from 1 to 3, then passed with `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_jit_regular_call_resolve_failure_is_transactional_058 -- --test-threads=1`; the broader transaction proof filter also passed with `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit transactional_058 -- --test-threads=1`. |
| VM-JIT-PREPARED-CALL-CALLBACK-WINDOW-TXN-058 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/vm/jit/frame.rs`, `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/vm/jit/transition/call.rs` | `vm_jit_prepared_call_resolve_failure_rolls_back_callback_window_058`. | Follow-up frame-call audit found prepared-call callbacks reserved the callee stack window and rewrote the caller pc before the VM transition could resolve or preflight the prepared callee. `jit_push_frame` no longer commits the caller pc; prepared call transition failures restore the callback-reserved `fiber.sp` from the caller `JitContext` before returning `JitError` or `FrameMaterializeError`. The proof failed first with caller pc moving from 7 to 8, then passed with `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_jit_prepared_call_resolve_failure_rolls_back_callback_window_058 -- --test-threads=1`; `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit 058 -- --test-threads=1` and JIT `vm_` hardening passed. |
| VM-JIT-REGULAR-CALL-BORROWED-BP-PREFLIGHT-058 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/vm/jit/materialize.rs` | `vm_jit_regular_call_actual_borrowed_bp_capacity_failure_is_transactional_058`. | Follow-up interpreter/frame-call audit found regular JIT call capacity preflight treated `call_arg_start` as an absolute callee bp even though the borrowed callee frame is pushed at `caller_bp + call_arg_start`. Nested resume-stack materialization could therefore rewrite frames and clear `resume_stack` before the true borrowed callee stack window overflowed. The preflight now computes the future materialized caller bp first and checks the actual borrowed callee bp before any frame mutation. The proof failed first with an extra materialized frame, then passed with `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_jit_regular_call_actual_borrowed_bp_capacity_failure_is_transactional_058 -- --test-threads=1`; the JIT 058 proof filter passed with `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit 058 -- --test-threads=1`. |
| VM-JIT-RESUME-STACK-MATERIALIZE-TXN-058 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/vm/jit/materialize.rs` | `vm_jit_materialize_resume_stack_middle_func_id_failure_is_transactional_058`. | Follow-up GC/root audit found `materialize_jit_frames` validated only the innermost resume point before committing the entry frame pc and pushing outer frames. A malformed middle resume `func_id` could therefore return `FrameMaterializeError` after exposing an uncommitted frame/root window to later scans. JIT frame materialization now validates every resume point, scan extent, and stack extent into a temporary frame list before reserving capacity and committing `frames`, `pc`, `sp`, and `resume_stack` as one unit. The proof failed first with `frames.len()` growing from 1 to 2, then passed with `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_jit_materialize_resume_stack_middle_func_id_failure_is_transactional_058 -- --test-threads=1`; the proof is selected by `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit` through the `vm_` prefix. |
| VM-RUNTIME-REMOTE-BATCH-PREFLIGHT-058 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-runtime/src/island_transport.rs`, `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/vm/types.rs`, `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/runtime_boundary.rs` | `vm_runtime_transition_remote_command_batch_failure_is_transactional_058`. | Follow-up runtime-boundary audit found remote island commands were published one by one before transition commit, so a later disconnected route could leave an earlier remote command externally visible while the local transition returned an error. `IslandSender` now exposes a route preflight hook, `VmState::can_route_to_island` consults it during transition preflight, and preflight failures roll back the current remote endpoint wait before any command is sent. The proof failed first by recording the first remote command, then passed with `cargo test -p vo-vm vm_runtime_transition_remote_ -- --test-threads=1`; non-JIT and JIT 058 filters both passed. |
| VM-RUNTIME-REMOTE-RESERVATION-TXN-058 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-runtime/src/island_transport.rs`, `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/vm/types.rs`, `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/runtime_boundary.rs` | `vm_runtime_transition_remote_command_batch_late_send_failure_is_transactional_058`, `vm_runtime_remote_reservation_058_keeps_in_thread_route_alive_until_commit`. | Follow-up runtime-boundary red-team found the preflight row still relied on `send_command` succeeding later: a sender could pass preflight, publish the first remote command, then fail the second send and return an error after external side effects. Cross-island sends now use an `IslandSendReservation` as the transaction fact source; runtime transitions reserve every remote command before local mutation, commit local resume/wake/block/spawn state, and only then publish reserved commands or external-transport outbound frames. In-thread transport keeps receivers alive while reservations are active, so recoverable route failure happens before external publication. The VM proof failed first by recording the first remote command on a late send failure, then passed with `cargo test -p vo-vm vm_runtime_transition_remote_command_batch_late_send_failure_is_transactional_058 -- --test-threads=1`; the runtime transport proof passed with `cargo test -p vo-runtime 058 -- --test-threads=1`; the `vm_` prefix and runtime 058 filter select the proofs in hardening gates. |
| VM-RUNTIME-CLOSED-SENDER-REPLAY-PREFLIGHT-058 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/runtime_boundary.rs` | `vm_runtime_transition_remote_publish_waits_for_closed_sender_replay_preflight_058`. | Follow-up runtime-boundary audit found remote command publication happened before local simple closed-sender replay validation. If the sender frame could not replay the current instruction, the remote command was already sent and the local sender was half-woken. Closed-sender wake preflight now validates simple-sender replay with the same `replay_current_instruction_policy` used at apply time, so invalid replay rejects before remote publication or scheduler mutation. The proof failed first by recording the remote command, then passed with `cargo test -p vo-vm vm_runtime_transition_remote_ -- --test-threads=1`; non-JIT and JIT 058 filters both passed. |
| VM-JIT-SIDE-EXIT-STATS-TXN-058 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/vm/jit/{transition/call.rs,transition/wait.rs}`, `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/vm/jit_mgr.rs` | `vm_jit_regular_cold_call_materialize_failure_does_not_record_side_exit_058`, `vm_jit_prepared_cold_call_materialize_failure_does_not_record_side_exit_058`, `vm_jit_special_call_materialize_failure_does_not_record_side_exit_058`, `vm_jit_waitqueue_materialize_006_*`, `vm_jit_extern_suspend_materialize_007_does_not_publish_wait_io_token_on_failure`. | Follow-up JIT/OSR audit found side-exit statistics could be recorded before a JIT-to-VM boundary actually materialized: cold callee resolution recorded `InterpretedCold` inside `JitManager::resolve_call`, and special/wait/extern-suspend transitions recorded their side-exit reason before frame materialization. `resolve_call` now only resolves and updates hotness; transition handlers record `InterpretedCold`, `RegularCall`, `PreparedDynamicCall`, `Yield`, `QueueBlock`, `WaitIo`, `WaitQueue`, and `Replay` only after materialization/setup succeeds. The proof assertions failed first with side-exit counts incrementing on `FrameMaterializeError`, then passed with `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit does_not_record_side_exit_058 -- --test-threads=1`, `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_jit_waitqueue_materialize_006 -- --test-threads=1`, and `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_jit_extern_suspend_materialize_007 -- --test-threads=1`; the proofs are selected by `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit` through the `vm_` prefix. |
| VM-JIT-EXTERN-SUSPEND-PAYLOAD-TXN-058 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/vm/jit/transition/wait.rs`, `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/fiber.rs`, `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/gc_roots.rs` | `vm_jit_extern_suspend_materialize_007_does_not_publish_wait_io_token_on_failure`. | Follow-up GC/JIT audit found `handle_extern_suspend_transition` took `fiber.jit_extern_suspend` before frame materialization. A malformed replay point could therefore return `FrameMaterializeError` after dropping the suspend payload that owns IO replay state or closure-call roots. Extern-suspend handling now clones the payload for decision-making and clears it only after materialization succeeds and the boundary or closure replay is ready to publish. The proof failed first with `fiber.jit_extern_suspend == None`, then passed with `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_jit_extern_suspend_materialize_007_does_not_publish_wait_io_token_on_failure -- --test-threads=1`; the JIT VM hardening gate selects it through the `vm_` prefix. |
| VM-RUNTIME-DUPLICATE-WAKE-TXN-058 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/runtime_boundary.rs` | `vm_runtime_transition_duplicate_simple_wake_rejects_before_partial_apply_058`, `vm_runtime_transition_mixed_simple_wake_rejects_before_partial_apply_058`. | Follow-up runtime-boundary audit found duplicate and mixed simple queue wakes for the same waiter were detected only after the first wake could make that waiter runnable, so the transition returned an error with scheduler state half-applied. Wake preflight now derives one activation key from the waiter identity for simple queue and select wakes and rejects same-waiter activations before any resume, block, wake, spawn, or remote publication work. The proofs failed first by making the first waiter runnable before the duplicate/mixed wake was rejected, then passed with `cargo test -p vo-vm 058 -- --test-threads=1` and `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit 058 -- --test-threads=1`; the `vm_` prefix selects them in VM hardening gates. |
| VM-JIT-ENTRY-SCAN-MATERIALIZE-TXN-058 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/vm/jit/materialize.rs` | `vm_jit_materialize_resume_stack_entry_scan_extent_failure_is_transactional_058`. | Follow-up GC/root audit found zero-resume JIT materialization could rewrite the existing entry frame pc and grow `sp` before validating that the existing frame scan extent stayed inside the future stack extent. `materialize_jit_frames` now preflights existing frame function ids, base pointers, local slots, scan slots, and scan extent before committing entry-frame pc or `sp`. The proof failed first with a frame/pc mutation on invariant failure, then passed with `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_jit_materialize_resume_stack_entry_scan_extent_failure_is_transactional_058 -- --test-threads=1`; the JIT VM hardening gate selects it through the `vm_` prefix. |
| VM-JIT-RESUME-RETURN-WINDOW-TXN-058 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/vm/jit/materialize.rs` | `vm_jit_materialize_resume_point_return_window_failure_is_transactional_058`. | Follow-up interpreter/verifier/frame-call audit found JIT resume-point materialization trusted the saved return destination window before proving it matched the callee return shape and the materialized caller frame. A malformed resume point could therefore materialize frames or rewrite the entry pc before later code observed the impossible return window. `materialize_jit_frames` now validates `ret_slots`, caller bp ownership, and `ret_reg + ret_slots <= caller.local_slots` for every resume point before committing frames, pc, sp, or resume-stack state. The proof failed first by accepting an out-of-frame return window, then passed with `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_jit_materialize_resume_point_return_window_failure_is_transactional_058 -- --test-threads=1`; the JIT 058 proof filter passed with `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit 058 -- --test-threads=1`. |
| VM-JIT-CALLCLOSURE-REPLAY-STATS-058 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/vm/jit/transition/wait.rs` | `vm_jit_extern_suspend_callclosure_058_records_replay_only_after_setup_success`. | Follow-up JIT extern-suspend audit found `CallClosure` replay recorded a `Replay` side exit and cleared `fiber.jit_extern_suspend` after frame materialization but before `prepare_typed_extern_closure_replay_call` succeeded, so failed closure replay setup could publish successful side-exit stats and drop the suspend payload that owns closure replay roots. The `CallClosure` branch now records `Replay` and clears the payload only in the `ExecResult::FrameChanged` arm after closure replay setup commits. The proof failed first with the replay counter incrementing and the payload missing on a nil-closure setup error, then passed with `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_jit_extern_suspend_callclosure_058_records_replay_only_after_setup_success -- --test-threads=1`; the JIT 058 proof filter passed with `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit 058 -- --test-threads=1`. |
| VM-EXTERN-REPLAY-PC-COMMIT-TXN-058 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/vm/extern_call.rs`, `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/runtime_boundary.rs` | `vm_extern_replay_validation_058_setup_failure_does_not_rewrite_pc`. | Follow-up interpreter/frame-call audit found `prepare_extern_closure_replay_call` rewrote the caller frame pc after typed-arg validation but before closure replay frame setup succeeded. Runtime-boundary resume helpers now separate frame-index selection from pc commit, and extern replay commits the caller replay pc only after `prepare_typed_extern_closure_replay_call` returns `FrameChanged`. The proof failed first with pc moving from 9 to 3 on a setup `JitError`, then passed with `cargo test -p vo-vm vm_extern_replay_validation_058_setup_failure_does_not_rewrite_pc -- --test-threads=1`; the VM hardening gate selects it through the `vm_` prefix. |
| VM-REMOTE-RESPONSE-ROLLBACK-TXN-058 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/{runtime_boundary.rs,exec/queue.rs,exec/select.rs,vm/island_shared.rs,vm/mod.rs,vm/jit/callbacks/queue.rs}`, `lang/crates/vo-runtime/src/objects/queue_state.rs` | `vm_endpoint_request_send_route_reservation_failure_preserves_queue_state_058`, `vm_queue_send_remote_direct_reservation_failure_rolls_back_receiver_058`, `vm_queue_recv_remote_ack_reservation_failure_rolls_back_sender_and_stack_058`, `vm_select_recv_remote_ack_reservation_failure_rolls_back_sender_stack_and_select_058`, `vm_pending_runtime_transition_merge_preserves_rollback_058`, `vm_jit_queue_recv_remote_ack_rollback_058_source_attaches_local_queue_rollback`. | Follow-up runtime-boundary and queue/select/JIT audit found route preflight did not hold the later response reservation. Endpoint requests, direct queue sends to remote receivers, interpreter queue/select recvs, JIT queue recv, and merged pending transitions could mutate local queue state, stack destination slots, or select state before `stage_remote_island_commands_before_commit` failed to reserve the response route. `RuntimeTransition` now owns an optional rollback snapshot for local queue state, endpoint registry state, affected current-fiber stack slots, and active select state; queue/select remote-response actions and JIT queue recv attach that snapshot, pending-transition merge preserves it, and the boundary applier restores it on preflight or staging failure. The queue/select proofs failed first by leaving consumed waiters or written stack slots behind, then passed with `cargo test -p vo-vm remote_ack_reservation_failure -- --test-threads=1`; non-JIT and JIT 058 filters passed with `cargo test -p vo-vm 058 -- --test-threads=1` and `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit 058 -- --test-threads=1`. |
| VM-REMOTE-RECV-REPLAY-PAYLOAD-TXN-058 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/vm/mod.rs`, `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/vm/jit/callbacks/queue.rs` | `vm_queue_recv_remote_replay_058_source_consumes_response_only_after_success`, `vm_jit_queue_recv_remote_replay_058_rejects_bad_payload_without_consuming_response`. | Follow-up interpreter/JIT replay audit found remote recv replay cloned validation checks for queue handles and slot widths but still consumed `fiber.remote_recv_response` before payload replay validation. A malformed or rejected payload could therefore return `JitError` after losing the pending response, diverging retry and diagnostic state. Interpreter and JIT queue recv now replay from a cloned response and clear `fiber.remote_recv_response` only after replay succeeds. The JIT behavior proof failed first with the response missing after a bad payload, then passed under `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit 058 -- --test-threads=1`; the interpreter source-contract proof is selected by `cargo test -p vo-vm 058 -- --test-threads=1`. |
| VM-ROLLBACK-GC-DIRTY-058 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/runtime_boundary.rs`, `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/vm/island_shared.rs` | `vm_runtime_rollback_gc_dirty_058_restore_marks_all_roots_dirty`, `vm_endpoint_request_rollback_gc_dirty_058_manual_restore_marks_all_roots_dirty`. | Follow-up GC/root audit found rollback restore could reintroduce queue, endpoint-registry, stack-slot, or select-state roots after the transition's `GcRootEffect` accounting point, and the endpoint-request manual snapshot restore had the same dirty-root gap. Runtime rollback restore now marks all VM roots dirty after restoring snapshots, and the endpoint-request manual restore does the same after queue/registry restoration. The mechanical source-contract proofs lock both restore owners; they passed in `cargo test -p vo-vm 058 -- --test-threads=1` and `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit 058 -- --test-threads=1`. |
| VM-FINAL-GATE-TIMEOUT-SOURCE-058 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `cmd/vo-dev/src/{task_runner.rs,lint_system.rs}`, `eng/tasks.toml` | `lint_selected_gate_tasks_have_timeouts_rejects_unbounded_final_signoff_selector_task_058`. | Follow-up readiness gate audit found final signoff evidence used four selectors (`contract`, `vm-production`, `site`, and `release-verify`) while the selected-task timeout lint only checked `contract` and `vm-production`. The final selector list is now shared from `task_runner`, timeout lint iterates the same fact source, and every `site`/`release-verify` task has a bounded `timeout_sec`. The proof failed first because an unbounded `site` task was accepted, then passed with `cargo test -p vo-dev lint_selected_gate_tasks_have_timeouts -- --test-threads=1`; `cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- lint tasks --strict` and `task plan site`, `task plan release-verify`, `task plan contract`, and `task plan vm-production` all passed. |
| VM-EXTERN-RETURN-LAYOUT-058 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-codegen/src/{context.rs,expr/conversion.rs,expr/builtin.rs,expr/dyn_access.rs,stmt/dyn_assign.rs}`, `lang/crates/vo-codegen/tests/integration.rs` | `module_verifier_rejects_precise_call_extern_return_with_slots_only_shape_058`, `string_conversion_uses_gc_ref_source_and_destination_slots`, `discarded_dynamic_calls_encode_callee_return_layout`, `scheduled_interface_wrappers_encode_callee_return_layout_but_discard_results`. | Follow-up `vm-production` OSR run failed strict verifier checks because builtin externs such as `vo_conv_str_bytes` and dynamic helpers were registered through slots-only `ReturnShape`s while callsites carried precise `CallExternLayout.ret_layout`. Codegen now registers these callsite-shaped builtin externs through one precise return-layout owner and rejects/merges incompatible layouts at extern registration instead of letting same-name slots-only declarations survive. The verifier proof rejects slots-only precise returns, and codegen integration tests prove conversion, dynamic discard, scheduled wrapper, spread append, and dynamic access helpers emit extern table layouts that match callsite metadata. Focused validation passed `cargo test -p vo-common-core 058 -- --test-threads=1`, `cargo test -p vo-codegen --test integration string_conversion_uses_gc_ref_source_and_destination_slots -- --test-threads=1`, and full `cargo test -p vo-codegen -- --test-threads=1`; `vm-production` selects the common-core proof through `runtime-contract` and the codegen proofs through `codegen-contract`. |
| VM-DYNAMIC-EXTERN-PARAM-ABI-058 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-codegen/src/{context.rs,expr/dyn_access.rs}`, `lang/crates/vo-codegen/tests/integration.rs` | `dynamic_field_and_index_externs_are_keyed_by_precise_return_layout_058`, `variable_ret_dynamic_externs_preserve_exact_parameter_abi_058`. | Follow-up JIT/extern ABI audit found the variable-return extern helper preserved precise `ReturnShape` variants for `dyn_field` and `dyn_index` but downgraded their fixed parameter ABI to `CallSiteVariadic` with empty `param_kinds`. The variable-return registration path now reuses the same builtin parameter contract as ordinary dynamic access registration, so `dyn_field` keeps exact five-slot args with field-name bytes and `dyn_index` keeps exact six-slot args for every same-name return-layout variant. The integration proof failed first on variadic params, then passed with `cargo test -p vo-codegen --test integration dynamic_field_and_index_externs_are_keyed_by_precise_return_layout_058 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; the helper proof passed earlier with `cargo test -p vo-codegen --lib variable_ret_dynamic_externs_preserve_exact_parameter_abi_058 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, but later `vo-codegen` lib test binary startup/listing hung in the local target, so final evidence relies on the integration proof and records the lib harness issue as not passed in this final pass. |
| VM-SLICE-APPEND-UNREACHABLE-META-058 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-common-core/src/verifier.rs`, `lang/crates/vo-codegen/tests/integration.rs` | `deferred_loop_closure_slice_append_metadata_survives_verifier_058`. | Follow-up verifier audit found unreachable dead code after a panic in a deferred-loop closure could contain `SliceAppend` metadata registers that no longer had reachable constant facts, causing the module verifier to reject codegen output even though the instruction was not executable. Constant-fact-dependent metadata checks now skip only unreachable program counters while preserving all reachable layout/storage checks. The proof failed first with `CallShapeMismatch` on `SliceAppend element metadata register` and passed with `cargo test -p vo-codegen --test integration deferred_loop_closure_slice_append_metadata_survives_verifier_058 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; the focused OSR language rerun was attempted through `./d.py test osr ...` and direct `vo-dev test run --targets osr --verbose`, but both were interrupted after long no-output startup and are not counted as passing. |
| VM-RETURN-ERROR-FLAG-SLOT-058 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-common-core/src/verifier.rs` | `module_verifier_rejects_error_return_flag_without_error_ret_slot_058`, `module_verifier_rejects_heap_error_return_flag_without_error_ret_slot_058`. | Follow-up verifier/frame-call audit found `ReturnFlags::ERROR_RETURN` was validated only as a legal bit, not as a function-frame contract requiring `FunctionDef.error_ret_slot`. The verifier now rejects both stack and heap return instructions whose error-return flag is set without a declared error return slot, before any VM/JIT backend can interpret an impossible return frame. Both mechanical module proofs failed first by accepting malformed modules, then passed with exact `vo-common-core` filters under `--nocapture`. |
| VM-RUNTIME-REMOTE-WAKE-STAGING-058 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/runtime_boundary.rs` | `vm_runtime_transition_remote_wake_batch_failure_is_transactional_058`. | Follow-up runtime-boundary audit found remote queue wakes still published through `VmState::wake_*` during `apply_runtime_wake`, outside the existing staged `IslandCommandEffect` reservation/commit pipeline. A transition with an earlier local wake and a later remote route failure could therefore leave the local waiter runnable while returning an error. Runtime transitions now split remote queue wakes into `IslandCommandEffect`s and stage them with the same reservation batch as other island commands before applying resume, local wakes, tombstones, spawns, or commits. The proof failed first with the local waiter in `Runnable`, then passed with `cargo test -p vo-vm --lib vm_runtime_transition_remote_wake_batch_failure_is_transactional_058 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; the existing remote command batch proof also passed. |
| VM-EXTERN-REPLAY-SCOPE-058 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/vm/{mod.rs,jit/extern_call.rs}` | `vm_extern_replay_validation_058_callclosure_setup_failure_closes_replay_scope`, `vm_extern_replay_validation_058_debug_return_validation_failure_closes_replay_scope`, `vm_jit_extern_replay_validation_058_callclosure_setup_failure_closes_replay_scope`. | Follow-up interpreter/JIT audit found extern `CallClosure` setup failures preserved the active closure replay extern scope after returning a terminal VM/JIT error. The interpreter now closes the extern replay scope on non-`FrameChanged` setup results and on debug return-validation failure, and the JIT extern bridge closes it before reporting typed replay setup errors. The interpreter and JIT proofs failed first with `extern_scope` still active, then passed with focused `vo-vm` filters; the debug validation proof was already green and remains a regression guard. |
| VM-CI-CODEGEN-LANG-ROUTE-058 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `eng/ci.toml`, `cmd/vo-dev/src/lint_system.rs` | `lint_vm_readiness_changed_prefixes_requires_codegen_osr_route_058`, `lint_vm_readiness_changed_prefixes_requires_lang_tests_route_058`, `lint_vm_readiness_changed_prefixes_requires_lang_tests_full_matrix_route_058`. | Follow-up gate audit found codegen changes could miss OSR-only regressions and `tests/lang/**` changes had no explicit full-matrix route. Changed-mode policy now routes `lang/crates/vo-codegen/**` to `vo-test-osr` and routes `tests/lang/**` to `test-data-lint`, `vo-test`, `vo-test-osr`, `vo-test-nostd`, `vo-test-wasm`, and `vo-test-gc`; task lint rejects removing those routes. The lint proofs passed with exact `vo-dev` filters, and `cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- task plan pr --changed` completed and selected the new tasks in the actual changed-file plan. |
| VM-HEAP-RETURN-LAYOUT-059 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-common-core/src/verifier.rs`, `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/vm/jit/invoke.rs` | `module_verifier_rejects_non_final_error_ret_slot_058`, `module_verifier_rejects_heap_return_slot_sum_drift_058`, `module_verifier_rejects_error_ret_slot_without_interface_layout_058`, `vm_jit_heap_error_return_uses_final_heap_ref_058`. | Final verifier/JIT red-team audit found module loading accepted `error_ret_slot` values that were not the final two return slots, accepted heap-return logical slot sums that drifted from `ret_slots`, and let JIT heap-return errdefer checks interpret logical `error_ret_slot` as a heap-ref index. The module verifier now requires heap return widths to sum to `ret_slots` and requires `error_ret_slot` to be the final `Interface0/Interface1` pair; the JIT heap-return check derives the error ref from the final heap return, matching interpreter semantics. The common-core proofs failed first by accepting malformed modules, then passed with focused filters; the JIT source-contract proof passed with `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_jit_heap_error_return_uses_final_heap_ref_058 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`. |
| VM-HEAP-ERROR-PARTITION-059 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-common-core/src/verifier.rs` | `vm_module_verifier_rejects_heap_error_return_partition_drift_059`. | Red-team rerun found the heap-return layout fix still let the final heap-return ref be interpreted as the error object even when `heap_ret_slots` partitioned logical return slots before `error_ret_slot`. The module verifier now requires heap-return partitions with `error_ret_slot` to start the final heap partition exactly at `error_ret_slot` and to give that final partition width two, so malformed heap error-return layouts are rejected at module load instead of leaving the JIT/runtime to infer an error ref. The proof isolates that contract with `heap_ret_slots=[1,3]` and `error_ret_slot=2`, avoiding the separate interface-pair partition invariant, and passed with `cargo test -p vo-common-core vm_module_verifier_rejects_heap_error_return_partition_drift_059 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; related 058 heap-return and JIT heap-error-return focused proofs were rerun and passed. |
| VM-BUILTIN-EXTERN-PARAM-LAYOUT-059 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-common-core/src/{bytecode.rs,verifier.rs}`, `lang/crates/vo-codegen/src/context.rs`, `lang/crates/vo-codegen/src/expr/dyn_access.rs`, `lang/crates/vo-codegen/tests/integration.rs` | `vm_module_verifier_rejects_builtin_exact_extern_param_layout_drift_059`, `dynamic_field_and_index_externs_are_keyed_by_precise_return_layout_058`, `dynamic_call_and_method_externs_keep_callsite_param_abi_058`. | Red-team rerun found exact builtin externs such as `panic_with_error` could still be declared with the right slot count but an empty or wrong parameter layout, while callsites carried precise `CallExternLayout.arg_layout`. Builtin extern parameter layout is now a shared bytecode fact source consumed by codegen registration and module verification, and `CallExtern` verification rejects known builtin calls whose encoded argument layout drifts from that ABI. The verifier proof failed first by accepting an Exact(2) `panic_with_error` with `[Value, Value]` call layout, then passed with `cargo test -p vo-common-core vm_module_verifier_rejects_builtin_exact_extern_param_layout_drift_059 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; dynamic extern integration proofs were rerun and passed after moving the fixed-layout facts to the shared owner. |
| VM-CLOSURE-REPLAY-HEAP-RETURN-METADATA-059 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/exec/unwind.rs` | `vm_closure_replay_heap_return_metadata_059_uses_return_layout_not_partition_offsets`. | Red-team rerun found closure replay of heap-return results derived GC slot metadata by treating `FunctionDef.heap_ret_slots` as local slot offsets even though the bytecode contract defines them as per-heap-return widths. Valid heap-return replay could therefore reject with a false metadata error or cache the wrong root layout in `fiber.closure_replay.results`. Replay metadata now derives from `ret_slot_types`, with the heap partition widths only used to read heap boxes. The proof failed first by attempting to read `slot_types[3..6]` for a one-ref three-slot return, then passed with `cargo test -p vo-vm vm_closure_replay_heap_return_metadata_059_uses_return_layout_not_partition_offsets -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; the broader `cargo test -p vo-vm closure_replay -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture` replay filter passed 8/8. |
| VM-EXTERN-REPLAY-ARG-ROOT-059 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/frame_call.rs`, `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/vm/jit/extern_call.rs`, `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/vm/jit/transition/wait.rs` | `vm_extern_replay_closure_rejects_invalid_gcref_arg_before_frame_push_059`, `typed_extern_replay_args_rejects_invalid_gcref_arg_059`, `typed_extern_replay_args_rejects_invalid_interface_gcref_arg_059`. | Red-team rerun found `ExternResult::CallClosure` replay args were typed by target slot metadata but raw `GcRef` and interface data slots were not canonicalized or rejected before they could be copied into a frame or stored in JIT extern suspend state, both of which are GC-visible roots. The typed replay payload owner now validates and canonicalizes `GcRef` and GC-backed interface data before constructing or publishing `TypedSlotPayload`, and the frame publication boundary revalidates typed payloads before copying into the callee frame. The proofs failed first by accepting `0xdead_beef` as a root-shaped arg, then passed with focused `vo-vm` filters; the existing JIT replay-scope proof also passed with `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_jit_extern_replay_validation_058_callclosure_setup_failure_closes_replay_scope -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`. |
| VM-JIT-EXTERN-SUSPEND-RESET-ROOTS-059 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/fiber.rs`, `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/gc_roots.rs` | `vm_fiber_reset_clears_jit_extern_suspend_roots_059`. | Final GC/root red-team rerun found `Fiber::reset()` cleared ordinary replay, panic, resume, wait, and IC state but left `jit_extern_suspend` intact. A dead fiber reused through the scheduler could therefore expose stale JIT extern suspend `closure_ref` and typed args as roots in the next fiber lifetime. Fiber reset now clears `jit_extern_suspend` with the rest of the per-run GC-visible state. The proof failed first by preserving a seeded `CallClosure` suspend payload through reset, then passed with `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_fiber_reset_clears_jit_extern_suspend_roots_059 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; the JIT `vo-vm 059` filter passed 6/6. |
| VM-HEAP-RETURN-READ-WIDTH-059 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-common-core/src/verifier.rs`, `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/exec/unwind.rs` | `vm_module_verifier_rejects_heap_return_partition_splitting_interface_pair_059`, `vm_heap_return_reader_rejects_short_allocation_before_deref_059`. | Final verifier/frame-call red-team rerun found heap-return partition metadata was accepted for non-error interface pairs split across separate heap refs, and runtime heap-return readers canonicalized refs but did not prove each heap object was wide enough before unsafe slot reads. The verifier now validates each `heap_ret_slots` partition against the corresponding `ret_slot_types` slice so interface pairs cannot be split, and both heap-return readers check allocation byte width before dereferencing. The verifier proof failed first by accepting `ret_slot_types=[Interface0, Interface1]` with `heap_ret_slots=[1,1]`; the runtime proof failed first by reading a one-slot allocation as a two-slot return. Focused validation passed `cargo test -p vo-common-core heap_return -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, `cargo test -p vo-vm 059 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, and `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit 059 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`. |
| VM-ERRDEFER-HEAP-RETURN-WIDTH-059 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/exec/unwind.rs` | `vm_errdefer_heap_return_check_rejects_short_error_allocation_before_deref_059`. | Narrow unwind red-team found `compute_include_errdefers` still had a heap-error return side path: before collecting deferred heap returns, it peeked at the error heap box slot0 to decide whether errdefers should run, bypassing the shared heap-return width validation. The errdefer check now uses the same heap-return partition metadata and validates the final error heap allocation width before reading slot0. The proof exercises the real `handle_return` entry with an errdefer, a declared two-slot error interface, and a one-slot heap box; it passed with `cargo test -p vo-vm vm_errdefer_heap_return_check_rejects_short_error_allocation_before_deref_059 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`. Follow-up focused gates passed `cargo test -p vo-vm 059 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture` (6/6), `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit 059 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture` (7/7), `cargo test -p vo-common-core heap_return -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, `node scripts/ci/docs_lint.mjs`, and `cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- lint tasks --strict`. |
| VM-JIT-ERRDEFER-HEAP-RETURN-WIDTH-059 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/{exec/unwind.rs,vm/jit/invoke.rs}` | `vm_jit_ok_errdefer_heap_return_check_rejects_short_error_allocation_before_defer_059`. | Follow-up GC/root red-team found the JIT `JitResult::Ok` path still had its own heap-error return slot0 peek for errdefer selection. It canonicalized the final heap return ref but did not validate the declared error interface width or allocation size before `unsafe` slot0 read, bypassing the interpreter helper fixed above. The JIT path now calls the same `read_heap_error_return_slot0_for_errdefer` owner used by interpreter unwinding, so heap error errdefer decisions share one canonicalize-and-width contract. The JIT proof failed first by returning `FrameChanged` and entering errdefer handling with a one-slot error box declared as a two-slot interface; after the helper unification it returned `JitError` before frame mutation. Focused validation passed `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_jit_ok_errdefer_heap_return_check_rejects_short_error_allocation_before_defer_059 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture` and `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit 059 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture` (8/8). |
| VM-DYNAMIC-CALL-PARAM-ABI-059 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-codegen/src/{context.rs,expr/dyn_access.rs}`, `lang/crates/vo-codegen/tests/integration.rs` | `dynamic_call_and_method_externs_keep_callsite_param_abi_058`. | Final JIT/ABI red-team audit found `dyn_call` and `dyn_method` still registered variable-return externs as naked `CallSiteVariadic` even though each callsite has a structured argument ABI: `dyn_call` uses `4 + 2*N` slots and `dyn_method` uses `5 + 2*N` slots. Variable-return extern identity now includes callsite parameter shape and `param_kinds` for these dynamic call externs, so the verifier and JIT bridge see exact arg-slot counts instead of arbitrary variadic acceptance. The integration proof failed first on missing exact `dyn_call` params, then passed with `cargo test -p vo-codegen --test integration dynamic_call_and_method_externs_keep_callsite_param_abi_058 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`. |
| VM-RUNTIME-LOCAL-SPAWN-COMMAND-059 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/runtime_boundary.rs` | `vm_runtime_transition_local_spawn_command_failure_is_transactional_058`. | Final runtime-boundary red-team audit found same-island `IslandCommandEffect::SpawnFiber` could bypass `transition.spawns`: `apply_runtime_transition` staged remote commands but allowed a local malformed spawn command to fail only after earlier local wakes had committed. Same-island spawn command effects are now rejected during transition preflight; local spawns must use the transactional `transition.spawns` owner. The proof failed first with the local waiter becoming `Runnable`, then passed with `cargo test -p vo-vm vm_runtime_transition_local_spawn_command_failure_is_transactional_058 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`. |
| VM-QUEUE-CLOSE-REMOTE-ROLLBACK-059 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/{exec/queue.rs,runtime_boundary.rs,vm/mod.rs,vm/jit/callbacks/queue.rs}` | `vm_queue_close_endpoint_late_reservation_failure_rolls_back_local_state_059`, `vm_queue_close_remote_proxy_late_reservation_failure_rolls_back_closed_059`. | Runtime-boundary red-team found `QueueClose` mutated queue state before remote close publication was reserved: local endpoint queues were closed and waiters drained, and remote proxies were marked closed, but the resulting close transitions carried no rollback if `reserve_send_to_island` failed after route preflight. `RuntimeRollback` now covers both local queue snapshots and remote proxy closed-state snapshots, `queue_close_core` attaches rollback to `Close` and `RemoteClose`, and interpreter/JIT transition builders install that rollback before staging remote close wakes or close requests. The proofs use a sender that passes preflight and fails reservation, then assert local closed bits, waiters, endpoint registry, remote proxy closed state, and outbound command state roll back. Focused validation passed `cargo test -p vo-vm vm_queue_close_ -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, `cargo test -p vo-vm 059 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture` (8/8), and `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit 059 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture` (10/10). |
| VM-CI-CHANGED-PR-SCOPE-059 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `cmd/vo-dev/src/lint_system.rs` | `lint_vm_readiness_changed_prefixes_requires_workflow_docs_lint_route_059`, `lint_vm_readiness_changed_prefixes_requires_pr_scope_for_required_tasks_059`. | Final test/gate red-team audit found VM readiness changed-prefix lint proved required tasks were listed on prefixes but did not prove those tasks were inside the `pr --changed` selector scope, and workflow route coverage lacked a direct `docs-lint` deletion proof. Task lint now derives the canonical `pr` selector closure through `resolve_selector` and rejects any required VM-readiness changed-mode task outside that scope; workflow changed-mode route coverage now has symmetric `docs-lint` and `ci-self-check` proofs. Both focused `vo-dev` proofs passed after the fix. |
| VM-DOCS-LINT-FIXTURE-059 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `cmd/vo-dev/src/lint_system.rs` | `lint_docs_contract_requires_docs_lint`, `lint_docs_contract_requires_docs_lint_source_fact_inputs`, `lint_docs_contract_requires_docs_lint_studio_wasm_source_input`, `lint_docs_contract_requires_docs_lint_studio_wasm_types_input`. | Test/gate red-team rerun found the real `docs-lint` task and lint contract both required `apps/studio/src/lib/studio_wasm.ts`, but the `docs_lint_task()` test fixture omitted that input, making the selected `cargo-test-vo-dev` proof gate fail even though the task manifest was correct. The fixture now mirrors the required Studio WASM type input, restoring the docs-contract proof.
