Focused validation passed `cargo test -p vo-dev lint_docs_contract_requires_docs_lint -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture` with 4/4 tests. |
| VM-READINESS-OPEN-STATUS-LINT-059 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `scripts/ci/docs_lint.mjs` | `selfTestVmProductionReadinessSignoffLint` hidden open status variants. | Test/gate red-team found readiness docs lint treated only `Open, ...` statuses as active blockers even though the ledger contract says rows marked `Open` remain blockers. A future row written as `Open - pending`, `Open (pending)`, or bare `Open` after `### Superseded` could therefore evade the restored-signoff guard. Docs lint now parses VM blocker table rows and classifies the status cell by an `Open` word boundary, sharing that fact source for hidden blockers, the startup blocker row, and current open-blocker signoff checks. The failing-first self-test rejected `Open - pending gate`, `Open (pending gate)`, and bare `Open` only after the helper fix; `node scripts/ci/docs_lint.mjs` passed afterward. |
| VM-IFACE-ASSIGN-BOXED-NIL-LAYOUT-058 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-common-core/src/verifier.rs`, `lang/crates/vo-codegen/tests/integration.rs` | `vm_iface_assign_receiver_layout_058_accepts_boxed_value_receiver_wrapper`, `vm_iface_assign_receiver_layout_058_nil_source_does_not_validate_itab_zero`, `discarded_dynamic_calls_encode_callee_return_layout`, `scheduled_interface_wrappers_encode_callee_return_layout_but_discard_results`. | Follow-up OSR/common verifier audit found the new `IfaceAssign` receiver-layout check treated boxed value-receiver interface wrappers as if their target function should receive the concrete value layout, and also interpreted nil/Void interface assignment metadata as itab zero. The verifier now derives boxed wrapper receiver ABI from `MethodInfo.receiver_is_iface_boxed` and treats Void source metadata as the nil-interface sentinel instead of validating itab zero. The boxed proof failed first with a `GcRef` receiver mismatch, the nil/dynamic integration paths failed first by validating `Void` metadata against itab zero, and both now pass with `cargo test -p vo-common-core 058 -- --test-threads=1` plus the two focused `vo-codegen` integration filters; full `cargo test -p vo-codegen -- --test-threads=1` also passed. The `vm_` proof names are selected by common-core hardening, and the integration proofs are selected by `codegen-contract` in `vm-production`. |
| VM-ZERO-BYTE-ELEM-LAYOUT-058 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-codegen/src/func.rs`, `lang/crates/vo-common-core/src/verifier.rs`, `lang/crates/vo-jit/src/metadata.rs`, `lang/crates/vo-codegen/tests/integration.rs` | `zero_byte_elem_metadata_preserves_logical_slot_layout`, `module_verifier_checks_instruction_metadata_layout_shape`, `metadata_facts_allow_zero_size_empty_struct_logical_layout`, `anonymous_empty_struct_slice_reuses_single_struct_meta_identity`. | Follow-up codegen gate validation found `make([]struct{}, n)` emitted runtime `StructMeta` layout `[Value]` for the empty-struct sentinel while `ElemLayout` metadata collapsed the zero-byte element to an empty slot layout; after codegen preserved `[Value]`, the common verifier and strict JIT still carried the older assumption that `elem_bytes == 0` required an empty logical layout. Element metadata now carries two facts: `elem_bytes` owns physical sequence width, while `slot_layout` owns the logical runtime element layout. Common verifier compares runtime metadata against logical layout for `ArrayNew`/`SliceNew`/`SliceAppend`, but indexed get/set physical read/write ranges still derive from `elem_bytes`. JIT metadata accepts the zero-byte `[Value]` empty-struct sentinel while reporting physical `slots=0`. Proofs passed with `cargo test -p vo-codegen --lib zero_byte_elem_metadata_preserves_logical_slot_layout -- --test-threads=1`, `cargo test -p vo-common-core module_verifier_checks_instruction_metadata_layout_shape -- --test-threads=1`, `cargo test -p vo-jit metadata_facts -- --test-threads=1`, the focused `anonymous_empty_struct_slice_reuses_single_struct_meta_identity` integration test, and full `cargo test -p vo-codegen -- --test-threads=1`. `vm-production` selects these through common-core, JIT, and codegen contract tasks. |
| VM-JIT-CALLEXTERN-CALLCLOSURE-PANIC-SCOPE-059 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/vm/jit/transition/wait.rs` | `vm_jit_extern_suspend_callclosure_panic_branch_closes_terminal_scope_059`. | Red-team rerun found JIT `ExternSuspend(CallClosure)` kept `fiber.jit_extern_suspend` and the active closure replay extern scope when replay setup returned a recoverable `ExecResult::Panic`, unlike the interpreter terminal replay path. The JIT transition now treats recoverable `Panic` as terminal for the suspend payload, clears `jit_extern_suspend`, and calls `finish_extern_terminal()` while preserving existing `JitError` setup-failure rooting semantics. The source-contract proof failed first on the missing cleanup branch, then passed with `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_jit_extern_suspend_callclosure_panic_branch_closes_terminal_scope_059 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`. |
| VM-SAME-NAME-EXTERN-ABI-059 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-common-core/src/verifier.rs` | `vm_module_verifier_rejects_same_name_extern_param_shape_drift_059`, `vm_module_verifier_allows_dynamic_variable_return_extern_shapes_059`. | Red-team rerun found a hand-written module could declare two ordinary externs with the same provider name but incompatible parameter or return ABI shapes; runtime resolution would bind both ids to the same provider name and trust each bytecode ABI independently. The common module verifier now rejects same-name ordinary extern ABI drift before runtime resolution, while retaining the VM-owned dynamic extern exception for `dyn_call`, `dyn_method`, `dyn_field`, and `dyn_index`. The ordinary-host proof failed first by accepting `host_shape` with `Exact(1)` and `Exact(2)` params, then passed; the dynamic exception proof passed. Focused validation passed `cargo test -p vo-common-core vm_module_verifier_ -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture` with 5/5 tests. |
| VM-JIT-ENV-HERMETIC-059 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `cmd/vo-test/src/main.rs` | `vm_test_runner_jit_jobs_restore_all_runner_owned_jit_env_059`, `vm_test_runner_apply_job_env_clears_unset_jit_env_059`. | Test/gate red-team found `vo-test` jobs inherited parent-process `VO_JIT_LOOP_THRESHOLD` and `VO_JIT_DEBUG` whenever a target env set only `VO_JIT_CALL_THRESHOLD`, so local or CI ambient env could alter JIT/GC-JIT behavior. The job runner now treats known `VO_JIT_*` keys as runner-owned for JIT jobs: it saves them, removes them before applying target env, and restores them afterward. The key-selection proof failed first on missing loop/debug keys, then both env proofs passed with `cargo test -p vo-test vm_test_runner_ -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`. |
| VM-HARDENING-UNFILTERED-GATE-059 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `eng/tasks.toml`, `cmd/vo-dev/src/lint_system.rs` | `lint_vm_hardening_tasks_accepts_unfiltered_crate_tests_059`, `lint_vm_hardening_tasks_rejects_name_filtered_crate_tests_059`, `cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- lint tasks --strict`. | Test/gate red-team found `vm-hardening` still relied on `vm_` cargo filters, so proof tests outside the naming convention or outside the scanned JIT subtree could drift out of the hardening gate even when full crate contract gates would run them. The hardening tasks now run the corresponding full crate unit suites (`vo-common-core`, `vo-jit`, `vo-vm`, and `vo-vm --features jit`) and task lint rejects reintroducing name filters. The strict task lint failed first on `cargo-test-common-core-hardening` carrying `vm_`, then passed after the task commands were made unfiltered; the focused vo-dev lint proofs also passed. |
| VM-DYNAMIC-EXTERN-PREFIX-060 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-common-core/src/verifier.rs` | `vm_module_verifier_rejects_dyn_call_short_variadic_argument_prefix_060`, `vm_module_verifier_rejects_dyn_method_short_exact_argument_prefix_060`. | Red-team verifier audit found VM-owned `dyn_call` / `dyn_method` same-name extern exceptions were name-based but did not enforce the runtime protocol prefix for adversarial bytecode. The shared module verifier now rejects too-short or malformed dynamic-call extern layouts at load time while still allowing VM-owned variable return shapes. Both proofs failed first on malformed hand-built modules, then passed with `cargo test -p vo-common-core vm_module_verifier_rejects_dyn_ -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`. |
| VM-DYNAMIC-MAP-STRING-FIELD-TYPE-060 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-runtime/src/builtins/dynamic.rs` | `dynamic_container_setters_use_container_layout_as_storage_fact_058`. | Red-team GC/dynamic audit found string-key map field writes validated storage width and barriers but not the source value kind, unlike indexed map writes; a wrong value could be stored under map value metadata and later scanned as the wrong GC layout. Dynamic map and slice writes now share `validate_dynamic_container_value_type` before unboxing, barriers, or `set_checked`. The source-contract proof failed first on the missing shared validation and passed with `cargo test -p vo-runtime dynamic_container_setters_use_container_layout_as_storage_fact_058 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`. |
| VM-JIT-PANIC-SETUP-PAYLOAD-060 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/vm/jit/panic_setup.rs` | `vm_jit_panic_setup_materializes_before_consuming_recoverable_payload_060`. | Red-team JIT audit found `setup_jit_panic` consumed the recoverable panic payload before frame materialization, so a materialization failure could drop a newly allocated runtime trap message from the fiber root set. Panic setup now peeks at the recoverable payload for location selection and consumes it only after frame materialization succeeds, preserving payload rooting on setup failures. The source proof failed first on the old ordering and passed with `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_jit_panic_setup_materializes_before_consuming_recoverable_payload_060 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`. |
| VM-JIT-HELPER-ABI-MANIFEST-060 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-jit/src/helpers.rs`, `lang/crates/vo-jit/src/translator.rs` | `declared_helper_imports_cover_runtime_helper_abi_manifest_060`. | Red-team JIT/ABI audit found the runtime helper ABI manifest contained helper rows that the JIT import table did not declare, leaving a soft split between the manifest and import surface. The JIT helper table now declares every runtime helper ABI row, including `vo_gc_safepoint` and `vo_iface_pack_slot0`, and the reverse coverage proof rejects future manifest/import drift. The proof failed first on `vo_gc_safepoint` and passed with `cargo test -p vo-jit declared_helper_imports_cover_runtime_helper_abi_manifest_060 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`. |
| VM-VO-TEST-GATE-COVERAGE-060 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `eng/tasks.toml`, `cmd/vo-dev/src/lint_system.rs` | `lint_vo_dev_contract_requires_vo_test_runner_tests_060`, `vm_test_runner_jit_jobs_restore_all_runner_owned_jit_env_059`, `vm_test_runner_apply_job_env_clears_unset_jit_env_059`. | Test/gate red-team found the `vo-test` JIT env hermetic proofs passed locally but were not selected by the final `contract` / `vm-production` gates. `cargo-test-vo-test` is now a first-class contract task inside `vo-dev-contract`, and strict lint requires that contract group to select the runner tests. The task lint failed first with `vo-dev-contract must select cargo-test-vo-test`, then passed; `contract` and `vm-production` task plans now include `cargo-test-vo-test`, and `cargo test -p vo-test vm_test_runner_ -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture` passed. |
| VM-FINAL-SELECTOR-FACT-SOURCE-060 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `cmd/vo-dev/src/{task_runner.rs,task_system.rs,lint_system.rs}`, `scripts/ci/docs_lint.mjs`, `eng/tasks.toml` | `final_selectors_json_schema_is_stable_060`, `lint_docs_contract_requires_docs_lint_final_selector_source_input_060`, `node scripts/ci/docs_lint.mjs`. | Second-round test/gate red-team found final signoff selectors were duplicated between Rust and JS: `vo-dev` owned task-run evidence selectors, while docs lint had its own hard-coded list. `vo-dev task final-selectors --format json` now exposes the Rust selector list as the single fact source, docs lint resolves selectors through that command, and `docs-lint` inputs include the Rust owner files so changed-mode routing sees selector changes. The CLI failed first with `unknown task command: final-selectors`; after the fix the JSON command, Rust schema/input proofs, strict task lint, and docs lint passed. |
| VM-DYNAMIC-CONTAINER-NIL-ASSIGN-060 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-runtime/src/builtins/dynamic.rs` | `dynamic_container_value_type_rejects_nil_for_concrete_scalar_values_060`, `dynamic_container_setters_use_container_layout_as_storage_fact_058`. | Second-round GC/dynamic red-team found the new dynamic map value check still let a nil interface bypass concrete container value validation, and slice writes had a sibling nil/type check path. Dynamic map string-key, map index, and slice index writes now share one container assignability helper: nil is accepted only for interface/nillable reference-like targets and rejected for concrete scalar/aggregate values before unboxing or barriers. The nil proof failed first on `map[string]int`-style scalar assignment and passed after the shared helper; the source-contract proof confirms map and slice writers use it. |
| VM-RAW-MAP-ENTRY-WIDTH-060 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-runtime/src/{objects/map.rs,ffi/mod.rs,ffi/containers.rs,jit_api.rs}`, `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/exec/map.rs` | `raw_map_set_checked_rejects_key_value_width_drift_060`, `runtime_map_surfaces_use_checked_map_api_and_pre_set_barriers_048`, existing `exec_map_get_rejects_runtime_value_width_drift_before_stack_write_035`. | Second-round GC/dynamic red-team found safe FFI map writers could publish malformed no-ref aggregate entries because `set_checked` accepted short value slices and FFI wrappers ran barriers before any width validation. Raw map publication now enforces key/value slot counts at `map::set_checked`, and FFI safe wrappers call the same `validate_entry_slot_counts` before barriers. VM/JIT callers distinguish slot-count mismatch from unhashable interface keys instead of mapping layout drift to missing/no-op. The raw-map proof failed first by accepting a short value and wide key; the FFI source proof failed first on missing barrier-before-width validation, then both passed. |
| VM-DYNAMIC-NIL-ASSIGNABILITY-060 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-runtime/src/builtins/dynamic.rs` | `dynamic_nil_assignability_matches_checker_value_kinds_060`, `dynamic_container_setters_use_container_layout_as_storage_fact_058`. | Final GC/dynamic red-team found the dynamic runtime nil rule still diverged from checker assignability: strings accepted nil, while channel/port/island targets were rejected. Dynamic struct-field and container writes now share `nil_assignable_to_value_kind`, accepting nil only for interface, pointer, slice, map, closure, channel, port, and island targets, and rejecting nil for strings and concrete scalar/aggregate values before unboxing or barriers. The behavior proof failed first on string nil acceptance and passed after the shared helper update; focused validation passed `cargo test -p vo-runtime --lib dynamic_nil_assignability_matches_checker_value_kinds_060 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, `cargo test -p vo-runtime --lib dynamic_container_setters_use_container_layout_as_storage_fact_058 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, and `cargo test -p vo-runtime --lib 060 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture` (4/4). |
| VM-FFI-MAP-TYPED-LAYOUT-060 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-runtime/src/{ffi/mod.rs,ffi/containers.rs}` | `ffi_vo_map_string_gcref_rejects_scalar_value_map_before_mutation_060`, `ffi_map_set_string_key_rejects_non_string_key_map_before_mutation_060`, `runtime_map_surfaces_use_checked_map_api_and_pre_set_barriers_048`. | Final GC/dynamic red-team found FFI map helpers validated entry widths but not the typed accessor contract: `ExternCallContext::map_set_string_key` could write through non-string-key maps, and `VoMap<String, GcRef>` could publish a GC reference into scalar-valued map metadata. Raw string-key insertion now requires a string-keyed one-slot key before allocation, barriers, or mutation; typed `VoMap<String, String>`, `VoMap<String, i64>`, and `VoMap<String, GcRef>` validate key kind and value kind against map metadata before publishing. Both behavior proofs failed first by mutating the map, then passed; the source contract confirms width validation still precedes barriers and `set_checked`. |
| VM-CALLIFACE-RECEIVER-LAYOUT-060 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/{exec/call.rs,vm/jit/callbacks/closure_call.rs}` | `vm_call_iface_rejects_raw_interface_receiver_layout_drift_before_frame_push_060`, `vm_jit_prepare_iface_call_validates_receiver_layout_before_frame_push_060`. | Final interpreter/JIT red-team found raw extern interface pairs could bypass verifier-time `IfaceAssign` receiver layout checks: a forged slot0 value kind could resolve an itab target whose receiver frame slot used a different storage class, and `CallIface` would fill the IC or push a frame before proving the receiver layout. Interpreter and JIT prepared `CallIface` now share `validate_iface_receiver_layout`, deriving expected receiver storage from interface slot0 value kind plus module method metadata for boxed value-receiver wrappers, and rejecting drift before IC fill or frame push. The interpreter proof failed first by returning `FrameChanged`, then passed; the JIT source proof failed first on the missing pre-push validator, then passed with `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_jit_prepare_iface_call_validates_receiver_layout_before_frame_push_060 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`. |
| VM-DOCS-LINT-TASK-PLAN-SOURCE-060 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `eng/tasks.toml`, `cmd/vo-dev/src/lint_system.rs` | `lint_docs_contract_requires_docs_lint_task_plan_source_inputs_060`, `cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- lint tasks --strict`, `node scripts/ci/docs_lint.mjs`. | Final test/gate red-team found docs-lint shells out to `vo-dev task plan`, but the docs-lint task inputs and strict lint source contract covered only part of the task planner implementation. `docs-lint` inputs now include `cmd/vo-dev/src/task_graph.rs` and `cmd/vo-dev/src/task_planner.rs` alongside task runner/system owners, and strict lint requires those source inputs so changed-mode routing cannot miss task-plan fact-source edits. The strict task lint failed first on the missing `task_graph.rs` input, then passed after the manifest update; docs-lint also passed. |
| VM-DYNAMIC-TARGET-ASSIGNABILITY-060 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-runtime/src/builtins/dynamic.rs` | `dynamic_assignability_uses_target_rttid_and_interface_preparation_060`, `dynamic_call_args_share_target_assignability_060`, `dynamic_container_setters_use_container_layout_as_storage_fact_058`. | Follow-up GC/dynamic red-team found dynamic writes still validated only `ValueKind` for non-interface targets and stored caller interface pairs unchanged for interface-valued fields, maps, slices, and dynamic-call arguments. Dynamic assignment now has one target-owned helper, `prepare_dynamic_value_for_target`, that validates nil assignability, exact RTTID identity for reference/composite targets, non-empty interface implementation with target itab construction, and raw slot width before barriers or mutation. The source-contract proofs failed first on the missing shared helper and old dynamic-call nil list, then passed; `cargo test -p vo-runtime --lib 060 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture` passed 7/7 after the fix. |
| VM-CALLIFACE-RECEIVER-HOTPATH-060 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/exec/call.rs`, `lang/crates/vo-jit/src/call_helpers/dynamic/{ic.rs,iface.rs,mod.rs}`, `lang/crates/vo-runtime/src/jit_api.rs` | `vm_call_iface_rejects_raw_interface_kind_receiver_before_frame_push_060`, `vm_jit_iface_ic_key_includes_full_receiver_slot0_060`, `vm_call_iface_rejects_raw_interface_receiver_layout_drift_before_frame_push_060`. | Final red-team rerun found two siblings of the receiver-layout fix: raw `ValueKind::Interface` slot0 was still accepted as if it had proven receiver storage, and JIT `CallIface` IC hits keyed only on `(itab_id, method_idx)`, bypassing the prepared-call receiver validator after a valid warmup. `validate_iface_receiver_layout` now rejects raw interface-kind slot0 before IC fill/frame push, and JIT dynamic iface IC entries store a second exact key word containing full receiver slot0; hits require both the existing tagged method/itab key and the full slot0 extra key to match. The new interpreter proof and JIT source-contract proof failed first, then passed; the existing JIT prepared-call pre-push proof still passed. |
| VM-JIT-MAP-NEW-WIDTH-060 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-runtime/src/jit_api.rs` | `vm_jit_map_new_rejects_width_narrowing_contract_060`. | Final JIT/ABI red-team found `vo_map_new` accepted `u32` helper ABI widths and narrowed them with unchecked `as u16` casts before allocating map metadata. Although normal lowering passes instruction-packed u16 widths, the exported helper contract could materialize truncated metadata under malformed or stale helper calls. The helper now checks both widths with `u16::try_from` before allocation and returns null on overflow rather than silently truncating. The source-contract proof failed first on `key_slots as u16` / `val_slots as u16`, then passed as part of `cargo test -p vo-runtime --lib 060 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`. |
| VM-DYNAMIC-MAP-KEY-LAYOUT-060 | Source-closed, 2026-06-19; focused proof passed in runtime gate; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-runtime/src/{builtins/dynamic.rs,ffi/mod.rs}` | `dynamic_map_keys_use_target_rttid_before_raw_slot_reads_060`, `dynamic_spread_reads_use_physical_element_layout_060`, `cargo test -p vo-runtime --lib 060 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`. | Follow-up GC/dynamic red-team found two remaining target-layout bypasses: dynamic map keys still validated only `ValueKind` before reading raw key slots, and dynamic spread packing read packed non-`any` slices as logical 8-byte slot arrays. Map key access now derives the key `ValueRttid` from the map runtime type and uses `prepare_dynamic_value_for_target` before hashing, barriers, or raw slot reads; spread packing now uses `sequence_elem_raw_slots` with the slice backing array `elem_bytes`. The source-contract proofs failed first on the missing key RTTID helper and packed-spread physical read helper, then passed as part of the runtime 060 proof set (9/9). |
| VM-CALLIFACE-TARGET-OWNERSHIP-060 | Source-closed, 2026-06-19; verifier proof passed, VM runtime proof still blocked by test-binary startup hang; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-common-core/src/verifier.rs`, `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/exec/call.rs` | `vm_iface_assign_rejects_itab_target_not_owned_by_receiver_rttid_060`, `vm_call_iface_rejects_itab_target_not_owned_by_receiver_rttid_060`. | Follow-up interpreter/verifier red-team found storage compatibility was still treated as sufficient authority: a hostile or stale module could use receiver RTTID `A` while an itab pointed at storage-compatible method `B.M`. The verifier and runtime `CallIface` validator now require the target function to be present in the receiver runtime type's method metadata before deriving boxed-receiver storage; missing method ownership is rejected before IC fill or frame push. The common-core verifier proof now passes with `cargo test -p vo-common-core vm_iface_assign_rejects_itab_target_not_owned_by_receiver_rttid_060 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`; the `vo-vm` runtime proof compiles but remains stuck after launching `target/debug/deps/vo_vm-*` and was interrupted, so it is not counted as passed. |
| VM-CALLIFACE-POINTER-RECEIVER-OWNERSHIP-060 | Source-closed, 2026-06-19; focused execution blocked by test-binary startup hang; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-common-core/src/bytecode.rs`, `lang/crates/vo-common-core/src/verifier.rs`, `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/exec/call.rs` | `vm_iface_assign_rejects_pointer_receiver_target_for_non_pointer_reference_receiver_060`, `vm_call_iface_rejects_pointer_receiver_target_for_non_pointer_reference_receiver_before_frame_push_060`. | Follow-up interpreter/verifier red-team found a sibling of target ownership: stale or hostile itabs could route a non-pointer reference-kind receiver such as `R string` to a `(*R).M` target because both use `GcRef` storage. `MethodInfo` now owns the interface-source receiver contract through `iface_receiver_slot_type_for_source_kind`, and both verifier and runtime `CallIface` use it before accepting receiver layout, IC fill, or frame push. The common-core proof failed first by accepting the module; after the source fix `cargo test -p vo-common-core --no-run` and `cargo fmt --all -- --check` pass. Focused proof execution for common-core and `vo-vm` entered or approached test binaries and was interrupted due the recurring startup hang, so the new behavior proofs are not counted as passed yet. |
| VM-CALLIFACE-CANONICAL-RTTID-KIND-060 | Source-closed, 2026-06-19; focused execution blocked by test-binary startup hang; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-common-core/src/verifier.rs`, `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/exec/call.rs` | `vm_iface_assign_rejects_noncanonical_pointer_kind_rttid_without_itab_060`, `vm_call_iface_rejects_noncanonical_pointer_kind_rttid_before_frame_push_060`. | Final narrow CallIface red-team found the pointer-receiver fix still trusted caller-supplied `ValueKind`: hostile bytecode could pair `ValueKind::Pointer` with a non-pointer named RTTID, including the `IFACE_ASSIGN_NO_ITAB` path, and direct hostile interface slot0 could do the same at runtime. `IfaceAssign` now validates the source `ValueRttid` against module runtime types before no-itab or itab acceptance, and runtime `CallIface` rejects non-canonical slot0 RTTID/kind pairs before method lookup, IC fill, or frame push. The proof tests were added; focused execution was blocked by the recurring test-binary startup hang. `cargo test -p vo-common-core --no-run`, `cargo fmt --all -- --check`, docs-lint, and strict task lint pass; `cargo check -p vo-vm --lib` was interrupted during a long rebuild and is not counted as passed. |
| VM-LANG-COMPILE-CHANGED-MODE-060 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `eng/ci.toml`, `cmd/vo-dev/src/lint_system.rs` | `lint_vm_readiness_changed_prefixes_requires_lang_tests_full_matrix_route_058`, `lint_vm_readiness_changed_prefixes_requires_vm_production_compile_scope_060`, `cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- lint tasks --strict`. | Follow-up gate red-team found `tests/lang/**` changed-mode selected runtime/JIT/OSR/no_std/WASM/GC language gates but not `vo-test-compile`, so compile-only VM contract proofs could be missed when adding or editing manifest compile-fail cases. The changed-prefix route and strict lint contract now require `vo-test-compile`; the lint fixture derives from the production changed-prefix table, and the scope guard proves `pr` includes all changed-prefix tasks while `vm-production` includes the `tests/lang/**` language proof matrix. The proof failed first by returning `Ok(())` after `vo-test-compile` was removed from the synthetic route; after the source fix `cargo test -p vo-dev --no-run`, strict task lint, `task plan vm-production --format json`, and `task plan pr --changed --format json` pass and show `vo-test-compile` selected. |
| VM-RUNTIME-COMMAND-GC-ROOT-TXN-060 | Source-closed, 2026-06-19; focused execution blocked by test-binary startup hang; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/runtime_boundary.rs` | `vm_wake_registration_002_island_wake_command_requires_registration`. | Red-team runtime-boundary audit found `apply_runtime_command` applied `command.gc_roots` before command identity validation, so stale or wrong-source island wake/host/io/endpoint commands could dirty roots and advance GC epoch even while returning `applied=false`. Runtime commands now apply their command-level GC root effect only after source token, wake key, registration, payload, and target identity checks pass; endpoint-close keeps its explicit tombstone dirty-root effect after validation. The proof augments the existing wrong-registration island-wake test with dirty flag/epoch assertions. `cargo test -p vo-vm --no-run` passes; focused execution was not counted because filtered test binaries hang after startup in this workspace. |
| VM-ITAB-ZERO-NO-ITAB-SENTINEL-060 | Source-closed, 2026-06-19; focused execution blocked by test-binary startup hang; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-common-core/src/verifier.rs`, `lang/crates/vo-codegen/src/context.rs`, `lang/crates/vo-runtime/src/itab.rs`, `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/exec/iface.rs`, `lang/crates/vo-runtime/src/jit_api.rs`, `lang/crates/vo-jit/src/translate/runtime_ops/interface.rs` | `vm_iface_assign_rejects_concrete_itab_zero_collision_060`, `itab_cache_reserves_zero_for_no_itab_060`. | Red-team interpreter/verifier audit found concrete `IfaceAssign` to empty interface still lowered `IFACE_ASSIGN_NO_ITAB` to runtime `itab_id=0`, while compile-time or runtime-created real itabs could also occupy id 0. That let hostile `CallIface` borrow meaning from itab zero after an empty-interface assignment. The runtime now reserves itab id 0 for the no-itab sentinel, codegen starts real compile-time itabs at id 1, `ItabCache::new/from_module_itabs(empty)` seed an empty id-0 table, and the verifier rejects concrete `IfaceAssign` metadata that uses id 0 instead of `IFACE_ASSIGN_NO_ITAB`. Interface-to-interface assignment paths also use fallible itab construction; interpreter returns `Err`, and JIT helpers return a structured infra-error sentinel consumed by the translator. `cargo test -p vo-common-core --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-runtime --no-run`, `cargo check -p vo-jit`, and `cargo test -p vo-vm --no-run` pass; focused test execution is blocked by the recurring test-binary startup hang. |
| VM-DYNAMIC-METHOD-RECEIVER-OWNERSHIP-060 | Source-closed, 2026-06-19; focused execution blocked by test-binary startup hang; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-runtime/src/{ffi/mod.rs,builtins/dynamic.rs}` | `dynamic_method_receiver_uses_methodinfo_ownership_060`. | Red-team GC/dynamic audit found dynamic method lookup returned only `(func_id, is_pointer_receiver, signature_rttid)`, dropping `MethodInfo.receiver_is_iface_boxed` and bypassing the shared receiver ownership helper. A non-pointer named scalar stored in `any` could therefore produce a method closure for a pointer receiver and later make closure GC scan an integer as a `GcRef`. Dynamic method lookup now returns the full `MethodInfo`, and receiver capture uses `MethodInfo::iface_receiver_slot_type_for_source_kind` before allocating the method closure. `cargo test -p vo-runtime --no-run` passes; focused execution is not counted because filtered test binaries hang after startup. |
| VM-GC-STRING-MAP-TRACE-CONTRACT-060 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-runtime/src/gc_types.rs` | `vm_gc_map_string_key_value_slots_are_traceable_roots_060`. | GC red-team reported string map keys/values could be skipped by `may_contain_gc_refs`, but current `ValueKind::String` is above `Array` in the enum and already returns true for that predicate. The new scanner behavior proof locks the intended contract by inserting a string key and string value into a map and tracing only the map; both string refs must be visited. `cargo test -p vo-runtime --no-run` passes; the row is closed as a filtered false positive with a regression contract. |
| VM-ANALYSIS-CODEGEN-COMPILE-CHANGED-MODE-060 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `cmd/vo-dev/src/lint_system.rs`, `eng/tasks.toml` | `lint_vm_readiness_changed_prefixes_requires_analysis_compile_route_060`, `lint_vm_readiness_changed_prefixes_requires_codegen_compile_route_060`, `cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- lint tasks --strict`. | Test/gate red-team found changed-mode readiness lint did not pin `lang/crates/vo-analysis/**`, and the `lang/crates/vo-codegen/**` lint fixture missed `vo-test-compile`, even though `eng/ci.toml` routes both through compile checks. The readiness prefix table now requires analysis `cargo-test-analysis` plus `vo-test-compile`, codegen also requires `vo-test-compile`, and the PR selector now includes `typechecker-contract` so changed-mode plans can actually select `cargo-test-analysis`. Strict task lint failed first on the missing PR scope and now passes; `task plan pr --changed --format json` shows `cargo-test-analysis`, `cargo-test-codegen`, and `vo-test-compile`. |
| VM-JIT-HARDENING-UNFILTERED-NAME-LINT-060 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `cmd/vo-dev/src/lint_system.rs` | `lint_vm_hardening_uses_unfiltered_tasks_not_test_name_prefixes_060`, `lint_vm_hardening_tasks_accepts_unfiltered_crate_tests_059`, `lint_vm_hardening_tasks_rejects_name_filtered_crate_tests_059`. | Test/gate red-team found the old JIT hardening source lint still rejected non-`vm_` tests even though hardening tasks now run unfiltered crate tests. The stale name-prefix scanner and its tests were removed; coverage ownership remains in the unfiltered hardening task contracts, and the new source proof rejects reintroducing the old `must start with vm_` rule. `cargo test -p vo-dev --no-run` and strict task lint pass. |
| VM-PRODUCTION-WORKFLOW-FINAL-SELECTORS-060 | Closed, 2026-06-19; production signoff still pending final gates | `.github/workflows/production-readiness.yml`, `scripts/ci/docs_lint.mjs` | `selfTestProductionReadinessWorkflowLint`, `node scripts/ci/docs_lint.mjs`. | Test/gate red-team found the production-readiness workflow matrix still listed only `contract` and `vm-production`, while the final signoff selector fact source is `vo-dev task final-selectors` with `contract`, `vm-production`, `site`, and `release-verify`. The workflow matrix now includes all four, and docs lint checks the matrix against `vo-dev task final-selectors` instead of a two-selector hard-code. The self-test includes a missing-final-selector negative case; docs lint passes. |
| VM-EXTERN-INTERFACE-RETURN-EXPECTED-IFACE-ABI-060 | Source-closed, 2026-06-19; focused execution partly blocked by test-binary startup hang; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-common-core/src/{bytecode.rs,serialize.rs,verifier.rs}`, `lang/crates/vo-codegen/src/{context.rs,expr/call.rs,expr/dyn_access.rs,stmt/defer_go.rs,stmt/dyn_assign.rs,wrapper.rs}`, `lang/crates/vo-runtime/src/{ffi/mod.rs,itab.rs}`, `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/{exec/call.rs,vm/jit/callbacks/closure_call.rs}` | `return_shape_constructor_rejects_invalid_interface_metadata_060`, `deserialize_rejects_extern_return_shape_interface_metadata_drift_060`, `deserialize_rejects_extern_return_shape_slots_only_interface_metadata_060`, `module_verifier_rejects_extern_return_shape_slots_only_interface_metadata_060`, `declared_extern_rejects_same_name_different_return_interface_metadata_060`, `defer_extern_wrapper_cache_key_does_not_bypass_return_interface_metadata_060`, `vm_iface_assign_rejects_itab_method_name_mismatch_060`, `ffi_resolved_return_shape_rejects_slots_only_interface_metadata_060`, `ffi_resolved_return_shape_rejects_wrong_signature_itab_060`. | Follow-up ABI red-team found interface returns still lacked expected-interface ownership even after precise slot layout checks, and then found two remaining variants: decoded `ReturnShape` values could carry interface metadata with no `slot_types`, and same-shaped method vectors still accepted wrong signatures. `ReturnShape` now rejects metadata without return slot types at construction, serialization, module verification, and FFI resolution; FFI return verification also reuses the shared itab method/signature helper before result slots enter VM frames. `cargo test -p vo-common-core --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-codegen --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-runtime --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-vm --no-run`, and `cargo check -p vo-jit` pass; filtered proof execution still hangs after test-binary startup and is not counted. |
| VM-ITAB-INTERFACE-METHOD-BINDING-060 | Closed, 2026-06-19 | `lang/crates/vo-common-core/src/{bytecode.rs,serialize.rs,verifier.rs}`, `lang/crates/vo-codegen/src/context.rs`, `lang/crates/vo-runtime/src/{ffi/mod.rs,itab.rs}` | `vm_iface_assign_rejects_itab_method_name_mismatch_060`, `itab_cache_rejects_signature_mismatch_060`, `ffi_expected_interface_itab_methods_rejects_signature_mismatch_060`. | Interpreter/verifier red-team found an itab was only an ordered method list, so a hostile module could bind an itab to the wrong same-shaped interface method name; follow-up FFI/JIT ABI audit found the runtime itab cache still needed one shared expected-method fact source for name, pointer receiver, and signature. `expected_interface_itab_methods` is now the single helper used by `ItabCache`, FFI return verification, and extern replay validation. `cargo test -p vo-common-core --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-runtime --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-codegen --no-run`, and `cargo check -p vo-jit` pass. |
| VM-PTRNEW-PHYSICAL-BOXING-META-060 | Closed, 2026-06-19 | `lang/crates/vo-codegen/src/{assign.rs,context.rs,expr/builtin.rs,expr/pointer.rs,func.rs,lib.rs,stmt/var_def.rs}`, `lang/crates/vo-common-core/src/verifier.rs`, `lang/crates/vo-jit/src/{semantics/rows.rs,semantics/tests.rs,verifier/tests/memory.rs}` | `vm_ptr_new_boxing_paths_use_physical_boxing_meta_060`, `module_verifier_rejects_ptr_new_runtime_meta_layout_drift_060`, `ptr_new_codegen_emits_verifier_layout_metadata_060`, `accepts_ptr_new_with_ptr_layout_metadata_060`, `rejects_ptr_new_without_ptr_layout_metadata_060`. | GC/root red-team found `PtrNew` lowering still passed logical value metadata for `new(T)`/address-of boxing paths, allowing a pointer allocation to masquerade as a logical array/map/slice/string object instead of the synthetic single-field boxing struct that the GC can scan precisely; follow-up verifier audit found hand-authored bytecode could still drift metadata from the physical pointer allocation layout; verifier/frame audit then found the verifier required `PtrLayout` while codegen still emitted naked `PtrNew` metadata; the final JIT red-team found strict JIT's semantic row still claimed `PtrNew` carried no metadata, so legal `PtrNew + PtrLayout` modules could pass common verification and fail the strict JIT metadata gate. `emit_ptr_new` now requires the physical slot layout, emits `JitInstructionMetadata::PtrLayout` itself, and all producers pass the same layout they use for boxing metadata and `PtrSet` copying. The module verifier proves the dominating `ValueMeta` layout matches the `PtrLayout` allocation width before accepting `PtrNew`, and the JIT semantic row now derives the same `PtrLayout` requirement, metadata-local verifier domain, metadata-helper fail-fast policy, and slot-metadata allocation contract for strict JIT checks. Clean-target validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codegen-ptrnew-060 cargo check -p vo-codegen`, the focused `ptr_new_codegen_emits_verifier_layout_metadata_060` integration proof, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codegen-ptrnew-060 cargo test -p vo-codegen --no-run`, `cargo test -p vo-vm --no-run`, `cargo check -p vo-vm --features jit`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-jit-ptrnew-060 cargo test -p vo-jit verifier::tests::memory::accepts_ptr_new_with_ptr_layout_metadata_060 -- --exact --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-jit-ptrnew-060 cargo test -p vo-jit verifier::tests::memory::rejects_ptr_new_without_ptr_layout_metadata_060 -- --exact --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-jit-ptrnew-060 cargo test -p vo-jit semantics::tests -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`, and `cargo fmt --all -- --check`; default-target exact proof attempts that hung earlier remain covered by VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002 and are not counted. |
| VM-VO-TEST-GC-ENV-HERMETICITY-060 | Closed, 2026-06-19 | `cmd/vo-test/src/main.rs` | `vm_test_runner_apply_job_env_clears_unset_gc_env_060`, `vm_test_runner_apply_job_env_preserves_explicit_gc_target_env_060`. | Test/gate red-team found VM/JIT/GC language-test jobs could inherit ambient `VO_GC_STRESS`, `VO_GC_VERIFY`, or `VO_GC_DEBUG` from the developer shell, making backend results depend on global process environment. The runner now treats GC env keys like JIT threshold keys for VM/JIT backends: unset target env clears ambient values, while explicit target env remains authoritative. `cargo test -p vo-test --no-run` passes. |
| VM-WAKE-REGISTRATION-PER-WAIT-060 | Source-closed, 2026-06-19; filtered execution blocked by test-binary startup hang; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-runtime/src/{objects/queue_state.rs,island_msg.rs}`, `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/{fiber.rs,scheduler.rs,runtime_boundary.rs,vm/mod.rs,vm/jit/callbacks/queue.rs,vm/jit/transition/wait.rs}` | `vm_wake_registration_060_simple_queue_wake_rejects_stale_wait_registration`, `vm_wake_registration_002_simple_queue_registration_includes_queue_identity`. | Follow-up scheduler/wake red-team found the previous queue identity fix still keyed simple queue wakes by fiber generation plus queue/kind shape, so an old wake could match a later wait on the same queue and same operation. `QueueWaiter::simple_queue` and select waiters now carry a nonzero per-registration id through queue state, island encoding, fiber wait state, runtime-boundary activation keys, and wake registration hashing; runtime boundaries reject identityless non-endpoint queue wakes, and tests that meant real queue waits now register `simple_queue` waiters instead of legacy `simple` waiters. `cargo check -p vo-vm`, `cargo test -p vo-vm --no-run`, and `cargo test -p vo-runtime --no-run` pass; filtered `vo-vm` proof and `--list` invocations still hang after launching the test binary and were interrupted, so runtime execution is not counted. |
| VM-EXTERN-REPLAY-INTERFACE-ARG-ABI-060 | Source-closed, 2026-06-19; focused execution blocked by test-binary startup hang; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/{frame_call.rs,vm/extern_call.rs,vm/jit/extern_call.rs}`, `lang/crates/vo-runtime/src/itab.rs` | `typed_extern_replay_args_rejects_wrong_interface_arg_metadata_060`. | FFI/JIT ABI red-team found `ExternResult::CallClosure` replay accepted raw interface `(slot0, slot1)` pairs by layout alone, so a closure expecting interface A could receive an itab for interface B before interpreter frame push or JIT suspend replay. Replay arg typing now derives expected interface ownership from the target `FunctionDef.param_types`, validates canonical transfer metadata, checks nil-interface shape, rejects raw interface-kind slot0, and verifies the itab owner plus method vector through the shared itab helper before publishing replay frames. `cargo test -p vo-vm --no-run` and `cargo check -p vo-vm --features jit` pass; exact proof execution is blocked by test-binary startup hang and is not counted. |
| VM-ISLAND-CODEC-STRICT-TRAILING-BYTES-060 | Closed, 2026-06-19 | `lang/crates/vo-runtime/src/island_msg.rs` | `island_command_codec_rejects_trailing_bytes_and_preserves_queue_identity_060`. | Runtime-boundary red-team found island command decode accepted valid command prefixes with trailing bytes, weakening protocol drift and wake-identity fuzz coverage. `decode_island_command` now requires full input consumption, transport-frame decode inherits that invariant, and the proof covers trailing-byte rejection plus `QueueWaiter::simple_queue` registration, queue, and kind roundtrip. `cargo test -p vo-runtime --no-run` passes. |
| VM-ENDPOINT-SAME-ISLAND-RESPONSE-TXN-060 | Source-closed, 2026-06-19; focused execution blocked by test-binary startup hang; production signoff still pending final gates | `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/{vm/island_shared.rs,vm/mod.rs,vm/island_thread.rs}` | `vm_endpoint_request_same_island_rejected_response_rolls_back_queue_state_060`. | Runtime-boundary red-team found same-island `EndpointRequest` could mutate a local queue, generate a stale same-island endpoint response, have `RuntimeCommand::endpoint_response` reject it, and still return outer success because `handle_endpoint_response_command` discarded the outcome. The handler now returns `Result`, VM/island-thread dispatch propagate rejection, and the request handler's queue/registry snapshot rollback now fires when a same-island response is rejected. `cargo test -p vo-vm --no-run` passes; exact proof execution is blocked by test-binary startup hang and is not counted. |
| VM-REMOTE-ISLAND-COMMAND-SHAPE-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/runtime_boundary.rs` | `vm_remote_wake_fiber_shape_061_rejects_forged_waiter_before_publish`, `vm_remote_endpoint_effect_shape_061_rejects_forged_source_before_publish`. | Runtime-boundary red-team found remote island command preflight validated only routes, not command source or shape. `preflight_remote_island_command_effect` now owns remote WakeFiber waiter identity, endpoint-wait exclusion, endpoint request/response source identity, and pending-response shape before staging/publish. Focused validation passed `cargo test -p vo-vm _061` in the round2 clean target; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening`. |
| VM-REMOTE-OPAQUE-WAKE-IDENTITY-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/runtime_boundary.rs` | `vm_remote_wake_fiber_shape_061_rejects_raw_slot_identity_before_publish`, `vm_remote_endpoint_request_shape_061_rejects_raw_slot_response_identity_before_publish`, `vm_remote_endpoint_response_shape_061_rejects_raw_slot_response_identity_before_publish`. | Runtime-boundary red-team found remote wake and endpoint request/response publication could carry generation-zero raw fiber slots. The shared canonical opaque-key preflight now rejects raw slot identity before outbound publication or pending-response accounting. Focused validation passed the three proofs plus `cargo test -p vo-vm _061` and `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit _061` in the round2 clean target; selected by `vm-production` through VM hardening gates. |

Focused validation passed both proof filters and the full `_061` VM proof set in the round2 clean target; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening`. |

Local closed receiver/sender preflight now validates the queue handle and closed bit before scheduler wake ownership checks. Focused validation passed the proof plus `cargo test -p vo-vm _061` and `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit _061` in the round2 clean target; selected by VM hardening gates. |

| VM-REMOTE-SELECT-WAKE-PAYLOAD-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/runtime_boundary.rs` | `vm_remote_select_recv_wake_061_rejects_unrepresentable_payload_before_publish`, `vm_remote_select_send_wake_061_rejects_unrepresentable_payload_before_publish`. | Runtime-boundary final red-team found remote select recv and send wakes from `RuntimeTransition.wakes` passed local payload/result validation, then split into payload-less `IslandCommand::WakeFiber` outbound commands. `preflight_queue_waiter_wake` now rejects every remote select waiter wake because the current remote WakeFiber protocol cannot represent the selected outcome. | VM-ENDPOINT-REQUEST-TARGET-IDENTITY-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/vm/island_shared.rs`, `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/runtime_boundary.rs` | `vm_endpoint_request_target_061_rejects_raw_or_zero_response_identity_before_registering_waiter`. | Runtime-boundary red-team found incoming endpoint `Send`/`Recv` requests at the home island could register raw-slot or zero-wait response identities in queue state. | VM-SAME-ISLAND-ENDPOINT-REQUEST-SHAPE-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/runtime_boundary.rs` | `vm_same_island_endpoint_request_shape_061_requires_pending_response_before_waiter`, `vm_same_island_endpoint_request_shape_061_rejects_spurious_pending_response`, `vm_same_island_endpoint_request_shape_061_rejects_forged_source_before_transfer`, `vm_same_island_endpoint_request_shape_061_rejects_forged_source_before_waiter`. | Runtime-boundary red-team found same-island `EndpointRequest` effects skipped the pending-response envelope and source-owner checks already enforced for remote endpoint requests, while apply-time accounting trusted the bit and the handler authorized by payload `from_island`. Same-island and remote endpoint request preflight now share the pending-response helper, and same-island request preflight rejects forged sources before queue mutation, pending-response accounting, or endpoint peer mutation. Failing-first proofs reproduced missing/spurious envelope bits and forged peer impersonation, then focused validation passed `cargo test -p vo-vm same_island_endpoint_request_shape_061` in the round2 clean target; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening`. |
| VM-DIRECT-ISLAND-ENDPOINT-SOURCE-061 | Closed, 2026-06-21 | `lang/crates/vo-runtime/src/{island.rs,island_transport.rs,island_msg.rs}`, `lang/crates/vo-vm/src/vm/{mod.rs,types.rs,island_thread.rs}`, `lang/crates/vo-app-runtime/src/session.rs`, `lang/crates/vo-web/src/island.rs` | `vm_direct_endpoint_request_ingress_061_rejects_forged_transfer_source_before_peer_mutation`, `vm_direct_endpoint_request_ingress_061_rejects_forged_recv_source_before_waiter`. | Runtime-boundary red-team found direct island command ingress carried naked `IslandCommand` values, so transport, external frames, and VM queue injection could forge endpoint `from_island` before endpoint authorization or queue mutation. `IslandCommandEnvelope` now carries the authenticated source through in-thread transport, external frames, VM command queues, outbound publication, and island-thread dispatch; endpoint request/response ingress rejects source/payload drift before peer mutation, waiter registration, or response accounting. The runtime boundary now derives local endpoint-response consumption keys from wakes and island commands, preflights the aggregate count before any commit, and keeps the pending-response owner inside one transition-level preflight. Focused validation passed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codex-focused-main cargo test -p vo-vm vm_endpoint_response_activation_062 -- --test-threads=1`; selected by `vm-production` through `cargo-test-vm-hardening`, final gates passed in the 2026-06-23 signoff evidence. |

| VM-BUILTIN-EXTERN-RETURN-ABI-062 | Closed, 2026-06-23 | `lang/crates/vo-common-core/src/{bytecode.rs,verifier.rs}` | `module_verifier_rejects_builtin_extern_return_slot_drift_062`, `module_verifier_rejects_builtin_extern_slots_only_return_layout_062`, `module_verifier_rejects_builtin_extern_fixed_return_layout_drift_062`, `module_verifier_rejects_builtin_extern_fixed_return_count_drift_062`. | Interpreter/verifier red-team found `CallExternLayout` proved only module self-consistency: a module could declare a runtime builtin with matching but wrong return slot count, declare only a slot count without precise return slot types, or spoof fixed-return builtins such as `vo_copy` and `vo_slice_append_slice` whose parameter facts were known but return layouts were not. ### Follow-Up Closure Evidence, 2026-06-17

`VM-DIRECT-METHOD-CAPTURE-PROTOCOL-006` now treats direct method receivers as a
wire-level payload shape instead of overloading ordinary closure capture boxes:
raw receiver payloads carry their raw slot count, multi-slot receivers allocate
the matching capture count on the destination island, and interface boxed
value-receiver wrappers keep the boxed one-slot ABI through explicit method
metadata instead of a function-name suffix. Ordinary one-slot struct receivers
stay raw even when their single physical slot is a `GcRef`. The focused Rust
proof set covers payload unpack, sender-side one-slot and multi-slot transfer
with a nested port, receiver-side closure allocation, boxed value-wrapper
transfer planning, a suffixed raw-receiver counterexample, and JIT direct-method
`GoIsland` terminal-policy behavior.

`VM-RAW-RECEIVER-ROOT-CONTRACT-006` moves the receiver-capture/root-scanning
choice into load-time metadata contracts: closure-shaped functions cannot also
declare receiver slots, and receiver-frame functions cannot carry ordinary
closure capture metadata. The GC closure scanner can therefore use receiver
slots as the authoritative layout for raw receiver captures instead of guessing
between two metadata sources.

`VM-METHOD-EXPRESSION-PARAM-METADATA-006` makes GoIsland argument transfer
metadata derive from one helper on both sender and receiver. If a method
expression sends the receiver in the argument buffer, the helper prepends the
receiver transfer type; `$iface` wrappers now also copy forwarded parameter
transfer metadata from the original method.

`VM-IFACE-WRAPPER-SUFFIX-AUTHORITY-006` moves boxed interface-wrapper authority
into `MethodInfo.receiver_is_iface_boxed`. Codegen sets that bit only for real
boxed value-receiver wrappers, serialization persists it, and the verifier
rejects metadata whose target function is not a one-slot `GcRef` receiver ABI.
`direct_method_receiver_transfer_plan` now reads that verified metadata rather
than `func.name.ends_with("$iface")`.

`VM-JIT-GOISLAND-ENDPOINT-TERMINAL-006` closes the remaining JIT transaction
split: queue-transfer preparation now returns a `QueueTransferCommit` summary
from the same walker that installs local `HomeInfo` and endpoint registry
state, and JIT `GoIsland` promotes only those already-committed endpoint
transitions to `CommitOnAnyTerminal`. Pure pending spawns remain governed by the
existing language-panic policy.

`VM-GOISLAND-TRANSFER-PACKABILITY-TXN-006` extends that transaction boundary to
the whole payload. The validation pass now walks direct receiver captures,
captures, args, and remote-send elements through the same value-layout
preflight, validating strings, slices, arrays, maps, structs, and scalar slot
widths before the commit pass can publish endpoint state. The malformed-later
string proof shows endpoint publication does not happen when a non-queue
root-bearing value is not packable.

`VM-JIT-REMOTE-SEND-ENDPOINT-TERMINAL-006` applies the same terminal policy to
JIT remote queue sends. Remote-send actions now carry the
`QueueTransferCommit` summary returned by payload preparation into the JIT
callback, so sends whose payload transfer publishes local endpoint state use
`CommitOnAnyTerminal`; uncommitted endpoint requests still use terminal discard.

`VM-JIT-WAITQUEUE-MATERIALIZE-006` rolls back registered wait state on failed
JIT wait materialization. `cleanup_unmaterialized_wait_queue_state` cancels
simple waiters, select waiters, select state, and remote endpoint wait state
before returning `FrameMaterializeError`, so a failed materialization cannot
strand queue waiters that no frame can resume.

`VM-JIT-WAITQUEUE-PENDING-ENDPOINT-CLEANUP-007` closes the paired-obligation
variant of the same failure path. When failed wait materialization clears
`remote_endpoint_wait`, pending runtime transitions also drop island commands
with `pending_response=true`, while keeping non-response endpoint transfer
effects needed to honor already-published endpoint state. A terminal JIT error
can therefore still commit endpoint publication repair work without creating a
response obligation that no fiber wait state can accept.

`VM-CONTRACT-TASK-TIMEOUT-006` turns bounded gate execution into a source
contract. Strict task lint now resolves `contract` and `vm-production` and
rejects every concrete task selected by either gate without `timeout_sec`,
keeping future proof-test routes bounded while VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002
remains open.

`VM-CALLIFACE-PC-ANCHOR-010` removes the last dynamic-call pc anchoring split:
`CallIface` now rejects an unadvanced caller pc with `checked_sub(1)` before
probing or filling the inline cache, matching `CallClosure` and extern replay
ownership. The proof constructs valid pc-0 `CallLayout` metadata and asserts
that a caller frame with `pc == 0` returns `JitError` without frame push or IC
allocation.

`VM-ENDPOINT-CLOSE-RESPONSE-010` makes endpoint waiter identity, not island
locality, own closed queue wake routing. Any closed receiver or sender wake with
`endpoint_wait_id != 0` now becomes an `EndpointResponse` effect; same-island
responses dispatch back through `RuntimeCommand::endpoint_response`, while
ordinary queue waiters keep the scheduler wake path. The receiver and sender
proofs assert `pending_island_responses` decrements and the fiber endpoint wait
state is materialized or cleared through `fiber.apply_endpoint_response`.

`VM-JIT-DYNAMIC-CALLSITE-LAYOUT-010` routes JIT prepared closure and interface
calls through the same resolved-target callsite layout validators used by the
interpreter. The callback now reads caller `CallLayout` metadata before
`push_frame_fn` and before `PreparedCall`/IC publication; same-count
`SlotType` drift returns a JIT infra error and leaves the push-frame counter at
zero.

`VM-JIT-GO-CALLSITE-LAYOUT-010` applies the same contract to JIT `GoStart` and
`GoIsland`: after target resolution and raw buffer width checks, both static and
closure-shaped JIT go callbacks validate callsite arg layout from
`ctx.runtime_trap_pc` and the current caller frame before publishing spawn or
island-command runtime-boundary effects.

`VM-CODEGEN-CONTRACT-GATE-010` makes codegen metadata a VM production gate.
`vm-production` now selects `codegen-contract`, strict task lint asserts that
`cargo-test-codegen` remains selected, and changed-mode CI for
`lang/crates/vo-codegen/**` also selects runtime and JIT language contracts.
The codegen wrapper method-index guard was also aligned to the actual `CallIface`
`u8` flag width.

`VM-DEV-RUNNER-SIBLING-FRESHNESS-010` narrows the `vo-test` sibling fast path to
profile-compatible binaries that are fresh against both the current `vo-dev`
binary and the native runner source inputs: `Cargo.lock`, `Cargo.toml`,
`rust-toolchain.toml`, `cmd/vo-test`, and `lang/crates`. Debug
`vo-dev --release` now falls back to `cargo run -p vo-test --release`, and any
sibling `vo-test` older than the runner/compiler/VM source contract falls back
to cargo rebuild instead of silently executing old source.

`VM-ENDPOINT-DIRECT-RESPONSE-011` closes the same-island direct-completion
variant of endpoint response ownership. Queue send/recv completion now treats
`endpoint_wait_id != 0` as the authority for endpoint response routing; island
locality no longer converts endpoint waiters into ordinary queue wakes. The
interpreter and JIT both consume the shared `QueueAction::RemoteRecvData` /
`RemoteSendAck` result, so endpoint waiters are materialized through
`RuntimeCommand::endpoint_response` while ordinary queue waiters keep the
scheduler wake path.

`VM-DEV-RUNNER-STDLIB-FRESHNESS-011` extends sibling runner freshness to the
embedded standard library source. `lang/stdlib` is now part of the native
runner input set, matching Cargo's `vo-stdlib` build script rebuild trigger, so
focused `vo-dev test run` proof evidence cannot reuse a `vo-test` binary with
stale embedded stdlib contents.

`VM-CODEGEN-CONTRACT-GROUP-011` makes `codegen-contract` the selected fact
source instead of merely requiring the currently expanded `cargo-test-codegen`
task. Strict task lint now requires both `contract` and `vm-production` to
include the `codegen-contract` group by name, so future additions to that group
are inherited by the production and contract gates.

`VM-ENDPOINT-DIRECT-PREFLIGHT-012` aligns the send preflight helper with the
queue state machine's endpoint response ownership rule. A receiver with
`endpoint_wait_id != 0` is now treated as endpoint-direct completion even when
the receiver is on the local island, so missing `HomeInfo` and transfer
packability failures are rejected before the waiting receiver is popped. The
interpreter send path, select send path, and JIT queue-send callback all share
that preflight fact source.

`VM-STDLIB-CONTRACT-CI-012` makes `lang/stdlib/**` a first-class engineering
input instead of an implicit runner freshness detail. `cargo-test-stdlib` and
`cargo-test-web-runtime-wasm` now declare the embedded stdlib source tree as an
input, and changed-mode CI routes `lang/stdlib/**` changes through the stdlib
crate contracts plus runtime-language and WASM language contracts. Strict task
lint now rejects future drift in both the task inputs and changed-prefix route.

`VM-ENDPOINT-SENDER-PREFLIGHT-012` closes the recv-side symmetry of endpoint
ownership. Endpoint senders are now identified by `endpoint_wait_id != 0` before
recv/select/JIT recv mutates queue state or writes destination slots; island
locality no longer decides whether endpoint ack validation runs. The old
`next_recv_remote_endpoint_sender` fact source was replaced with
`next_recv_endpoint_sender` so the helper name matches the invariant.

`VM-JIT-BRIDGE-SUSPEND-DIRTY-012` makes JIT bridge side exits conservative at
the runtime-boundary layer. Because generated code may have touched stack,
defer, replay, panic, or materialized-frame roots before yielding or blocking,
bridge-created runtime transitions now default to `CurrentFiberDirty`.

`VM-JIT-EXTERN-HARDENING-GATE-012` originally moved JIT extern proof naming
into the same `vm_` hardening route as other VM readiness tests. The later
`VM-HARDENING-UNFILTERED-GATE-059` closure supersedes the selector dependency:
hardening tasks now run full crate unit suites, while the JIT prefix lint
remains only a local naming guard for the VM-owned JIT subtree.

`VM-GATE-TASK-TIMEOUT-012` upgrades the timeout contract from "test-shaped
tasks" to every concrete task selected by `contract` and `vm-production`.
Strict task lint now rejects any selected concrete task without `timeout_sec`;
the currently selected lint, docs, benchmark, and wasm-check tasks now have
bounded timeouts.

`VM-EXTERN-REPLAY-DIRTY-012` closes the interpreter extern replay dirty-root
gap. `HostEventWaitAndReplay` and `WaitIo` transitions now mark the current
fiber dirty because extern providers may have written root-bearing return slots
before the wait boundary.

`VM-ENDPOINT-RECV-PACK-PREFLIGHT-012` makes endpoint recv response
serialization the fact source for preflight. `endpoint_wait_id != 0` or a
non-home target island now triggers payload preparation before queue mutation,
so same-island endpoint responses prepare local port `HomeInfo` before pack and
reject non-packable payloads without consuming buffered values or waiting
senders.

`VM-JIT-CALLBACK-ABI-HARDENING-GATE-012` extended the historical JIT hardening
selector guard from extern calls to callback modules. After
`VM-HARDENING-UNFILTERED-GATE-059`, queue/defer callback pointer and width
checks are selected because `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit` runs the full JIT
feature VM unit suite; the prefix lint is no longer the release-gate selector.

`VM-VO-DEV-CONTRACT-GATE-012` moves gate-policy ownership into the gates it
certifies. The `vo-dev-contract` group is selected by both `contract` and
`vm-production`, contains strict task lint plus `cargo-test-vo-dev`, and
changed-mode CI routes `cmd/vo-dev/**` through those same proof tasks.

`VM-JIT-RAW-ABI-PREFLIGHT-012` moves raw pointer validation ahead of nil trap
publication in JIT prepared-call and goroutine callbacks. Prepared closure and
interface calls reject null `out` before writing a trapped prepared-call
sentinel, while `GoStart` and `GoIsland` reject non-empty null arg buffers
before recording nil closure/island traps or publishing spawn/island effects.

`VM-JIT-HARDENING-TREE-SELECTION-012` made the `vm_` selector the historical
ownership boundary for VM-owned JIT proofs. Current source no longer relies on
that naming convention for release selection: `VM-HARDENING-UNFILTERED-GATE-059`
changed VM hardening tasks to full crate unit suites, so bridge, OSR,
transition, and callback contracts cannot drift out because there is no
hardening name filter to bypass.

`VM-JIT-TREE-TEST-SELECTION-013` completes that ownership boundary by requiring
every `#[test] fn` under `vo-vm/src/vm/jit/**/*.rs` to start with `vm_`.
Plainly named context/invoke/materialization/frame/side-exit proofs and
GC-prefixed JIT tests were renamed into the same selector rather than adding a
second production path.

`VM-JIT-SELECT-CALLBACK-ABI-013`,
`VM-JIT-PREPARED-RESUME-PC-013`, and `VM-JIT-DEFER-CALLBACK-ABI-013` move
scalar ABI validation ahead of VM-visible mutation. Select execution validates
`result_reg` before select-state mutation, prepared closure/interface nil trap
paths validate `caller_resume_pc` before publishing runtime traps, and defer
push validates `arg_start` before deriving layout or pushing defer entries.

`VM-ENDPOINT-REQUEST-OWNERSHIP-013` removes unused direct endpoint request
publisher helpers from `VmState` and makes raw island-command routing/output
queues crate-owned. Endpoint request publication and `pending_island_responses`
increments remain owned by `IslandCommandEffect` commit through the runtime
boundary, while external callers inspect and drain outbound commands only
through `Vm::has_outbound_commands` and `Vm::take_outbound_commands`.

`VM-RUNTIME-PUBLIC-STATE-OWNERSHIP-013` closes the remaining public VM runtime
surface side doors by making `Vm.state`, `Vm.scheduler`, and `Vm.module`
crate-owned. Host loops and embedding crates now use semantic VM APIs for
host-event polling, host-event key lookup, output sink setup, extern registry
registration, and module inspection; raw scheduler wake appliers and endpoint
response accounting stay VM-boundary owned.

`VM-RUNTIME-CHANGED-MODE-HARDENING-012` and
`VM-ENG-CONTRACT-CHANGED-MODE-012` close changed-mode routing gaps. Runtime
source changes now select VM hardening because runtime queue/pack facts can own
VM boundary invariants, and `eng/tasks.toml` / `eng/ci.toml` changes now select
the vo-dev contract proofs that certify task graph and CI route policy.

The 2026-06-18 019/020/021/022 slices closed the latest confirmed red-team findings
without restoring signoff. Endpoint request handling now uses one authorization fact for
`Send`/`Recv`/`Close`/`Transfer`, and endpoint responses carry `from_island`
through the runtime protocol so remote proxies accept responses only from their
home island while same-island endpoint responses still replay through the same
endpoint-response boundary. Unauthorized pending `Send`/`Recv` requests now
produce existing endpoint rejection responses instead of silently orphaning the
source wait. Closed endpoint tombstones now preserve the response-source island
that owns later targeted `SendAck`/`RecvData` responses, so a legitimate home
close cannot orphan an already pending source wait. Remote-close runtime
transitions now carry the endpoint home island as the tombstone response source,
so the interpreter and JIT queue-close paths share the same authorization fact.
Select wake ingestion now
proves exact recv payload slot kinds
against the queue layout before storing a woken payload, preventing same-width
root metadata drift. Interface method-value and embedded-interface
method-expression wrappers now share the checked dynamic-call shape owner with
ordinary `CallIface`; the old panic-only `encode_call_args` wrapper path was
removed. `CallIface` method index narrowing is also centralized in
`CodegenContext`, so direct interface calls, method values, embedded method
expressions, and defer wrappers fail as codegen diagnostics instead of panics
when the u8 flag width is exceeded. Dynamic call, wrapper, and defer/go
aggregate argument-slot counts now remain `usize` until `CodegenContext`
records the u16 layout contract error, removing the remaining local cast and
sum forks. `CallExtern` and `GoIsland` argument-slot counts now fail through the
same `FuncBuilder` layout-error channel instead of panic-only u8 packing,
deferred extern wrapper caches include arity in their identity, and
`ReturnShape` slot-vector construction is non-lossy before extern return
layouts reach codegen. Generated wrapper builders now register through one
`CodegenContext` owner that records builder layout errors, and `CallExtern`
extern-id operand narrowing records a layout diagnostic instead of panicking.
The endpoint command-queue wake proof now installs endpoint response authority
before dispatching a targeted response, matching the current source contract.
Docs lint now requires exactly one current
`VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002` row before `### Superseded`, and the
direct-method capture runtime proof validates cross-island object semantics
instead of source `GcRef` identity. Focused validation passed
`CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-endpoint-owner-019 cargo test -p vo-vm
vm_endpoint_request_owner_019 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`,
`CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-endpoint-response-source-019 cargo test
-p vo-vm vm_endpoint_response_source_019 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture`,
`CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-019-vm cargo test -p vo-vm 019 --
--test-threads=1 --nocapture`, the JIT-enabled twin,

island_msg -- --test-threads=1`, the two new compile-fail wrapper cases via
`./d.py test compile`, the `CallIface` method-index compile proof, and
`CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-019-codegen-method-index-2 cargo test -p
vo-codegen defer_iface_wrapper_records_method_index_truncation --

Follow-up 043 focused validation covered host-wake ingress rejection,
same-island endpoint response rejection, JIT compile-env cache ownership, and
JIT helper invalid-metadata detail ownership. The app-runtime proof
`app_session_host_wake_rejection_does_not_consume_mailbox_key_043` models a
mailbox entry whose VM waiter has already disappeared and requires the public
wake API to report `HostWakeRejected` without consuming the mailbox dedup key.
The legacy Playground proof `handle_event_reports_rejected_host_wake_043`
models a stale generation-bearing host key and requires the WASM adapter to
return an error instead of `ok`. Studio web-backend source-contract proof
`selfTestStudioRuntimeSourceContractLint` rejects empty scheduled-event catches
and requires scheduled wake failures to flow through `recordGuiFatalError`.

After source fixes, validation passed
`CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-app-runtime-043-proof cargo test -p
vo-app-runtime app_session_host_wake_rejection_does_not_consume_mailbox_key_043
-- --test-threads=1`, full `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-app-runtime-043-proof
cargo test -p vo-app-runtime -- --test-threads=1`, `cargo check -p
vo-app-runtime`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-playground-043-proof
cargo test -p vo-playground handle_event_reports_rejected_host_wake_043 --
--test-threads=1`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-web-043-proof cargo
check -p vo-web --target wasm32-unknown-unknown`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-studio-wasm-043-proof
cargo check --manifest-path apps/studio/wasm/Cargo.toml --target
wasm32-unknown-unknown`, and `npm --prefix apps/studio run build`.

Endpoint/JIT/runtime focused validation passed
`CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-vm-043-proof cargo test -p vo-vm
vm_endpoint_runtime_wake_043_reports_rejected_same_island_endpoint_response --
--test-threads=1`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-vm-043-proof cargo
test -p vo-vm same_island -- --test-threads=1`,
`CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-jit-043-proof cargo test -p vo-jit
env_scope_change -- --test-threads=1`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-jit-043-proof
cargo test -p vo-jit module_scope_change -- --test-threads=1`,
`CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-runtime-043-proof cargo test -p

and `slice_append_metadata_drift_returns_sentinel_instead_of_panicking`.

Gate ownership validation passed `node scripts/ci/docs_lint.mjs`,
`CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-vo-dev-043-proof cargo test -p vo-dev
runtime_surface_contract -- --test-threads=1`, full
`CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-vo-dev-043-proof cargo test -p vo-dev --
--test-threads=1`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-vo-dev-043-proof
cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- lint tasks --strict`, `task plan contract --format
json`, and `task plan vm-production --format json`, with
`cargo-test-vo-app-runtime` selected by both composed gates. `docs-lint` is also
selected by `apps/studio/**` changed-mode routing, and its task inputs now
include `apps/studio/src/lib/backend/web_backend.ts`, so the Studio
source-contract proof has a declared file owner. Final `contract` and
`vm-production` task runs have not yet passed from this source state, so
production signoff remains withdrawn.
