production signoff remains withdrawn.

The 2026-06-19 046 follow-up red-team findings were proof/gate coverage gaps,
not new VM runtime semantic failures. Closure tightened final signoff evidence
to require tracked clean-run JSON for `contract`, `vm-production`, `site`, and
`release-verify`; added `cargo-test-web-hardening` to the vo-web changed-mode
route; made `vo-web` fetch replay prove `HostEventReplaySource::Fetch`; made
legacy Playground prove GUI event loops record only GUI replay keys; widened
the Playground host-wake task filter to run both 043 and 045 proofs; and added
Studio wasm wake rejection source-contract coverage to docs-lint. Validation
passed `node scripts/ci/docs_lint.mjs`, `cargo fmt --check`, `git diff
--check`, focused/full
`CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-vo-dev-046-proof cargo test -p vo-dev`,
strict task lint, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-web-046-proof cargo
test -p vo-web vm_wasm_fetch -- --test-threads=1`,
`CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-playground-046-proof cargo test -p
vo-playground host_wake -- --test-threads=1`,
`CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-web-046-proof cargo run -q -p vo-dev --
task run task:cargo-test-web-hardening`, and
`CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-runtime-surface-046-proof cargo run -q -p
vo-dev -- task run runtime-surface-contract`. PR changed-mode planning now
selects `cargo-test-web-hardening`. Final `contract` and `vm-production` task
runs have not yet passed from this source state, so production signoff remains
withdrawn.

The 2026-06-19 047 interpreter/API red-team audit found one remaining public
closure-call ownership split: `vo-web` shaped closure arguments through
`vo_vm::vm::helpers` before entering `Vm::spawn_call`. Closure moved that
layout ownership into `Vm::spawn_closure_call`, which validates the closure
target, user arg count, constructed call-frame args, and root-bearing slots
before enqueueing a fiber; `vo-web` now uses that semantic API and
`vm::helpers` is crate-owned. Follow-up task-gate validation found the helper
module was still private to `vm`, which broke fresh `vo-web` and legacy
Playground contract compilation for crate-owned `exec/*` and `frame_call`
users. `vm::helpers` is now `pub(crate)` but not `pub`, so raw stack and closure
helpers remain internal to `vo-vm`; `SliceAppend` also explicitly types its
dynamic element metadata tuple so clean builds do not depend on ambiguous
integer inference. Follow-up fresh `vo-vm` validation then found closure-call
layout ownership split between an unused helper re-export and direct
`frame_call` runtime access; `vm::helpers` now owns a crate-private
`closure_call_layout` wrapper and `frame_call` uses that fact source. Validation
passed
`CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-vm-047-proof cargo test -p vo-vm 047 --
--test-threads=1` and `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-web-047-proof cargo
test -p vo-web vm_web_closure_calls_use_vm_owned_frame_entry_047 --
--test-threads=1`; after the crate-ownership fix,
`CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-task-web-hardening-046 cargo run -q -p
vo-dev -- task run task:cargo-test-web-hardening` and
`CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-task-playground-046 cargo run -q -p vo-dev
-- task run task:cargo-test-vo-playground-host-wake` also passed. Follow-up
clean-target validation passed
`CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-vm-047-contract cargo test -p vo-vm
vm_exec_helper_module_is_crate_owned_for_opcode_helpers_047 --
--test-threads=1`,
`CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-vm-047-wake cargo test -p vo-vm
wake_host_event_with_data_rejects_replay_source_mismatch_045 --
--test-threads=1`,
`CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-vm-047-island cargo test -p vo-vm
island_thread_wake_ingress_rejection_exits_loop_045 -- --test-threads=1`,
`CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-app-runtime-047-proof cargo test -p
vo-app-runtime resume_waiting_event_rejects_non_gui_replay_key_045 --
--test-threads=1`, and
`CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-studio-wasm-047-proof cargo check
--manifest-path apps/studio/wasm/Cargo.toml --target wasm32-unknown-unknown`.
Additional clean-target validation passed
`CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-vm-047-proof cargo test -p vo-vm --
--test-threads=1` (353/353), `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-vm-jit-047-proof cargo
test -p vo-vm --features jit 047 -- --test-threads=1`,
`CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-web-047-proof cargo test -p vo-web vm_ --
--test-threads=1`, and
`CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-web-wasm-047-proof cargo check -p vo-web
--target wasm32-unknown-unknown`; `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/
volang-runtime-surface-047-proof cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- task run
runtime-surface-contract` also passed.
The 047 GC/root red-team audit after these gate/source-contract changes found
no new actionable root-scanning blocker. Final `contract` and `vm-production`
task runs have not yet passed from this source state, so production signoff
remains withdrawn.

The 047 contract-gate continuation then exposed two codegen/shared-verifier
blockers in OSR mode rather than a loopback environment issue. Stack-array bulk
copy and stack-array `range` value loads generated `SlotSet`/`SlotGet` without
a verifier-visible constant-length `IndexCheck` fact, and `MapNew` could pack a
struct-key `ValueMeta` that did not match the canonical metadata for its key
`ValueRttid`. Closure added focused codegen proofs
`stack_array_copy_slot_accesses_are_index_checked_047` and
`map_new_struct_key_uses_canonical_key_metadata_047`; moved stack-array
IndexCheck emission behind `FuncBuilder::emit_stack_array_index_check`; and
made `get_or_create_map_metas` derive key/value metadata, slot counts, and bare
key RTTID from canonical transfer metadata. Validation passed
`CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codegen-047-proof cargo test -p
vo-codegen 047 -- --test-threads=1`, full
`CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-codegen-047-proof cargo test -p
vo-codegen -- --test-threads=1`,
`CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-osr-047 ./d.py test osr
tests/lang/cases/runtime/array/array_value_semantics.vo
tests/lang/cases/runtime/array/big_array.vo
tests/lang/cases/runtime/map/map_iteration_order.vo`, the focused
`map_iteration_order.vo` OSR rerun, the two MapNew u16 compile-fail manifest
cases, and the full
`CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/private/tmp/volang-osr-047 ./d.py test osr` suite (1082/1082).
Final `contract` and `vm-production` task runs have not yet passed from this
source state, so production signoff remains withdrawn.

The final 047 red-team audit then found one gate/signoff blocker and one GC
boundary blocker. The gate issue was not VM behavior: docs-lint required
machine evidence for `contract`, `vm-production`, `site`, and `release-verify`,
while `vo-dev task run` only wrote evidence for the first two selectors.
Closure added failing-first proof
`final_signoff_evidence_writes_all_required_selectors_047` and made the
evidence writer share the same final selector fact source as task-plan
injection. The GC issue was behavioral: transition results with
`GcRootEffect::None` could suppress the current-fiber dirty mark even though the
fiber had just run and could have changed scanned roots before sweep. Closure
added `vm_gc_transition_boundary_dirties_current_fiber_047` and made transition
boundaries dirty the current fiber unless the result is `Interrupted`.
Validation passed full `vo-dev` tests (88/88), focused VM GC tests
`vm_gc_transition_boundary_dirties_current_fiber_047` and `vm_gc_` (12/12),
`cargo fmt --check`, `git diff --check`, and docs-lint. Final signoff remains
withdrawn because the tracked-source evidence blocker is still open.

The final 047 JIT/OSR red-team pass found a remaining pending-terminal policy
split in full-function JIT fatal errors: full JIT decoded
`JitBridgeTransition::JitError` as a `RuntimeTransition::fatal_infra`, while
OSR preserved it as `OsrResult::JitError`. That let
`attach_pending_runtime_transitions` commit pending effects that are valid only
for language panic or non-terminal outcomes. Closure added failing-first proof
`vm_jit_fatal_infra_transport_preserves_jit_error_terminal_policy_047`; changed
the bridge decoder to preserve JIT errors until the shared
`ExecResult::JitError` / `OsrResult::JitError` policy owner; and validated the
focused proof plus all `bridge_result` tests under `vo-vm --features jit`.

Default-target startup evidence remains intentionally non-signoff evidence:
prior direct `vo-codegen`, `vo-dev`, and non-JIT `vo-vm` test binaries showed
startup delays before useful harness output in this workspace, while clean
target directories ran the same codegen and runner proofs successfully. On
2026-06-25, direct sampling of the current `vo-vm` source-contract test
binaries found the slow path still in `_dyld_start` before Rust/libtest main;
after that first launch completed, warmed `vo-vm` source-contract reruns passed
for both default and `--features jit` targets. Final `vm-production` and
`contract` task runs have not yet passed from this source state.

wait for a fresh no-actionable red-team result and final gates.

The signoff-withdrawal closure run passed the required widened gates in this

production-readiness signoff while the active blocker ledger remains open:

### Closed 2026-06-15 Hardening Blocker Ledger

The following blockers remain closed for the previously scoped hardening pass.
They are retained as evidence and regression inventory, not as current
production-readiness signoff.

| ID | Contract | Blocking Shape | First Proof Artifact |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| VM-WAKE-REMOTE-ENDPOINT-001 | Remote endpoint waiters must resume through endpoint responses so wait IDs and response accounting stay authoritative. | A remote endpoint waiter can be awakened through bare `WakeFiber`, which does not clear endpoint wait state or decrement `pending_island_responses`. | Home-island queue/select tests where a local send/recv wakes a remote endpoint waiter and the outbound command is `EndpointResponse`, not `WakeFiber`. |
| VM-SCHED-TRANSPORT-HOST-001 | Host-event suspension and deadlock detection must still run after an island transport exists. | `wait_for_work` can enter the main-transport timeout loop before checking host-event waiters or blocked/deadlock outcomes. | Scheduler/VM test with `main_transport` present, no island command, and a blocked host-event waiter that must return `SuspendedForHostEvents`; add a blocked local queue variant that must not spin forever. |
| VM-CALL-CLOSURE-CANON-001 | Closure calls must store canonical closure references in callee slot0 before `ClosureGet`. | Closure validation reads the canonical object header but can pass an interior raw reference into the callee frame. | VM call/replay/go test that invokes a closure through an interior ref and asserts callee slot0 is canonical and `ClosureGet` reads capture 0 correctly. |
| VM-VER-ZERO-SLOT-RANGE-001 | Verified bytecode must not panic VM borrowed-frame helpers through empty arg/ret ranges. | `verify_range` accepts `count == 0` without checking `start`, so no-arg/no-ret calls can use an out-of-frame buffer start and panic in runtime helpers. | `vo-common-core` verifier tests rejecting out-of-frame zero-slot `Call`, `CallClosure`, and `CallIface` buffers before `Vm::load`. |
| VM-GC-PANIC-ISLAND-ROOT-001 | Panic/recover payload roots must use the same interface GC-ref predicate as ordinary interface scanning. | Recoverable panic state and JIT panic messages use a hand-written `InterfaceSlot::is_ref_type()` list that misses `ValueKind::Island`. | Forced-GC root test with `panic(make(island))` or equivalent `InterfaceSlot` in `PanicState::Recoverable`, plus the JIT panic-message variant under `--features jit`. |
| VM-JIT-GOSTART-PENDING-001 | JIT and interpreter must agree on when `GoStart`/`GoIsland` side effects are committed. | JIT callbacks push spawn/island transitions as pending work and continue; terminal panic or JIT error clears the pending transition even though interpreter semantics have already published the side effect. | JIT parity test for `go child(); panic(...)` and `go island(...); panic(...)` proving side effects observed before panic are identical to interpreter behavior. |
| VM-OSR-PREPARED-CALL-STACK-001 | OSR code must refresh cached stack bases after helpers that can reallocate fiber stacks. | OSR dynamic prepared-call OK path can return after `jit_push_frame` grows the stack without refreshing the loop compiler's cached locals/base pointer. | OSR stress test with dynamic closure/interface call that forces stack growth, then resumes caller local writes and compares with interpreter behavior. |
| VM-VER-CLOSUREGET-SCOPE-001 | `ClosureGet` is legal only in closure-shaped functions with runtime-compatible capture layout. | Verifier checks slot shape but does not require the function to be a closure or the runtime object to have the requested capture count. | Verifier test rejecting `ClosureGet` in a non-closure function and VM defensive test for non-closure slot0 or too-short capture layout returning structured error. |
| VM-ARCH-BOUNDARY-FACT-SOURCES-001 | Runtime-boundary, wake, call-frame, GC-ref, and JIT callback facts must each have one source of truth. | Current behavior is split across `WakeFiber` vs `EndpointResponse`, `InterfaceSlot::is_ref_type()` vs `data_is_gc_ref`, discarded `RuntimeTransition.resume`, duplicated frame builders, and callback-specific stack-refresh assumptions. | Source audit plus focused tests showing each fact source has one owning helper/contract and no parallel active path for the same semantic event. |

### Historical Gate Coverage

Post-red-team update, 2026-06-15: the previous hardening gaps were folded into
the hardening checklist and `vm-production` route, and the blockers in the
closure checklist below were closed by coverage selected by the production
gates at that source state. This evidence is now historical and is not current
signoff until the 2026-06-16 blocker ledger is closed:
- interface comparability and map-key hashability use recursive runtime
  predicates with language regressions for nested uncomparable/unhashable
  shapes;
- queue/island wake coverage proves wrong-select, wrong-source, duplicate, and
  stale commands leave wait state unchanged;
- transport unpack coverage rejects endpoint handle metadata mismatches instead
  of aliasing live endpoints by id alone;
- JIT array/slice store coverage proves typed-barrier metadata is used for
  composite 8-byte values, including no-ref structs whose payloads look like
  object refs;
- callback ABI coverage includes direct exported-helper drift tests for null
  pointers, oversized counts, and raw-buffer validation;
- JIT/OSR boundary coverage proves scheduler/runtime mutation happens at the
  boundary owner, not while callbacks or OSR hold incompatible raw borrows;
- docs lint passed after this document was updated with the closed blocker
  state.

Previously closed, 2026-06-15: these gaps remain listed as the gap-to-proof map
for the completed hardening slice and future regression audits:

- endpoint response accounting is covered by remote-waiter queue/select tests,
  not only stale response rejection tests;
- scheduler coverage includes host-event, island-transport, and local
  blocked-state combinations;
- verifier hardening includes empty ranges and accepted-module no-panic
  execution through focused `vm_` selectors;
- GC root coverage includes every `ValueKind` carried by panic/recover
  interface payloads through the shared interface GC-ref predicate;
- JIT parity covers pending runtime side effects followed by terminal
  outcomes;
- OSR stress covers dynamic calls that can reallocate fiber stacks;
- readiness docs now carry closed blocker IDs plus final validation evidence,
  and the final `contract`/`site` gates ran docs lint.

### Final Gate Validation, 2026-06-15

The 2026-06-15 active-blocker closure run passed the required widened gates in
this workspace. This remains historical evidence for that source state only; it
does not restore current VM production-readiness signoff while the 2026-06-17
active ledger and VM-TEST-BINARY-STARTUP-HANG-002 remain open:

- `cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- task run vm-production` passed. The run selected
  and passed the new `vm-hardening` route: `cargo test -p vo-common-core vm_`
  passed 4/4, `cargo test -p vo-jit vm_` passed 8/8, `cargo test -p vo-vm
  vm_` passed 59/59, and `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_` passed
  75/75.
- `vm-production` also passed the widened VM/JIT/GC/verifier and language
  gates, including `vo-test-osr` 1045/1045, `vo-test-gc` 36/36,
  `vo-test-jit-contract` 197/197, full `vo-runtime` tests 129/129 plus GC
  model 8/8 and GC perf 3/3, and release smoke coverage for language,
  JIT/OSR stress, runtime model, benchmark, and GC root-table performance.
- `cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- task run contract` passed, covering the composed
  common-core, VM, JIT, manifest, OSR, nostd, WASM, scheduler-contract,
  runtime, stdlib, web-runtime, analysis, codegen, module, release-lint,
  docs-lint, and WASM-check route.
- `cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- task run site` passed after rerunning with
  filesystem escalation for the sibling `vogui` npm workspace; it built
  `vo-web`, `vogui`, Studio WASM, Studio, and quickplay/static smoke assets.
- `cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- task run release-verify` passed for `vogui`,
  `voplay`, `vopack`, and `vostore`.

Additional release-gate repairs discovered during validation are covered by
source regressions: `global_array_root_metadata_references_array_runtime_type`
guards global array runtime metadata in `vo-codegen`, and
`gc_scan_zero_byte_struct_array_elements_do_not_use_synthetic_slots` plus
`gc_scan_struct_array_elements_uses_metadata_width_not_physical_stride` guard
struct-array GC scanning in `vo-runtime`.

Hot-path evidence: the final `vm-production` smoke benchmark reported
`Vo-JIT-Hot` 58.10x faster than `Vo-VM` on `jit-loop`, and the GC root-table
perf smoke completed with 4096 objects, 4096 roots, 16 steps, max work 8192,
and total time 0.148 ms. The hardening changes avoid new work in the interpreter
opcode loop; scheduler changes stay on idle/wait edges, JIT go-side-effect
changes commit at callback boundaries, and GC scanner changes add exact
metadata checks only while tracing array elements.

#### Closure Evidence: VM-WAKE-REMOTE-ENDPOINT-001

Regression coverage:
`exec::queue::tests::vm_wake_remote_endpoint_001_local_recv_acks_remote_endpoint_sender`,
`exec::select::tests::vm_wake_remote_endpoint_001_select_send_responds_to_remote_receiver`,
and
`exec::select::tests::vm_wake_remote_endpoint_001_select_recv_acks_remote_sender`.
The source fix routes remote endpoint senders through `RemoteSendAck` and
remote endpoint receivers through `RemoteRecvData`, then converts both
interpreter and JIT select/queue outcomes into `EndpointResponse` island
commands instead of bare `WakeFiber`.

Task-graph coverage: `vm-production` now includes `vm-hardening`, which selects
`cargo-test-vm-hardening` and `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`; `vo-dev task plan`
confirmed both tasks are present in the `vm-production` expansion.

Validation evidence, 2026-06-15: `cargo test -p vo-vm
vm_wake_remote_endpoint_001` passed 3/3; `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit
vm_wake_remote_endpoint_001` passed 3/3; `cargo test -p vo-vm vm_` passed 42/42;
`cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_` passed 50/50; `cargo run -q -p vo-dev
-- task plan vm-production --format json` selected the new hardening tasks.

Hot-path note: the change stays on already-ready queue/select completion paths
and JIT callback transition commits. It adds no scheduler polling, GC scan, or
per-op broad boundary virtualization to the interpreter opcode loop.

#### Closure Evidence: VM-SCHED-TRANSPORT-HOST-001

Regression coverage:
`vm::tests::vm_sched_transport_host_001_transport_does_not_hide_host_event_waiter`
and
`vm::tests::vm_sched_transport_host_001_transport_does_not_spin_on_local_queue_block`.
The first test installs an idle `main_transport` and proves a blocked host event
waiter returns `SuspendedForHostEvents`; the second proves a local queue block
with no live endpoint, I/O waiter, or pending island response returns `Blocked`
instead of cycling through transport timeouts.

Source fix: `wait_for_work` now preserves pending island response priority,
then checks host-event waiters and local blocked/deadlock state before entering
the main-transport timeout path. Remote/live-endpoint blocked states can still
wait on transport commands.

Task-graph coverage: the blocker tests are named with the `vm_` prefix and are
selected by `cargo-test-vm-hardening` and `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`, both of
which are part of `vm-production`.

Validation evidence, 2026-06-15: `cargo test -p vo-vm
vm_sched_transport_host_001` passed 2/2; `cargo test -p vo-vm vm_` passed
44/44; `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_` passed 52/52.

Hot-path note: this touches only the scheduler idle/wait path after no runnable
fiber exists. It removes avoidable 100 ms transport timeout retries for host
events and local blocked states, and does not add work to interpreter, JIT, GC,
or queue hot paths.

#### Closure Evidence: VM-CALL-CLOSURE-CANON-001

Regression coverage:
`frame_call::tests::vm_call_closure_canon_001_borrowed_call_stores_canonical_slot0_for_closure_get`,
`frame_call::tests::vm_call_closure_canon_001_extern_replay_closure_uses_canonical_ref_for_slot0`,
and
`vm::helpers::tests::vm_call_closure_canon_001_go_closure_fiber_stores_canonical_slot0_from_interior_ref`.
The first test failed before the fix with callee slot0 containing an interior
closure pointer; after the fix, `ClosureGet` reads capture 0 from the canonical
slot0.

Source fix: closure call-frame layout now receives the canonical closure ref
after GC canonicalization. The go-spawn fiber builder now accepts the GC,
canonicalizes and kind-checks the closure object before reading its header, and
uses the canonical ref for slot0 layout. Interpreter `GoStart`, same-island
`GoIsland`, remote island spawn unpacking, and JIT goroutine callbacks route
through that helper.

Task-graph coverage: all three regression names carry the `vm_` prefix and are
selected by `cargo-test-vm-hardening` and `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`, both of
which are part of `vm-production`.

Validation evidence, 2026-06-15: `cargo test -p vo-vm
vm_call_closure_canon_001` passed 3/3; `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit
vm_call_closure_canon_001` passed 3/3; `cargo test -p vo-vm vm_` passed 47/47;
`cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_` passed 55/55.

Hot-path note: the added canonicalization is on call/goroutine frame
construction boundaries, not per-op execution. It replaces unsafe raw closure
header/layout use with the existing GC canonical-ref index and does not add
work to the interpreter opcode loop after the frame is built.

#### Closure Evidence: VM-VER-ZERO-SLOT-RANGE-001

Regression coverage:
`verifier::tests::vm_ver_zero_slot_range_001_rejects_static_call_zero_arg_ret_out_of_frame_start`,
`verifier::tests::vm_ver_zero_slot_range_001_rejects_call_closure_zero_arg_ret_out_of_frame_start`,
and
`verifier::tests::vm_ver_zero_slot_range_001_rejects_call_iface_zero_arg_ret_out_of_frame_start`.
Before the fix, these cases panicked in `local_layout` because `verify_range`
accepted `count == 0` without checking an out-of-frame `start`.

Source fix: `verify_range` now rejects zero-slot ranges whose `start` is beyond
`local_slots` or beyond the function's `slot_types` table before any caller can
slice the layout. The change is verifier-only and preserves valid empty ranges
at the end of a frame.

Task-graph coverage: the tests carry the `vm_` prefix and are selected by
`cargo-test-common-core-hardening`, which is part of `vm-production`.

Validation evidence, 2026-06-15: `cargo test -p vo-common-core
vm_ver_zero_slot_range_001` passed 3/3; `cargo test -p vo-common-core vm_`
passed 3/3.

Hot-path note: this is a load-time verifier check only. It adds no interpreter,
JIT, scheduler, or GC runtime cost.

#### Closure Evidence: VM-GC-PANIC-ISLAND-ROOT-001

Regression coverage:
`vm::tests::vm_gc_panic_island_root_001_recoverable_panic_scans_island_interface_payload`
and
`vm::tests::vm_gc_panic_island_root_001_jit_panic_msg_scans_island_interface_payload`.
The recoverable panic test failed before the fix because the island handle was
collected during forced GC. `objects::interface::tests::interface_slot_ref_predicate_matches_interface_gc_scanner`
locks the predicate source itself.

Source fix: `InterfaceSlot::is_ref_type()` now delegates to
`data_is_gc_ref(slot0)`, the same predicate used by ordinary interface GC
scanning. This pulls panic/recover payload roots, JIT panic messages, and stack
interface roots through one ValueKind-to-GC-ref fact source, including
`ValueKind::Island`.

Task-graph coverage: the VM regressions carry the `vm_` prefix and are selected
by `cargo-test-vm-hardening` and `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`; `cargo-test-runtime`
in `vm-production` also selects the runtime predicate test.

Validation evidence, 2026-06-15: `cargo test -p vo-vm
vm_gc_panic_island_root_001` passed 1/1; `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit
vm_gc_panic_island_root_001` passed 2/2; `cargo test -p vo-runtime
interface_slot_ref_predicate_matches_interface_gc_scanner` passed 1/1; `cargo
test -p vo-vm vm_` passed 48/48; `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit vm_`
passed 57/57.

Hot-path note: this changes an existing interface predicate used only when
scanning or classifying interface roots. It does not add new root containers or
extra GC traversal work; it corrects the predicate result for `Island`.

#### Closure Evidence: VM-JIT-GOSTART-PENDING-001

Regression coverage:
`vm::jit::callbacks::goroutine::tests::vm_jit_gostart_pending_001_go_start_spawn_survives_later_terminal_result`
and
`vm::jit::callbacks::goroutine::tests::vm_jit_gostart_pending_001_go_island_command_survives_later_terminal_result`.
The tests prove JIT `go` side effects are scheduler-visible or outbound before
a later terminal JIT result clears pending runtime transitions.

Source fix: JIT `GoStart` and `GoIsland` callbacks now commit spawned fibers and
remote island commands immediately through `apply_runtime_transition`. They
leave only an empty pending yield cue so normal JIT success still reaches the
same scheduler boundary shape, while panic/JIT-error discard can no longer drop
the already-published side effect.

Task-graph coverage: both regressions carry the `vm_` prefix and are selected
by `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`, which is part of `vm-production`.

Validation evidence, 2026-06-15: `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit
vm_jit_gostart_pending_001` passed 2/2; `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit
vm_` passed 59/59.

Hot-path note: the callback still performs one cold runtime-transition apply at
the go boundary and keeps the pre-existing single pending boundary cue for
normal success. It does not add per-instruction scheduler checks or broaden the
interpreter/JIT opcode loop.

#### Closure Evidence: VM-OSR-PREPARED-CALL-STACK-001

Regression coverage:
`call_helpers::dynamic::tests::vm_osr_prepared_call_stack_001_dynamic_ok_path_refreshes_stack_base_before_returns`
proves the shared dynamic prepared-call OK path refreshes the cached stack base
before return copying, including the zero-return case where the caller still
needs refreshed locals for subsequent writes. The language regression
`tests/lang/cases/bugs/2026_06_15_osr_prepared_call_stack.vo` warms a large-frame
closure target, enters a single hot OSR loop, calls the closure through a
prepared dynamic call that can grow `fiber.stack`, and then writes caller locals
after the OK return.

Source fix/proof: current source already routes dynamic closure and interface
miss paths through the shared `copy_dynamic_call_returns` helper, which calls
`refresh_stack_base_after_reallocation()` before any caller-local writes.
`LoopCompiler::refresh_stack_base_after_reallocation` reloads `ctx.stack_ptr`
and `ctx.jit_bp`, then redefines the cached `locals_ptr` variable. The closure
for this blocker is therefore a source-proof test plus a durable gate route,
not a semantic rewrite.

Task-graph coverage: `vm-production` now includes `cargo-test-jit-hardening`
through `vm-hardening`; the OSR language case is selected by the existing
`vo-test-osr` route in `vm-production`.

Validation evidence, 2026-06-15: `cargo test -p vo-jit
vm_osr_prepared_call_stack_001` passed 1/1; `cargo test -p vo-jit vm_` passed
4/4; `./d.py test osr cases/bugs/2026_06_15_osr_prepared_call_stack.vo` passed
1/1; `cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- task plan vm-production --format json`
selected `cargo-test-jit-hardening`.

Hot-path note: no new runtime work was added. The existing refresh remains a
single context reload after callbacks that can reallocate `fiber.stack`, and
the new Rust test protects that ordering without adding interpreter, scheduler,
or GC overhead.

#### Closure Evidence: VM-VER-CLOSUREGET-SCOPE-001

Regression coverage:
`verifier::tests::vm_ver_closureget_scope_001_rejects_closure_get_in_non_closure_function`
failed before the fix because a non-closure function with forged capture
metadata verified successfully.
`exec::closure::tests::vm_ver_closureget_scope_001_rejects_non_closure_slot0_without_header_panic`
and
`exec::closure::tests::vm_ver_closureget_scope_001_rejects_too_short_runtime_capture_layout`
prove malformed runtime slot0 or capture-count state returns a typed error
before raw capture memory is read.

Source fix: the module verifier now rejects `ClosureGet` unless
`FunctionDef::is_closure` marks the current function as closure-shaped.
`exec_closure_get` now canonicalizes slot0 through the GC, verifies
`ValueKind::Closure`, and checks `capture_count` before calling
`closure::get_capture`. The interpreter converts those typed errors into
structured `JitError` results for malformed runtime state.

Task-graph coverage: the verifier test is selected by
`cargo-test-common-core-hardening`; the VM defensive tests are selected by
`cargo-test-vm-hardening` and `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`, all under
`vm-production`.

Validation evidence, 2026-06-15: `cargo test -p vo-common-core
vm_ver_closureget_scope_001` passed 1/1 after first failing; `cargo test -p
vo-vm vm_ver_closureget_scope_001` passed 2/2; `cargo test -p vo-common-core
vm_` passed 4/4; `cargo test -p vo-vm vm_` passed 50/50; `cargo test -p vo-vm
--features jit vm_` passed 61/61.

Hot-path note: the verifier scope check is load-time only. The defensive
runtime checks are on `ClosureGet`, which already dereferenced the closure
object; the change replaces unchecked header/capture reads with canonicalization
and bounds checks for malformed state rather than adding broad interpreter-loop
boundary machinery.

#### Closure Evidence: VM-ARCH-BOUNDARY-FACT-SOURCES-001

Regression/source-audit coverage:
`runtime_boundary::tests::vm_arch_boundary_fact_sources_001_source_owners_are_documented_in_code`
guards the source owners for remote endpoint response identity, closure
call-frame layout, interface GC-ref predicates, checked `ClosureGet`, and JIT
callback raw-slot validation.
`runtime_boundary::tests::vm_arch_boundary_fact_sources_001_resume_policy_owner_is_explicit`
proves `apply_runtime_transition` is not a parallel resume-PC owner, while
`vm::extern_call::tests::vm_arch_boundary_fact_sources_001_resume_helper_owns_frame_pc_update`
proves `set_current_frame_pc_for_resume` owns frame-PC updates for runtime
transition resume policies.
`call_helpers::callback_abi::tests::vm_arch_boundary_fact_sources_001_jit_callback_abi_manifest_owns_callsite_policy`
locks JIT callback lowering wrappers to the runtime ABI manifest.

Source fix/proof: the previous blockers consolidated the reachable split facts:
remote endpoint waiters resume through `EndpointResponse`; closure frame
builders share `closure::call_layout`; interface panic/recover roots call
`data_is_gc_ref`; dynamic prepared-call OK paths refresh cached stack bases
through `StackRefresh`; and JIT callback callsites validate against the ABI
manifest. This blocker adds source-level guardrails for those owners and keeps
the runtime-transition resume update in the extern replay helper instead of the
scheduler applier.

Task-graph coverage: `vm-production` selects `vm-hardening`, including
`cargo-test-common-core-hardening`, `cargo-test-jit-hardening`,
`cargo-test-vm-hardening`, and `cargo-test-vm-hardening-jit`. The production
plan also selects the full runtime, JIT, VM, OSR, GC, contract, and stress
routes.

Validation evidence, 2026-06-15: `cargo test -p vo-vm
vm_arch_boundary_fact_sources_001` passed 3/3; `cargo test -p vo-jit
vm_arch_boundary_fact_sources_001` passed 1/1; `cargo test -p vo-jit vm_`
passed 5/5; `cargo test -p vo-vm vm_` passed 53/53; `cargo test -p vo-vm
--features jit vm_` passed 64/64; `cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- task plan
vm-production --format json` selected `cargo-test-jit-hardening` as well as
the existing VM hardening tasks.

Hot-path note: this closure adds tests and source audits only. No interpreter,
JIT, scheduler, or GC hot path changed for this blocker.

### Prior Closed Blocker Ledger, 2026-06-14

The table below is retained as historical evidence from the prior bounded
closure slice. It does not override the active blockers above.

| ID | Status | Closure Evidence |
| --- | --- | --- |
| VM-JIT-004 | Closed in source. | JIT callback raw slot validation is shared in `vo-vm/src/vm/jit/callbacks/helpers.rs` and applied to closure `go`, island `go`, queue send/recv, extern-call scratch buffers, and defer push. Focused proof: `cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit jit_callback_abi` covers arg-slot drift, null non-empty buffers, width overflow, and no pending transition/spawn/wake/island publication before validation. |
| CI-TOOL-001 | Closed in tooling and workflow policy. | `eng/toolchains.toml` declares pinned `hyperfine` with a `cargo install` bootstrap command; `vo-dev tool bootstrap --task ... --apply` accepts task/group selectors; PR workflow runs centralized `vo-dev` bootstrap/check before each selected task; `vo-dev lint all` self-checks that PR-selected required tools have CI provisioning. |
| STDLIB-EXT-001 | Closed in provider, manifest, and tests. | `bytes.ToTitle` and `bytes.EqualFold` have `vo-stdlib` providers, effect-manifest entries, native/JIT/WASM language coverage in `tests/lang/cases/stdlib/bytes_casefold_title.vo`, and a declaration-to-manifest/provider ownership contract in `vo-stdlib`. |
| GATE-001 | Closed in task graph. | `stdlib-contract` is part of `vm-production`; `cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- task plan vm-production --format json` expands to `cargo-test-stdlib` and `cargo-test-web-runtime-wasm`. |
| GATE-002 | Closed in CI configuration. | `.github/workflows/production-readiness.yml` adds a manual VM production gate workflow that reads `vo-dev` metadata, bootstraps/checks tools through `vo-dev`, and runs `cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- task run vm-production`. |

### Prior Closure Work Packages, 2026-06-14

Work package 1: close VM-JIT-004.

1. Add a small shared validation helper for callback slot counts and raw buffers
   in the JIT callback layer.
2. In `jit_go_start` and `jit_go_island`, resolve the closure target and
   closure call layout before copying `args_ptr`; reject when supplied user arg
   slots do not match `func.param_slots - layout.arg_offset`, or when a
   non-empty argument buffer is null.
3. In queue callbacks, reject element slot widths and null non-empty source or
   destination buffers before calling queue core or replay helpers.
4. In `jit_call_extern`, reject a null scratch buffer whenever the resolved
   argument/return shape requires a non-empty buffer before calling
   `from_raw_parts_mut`.
5. Audit `jit_defer_push` under the same helper: reject `arg_count` overflow
   before truncating to `u16`, and reject null non-empty `args_ptr` before GC
   allocation/copy.
6. Add focused negative tests that assert the result is `JitError` or the
   callback-specific infra error and that no pending transition, spawn, wake,
   island command, or GC-visible mutation is published before validation.

Work package 2: close CI-TOOL-001.

1. Choose one policy and encode it in `eng/*.toml` plus `cmd/vo-dev`, not in
   ad hoc shell snippets:
   - preferred: declare a pinned `hyperfine` version and teach
     `vo-dev tool bootstrap --task <task> --apply` how to install it
     reproducibly on CI;
   - fallback: remove benchmark smoke from PR selection and run it only from
     `vm-production` or an explicit production workflow.
2. Update CI to call the centralized task-tool bootstrap/check path before
   running each selected task.
3. Add a self-check that fails if a task selected for CI requires a tool that
   has neither a CI provisioning branch nor an explicit non-PR policy.

Work package 3: close STDLIB-EXT-001.

1. Implement and register `bytes.ToTitle` and `bytes.EqualFold`, or remove the
   declarations if they are intentionally out of production scope.
2. Add `bytes_ToTitle` and `bytes_EqualFold` manifest entries with the correct
   effect facts.
3. Add native and WASM language tests that call both APIs.
4. Add an extern declaration coverage contract that filters known runtime
   builtins and platform-native routes, then requires every remaining stdlib
   `.vo` extern declaration to have manifest/provider ownership.

Work package 4: close GATE-001 and GATE-002.

1. Add `stdlib-contract` to `vm-production`, or revise this document so
   `vm-production` does not claim stdlib/web-runtime cargo coverage.
2. Add a production-readiness workflow path that runs `vm-production` or
   `full` through `vo-dev` on GitHub Actions.
3. Keep all gate composition in `eng/tasks.toml` and `cmd/vo-dev`; workflows
   should invoke named `vo-dev` tasks rather than duplicating task policy.

### Prior Closure Validation Sequence, 2026-06-14

Run these in order after the work packages land. Completed locally on
2026-06-14; commands requiring localhost sockets or first-party workspace writes
were rerun outside the sandbox with the same named `vo-dev` task:

```sh
cargo fmt --all -- --check
cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- lint all
cargo test -p vo-vm --features jit jit_callback_abi
cargo test -p vo-stdlib --features std
cargo test -p vo-web-runtime-wasm
cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- task plan pr --changed
cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- task run vm-production
cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- task run contract
cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- task run site
cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- task run release-verify
```

All commands above passed in that workspace snapshot. They remain useful
historical evidence for the listed closure slice, but they are no longer enough
for production signoff until the active blockers and gate gaps above are closed.

### Closed Blocker Ledger, 2026-06-13

The table below is retained as the closed blocker ledger from the prior landing
slice: each item was release-blocking until the current Rust owner paths and
the named proof artifact agreed. New signoff blockers must be tracked in the
active table above.

| ID | Contract | Blocking Shape | Owning Area | First Proof Artifact |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| VM-RT-001 | Pending side effects commit or discard as one unit. | JIT queue/select can mutate queue state, enqueue a pending wake, then lose that wake on terminal discard. | `vo-vm/src/runtime_boundary.rs`, `vo-vm/src/vm/jit/callbacks/queue.rs`, `vo-vm/src/vm/jit/callbacks/select.rs` | JIT callback unit test that injects a terminal result after queue-local mutation and proves no waiter is stranded. |
| VM-RT-002 | Fatal terminal results do not commit uncommitted side effects. | `JitError` maps into `FatalInfra` while still applying pending spawns/wakes. | `vo-vm/src/runtime_boundary.rs`, `vo-vm/src/vm/jit/invoke.rs` | `vo-vm --features jit` test where a pending spawn or wake is followed by `JitError` and scheduler state is unchanged. |
| VM-GC-001 | Root-bearing spawns dirty or expose roots before GC. | Interpreter `GoStart` and public `spawn_call` create new fiber roots without dirtying all affected root owners. | `vo-vm/src/vm/mod.rs`, `vo-vm/src/runtime_boundary.rs`, `vo-vm/src/gc_roots.rs` | Forced-GC test that allocates an argument root, spawns a fiber, runs GC before the child executes, and proves the root survives. |
| VM-WAKE-001 | Rejected external wake commands must not mutate wait state. | Stale or wrong-state I/O ready can consume the registration before rejection. | `vo-vm/src/scheduler.rs`, `vo-vm/src/runtime_boundary.rs` | Scheduler/runtime-command test that checks rejected I/O ready leaves fiber state, registration map, blocked count, and resume token unchanged. |
| VM-VER-001 | Closure object layout is verified before execution. | `ClosureNew` verifies the target function exists, but not that the encoded capture count matches the target capture layout. | `vo-common-core/src/verifier.rs`, `vo-vm/src/exec/closure.rs` | `vo-common-core` verifier test rejecting a `ClosureNew` capture-count mismatch before `Vm::load` dispatch. |
| VM-VER-002 | Heap return `GcRef` slots are initialized and canonical before deref. | Heap-return paths directly dereference runtime `GcRef` values that verifier cannot prove valid. | `vo-vm/src/exec/unwind.rs`, `vo-common-core/src/verifier.rs` | Heap-return unit test that invalid, nil, or uninitialized `GcRef` slots become structured `VmError` or verifier rejection. |
| VM-VER-003 | Runtime object layout operands agree with metadata. | `MapNew` and `QueueNew` validate register shape, but do not prove encoded key/value/element slot counts match the metadata the runtime stores. | `vo-common-core/src/verifier.rs`, `vo-vm/src/exec/map.rs`, `vo-vm/src/exec/queue.rs` | `vo-common-core` verifier tests for map and queue metadata drift plus `Vm::load` rejection tests. |
| VM-JIT-001 | JIT callback ABI drift is rejected before memory mutation. | `jit_go_start` regular-function branch copies `arg_slots` without checking function parameter/local slot bounds. | `vo-vm/src/vm/jit/callbacks/goroutine.rs` | JIT callback test that mismatched `arg_slots` returns `JitError` before stack writes or pending spawn publication. |
| VM-ARCH-001 | No active side path for the same semantic event. | Remote island spawn/transfer/close still performs direct side effects outside the transition/command kernel. | `vo-vm/src/exec/island.rs`, `vo-vm/src/vm/types.rs`, `vo-vm/src/vm/jit/callbacks/goroutine.rs` | Source audit plus focused island test proving command bridge validation owns the remote side effect. |
| VM-ARCH-002 | Public boundary APIs are either implemented or absent. | `HostCommand` and `RuntimeBoundary::Wait` are partially wired surfaces with no complete producer/applier story. | `vo-vm/src/runtime_boundary.rs` | Compile-time/source audit where unused surfaces are deleted or a producer/applier/test chain exists. |
| VM-JIT-002 | Missing semantic context is an error. | JIT register-effect queries can fall back to static effects when module facts are missing. | `vo-jit/src/effects.rs`, `vo-jit/src/effects/dynamic.rs` | `vo-jit` effect tests proving every metadata-dependent opcode errors without facts instead of returning a static approximation. |
| VM-JIT-003 | Opcode families are not duplicated in consumers. | Loop analysis and VM OSR maintain call-family matches outside the semantic row. | `vo-jit/src/loop_analysis.rs`, `vo-vm/src/vm/jit/osr.rs` | Semantic-row consistency test that derives may-call/call-target behavior from `vo-jit/src/semantics`. |

Implementation update, 2026-06-13:

- Closed in source with focused tests: VM-RT-001, VM-RT-002, VM-GC-001,
  VM-WAKE-001, VM-VER-001, VM-VER-002, VM-VER-003, VM-JIT-001,
  VM-JIT-002, VM-JIT-003, and VM-ARCH-001. Remote island spawn,
  endpoint send/recv/recv-data/close, endpoint close finalization, endpoint
  response, and queue-handle transfer now publish `IslandCommandEffect` through
  `RuntimeTransition`; terminal JIT errors discard uncommitted island effects.
- Also closed under VM-ARCH-001: `RuntimeBoundary::Continue` is not a
  scheduler quiescence marker. The scheduling loop keeps the current running
  fiber schedulable after a side-effect-carrying `Continue`, which restores
  interpreter/JIT parity for island/port work that commits an endpoint effect
  and then continues in the same fiber.
- Closed by source deletion/compile-time audit: VM-ARCH-002. The current
  runtime boundary has no inert `HostCommand` or `RuntimeBoundary::Wait`
  surface.
- `vo-dev task run vm-production` passed on 2026-06-13 in this workspace after
  the benchmark group was expanded to include VM/JIT hot-loop smoke and GC
  root-table perf smoke. `eng/tasks.toml` also routes the release-grade `full`
  validation surface through `vm-production`.
- Deterministic fuzz-like smoke now lives inside existing crate tests:
  `module_verifier_deterministic_fuzz_smoke_returns_structured_results`,
  `scheduler_wake_model_deterministic_fuzz_smoke_preserves_wait_invariants`,
  and `gc_model_deterministic_fuzz_smoke_preserves_reachable_roots`. Remaining
  hardening work is gate expansion, not an open blocker in this audit: add
  dedicated external fuzz/sanitizer runners and historical benchmark thresholds
  when those facilities land.

## Readiness Matrix

Every production invariant must map to an owner, a proof artifact, and a
performance guard.

| Invariant | Owner Paths | Proof Artifact | Performance Guard |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Verified bytecode is the safety boundary for VM object/layout facts. | `vo-common-core/src/verifier.rs`, `vo-vm/src/vm/mod.rs`, `vo-vm/src/exec/*` | `cargo test -p vo-common-core verifier`, VM load rejection tests, and deterministic bytecode verifier fuzz-like no-panic corpus. | Verification cost measured on representative modules; runtime hot paths do not repeat expensive layout walks. |
| Runtime side effects commit or discard through one boundary policy. | `vo-vm/src/runtime_boundary.rs`, `vo-vm/src/vm/jit/*`, `vo-vm/src/exec/island.rs` | Focused tests for commit, discard, stale wake, wrong-source wake, terminal cleanup, and remote island command routing. | No heap allocation, trait dispatch, or broad virtualization added to ordinary interpreter opcode arms. |
| Scheduler and wake state survive stale, duplicate, cancelled, and wrong-source commands. | `vo-vm/src/scheduler.rs`, `vo-vm/src/exec/queue.rs`, `vo-vm/src/exec/select.rs`, `vo-runtime/src/objects/queue_state.rs` | Model tests for wait/wake/cancel/close/stale generation/source mismatch plus deterministic wake-model fuzz-like state-preservation assertions. | Runqueue and wait operations keep current expected complexity; common wake paths do not scan unrelated waiter classes. |
| GC sees every root-bearing boundary before collection can reclaim it. | `vo-vm/src/gc_roots.rs`, `vo-runtime/src/gc.rs`, `vo-vm/src/runtime_boundary.rs`, `vo-vm/src/vm/jit/materialize.rs` | Forced-GC matrix, dirty-epoch tests for go/spawn, waits, queues/select, endpoints, extern replay, side exits, materialized frames, and deterministic GC model fuzz-like root-preservation smoke. | Dirty marking is precise enough to avoid permanent `AllRootsDirty` on hot paths unless justified by measurement. |
| JIT and interpreter have the same semantics for production-supported bytecode. | `vo-jit/src/semantics`, `vo-jit/src/effects.rs`, `vo-vm/src/vm/jit`, `vo-vm/src/vm/jit_mgr.rs` | Interpreter/JIT parity tests for every VM regression, semantic-row consistency tests, strict JIT verifier tests. | Direct calls, OSR, inline caches, and intrinsic fast paths stay enabled only with measured thresholds. |
| Unsafe runtime object access has a local proof chain. | `vo-runtime/src/objects`, `vo-vm/src/exec`, `vo-vm/src/vm`, `vo-vm/src/vm/jit/callbacks` | Unsafe audit ledger, Miri/sanitizer subsets where practical, and deterministic regressions for every fuzz crash. | Defensive checks are load-time or cold-path unless a benchmark accepts the cost. |
| Web/WASM/Studio ingress uses the same wake and boundary validators. | `vo-web`, `vo-app-runtime`, `apps/studio`, `apps/playground-legacy` | Host/I/O/event wake command tests prove browser/app ingress reaches the same command bridge before scheduler mutation. | Browser/app event dispatch remains bounded and avoids duplicate polling or repeated full-state scans. |
| A release gate composes the production evidence. | `eng/tasks.toml`, `eng/tests.toml`, `eng/toolchains.toml`, `eng/ci.toml`, `cmd/vo-dev/src/task_*`, `cmd/vo-dev/src/test_*` | `cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- task run vm-production`, plus `vo-dev task plan full` proving the release-grade surface expands through the gate, with blocker-aware failure output plus deterministic fuzz-like smoke, `vo-bench-jit-loop-smoke`, and `vo-gc-perf-root-table-smoke`. | Gate uses existing task graph and cached/subset routing; perf smoke stays narrow enough for the production gate, while heavier external fuzz/sanitizer jobs remain future dedicated tasks. |

## Workstream 0: Governance And Baseline

Goal: stop accidental churn and make production readiness measurable.

Tasks:

- create a VM readiness issue or tracker that mirrors `Current Release
  Blockers`;
- record the current passing/failing state for VM, JIT, GC, web, Studio, and
  language tests;
- record current interpreter and JIT performance baselines;
- tag every active VM/JIT/GC/verifier change as readiness work, feature work,
  or unrelated maintenance;
- reject new VM/JIT feature work that changes runtime boundaries without a
  readiness-matrix entry.

Completion:

- the blocker table has owners and landing order;
- baseline commands and results are recorded in the tracker;
- no open VM/JIT readiness patch lacks a correctness proof and performance
  note.

Blocker closing protocol:

- the first patch for a blocker lands or updates its proof artifact;
- the fix patch references the blocker ID in the test name, commit message, or
  tracker entry;
- the final patch moves any durable invariant into the focused subsystem doc;
- the blocker is removed from `Current Release Blockers` only after the
  relevant `vo-dev` route or crate test is stable enough to run in CI.

## Workstream 1: Regression Tests First

Goal: each known bug shape becomes executable proof before or with its fix.

Required regression tests:

- interface equality over nested uncomparable dynamic values raises
  `UncomparableType`, in interpreter and supported JIT modes;
- `map[any]` get, set, and delete reject top-level and nested unhashable
  dynamic keys through one shared hashability helper;
- cross-island queue wakes validate queue/select/endpoint wait identity, source,
  generation, and registration before mutating scheduler state;
- transport unpack rejects live endpoint aliases whose handle metadata differs
  from the registered endpoint;
- JIT composite array/slice stores use typed-barrier metadata for 8-byte
  no-ref structs and fixed arrays;
- exported JIT callbacks fail fast on null out pointers, oversized counts, and
  raw-buffer drift before writing memory or publishing effects;
- OSR and callback paths prove borrow discipline through Miri, a debug lease
  guard, or an equivalent source-level invariant test;
- remote endpoint waiters woken by local queue/select operations produce
  `EndpointResponse` commands and preserve `pending_island_responses`
  accounting;
- `wait_for_work` with `main_transport` present still returns
  `SuspendedForHostEvents` for host-event waiters and does not spin forever for
  purely local blocked queues;
- closure calls through interior refs canonicalize the closure object before
  writing callee slot0;
- zero-slot `Call`, `CallClosure`, and `CallIface` buffers are rejected when the
  start register is outside the caller frame;
- `ClosureGet` is rejected outside closure-shaped functions and defensively
  traps on non-closure or too-short runtime objects;
- panic/recover interface roots use the same GC-ref predicate as ordinary
  interface scanning, including `ValueKind::Island`;
- JIT `GoStart` and `GoIsland` side effects followed by panic or JIT error have
  interpreter-equivalent commit/discard behavior;
- OSR dynamic prepared calls refresh cached stack bases after any helper that
  can reallocate the fiber stack;
- every production blocker above has at least one focused crate test or
  manifest-driven language test selected by the production gate.

Completion:

- every test fails against the buggy shape or is paired with a source comment
  explaining why current source already blocks the shape;
- each fix turns only its targeted tests green before broader cleanup.

## Workstream 2: Runtime Boundary Closure

Goal: the runtime-boundary architecture is true for all active sources, not
only the main happy paths.

Tasks:

- define one pending-effect object that owns pending transitions, spawns,
  wakes, host commands, and root effects until boundary application;
- define one resume owner for `RuntimeTransition::resume`; either the applier
  applies it or the boundary producer must prove that PC/frame state was already
  updated before the transition is returned;
- make `Panic`, `CallClosure`, `Interrupted`, `JitError`, and `FatalInfra`
  terminal policies explicit and tested;
- prevent queue/select object mutation from being half-committed without the
  corresponding scheduler wake;
- prevent remote endpoint waiters from using bare `WakeFiber`; response-bearing
  waits must route through endpoint responses with endpoint ID, wait ID, and
  response accounting;
- route remote island spawn, transfer, endpoint close, and wake commands
  through the same command bridge or explicitly documented applier adapter;
- remove `HostCommand` and `RuntimeBoundary::Wait` if no complete current
  producer exists, or implement them with scheduler state mutation, validation,
  and tests;
- ensure every external command validates source, generation, registration,
  and source token before mutation.

Architecture gate:

- no replaced semantic event has both a direct mutation path and a
  transition/command path active.

Performance gate:

- interpreter dispatch does not gain per-instruction heap allocation, trait
  object dispatch, or broad virtualized opcode handling.

## Workstream 3: Shared Verifier Safety

Goal: `Vm::load` is the bytecode safety boundary for VM-visible layout facts.

Tasks:

- reject zero-width ranges whose start register is outside the owning frame,
  even when the arg/return count is zero;
- cross-check `ClosureNew` operand capture count with target function capture
  layout;
- require `ClosureGet` only in closure-shaped functions and align its static
  capture metadata with the runtime object checks used before capture reads;
- cross-check map key/value slot counts, RTTID, and metadata layouts;
- cross-check queue element slot count with element metadata and GC scan
  layout;
- define heap-return `GcRef` initialization and nil semantics, then enforce
  them in verifier and unwind paths;
- extract shared layout helpers for interface pairs, composite slot widths,
  closure captures, map keys, queue elements, heap returns, and extern return
  layouts;
- keep JIT-only policy in `vo-jit`, after the shared verifier accepts the
  module.

Architecture gate:

- every runtime object constructor with layout operands has a verifier
  cross-check against the metadata the runtime will later trust.

Performance gate:

- layout walking happens at load time unless a cold runtime error path is
  required for defensive validation.

## Workstream 4: GC Root Soundness

Goal: no GC phase can observe stale root state after a boundary mutation.

Tasks:

- audit every `GcRootEffect::None` transition and document the proof that it
  does not change roots;
- replace GC-sensitive uses of hand-written interface ref-kind lists with the
  same predicate used by ordinary interface scanning;
- add forced-GC tests for go, spawn, closure replay, extern wait, host wait,
  I/O ready, queue/select, endpoint response, island commands, JIT side exit,
  and JIT materialization;
- include panic/recover payloads in the forced-GC matrix for every
  root-bearing `ValueKind`, including `Island`;
- ensure pending transition payloads that may survive to GC are typed roots or
  consumed before GC can run;
- ensure replay logs, wait payloads, endpoint payloads, queue sender payloads,
  and JIT extern suspend values expose precise slot metadata;
- canonicalize `GcRef` values before object header reads and reject invalid
  refs through structured errors where user input can reach the path.

Architecture gate:

- root-bearing state has one of: live fiber/root owner, typed pending-root
  owner, dirty-root effect before GC, or synchronous consumption before GC.

Performance gate:

- `AllRootsDirty` is allowed as a conservative bridge, but each permanent use
  needs a measured justification or a tracked follow-up for a narrower domain.

## Workstream 5: Scheduler And Wake Model

Goal: wake behavior is deterministic under stale, duplicate, cancelled, and
wrong-source commands.

Tasks:

- build model tests for wait, wake, cancel, close, duplicate wake, stale
  generation, source mismatch, registration mismatch, and select-case mismatch;
- model host-event waiters together with island transport presence so transport
  polling cannot starve host-event suspension or deadlock detection;
- make queue/select, host, I/O, endpoint, island, and app-runtime wake paths
  use the same identity model;
- distinguish response-bearing endpoint waiters from ordinary queue waiters in
  the wake model;
- make public API names match behavior, especially snapshot-vs-take surfaces;
- verify rejected commands leave blocked counts, registrations, endpoint
  counters, replay state, and run queues unchanged.

Architecture gate:

- every cross-turn wake carries generation-bearing fiber identity plus source
  and registration identity.

Performance gate:

- common wake paths do not scan unrelated waiter classes.

## Workstream 6: JIT Production Gate

Goal: JIT is production-enabled only where it is semantically equivalent to the
interpreter and contract-checked at compile/runtime boundaries.

Tasks:

- make missing module context in effect queries an error, not a static fallback;
- derive call-family and may-call consumers from semantic rows instead of
  duplicated opcode matches;
- align JIT callback manifests, runtime dependency rows, and callback callsites;
- encode whether a runtime helper may continue, must form a scheduling
  boundary, or may reallocate the fiber stack;
- ensure JIT/OSR transport values decode into the same `RuntimeTransition`
  shape as interpreter and extern bridge outcomes;
- prove `GoStart` and `GoIsland` publish or discard side effects identically in
  JIT and interpreter paths when followed by panic or infrastructure errors;
- refresh or invalidate cached OSR stack bases after every helper path that can
  reallocate the fiber stack;
- run every VM regression in interpreter and JIT modes where the opcode is
  production-supported;
- treat fatal infra as contract drift, not a legal side exit or silent
  fallback.

Architecture gate:

- `vo-jit/src/semantics` remains the only opcode fact source.

Performance gate:

- direct JIT calls, OSR, inline caches, and intrinsic fast paths remain enabled
  only when their semantic contracts are proven.

## Workstream 7: Unsafe, Fuzz, And Sanitizers

Goal: catch boundary combinations not covered by hand-written tests.

Tasks:

- add bytecode verifier fuzzing that produces valid, invalid, and near-valid
  modules; accepted modules must not panic the VM;
- add scheduler model fuzzing over waits, wakes, close, cancel, and stale keys;
- add GC stress fuzzing that inserts GC steps around allocations and boundary
  returns;
- run Miri or sanitizer subsets for unsafe-heavy VM/runtime object tests where
  tool support is practical;
- reduce every crash to a deterministic regression before landing the fix.

Architecture gate:

- fuzz harnesses live in the existing test/task system, not as detached local
  scripts.

Performance gate:

- fuzz-only instrumentation does not affect release builds or normal test
  throughput outside the dedicated task group.

## Workstream 8: Production Gate In `vo-dev`

Goal: one command proves readiness at the level expected before a release.

Target task:

```sh
cargo run -q -p vo-dev -- task run vm-production
```

The task should compose existing and new checks rather than duplicating command
policy in shell scripts.

Current gate baseline, 2026-06-15:

- common-core verifier, VM, JIT, runtime, and GC cargo contract tests;
- stdlib extern manifest/provider contract tests and WASM provider contract
  tests through `stdlib-contract`;
- deterministic fuzz-like smoke for verifier module shapes, scheduler wake
  bookkeeping, and GC model root preservation;
- VM/JIT/OSR/nostd/wasm language matrices, including runtime-contract and
  JIT-contract subsets;
- GC stress repeat and JIT/OSR stress repeat language tests;
- runtime GC model tests;
- benchmark manifest lint, VM/JIT hot-loop benchmark smoke, and GC root-table
  perf smoke.

This baseline has expanded to include the active blocker proof artifacts:

- interface comparability and `map[any]` hashability regressions;
- queue wake identity model tests for wrong wait/source/registration cases;
- transport handle validation tests for mismatched endpoint metadata;
- JIT typed-barrier lowering and GC stress coverage for composite stores;
- callback ABI drift tests and boundary-owner guards;
- OSR borrow-discipline guards;
- remote endpoint response accounting model tests;
- host-event plus island-transport scheduler tests;
- zero-slot range and `ClosureGet` verifier rejection tests;
- closure canonical slot0 call/replay tests;
- panic/recover interface root matrix for root-bearing `ValueKind`s;
- JIT `GoStart`/`GoIsland` terminal parity tests;
- OSR prepared-call stack-reallocation stress tests;
- docs lint coverage for the updated signoff-withdrawal/startup-row claim and
  closure evidence.

Closed closure items, 2026-06-14:

- GATE-001 is closed by adding `stdlib-contract` to `vm-production`.
- CI-TOOL-001 is closed by routing task tool bootstrap/check through
  `vo-dev`, including pinned `hyperfine` bootstrap.
- GATE-002 is closed by adding the `Production Readiness` GitHub Actions
  workflow for the named `vm-production` gate.

Future gate expansions:

- external fuzz and sanitizer smoke tasks;
- historical benchmark threshold comparison for interpreter hot loop, JIT
  compile, JIT execution, scheduler wake, and GC stress paths;
- docs/readiness lint that checks this plan's blocker IDs are either open in
  the tracker or marked complete with tests.

Completion:

- the task is defined in `eng/tasks.toml` and routed through `cmd/vo-dev`;
- the release-grade `full` validation surface expands through the production
  gate;
- failure output points to the owning workstream and blocker ID.

## Landing Order

The first wave should be small enough to review but strong enough to change the
readiness posture.

Wave 0: governance reset.

1. Keep the signoff withdrawal in this document until the active blocker table
   is empty or explicitly scoped out of production.
2. Create or update the readiness tracker from `Current Release Blockers`.
3. Record the current focused test results for VM, JIT, GC, verifier, and docs.
4. Record interpreter, scheduler, GC stress, JIT compile, and JIT execution
   baselines before hot-path edits.

Wave 1: tests before fixes.

1. Add failing or source-proving tests for all active blockers.
2. Ensure each test name or tracker entry includes the blocker ID.
3. Wire each proof artifact into the smallest stable crate, language, model, or
   `vo-dev` route that will eventually be selected by `vm-production`.

Wave 2: safety-critical fixes.

1. Fix VM-IFACE-UNCOMPARABLE-001 and VM-MAP-ANY-HASHABLE-001 first, since they
   are ordinary user-language semantic bugs with direct interpreter reachability.
2. Fix VM-WAKE-QUEUE-IDENTITY-001 and VM-TRANSPORT-HANDLE-VALIDATION-001 next,
   since they affect scheduler progress, cross-island identity, and endpoint
   layout trust.
3. Fix VM-JIT-TYPED-BARRIER-001 before any GC/JIT signoff claim, because it is
   the active precise-GC parity gap between interpreter and JIT stores.

Wave 3: JIT and OSR parity.

1. Keep JIT production status conditional until VM-JIT-CALLBACK-BOUNDARY-001,
   VM-OSR-BORROW-BOUNDARY-001, and VM-JIT-TYPED-BARRIER-001 have parity,
   fail-fast, and stress coverage.
2. Encode helper boundary, callback ABI, typed-barrier, and stack-reallocation
   facts in the JIT contract so future callbacks cannot silently choose the
   wrong policy.
3. Run every relevant VM regression in interpreter, JIT, and OSR modes where
   the opcode is production-supported.

Wave 4: architecture consolidation.

1. Collapse interface comparability and hashability around one recursive
   fallible helper used by equality and map operations.
2. Collapse wake identity around endpoint responses, ordinary queue wakes,
   select waits, and island wake commands.
3. Collapse call-frame construction around one fallible prepared-frame helper.
4. Collapse interface GC-ref decisions around one scanning predicate.
5. Collapse runtime-transition resume ownership around one boundary policy.
6. Collapse JIT callback ABI facts around semantic rows or an explicit helper
   contract.

Wave 5: production gate and clean-runner signoff.

1. Add the new proof artifacts to `vm-production` through `eng/tasks.toml` and
   `cmd/vo-dev`.
2. Add a readiness-doc lint or task check that prevents a false "no active
   blockers" claim.
3. Run focused checks first, then `vm-production`, `contract`, `site`, and
   `release-verify` on a clean checkout.
4. Treat performance benchmark regressions as release blockers unless accepted
   with measurements and owner signoff.

## Definition Of Done

The readiness program is complete only when:

- `Current Release Blockers` is empty or every remaining item is explicitly
  classified as non-production scope with a disabled feature gate;
- the production gate passes on a clean checkout;
- PR CI and the production-readiness workflow pass on a clean GitHub runner
  using only declared `vo-dev` task/tool bootstrap policy;
- every accepted bytecode fuzz case either executes without host panic or is
  reduced to a structured VM/language error;
- every runtime-boundary invariant has at least one focused regression and one
  source-level owner;
- every GC-visible boundary has forced-GC coverage or a documented synchronous
  no-GC proof;
- every JIT production opcode has interpreter/JIT parity coverage;
- performance benchmarks show no unaccepted regression for interpreter hot
  loop, JIT compile, JIT execution, scheduler wake, and GC stress paths;
- docs that claim implementation status point to the tests or tasks that prove
  the claim.
